this option. Don’t use if you do not understand the full consequence of doing so.
Default is INT_MAX.

options.name: generic
options.description:
These options can be set for any container, codec or device. Generic options
are listed under AVFormatContext options for containers/devices and under
AVCodecContext options for codecs.

options.name: private
options.description:
These options are specific to the given container, device or codec. Private
options are listed under their corresponding containers/devices/codecs.

options.name: -x width
options.description: Force displayed width.

options.name: -y height
options.description: Force displayed height.

options.name: -fs
options.description: Start in fullscreen mode.

options.name: -an
options.description: Disable audio.

options.name: -vn
options.description: Disable video.

options.name: -sn
options.description: Disable subtitles.

options.name: -ss pos
options.description:
Seek to pos . Note that in most formats it is not possible to seek
exactly, so ffplay will seek to the nearest seek point to pos .
pos must be a time duration specification,
see (ffmpeg-utils)the Time duration section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual .

options.name: -t duration
options.description:
Play duration seconds of audio/video.
duration must be a time duration specification,
see (ffmpeg-utils)the Time duration section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual .

options.name: -bytes
options.description: Seek by bytes.

options.name: -seek_interval
options.description: Set custom interval, in seconds, for seeking using left/right keys. Default is 10 seconds.

options.name: -nodisp
options.description: Disable graphical display.

options.name: -noborder
options.description: Borderless window.

options.name: -alwaysontop
options.description: Window always on top. Available on: X11 with SDL >= 2.0.5, Windows SDL >= 2.0.6.

options.name: -volume
options.description:
Set the startup volume. 0 means silence, 100 means no volume reduction or
amplification. Negative values are treated as 0, values above 100 are treated
as 100.

options.name: -f fmt
options.description: Force format.

options.name: -window_title title
options.description: Set window title (default is the input filename).

options.name: -left title
options.description: Set the x position for the left of the window (default is a centered window).

options.name: -top title
options.description: Set the y position for the top of the window (default is a centered window).

options.name: -loop number
options.description: Loops movie playback <number> times. 0 means forever.

options.name: -showmode mode
options.description:
Set the show mode to use.
Available values for mode are:
Default value is "video", if video is not present or cannot be played
"rdft" is automatically selected.
You can interactively cycle through the available show modes by
pressing the key w .
options.default: "video", if video is not present or cannot be played

possible_values.description: show audio frequency band using RDFT ((Inverse) Real Discrete Fourier Transform)

options.name: -vf filtergraph
options.description:
Create the filtergraph specified by filtergraph and use it to
filter the video stream.
filtergraph is a description of the filtergraph to apply to
the stream, and must have a single video input and a single video
output. In the filtergraph, the input is associated to the label in , and the output to the label out . See the
ffmpeg-filters manual for more information about the filtergraph
syntax.
You can specify this parameter multiple times and cycle through the specified
filtergraphs along with the show modes by pressing the key w .

options.name: -af filtergraph
options.description:
filtergraph is a description of the filtergraph to apply to
the input audio.
Use the option "-filters" to show all the available filters (including
sources and sinks).

options.name: -i input_url
options.description: Read input_url .

options.name: -stats
options.description:
Print several playback statistics, in particular show the stream
duration, the codec parameters, the current position in the stream and
the audio/video synchronisation drift. It is shown by default, unless the
log level is lower than info . Its display can be forced by manually
specifying this option. To disable it, you need to specify -nostats .

options.name: -fast
options.description: Non-spec-compliant optimizations.

options.name: -genpts
options.description: Generate pts.

options.name: -sync type
options.description:
Set the master clock to audio ( type=audio ), video
( type=video ) or external ( type=ext ). Default is audio. The
master clock is used to control audio-video synchronization. Most media
players use audio as master clock, but in some cases (streaming or high
quality broadcast) it is necessary to change that. This option is mainly
used for debugging purposes.

options.name: -ast audio_stream_specifier
options.description:
Select the desired audio stream using the given stream specifier. The stream
specifiers are described in the Stream specifiers chapter. If this option
is not specified, the "best" audio stream is selected in the program of the
already selected video stream.

options.name: -vst video_stream_specifier
options.description:
Select the desired video stream using the given stream specifier. The stream
specifiers are described in the Stream specifiers chapter. If this option
is not specified, the "best" video stream is selected.

options.name: -sst subtitle_stream_specifier
options.description:
Select the desired subtitle stream using the given stream specifier. The stream
specifiers are described in the Stream specifiers chapter. If this option
is not specified, the "best" subtitle stream is selected in the program of the
already selected video or audio stream.

options.name: -autoexit
options.description: Exit when video is done playing.

options.name: -exitonkeydown
options.description: Exit if any key is pressed.

options.name: -exitonmousedown
options.description: Exit if any mouse button is pressed.

options.name: -codec: media_specifier codec_name
options.description: Force a specific decoder implementation for the stream identified by media_specifier , which can assume the values a (audio), v (video), and s subtitle.

options.name: -acodec codec_name
options.description: Force a specific audio decoder.

options.name: -vcodec codec_name
options.description: Force a specific video decoder.

options.name: -scodec codec_name
options.description: Force a specific subtitle decoder.

options.name: -autorotate
options.description:
Automatically rotate the video according to file metadata. Enabled by
default, use -noautorotate to disable it.

options.name: -framedrop
options.description:
Drop video frames if video is out of sync. Enabled by default if the master
clock is not set to video. Use this option to enable frame dropping for all
master clock sources, use -noframedrop to disable it.

options.name: -infbuf
options.description:
Do not limit the input buffer size, read as much data as possible from the
input as soon as possible. Enabled by default for realtime streams, where data
may be dropped if not read in time. Use this option to enable infinite buffers
for all inputs, use -noinfbuf to disable it.

options.name: -filter_threads nb_threads
options.description:
Defines how many threads are used to process a filter pipeline. Each pipeline
will produce a thread pool with this many threads available for parallel
processing. The default is 0 which means that the thread count will be
determined by the number of available CPUs.
options.default: 0 which means that the thread count will be

options.name: -enable_vulkan
options.description: Use vulkan renderer rather than SDL builtin renderer. Depends on libplacebo.

options.name: -vulkan_params
options.description:
Vulkan configuration using a list of key = value pairs separated by
":".

options.name: -hwaccel
options.description:
Use HW accelerated decoding. Enable this option will enable vulkan renderer
automatically.

options.name: -video_bg pattern
options.description:
Set the video background pattern used for transparent videos. For solid color
patterns you can use the color name or color code as specified in the (ffmpeg-utils)Color section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual .
You can also use the special tiles keyword for a checker board style
pattern. This is also the default. The none pattern does rendering
without alpha blending effectively ignoring alpha channel.

order of the streams as detected by libavformat except when a stream group

value. The colon character ’:’ in key or value needs to be

Some devices may provide system-dependent source names that cannot be autodetected.
The returned list cannot be assumed to be always complete.

options.name: -sinks device...]
options.description:
Show autodetected sinks of the output device.
Some devices may provide system-dependent sink names that cannot be autodetected.
The returned list cannot be assumed to be always complete.

options.name: -loglevel loglevel | -v loglevel
options.description:
Set logging level and flags used by the library.
The optional flags prefix can consist of the following values:
Flags can also be used alone by adding a ’+’/’-’ prefix to set/reset a single
flag without affecting other flags or changing loglevel . When
setting both flags and loglevel , a ’+’ separator is expected
between the last flags value and before loglevel .
loglevel is a string or a number containing one of the following values:
For example to enable repeated log output, add the level prefix, and set loglevel to verbose :
Another example that enables repeated log output without affecting current
state of level prefix flag or loglevel :
By default the program logs to stderr. If coloring is supported by the
terminal, colors are used to mark errors and warnings. Log coloring
can be disabled setting the environment variable AV_LOG_FORCE_NOCOLOR , or can be forced setting
the environment variable AV_LOG_FORCE_COLOR .

possible_values.description:
Indicates that repeated log output should not be compressed to the first line
and the "Last message repeated n times" line will be omitted.

Indicates that log output should add a [level] prefix to each message
line. This can be used as an alternative to log coloring, e.g. when dumping the
log to file.
Indicates that log lines should be prefixed with time information.
Indicates that log lines should be prefixed with date and time information.

options.name: -report
options.description:
Dump full command line and log output to a file named program - YYYYMMDD - HHMMSS .log in the current
directory.
This file can be useful for bug reports.
It also implies -loglevel debug .
Setting the environment variable FFREPORT to any value has the
same effect. If the value is a ’:’-separated key=value sequence, these
options will affect the report; option values must be escaped if they
contain special characters or the options delimiter ’:’ (see the
“Quoting and escaping” section in the ffmpeg-utils manual).
The following options are recognized:
For example, to output a report to a file named ffreport.log using a log level of 32 (alias for log level info ):
Errors in parsing the environment variable are not fatal, and will not
appear in the report.

possible_values.description:
set the file name to use for the report; %p is expanded to the name
of the program, %t is expanded to a timestamp, %% is expanded
to a plain %
set the log verbosity level using a numerical value (see -loglevel ).

options.name: -hide_banner
options.description:
Suppress printing banner.
All FFmpeg tools will normally show a copyright notice, build options
and library versions. This option can be used to suppress printing
this information.

options.name: -cpuflags flags
options.description:
Allows setting and clearing cpu flags. This option is intended
for testing. Do not use it unless you know what you’re doing.
Possible flags for this option are:

options.name: -cpucount count
options.description:
Override detection of CPU count. This option is intended
for testing. Do not use it unless you know what you’re doing.

options.name: -max_alloc bytes
options.description:
Set the maximum size limit for allocating a block on the heap by ffmpeg’s
family of malloc functions. Exercise extreme caution when using
this option. Don’t use if you do not understand the full consequence of doing so.
Default is INT_MAX.

options.name: generic
options.description:
These options can be set for any container, codec or device. Generic options
are listed under AVFormatContext options for containers/devices and under
AVCodecContext options for codecs.

options.name: private
options.description:
These options are specific to the given container, device or codec. Private
options are listed under their corresponding containers/devices/codecs.

options.name: -f format
options.description: Force format to use.

options.name: -unit
options.description: Show the unit of the displayed values.

options.name: -prefix
options.description:
Use SI prefixes for the displayed values.
Unless the "-byte_binary_prefix" option is used all the prefixes
are decimal.

options.name: -byte_binary_prefix
options.description: Force the use of binary prefixes for byte values.

options.name: -sexagesimal
options.description: Use sexagesimal format HH:MM:SS.MICROSECONDS for time values.

options.name: -pretty
options.description:
Prettify the format of the displayed values, it corresponds to the
options "-unit -prefix -byte_binary_prefix -sexagesimal".

options.name: -output_format
options.description:
Set the output printing format.
writer_name specifies the name of the writer, and writer_options specifies the options to be passed to the writer.
For example for printing the output in JSON format, specify:
For more details on the available output printing formats, see the
Writers section below.

options.name: -sections
options.description:
Print sections structure and section information, and exit. The output
is not meant to be parsed by a machine.

options.name: -select_streams stream_specifier
options.description:
Select only the streams specified by stream_specifier . This
option affects only the options related to streams
(e.g. show_streams , show_packets , etc.).
For example to show only audio streams, you can use the command:
To show only video packets belonging to the video stream with index 1:

options.name: -show_data
options.description:
Show payload data, as a hexadecimal and ASCII dump (other formats can be
selected using -data_dump_format ). Coupled with -show_packets , it will dump the packets’ data. Coupled with -show_streams , it will dump the codec extradata.
The dump is printed as the "data" field. It may contain newlines.

options.name: -show_data_hash algorithm
options.description:
Show a hash of payload data, for packets with -show_packets and for
codec extradata with -show_streams .

options.name: -data_dump_format format
options.description:
Select a format used for the data dumps enabled with the -show_data option. The default is xxd which is a hexdump format compatible with the
well-known xxd program. base64 is also supported.
options.default: xxd which is a hexdump format compatible with the

options.name: -show_error
options.description:
Show information about the error found when trying to probe the input.
The error information is printed within a section with name "ERROR".

options.name: -show_format
options.description:
Show information about the container format of the input multimedia
stream.
All the container format information is printed within a section with
name "FORMAT".

options.name: -show_entries section_entries
options.description:
Set list of entries to show.
Entries are specified according to the following
syntax. section_entries contains a list of section entries
separated by : . Each section entry is composed by a section
name (or unique name), optionally followed by a list of entries local
to that section, separated by , .
If section name is specified but is followed by no = , all
entries are printed to output, together with all the contained
sections. Otherwise only the entries specified in the local section
entries list are printed. In particular, if = is specified but
the list of local entries is empty, then no entries will be shown for
that section.
Note that the order of specification of the local section entries is
not honored in the output, and the usual display order will be
retained.
The formal syntax is given by:
For example, to show only the index and type of each stream, and the PTS
time, duration time, and stream index of the packets, you can specify
the argument:
To show all the entries in the section "format", but only the codec
type in the section "stream", specify the argument:
To show all the tags in the stream and format sections:
To show only the title tag (if available) in the stream
sections:

options.name: -show_packets
options.description:
Show information about each packet contained in the input multimedia
stream.
The information for each single packet is printed within a dedicated
section with name "PACKET".

options.name: -show_frames
options.description:
Show information about each frame and subtitle contained in the input
multimedia stream.
The information for each single frame is printed within a dedicated
section with name "FRAME" or "SUBTITLE".

options.name: -show_log loglevel
options.description:
Show logging information from the decoder about each frame according to
the value set in loglevel , (see -loglevel ). This option requires -show_frames .
The information for each log message is printed within a dedicated
section with name "LOG".

options.name: -show_streams
options.description:
Show information about each media stream contained in the input
multimedia stream.
Each media stream information is printed within a dedicated section
with name "STREAM".

options.name: -show_programs
options.description:
Show information about programs and their streams contained in the input
multimedia stream.
Each media stream information is printed within a dedicated section
with name "PROGRAM_STREAM".

options.name: -show_stream_groups
options.description:
Show information about stream groups and their streams contained in the
input multimedia stream.
Each media stream information is printed within a dedicated section
with name "STREAM_GROUP_STREAM".

options.name: -show_chapters
options.description:
Show information about chapters stored in the format.
Each chapter is printed within a dedicated section with name "CHAPTER".

options.name: -count_frames
options.description:
Count the number of frames per stream and report it in the
corresponding stream section.

options.name: -count_packets
options.description:
Count the number of packets per stream and report it in the
corresponding stream section.

options.name: -read_intervals read_intervals
options.description:
Read only the specified intervals. read_intervals must be a
sequence of interval specifications separated by ",". ffprobe will seek to the interval starting point, and will
continue reading from that.
Each interval is specified by two optional parts, separated by "%".
The first part specifies the interval start position. It is
interpreted as an absolute position, or as a relative offset from the
current position if it is preceded by the "+" character. If this first
part is not specified, no seeking will be performed when reading this
interval.
The second part specifies the interval end position. It is interpreted
as an absolute position, or as a relative offset from the current
position if it is preceded by the "+" character. If the offset
specification starts with "#", it is interpreted as the number of
packets to read (not including the flushing packets) from the interval
start. If no second part is specified, the program will read until the
end of the input.
Note that seeking is not accurate, thus the actual interval start
point may be different from the specified position. Also, when an
interval duration is specified, the absolute end time will be computed
by adding the duration to the interval start point found by seeking
the file, rather than to the specified start value.
The formal syntax is given by:
A few examples follow.

options.name: -show_private_data
options.description:
Show private data, that is data depending on the format of the
particular shown element.
This option is enabled by default, but you may need to disable it
for specific uses, for example when creating XSD-compliant XML output.

options.name: -show_program_version
options.description:
Show information related to program version.
Version information is printed within a section with name
"PROGRAM_VERSION".

options.name: -show_library_versions
options.description:
Show information related to library versions.
Version information for each library is printed within a section with
name "LIBRARY_VERSION".

options.name: -show_versions
options.description:
Show information related to program and library versions. This is the
equivalent of setting both -show_program_version and -show_library_versions options.

options.name: -show_pixel_formats
options.description:
Show information about all pixel formats supported by FFmpeg.
Pixel format information for each format is printed within a section
with name "PIXEL_FORMAT".

options.name: -show_optional_fields value
options.description:
Some writers viz. JSON and XML, omit the printing of fields with invalid or non-applicable values,
while other writers always print them. This option enables one to control this behaviour.
Valid values are always / 1 , never / 0 and auto / -1 .
Default is auto .

options.name: -analyze_frames
options.description:
Analyze frames and/or their side data up to the provided read interval,
providing additional information that may be useful at a stream level.
Must be paired with the -show_streams option or it will have no effect.
Currently, the additional fields provided by this option when enabled are the closed_captions and film_grain fields.
For example, to analyze the first 20 seconds and populate these fields:

options.name: -bitexact
options.description:
Force bitexact output, useful to produce output which is not dependent
on the specific build.

options.name: -i input_url
options.description: Read input_url .

options.name: -o output_url
options.description:
Write output to output_url . If not specified, the output is sent
to stdout.

options.name: -c: media_specifier codec_name
options.description: Force a specific decoder implementation for the stream identified by media_specifier , which can assume the values a (audio), v (video), s (subtitle), and d (data).

options.name: string_validation
options.description:
Set string validation mode.
The following values are accepted.
Default value is ‘ replace ’.

possible_values.description:
The writer will fail immediately in case an invalid string (UTF-8)
sequence or code point is found in the input. This is especially
useful to validate input metadata.

Any validation error will be ignored. This will result in possibly
broken output, especially with the json or xml writer.

The writer will substitute invalid UTF-8 sequences or code points with
the string specified with the string_validation_replacement .

options.name: string_validation_replacement
options.description:
Set replacement string to use in case string_validation is
set to ‘ replace ’.
In case the option is not specified, the writer will assume the empty
string, that is it will remove the invalid sequences from the input
strings.

options.name: nokey
options.description:
If set to 1 specify not to print the key of each field. Default value
is 0.

options.name: noprint_wrappers
options.description:
If set to 1 specify not to print the section header and footer.
Default value is 0.

options.name: item_sep
options.description:
Specify the character to use for separating fields in the output line.
It must be a single printable character, it is "|" by default ("," for
the csv writer).

options.name: nokey
options.description:
If set to 1 specify not to print the key of each field. Its default
value is 0 (1 for the csv writer).

options.name: escape
options.description:
Set the escape mode to use, default to "c" ("csv" for the csv writer).
It can assume one of the following values:

possible_values.description:
Perform C-like escaping. Strings containing a newline (‘ \n ’), carriage
return (‘ \r ’), a tab (‘ \t ’), a form feed (‘ \f ’), the escaping
character (‘ \ ’) or the item separator character SEP are escaped
using C-like fashioned escaping, so that a newline is converted to the
sequence ‘ \n ’, a carriage return to ‘ \r ’, ‘ \ ’ to ‘ \\ ’ and
the separator SEP is converted to ‘ \ SEP ’.

Perform CSV-like escaping, as described in RFC4180.  Strings
containing a newline (‘ \n ’), a carriage return (‘ \r ’), a double quote
(‘ " ’), or SEP are enclosed in double-quotes.

options.name: print_section
options.description: Print the section name at the beginning of each line if the value is 1 , disable it with value set to 0 . Default value is 1 .

options.name: sep_char
options.description:
Separator character used to separate the chapter, the section name, IDs and
potential tags in the printed field key.
Default value is ‘ . ’.

options.name: hierarchical
options.description:
Specify if the section name specification should be hierarchical. If
set to 1, and if there is more than one section in the current
chapter, the section name will be prefixed by the name of the
chapter. A value of 0 will disable this behavior.
Default value is 1.

options.name: hierarchical
options.description:
Specify if the section name specification should be hierarchical. If
set to 1, and if there is more than one section in the current
chapter, the section name will be prefixed by the name of the
chapter. A value of 0 will disable this behavior.
Default value is 1.

options.name: compact
options.description:
If set to 1 enable compact output, that is each section will be
printed on a single line. Default value is 0.

options.name: fully_qualified
options.description:
If set to 1 specify if the output should be fully qualified. Default
value is 0.
This is required for generating an XML file which can be validated
through an XSD file.

options.name: xsd_strict
options.description:
If set to 1 perform more checks for ensuring that the output is XSD
compliant. Default value is 0.
This option automatically sets fully_qualified to 1.

options.name: abs
options.description: Compute absolute value of x .

options.name: acos
options.description: Compute arccosine of x .

options.name: asin
options.description: Compute arcsine of x .

options.name: atan
options.description: Compute arctangent of x .

options.name: atan2
options.description: Compute principal value of the arc tangent of y / x .

options.name: between
options.description:
Return 1 if x is greater than or equal to min and lesser than or
equal to max , 0 otherwise.

options.name: bitand
options.description:
Compute bitwise and/or operation on x and y .
The results of the evaluation of x and y are converted to
integers before executing the bitwise operation.
Note that both the conversion to integer and the conversion back to
floating point can lose precision. Beware of unexpected results for
large numbers (usually 2^53 and larger).

options.name: ceil
options.description:
Round the value of expression expr upwards to the nearest
integer. For example, "ceil(1.5)" is "2.0".

options.name: clip
options.description: Return the value of x clipped between min and max .

options.name: cos
options.description: Compute cosine of x .

options.name: cosh
options.description: Compute hyperbolic cosine of x .

options.name: eq
options.description: Return 1 if x and y are equivalent, 0 otherwise.

options.name: exp
options.description: Compute exponential of x (with base e , the Euler’s number).

options.name: floor
options.description:
Round the value of expression expr downwards to the nearest
integer. For example, "floor(-1.5)" is "-2.0".

options.name: gauss
options.description: Compute Gauss function of x , corresponding to exp(-x*x/2) / sqrt(2*PI) .

options.name: gcd
options.description: Return the greatest common divisor of x and y . If both x and y are 0 or either or both are less than zero then behavior is undefined.

options.name: gt
options.description: Return 1 if x is greater than y , 0 otherwise.

options.name: gte
options.description: Return 1 if x is greater than or equal to y , 0 otherwise.

options.name: hypot
options.description:
This function is similar to the C function with the same name; it returns
"sqrt( x * x + y * y )", the length of the hypotenuse of a
right triangle with sides of length x and y , or the distance of the
point ( x , y ) from the origin.

options.name: if
options.description:
Evaluate x , and if the result is non-zero return the result of
the evaluation of y , return 0 otherwise.

options.name: if
options.description:
Evaluate x , and if the result is non-zero return the evaluation
result of y , otherwise the evaluation result of z .

options.name: ifnot
options.description:
Evaluate x , and if the result is zero return the result of the
evaluation of y , return 0 otherwise.

options.name: ifnot
options.description:
Evaluate x , and if the result is zero return the evaluation
result of y , otherwise the evaluation result of z .

options.name: isinf
options.description: Return 1.0 if x is +/-INFINITY, 0.0 otherwise.

options.name: isnan
options.description: Return 1.0 if x is NAN, 0.0 otherwise.

options.name: ld
options.description:
Load the value of the internal variable with index idx , which was
previously stored with st( idx , expr ).
The function returns the loaded value.

options.name: lerp
options.description: Return linear interpolation between x and y by amount of z .

options.name: log
options.description: Compute natural logarithm of x .

options.name: lt
options.description: Return 1 if x is lesser than y , 0 otherwise.

options.name: lte
options.description: Return 1 if x is lesser than or equal to y , 0 otherwise.

options.name: max
options.description: Return the maximum between x and y .

options.name: min
options.description: Return the minimum between x and y .

options.name: mod
options.description: Compute the remainder of division of x by y .

options.name: not
options.description: Return 1.0 if expr is zero, 0.0 otherwise.

options.name: pow
options.description:
Compute the power of x elevated y , it is equivalent to
"( x )^( y )".

options.name: print
options.description:
Print the value of expression t with loglevel l . If l is not
specified then a default log level is used.
Return the value of the expression printed.

options.name: random
options.description:
Return a pseudo random value between 0.0 and 1.0. idx is the
index of the internal variable used to save the seed/state, which can be
previously stored with st(idx) .
To initialize the seed, you need to store the seed value as a 64-bit
unsigned integer in the internal variable with index idx .
For example, to store the seed with value 42 in the internal
variable with index 0 and print a few random values:

options.name: randomi
options.description:
Return a pseudo random value in the interval between min and max . idx is the index of the internal variable which will be used to
save the seed/state, which can be previously stored with st(idx) .
To initialize the seed, you need to store the seed value as a 64-bit
unsigned integer in the internal variable with index idx .

options.name: root
options.description:
Find an input value for which the function represented by expr with argument ld(0) is 0 in the interval 0.. max .
The expression in expr must denote a continuous function or the
result is undefined.
ld(0) is used to represent the function input value, which means that the
given expression will be evaluated multiple times with various input values that
the expression can access through ld(0) . When the expression evaluates to
0 then the corresponding input value will be returned.

options.name: round
options.description:
Round the value of expression expr to the nearest integer. For example,
"round(1.5)" is "2.0".

options.name: sgn
options.description: Compute sign of x .

options.name: sin
options.description: Compute sine of x .

options.name: sinh
options.description: Compute hyperbolic sine of x .

options.name: sqrt
options.description:
Compute the square root of expr . This is equivalent to
"( expr )^.5".

options.name: squish
options.description: Compute expression 1/(1 + exp(4*x)) .

options.name: st
options.description:
Store the value of the expression expr in an internal
variable. idx specifies the index of the variable where to store
the value, and it is a value ranging from 0 to 9. The function returns
the value stored in the internal variable.
The stored value can be retrieved with ld(var) .
Note: variables are currently not shared between expressions.

options.name: tan
options.description: Compute tangent of x .

options.name: tanh
options.description: Compute hyperbolic tangent of x .

options.name: taylor
options.description:
Evaluate a Taylor series at x , given an expression representing
the ld(idx) -th derivative of a function at 0.
When the series does not converge the result is undefined.
ld(idx) is used to represent the derivative order in expr ,
which means that the given expression will be evaluated multiple times
with various input values that the expression can access through ld(idx) . If idx is not specified then 0 is assumed.
Note, when you have the derivatives at y instead of 0, taylor(expr, x-y) can be used.

options.name: time
options.description: Return the current (wallclock) time in seconds.

options.name: trunc
options.description:
Round the value of expression expr towards zero to the nearest
integer. For example, "trunc(-1.5)" is "-1.0".

options.name: while
options.description:
Evaluate expression expr while the expression cond is
non-zero, and returns the value of the last expr evaluation, or
NAN if cond was always false.

options.name: PI
options.description: area of the unit disc, approximately 3.14

options.name: E
options.description: exp(1) (Euler’s number), approximately 2.718

options.name: PHI
options.description: golden ratio (1+sqrt(5))/2, approximately 1.618

options.name: scaler
options.description:
Choose the scaling algorithm to use. Default value is ‘ auto ’ for both.
It accepts the following values:

possible_values.description:
Aumotic choice. For ‘ scaler_sub ’, this means the same algorithm as
‘ scaler ’. For ‘ scaler ’, this defaults to the scaler flag selected
by ‘ sws_flags ’.

2-tap cubic BC-spline (AKA Mitchell-Netravali spline). The B and C parameters
can be configured by setting param0 and param1 , defaulting to
0.0 and 0.6 respectively.
Area averaging. Equivalent to ‘ bilinear ’ for upscaling.

2-tap Gaussian filter approximation. The sharpness parameter can be configured
by setting param0 , defaulting to 3.0.

Lanczos resampling (sinc windowed sinc). The number of filter taps can
be configured by setting param0 , defaulting to 3.

options.name: sws_flags
options.description:
Set the scaler flags. This is also used to set the scaling
algorithm, though this usage is deprecated in favor of setting ‘ scaler ’.
Only a single algorithm may be selected. Default value is ‘ bicubic ’.
It accepts the following values:

possible_values.description:
Select bicubic scaling algorithm for the luma component, bilinear for
chroma components. (Deprecated)

Select Lanczos rescaling algorithm. The default width (alpha) is 3 and can be
changed by setting param0 . (Deprecated)
Select natural bicubic spline rescaling algorithm. (Deprecated)

Allow the use of experimental new code. May subtly affect the output or even
produce wrong results. For testing only.

options.name: srcw
options.description: Set source width.

options.name: srch
options.description: Set source height.

options.name: dstw
options.description: Set destination width.

options.name: dsth
options.description: Set destination height.

options.name: src_format
options.description: Set source pixel format (must be expressed as an integer).

options.name: dst_format
options.description: Set destination pixel format (must be expressed as an integer).

options.name: src_range
options.description: If value is set to 1 , indicates source is full range. Default value is 0 , which indicates source is limited range.
options.default: 0 , which indicates source is limited range

options.name: dst_range
options.description:
If value is set to 1 , enable full range for destination. Default value
is 0 , which enables limited range.

options.name: gamma
options.description: If value is set to 1 , enable gamma correct scaling. Default value is 0 .

options.name: param0
options.description:
Set scaling algorithm parameters. The specified values are specific of
some scaling algorithms and ignored by others. The specified values
are floating point number values.

options.name: sws_dither
options.description:
Set the dithering algorithm. Accepts one of the following
values. Default value is ‘ auto ’.

possible_values.description:
arithmetic dither, based using xor (more random/less apparent patterning that
a_dither).

options.name: alphablend
options.description:
Set the alpha blending to use when the input has alpha but the output does not.
Default value is ‘ none ’.

possible_values.description: Blend onto a uniform background color

options.name: uchl
options.description:
Set used input channel layout. Default is unset. This option is
only used for special remapping.

options.name: isr
options.description: Set the input sample rate. Default value is 0.

options.name: osr
options.description: Set the output sample rate. Default value is 0.

options.name: isf
options.description: Specify the input sample format. It is set by default to none .

options.name: osf
options.description: Specify the output sample format. It is set by default to none .

options.name: tsf
options.description:
Set the internal sample format. Default value is none .
This will automatically be chosen when it is not explicitly set.

options.name: ichl
options.description:
Set the input/output channel layout.
See (ffmpeg-utils)the Channel Layout section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual for the required syntax.

options.name: clev
options.description:
Set the center mix level. It is a value expressed in deciBel, and must be
in the interval [-32,32].

options.name: slev
options.description:
Set the surround mix level. It is a value expressed in deciBel, and must
be in the interval [-32,32].

options.name: lfe_mix_level
options.description:
Set LFE mix into non LFE level. It is used when there is a LFE input but no
LFE output. It is a value expressed in deciBel, and must
be in the interval [-32,32].

options.name: rmvol
options.description: Set rematrix volume. Default value is 1.0.

options.name: rematrix_maxval
options.description:
Set maximum output value for rematrixing.
This can be used to prevent clipping vs. preventing volume reduction.
A value of 1.0 prevents clipping.

options.name: flags
options.description:
Set flags used by the converter. Default value is 0.
It supports the following individual flags:

possible_values.description:
force resampling, this flag forces resampling to be used even when the
input and output sample rates match.

options.name: dither_scale
options.description: Set the dither scale. Default value is 1.

options.name: dither_method
options.description:
Set dither method. Default value is 0.
Supported values:

possible_values.description: select triangular dither with high pass
select low Shibata noise shaping dither.
select high Shibata noise shaping dither.

options.name: resampler
options.description:
Set resampling engine. Default value is swr.
Supported values:

possible_values.description:
select the native SW Resampler; filter options precision and cheby are not
applicable in this case.

select the SoX Resampler (where available); compensation, and filter options
filter_size, phase_shift, exact_rational, filter_type & kaiser_beta, are not
applicable in this case.

options.name: filter_size
options.description: For swr only, set resampling filter size, default value is 32.

options.name: phase_shift
options.description:
For swr only, set resampling phase shift, default value is 10, and must be in
the interval [0,30].

options.name: linear_interp
options.description:
Use linear interpolation when enabled (the default). Disable it if you want
to preserve speed instead of quality when exact_rational fails.

options.name: exact_rational
options.description:
For swr only, when enabled, try to use exact phase_count based on input and
output sample rate. However, if it is larger than 1 << phase_shift ,
the phase_count will be 1 << phase_shift as fallback. Default is enabled.

options.name: cutoff
options.description:
Set cutoff frequency (swr: 6dB point; soxr: 0dB point) ratio; must be a float
value between 0 and 1.  Default value is 0.97 with swr, and 0.91 with soxr
(which, with a sample-rate of 44100, preserves the entire audio band to 20kHz).

options.name: precision
options.description:
For soxr only, the precision in bits to which the resampled signal will be
calculated.  The default value of 20 (which, with suitable dithering, is
appropriate for a destination bit-depth of 16) gives SoX’s ’High Quality’; a
value of 28 gives SoX’s ’Very High Quality’.

options.name: cheby
options.description:
For soxr only, selects passband rolloff none (Chebyshev) & higher-precision
approximation for ’irrational’ ratios. Default value is 0.

options.name: async
options.description:
For swr only, simple 1 parameter audio sync to timestamps using stretching,
squeezing, filling and trimming. Setting this to 1 will enable filling and
trimming, larger values represent the maximum amount in samples that the data
may be stretched or squeezed for each second.
Default value is 0, thus no compensation is applied to make the samples match
the audio timestamps.
options.default: 0, thus no compensation is applied to make the samples match

options.name: first_pts
options.description:
For swr only, assume the first pts should be this value. The time unit is 1 / sample rate.
This allows for padding/trimming at the start of stream. By default, no
assumption is made about the first frame’s expected pts, so no padding or
trimming is done. For example, this could be set to 0 to pad the beginning with
silence if an audio stream starts after the video stream or to trim any samples
with a negative pts due to encoder delay.

options.name: min_comp
options.description:
For swr only, set the minimum difference between timestamps and audio data (in
seconds) to trigger stretching/squeezing/filling or trimming of the
data to make it match the timestamps. The default is that
stretching/squeezing/filling and trimming is disabled
( min_comp = FLT_MAX ).

options.name: min_hard_comp
options.description:
For swr only, set the minimum difference between timestamps and audio data (in
seconds) to trigger adding/dropping samples to make it match the
timestamps.  This option effectively is a threshold to select between
hard (trim/fill) and soft (squeeze/stretch) compensation. Note that
all compensation is by default disabled through min_comp .
The default is 0.1.

options.name: comp_duration
options.description:
For swr only, set duration (in seconds) over which data is stretched/squeezed
to make it match the timestamps. Must be a non-negative double float value,
default value is 1.0.

options.name: max_soft_comp
options.description:
For swr only, set maximum factor by which data is stretched/squeezed to make it
match the timestamps. Must be a non-negative double float value, default value
is 0.

options.name: matrix_encoding
options.description:
Select matrixed stereo encoding.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is none .

options.name: filter_type
options.description:
For swr only, select resampling filter type. This only affects resampling
operations.
It accepts the following values:

options.name: kaiser_beta
options.description:
For swr only, set Kaiser window beta value. Must be a double float value in the
interval [2,16], default value is 9.

options.name: output_sample_bits
options.description:
For swr only, set number of used output sample bits for dithering. Must be an integer in the
interval [0,64], default value is 0, which means it’s not used.

options.name: b
options.description: Set bitrate in bits/s. Default value is 200K.

options.name: ab
options.description: Set audio bitrate (in bits/s). Default value is 128K.

options.name: bt
options.description:
Set video bitrate tolerance (in bits/s). In 1-pass mode, bitrate
tolerance specifies how far ratecontrol is willing to deviate from the
target average bitrate value. This is not related to min/max
bitrate. Lowering tolerance too much has an adverse effect on quality.

options.name: flags
options.description:
Set generic flags.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Use four motion vector by macroblock (mpeg4).
Use internal 2pass ratecontrol in first pass mode.
Use internal 2pass ratecontrol in second pass mode.
Input bitstream might be randomly truncated.

Don’t output frames whose parameters differ from first decoded frame in stream.
Error AVERROR_INPUT_CHANGED is returned when a frame is dropped.
Place global headers in extradata instead of every keyframe.

Only write platform-, build- and time-independent data. (except (I)DCT).
This ensures that file and data checksums are reproducible and match between
platforms. Its primary use is for regression testing.
Apply H263 advanced intra coding / mpeg4 ac prediction.

options.name: time_base number
options.description:
Set codec time base.
It is the fundamental unit of time (in seconds) in terms of which
frame timestamps are represented. For fixed-fps content, timebase
should be 1 / frame_rate and timestamp increments should be
identically 1.

options.name: g
options.description: Set the group of picture (GOP) size. Default value is 12.

options.name: ar
options.description: Set audio sampling rate (in Hz).

options.name: ac
options.description: Set number of audio channels.

options.name: cutoff
options.description:
Set cutoff bandwidth. (Supported only by selected encoders, see
their respective documentation sections.)

options.name: frame_size
options.description:
Set audio frame size.
Each submitted frame except the last must contain exactly frame_size
samples per channel. May be 0 when the codec has
CODEC_CAP_VARIABLE_FRAME_SIZE set, in that case the frame size is not
restricted. It is set by some decoders to indicate constant frame
size.

options.name: frame_number
options.description: Set the frame number.

options.name: delay
options.description:
Set video quantizer scale compression (VBR). It is used as a constant
in the ratecontrol equation. Recommended range for default rc_eq:
0.0-1.0.

options.name: qblur
options.description: Set video quantizer scale blur (VBR).

options.name: qmin
options.description:
Set min video quantizer scale (VBR). Must be included between -1 and
69, default value is 2.

options.name: qmax
options.description:
Set max video quantizer scale (VBR). Must be included between -1 and
1024, default value is 31.

options.name: qdiff
options.description: Set max difference between the quantizer scale (VBR).

options.name: bf
options.description:
Set max number of B frames between non-B-frames.
Must be an integer between -1 and 16. 0 means that B-frames are
disabled. If a value of -1 is used, it will choose an automatic value
depending on the encoder.
Default value is 0.

options.name: b_qfactor
options.description: Set qp factor between P and B frames.

options.name: codec_tag
options.description:
Workaround not auto detected encoder bugs.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Workaround various bugs in microsoft broken decoders.

options.name: strict
options.description:
Specify how strictly to follow the standards.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: strictly conform to an older more strict version of the spec or reference software
strictly conform to all the things in the spec no matter what consequences

allow non standardized experimental things, experimental
(unfinished/work in progress/not well tested) decoders and encoders.
Note: experimental decoders can pose a security risk, do not use this for
decoding untrusted input.

options.name: b_qoffset
options.description: Set QP offset between P and B frames.

options.name: err_detect
options.description:
Set error detection flags.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: abort decoding on minor error detection

ignore decoding errors, and continue decoding.
This is useful if you want to analyze the content of a video and thus want
everything to be decoded no matter what. This option will not result in a video
that is pleasing to watch in case of errors.
consider things that violate the spec and have not been seen in the wild as errors
consider all spec non compliancies as errors
consider things that a sane encoder should not do as an error

options.name: has_b_frames
options.description: Set max bitrate tolerance (in bits/s). Requires bufsize to be set.

options.name: minrate
options.description:
Set min bitrate tolerance (in bits/s). Most useful in setting up a CBR
encode. It is of little use elsewise.

options.name: bufsize
options.description: Set ratecontrol buffer size (in bits).

options.name: i_qfactor
options.description: Set QP factor between P and I frames.

options.name: i_qoffset
options.description: Set QP offset between P and I frames.

options.name: dct
options.description:
Set DCT algorithm.
Possible values:

options.name: lumi_mask
options.description: Compress bright areas stronger than medium ones.

options.name: tcplx_mask
options.description: Set temporal complexity masking.

options.name: scplx_mask
options.description: Set spatial complexity masking.

options.name: p_mask
options.description: Set inter masking.

options.name: dark_mask
options.description: Compress dark areas stronger than medium ones.

options.name: idct
options.description:
Select IDCT implementation.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Automatically pick a IDCT compatible with the simple one

options.name: slice_count
options.description:
Set error concealment strategy.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: use strong deblock filter for damaged MBs
favor predicting from the previous frame instead of the current

options.name: bits_per_coded_sample
options.description: Set sample aspect ratio.

options.name: sar number
options.description: Set sample aspect ratio. Alias to aspect .

options.name: debug
options.description:
Print specific debug info.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: display complexity metadata for the upcoming frame, GoP or for a given duration.

options.name: cmp
options.description:
Set full pel me compare function.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: sum of absolute Hadamard transformed differences
sum of absolute DCT transformed differences
sum of squared quantization errors (avoid, low quality)
number of bits needed for the block
noise preserving sum of squared differences

options.name: subcmp
options.description:
Set sub pel me compare function.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: sum of absolute Hadamard transformed differences
sum of absolute DCT transformed differences
sum of squared quantization errors (avoid, low quality)
number of bits needed for the block
noise preserving sum of squared differences

options.name: mbcmp
options.description:
Set macroblock compare function.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: sum of absolute Hadamard transformed differences
sum of absolute DCT transformed differences
sum of squared quantization errors (avoid, low quality)
number of bits needed for the block
noise preserving sum of squared differences

options.name: ildctcmp
options.description:
Set interlaced dct compare function.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: sum of absolute Hadamard transformed differences
sum of absolute DCT transformed differences
sum of squared quantization errors (avoid, low quality)
number of bits needed for the block
noise preserving sum of squared differences

options.name: dia_size
options.description: Set diamond type & size for motion estimation.

options.name: last_pred
options.description: Set amount of motion predictors from the previous frame.

options.name: precmp
options.description:
Set pre motion estimation compare function.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: sum of absolute Hadamard transformed differences
sum of absolute DCT transformed differences
sum of squared quantization errors (avoid, low quality)
number of bits needed for the block
noise preserving sum of squared differences

options.name: pre_dia_size
options.description: Set diamond type & size for motion estimation pre-pass.

options.name: subq
options.description: Set sub pel motion estimation quality.

options.name: me_range
options.description: Set limit motion vectors range (1023 for DivX player).

options.name: global_quality
options.description:
Set macroblock decision algorithm (high quality mode).
Possible values:

options.name: rc_init_occupancy
options.description:
Set number of bits which should be loaded into the rc buffer before
decoding starts.

options.name: flags2
options.description: Possible values:

possible_values.description: Allow non spec compliant speedup tricks.
Place global headers at every keyframe instead of in extradata.
Frame data might be split into multiple chunks.
Show all frames before the first keyframe.

Export motion vectors into frame side-data (see AV_FRAME_DATA_MOTION_VECTORS )
for codecs that support it. See also doc/examples/export_mvs.c .
Do not skip samples and export skip information as frame side data.
Do not reset ASS ReadOrder field on flush.
Generate/parse embedded ICC profiles from/to colorimetry tags.
Force audio encoders to use a fixed frame size.

options.name: export_side_data
options.description: Possible values:

possible_values.description:
Export motion vectors into frame side-data (see AV_FRAME_DATA_MOTION_VECTORS )
for codecs that support it. See also doc/examples/export_mvs.c .

Export encoder Producer Reference Time into packet side-data (see AV_PKT_DATA_PRFT )
for codecs that support it.

Export video encoding parameters through frame side data (see AV_FRAME_DATA_VIDEO_ENC_PARAMS )
for codecs that support it. At present, those are H.264 and VP9.

Export film grain parameters through frame side data (see AV_FRAME_DATA_FILM_GRAIN_PARAMS ).
Supported at present by AV1 decoders.
Export picture enhancement metadata through frame side data, e.g. LCEVC (see AV_FRAME_DATA_LCEVC ).

options.name: threads
options.description:
Set the number of threads to be used, in case the selected codec
implementation supports multi-threading.
Possible values:
Default value is ‘ auto ’.

possible_values.description: automatically select the number of threads to set

options.name: dc
options.description: Set intra_dc_precision.

options.name: nssew
options.description: Set nsse weight.

options.name: skip_top
options.description: Set number of macroblock rows at the top which are skipped.

options.name: skip_bottom
options.description: Set number of macroblock rows at the bottom which are skipped.

options.name: profile
options.description:
Set encoder codec profile. Default value is ‘ unknown ’. Encoder specific
profiles are documented in the relevant encoder documentation.

options.name: level
options.description:
Set the encoder level. This level depends on the specific codec, and
might correspond to the profile level. It is set by default to
‘ unknown ’.
Possible values:

options.name: lowres
options.description: Decode at 1= 1/2, 2=1/4, 3=1/8 resolutions.

options.name: mblmin
options.description: Set min macroblock lagrange factor (VBR).

options.name: mblmax
options.description: Set max macroblock lagrange factor (VBR).

options.name: skip_loop_filter
options.description:
Make decoder discard processing depending on the frame type selected
by the option value.
skip_loop_filter skips frame loop filtering, skip_idct skips frame IDCT/dequantization, skip_frame skips decoding.
Possible values:
Default value is ‘ default ’.

possible_values.description: Discard all frames except I frames.

options.name: bidir_refine
options.description: Refine the two motion vectors used in bidirectional macroblocks.

options.name: keyint_min
options.description: Set minimum interval between IDR-frames.

options.name: refs
options.description: Set reference frames to consider for motion compensation.

options.name: trellis
options.description: Set rate-distortion optimal quantization.

options.name: mv0_threshold
options.description: See (ffmpeg-utils)the Channel Layout section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual for the required syntax.

options.name: rc_max_vbv_use
options.description: Possible values:

options.name: color_trc
options.description: Possible values:

options.name: colorspace
options.description: Possible values:

options.name: color_range
options.description:
If used as input parameter, it serves as a hint to the decoder, which
color_range the input has.
Possible values:

options.name: chroma_sample_location
options.description: Possible values:

options.name: alpha_mode
options.description: Possible values:

options.name: log_level_offset
options.description: Set the log level offset.

options.name: slices
options.description: Number of slices, used in parallelized encoding.

options.name: thread_type
options.description:
Select which multithreading methods to use.
Use of ‘ frame ’ will increase decoding delay by one frame per
thread, so clients which cannot provide future frames should not use
it.
Possible values:
Default value is ‘ slice+frame ’.

possible_values.description:
Decode more than one part of a single frame at once.
Multithreading using slices works only when the video was encoded with
slices.
Decode more than one frame at once.

options.name: audio_service_type
options.description:
Set audio service type.
Possible values:

options.name: request_sample_fmt sample_fmt
options.description: Set sample format audio decoders should prefer. Default value is none .

options.name: pkt_timebase number
options.description: Set the input subtitles character encoding.

options.name: field_order field_order
options.description:
Set/override the field order of the video.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Interlaced video, top field coded and displayed first
Interlaced video, bottom field coded and displayed first
Interlaced video, top coded first, bottom displayed first
Interlaced video, bottom coded first, top displayed first

options.name: skip_alpha bool
options.description:
Set to 1 to disable processing alpha (transparency). This works like the
‘ gray ’ flag in the flags option which skips chroma information
instead of alpha. Default is 0.

options.name: codec_whitelist
options.description: "," separated list of allowed decoders. By default all are allowed.

options.name: dump_separator
options.description:
Separator used to separate the fields printed on the command line about the
Stream parameters.
For example, to separate the fields with newlines and indentation:

options.name: max_pixels
options.description:
Maximum number of pixels per image. This value can be used to avoid out of
memory failures due to large images.

options.name: apply_cropping bool
options.description:
Enable cropping if cropping parameters are multiples of the required
alignment for the left and top parameters. If the alignment is not met the
cropping will be partially applied to maintain alignment.
Default is 1 (enabled).
Note: The required alignment depends on if AV_CODEC_FLAG_UNALIGNED is set and the
CPU. AV_CODEC_FLAG_UNALIGNED cannot be changed from the command line. Also hardware
decoders will not apply left/top Cropping.

options.name: operating_point
options.description: Select an operating point of a scalable AV1 bitstream (0 - 31). Default is 0.

options.name: view_ids
options.description:
Specify a list of view IDs that should be output. This option can also be set to
a single ’-1’, which will cause all views defined in the VPS to be decoded and
output.

options.name: view_ids_available
options.description:
This option may be read by the caller to retrieve an array of view IDs available
in the active VPS. The array is empty for single-layer video.
The value of this option is guaranteed to be accurate when read from the get_format() callback. It may also be set at other times (e.g. after
opening the decoder), but the value is informational only and may be incorrect
(e.g. when the stream contains multiple distinct VPS NALUs).

options.name: view_pos_available
options.description:
This option may be read by the caller to retrieve an array of view positions
(left, right, or unspecified) available in the active VPS, as AVStereo3DView values. When the array is available, its elements apply to
the corresponding elements of view_ids_available , i.e. view_pos_available[i] contains the position of view with ID view_ids_available[i] .
Same validity restrictions as for view_ids_available apply to
this option.

options.name: top top_field_first
options.description: Specify the assumed field type of the input video.

possible_values.description: the video is assumed to be progressive (default)

options.name: max_frame_delay
options.description:
Set max amount of frames the decoder may buffer internally. The default value is 0
(autodetect).

options.name: filmgrain
options.description:
Apply film grain to the decoded video if present in the bitstream. Defaults to the
internal default of the library.
This option is deprecated and will be removed in the future. See the global option export_side_data to export Film Grain parameters instead of applying it.

options.name: oppoint
options.description:
Select an operating point of a scalable AV1 bitstream (0 - 31). Defaults to the
internal default of the library.

options.name: alllayers
options.description: Output all spatial layers of a scalable AV1 bitstream. The default value is false.

options.name: frame_threads
options.description: Set amount of frame threads to use during decoding. The default value is 0 (autodetect).

options.name: threads
options.description: Force to use a specific number of threads

options.name: async_depth
options.description: Internal parallelization depth, the higher the value the higher the latency.

options.name: gpu_copy
options.description: A GPU-accelerated copy between video and system memory

options.name: load_plugin
options.description: A user plugin to load in an internal session

options.name: load_plugins
options.description: A :-separate list of hexadecimal plugin UIDs to load in an internal session

options.name: custom_stride
options.description:
Set the line size of the v210 data in bytes. The default value is 0
(autodetect). You can use the special -1 value for a strideless v210 as seen in
BOXX files.

options.name: -drc_scale value
options.description:
Dynamic Range Scale Factor. The factor to apply to dynamic range values
from the AC-3 stream. This factor is applied exponentially. The default value is 1.
There are 3 notable scale factor ranges:

possible_values.description: DRC disabled. Produces full range audio.

DRC enabled.  Applies a fraction of the stream DRC value.
Audio reproduction is between full range and full compression.

DRC enabled. Applies drc_scale asymmetrically.
Loud sounds are fully compressed.  Soft sounds are enhanced.

options.name: -use_buggy_lpc
options.description:
The lavc FLAC encoder used to produce buggy streams with high lpc values
(like the default value). This option makes it possible to decode such streams
correctly by using lavc’s old buggy lpc logic for decoding.

options.name: enhance
options.description:
Enable the enhancement of the decoded audio when set to 1. The default
value is 0 (disabled).

options.name: -aribb24-base-path path
options.description:
Sets the base path for the libaribb24 library. This is utilized for reading of
configuration files (for custom unicode conversions), and for dumping of
non-text symbols as images under that location.
Unset by default.

options.name: -aribb24-skip-ruby-text boolean
options.description:
Tells the decoder wrapper to skip text blocks that contain half-height ruby
text.
Enabled by default.

options.name: -sub_type subtitle_type
options.description:
Specifies the format of the decoded subtitles.
The default is ass as same as libaribb24 decoder.
Some present players (e.g., mpv ) expect ASS format for ARIB caption.
options.default: ass as same as libaribb24 decoder

possible_values.description: Simple text based output without formatting.

options.name: -caption_encoding encoding_scheme
options.description: Specifies the encoding scheme of input subtitle text.

possible_values.description:
8bit-char JIS encoding defined in ARIB STD B24.
This encoding used in Japan for ISDB captions.

UTF-8 encoding defined in ARIB STD B24.
This encoding is used in Philippines for ISDB-T captions.

Latin character encoding defined in ABNT NBR 15606-1.
This encoding is used in South America for SBTVD / ISDB-Tb captions.

options.name: -font font_name
options.description:
Specify comma-separated list of font family names to be used for bitmap or ass type subtitle rendering.
Only first font name is used for ass type subtitle.
If not specified, use internally defined default font family.

options.name: -ass_single_rect boolean
options.description:
ARIB STD-B24 specifies that some captions may be displayed at different
positions at a time (multi-rectangle subtitle).
Since some players (e.g., old mpv ) can’t handle multiple ASS rectangles
in a single AVSubtitle, or multiple ASS rectangles of indeterminate duration
with the same start timestamp, this option can change the behavior so that
all the texts are displayed in a single ASS rectangle.
The default is false .
If your player cannot handle AVSubtitles with multiple ASS rectangles properly,
set this option to true or define ASS_SINGLE_RECT=1 to change
default behavior at compilation.

options.name: -force_outline_text boolean
options.description:
Specify whether always render outline text for all characters regardless of
the indication by character style.
The default is false .

options.name: -outline_width number
options.description:
Specify width for outline text, in dots (relative).
The default is 1.5 .

options.name: -ignore_background boolean
options.description:
Specify whether to ignore background color rendering.
The default is false .

options.name: -ignore_ruby boolean
options.description:
Specify whether to ignore rendering for ruby-like (furigana) characters.
The default is false .

options.name: -replace_drcs boolean
options.description:
Specify whether to render replaced DRCS characters as Unicode characters.
The default is true .

options.name: -replace_msz_ascii boolean
options.description:
Specify whether to replace MSZ (Middle Size; half width) fullwidth
alphanumerics with halfwidth alphanumerics.
The default is true .

options.name: -replace_msz_japanese boolean
options.description:
Specify whether to replace some MSZ (Middle Size; half width) fullwidth
japanese special characters with halfwidth ones.
The default is true .

options.name: -replace_msz_glyph boolean
options.description:
Specify whether to replace MSZ (Middle Size; half width) characters
with halfwidth glyphs if the fonts supports it.
This option works under FreeType or DirectWrite renderer
with Adobe-Japan1 compliant fonts.
e.g., IBM Plex Sans JP, Morisawa BIZ UDGothic, Morisawa BIZ UDMincho,
Yu Gothic, Yu Mincho, and Meiryo.
The default is true .

options.name: -canvas_size image_size
options.description:
Specify the resolution of the canvas to render subtitles to; usually, this
should be frame size of input video.
This only applies when -subtitle_type is set to bitmap .
The libaribcaption decoder assumes input frame size for bitmap rendering as below:
If actual frame size of input video does not match above assumption,
the rendered captions may be distorted.
To make the captions undistorted, add -canvas_size option to specify
actual input video size.
Note that the -canvas_size option is not required for video with
different size but same aspect ratio.
In such cases, the caption will be stretched or shrunk to actual video size
if -canvas_size option is not specified.
If -canvas_size option is specified with different size,
the caption will be stretched or shrunk as specified size with calculated SAR.

possible_values.description: Compute clut once if no matching CLUT is in the stream.
Compute clut if no matching CLUT is in the stream.
Always compute CLUT and override the one provided in the stream.

options.name: dvb_substream
options.description: Selects the dvb substream, or all substreams if -1 which is default.

options.name: palette
options.description:
Specify the global palette used by the bitmaps. When stored in VobSub, the
palette is normally specified in the index file; in Matroska, the palette is
stored in the codec extra-data in the same format as in VobSub. In DVDs, the
palette is stored in the IFO file, and therefore not available when reading
from dumped VOB files.
The format for this option is a string containing 16 24-bits hexadecimal
numbers (without 0x prefix) separated by commas, for example 0d00ee,
ee450d, 101010, eaeaea, 0ce60b, ec14ed, ebff0b, 0d617a, 7b7b7b, d1d1d1,
7b2a0e, 0d950c, 0f007b, cf0dec, cfa80c, 7c127b .

options.name: ifo_palette
options.description:
Specify the IFO file from which the global palette is obtained.
(experimental)

options.name: forced_subs_only
options.description:
Only decode subtitle entries marked as forced. Some titles have forced
and non-forced subtitles in the same track. Setting this flag to 1 will only keep the forced subtitles. Default value is 0 .

options.name: txt_page
options.description:
List of teletext page numbers to decode. Pages that do not match the specified
list are dropped. You may use the special * string to match all pages,
or subtitle to match all subtitle pages.
Default value is *.

options.name: txt_default_region
options.description:
Set default character set used for decoding, a value between 0 and 87 (see
ETS 300 706, Section 15, Table 32). Default value is -1, which does not
override the libzvbi default. This option is needed for some legacy level 1.0
transmissions which cannot signal the proper charset.

options.name: txt_chop_top
options.description: Discards the top teletext line. Default value is 1.

options.name: txt_format
options.description: Specifies the format of the decoded subtitles.

possible_values.description:
The default format, you should use this for teletext pages, because certain
graphics and colors cannot be expressed in simple text or even ASS.
Simple text based output without formatting.

Formatted ASS output, subtitle pages and teletext pages are returned in
different styles, subtitle pages are stripped down to text, but an effort is
made to keep the text alignment and the formatting.

options.name: txt_left
options.description: X offset of generated bitmaps, default is 0.

options.name: txt_top
options.description: Y offset of generated bitmaps, default is 0.

options.name: txt_chop_spaces
options.description:
Chops leading and trailing spaces and removes empty lines from the generated
text. This option is useful for teletext based subtitles where empty spaces may
be present at the start or at the end of the lines or empty lines may be
present between the subtitle lines because of double-sized teletext characters.
Default value is 1.

options.name: txt_duration
options.description:
Sets the display duration of the decoded teletext pages or subtitles in
milliseconds. Default value is -1 which means infinity or until the next
subtitle event comes.
options.default: -1 which means infinity or until the next

options.name: txt_transparent
options.description:
Force transparent background of the generated teletext bitmaps. Default value
is 0 which means an opaque background.

options.name: txt_opacity
options.description:
Sets the opacity (0-255) of the teletext background. If txt_transparent is not set, it only affects characters between a start
box and an end box, typically subtitles. Default value is 0 if txt_transparent is set, 255 otherwise.

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bit rate in bits/s. Setting this automatically activates constant bit rate
(CBR) mode. If this option is unspecified it is set to 128kbps.

options.name: q
options.description:
Set quality for variable bit rate (VBR) mode. This option is valid only using
the ffmpeg command-line tool. For library interface users, use global_quality .

options.name: cutoff
options.description:
Set cutoff frequency. If unspecified will allow the encoder to dynamically
adjust the cutoff to improve clarity on low bitrates.

options.name: aac_coder
options.description: Set AAC encoder coding method. Possible values:

possible_values.description:
Two loop searching (TLS) method. This is the default method.
This method first sets quantizers depending on band thresholds and then tries
to find an optimal combination by adding or subtracting a specific value from
all quantizers and adjusting some individual quantizer a little.  Will tune
itself based on whether aac_is , aac_ms and aac_pns are enabled.

Average noise to mask ratio (ANMR) trellis-based solution.
This is an experimental coder which currently produces a lower quality, is more
unstable and is slower than the default twoloop coder but has potential.
Currently has no support for the aac_is or aac_pns options.
Not currently recommended.

Constant quantizer method.
Uses a cheaper version of twoloop algorithm that doesn’t try to do as many
clever adjustments. Worse with low bitrates (less than 64kbps), but is better
and much faster at higher bitrates.

options.name: aac_ms
options.description:
Sets mid/side coding mode. The default value of "auto" will automatically use
M/S with bands which will benefit from such coding. Can be forced for all bands
using the value "enable", which is mainly useful for debugging or disabled using
"disable".

options.name: aac_is
options.description:
Sets intensity stereo coding tool usage. By default, it’s enabled and will
automatically toggle IS for similar pairs of stereo bands if it’s beneficial.
Can be disabled for debugging by setting the value to "disable".

options.name: aac_pns
options.description:
Uses perceptual noise substitution to replace low entropy high frequency bands
with imperceptible white noise during the decoding process. By default, it’s
enabled, but can be disabled for debugging purposes by using "disable".

options.name: aac_tns
options.description:
Enables the use of a multitap FIR filter which spans through the high frequency
bands to hide quantization noise during the encoding process and is reverted
by the decoder. As well as decreasing unpleasant artifacts in the high range
this also reduces the entropy in the high bands and allows for more bits to
be used by the mid-low bands. By default it’s enabled but can be disabled for
debugging by setting the option to "disable".

options.name: aac_ltp
options.description:
Enables the use of the long term prediction extension which increases coding
efficiency in very low bandwidth situations such as encoding of voice or
solo piano music by extending constant harmonic peaks in bands throughout
frames. This option is implied by profile:a aac_low.
Use in conjunction with -ar to decrease the samplerate.

options.name: profile
options.description:
Sets the encoding profile, possible values:
If this option is unspecified it is set to ‘ aac_low ’.

possible_values.description:
The default, AAC "Low-complexity" profile. Is the most compatible and produces
decent quality.

Equivalent to -profile:a aac_low -aac_pns 0 . PNS was introduced with the
MPEG4 specifications.
Long term prediction profile, is enabled by and will enable the aac_ltp option. Introduced in MPEG4.

options.name: -per_frame_metadata boolean
options.description:
Allow Per-Frame Metadata. Specifies if the encoder should check for changing
metadata for each frame.

possible_values.description:
The metadata values set at initialization will be used for every frame in the
stream. (default)
Metadata values can be changed before encoding each frame.

options.name: -center_mixlev level
options.description:
Center Mix Level. The amount of gain the decoder should apply to the center
channel when downmixing to stereo. This field will only be written to the
bitstream if a center channel is present. The value is specified as a scale
factor. There are 3 valid values:

options.name: -surround_mixlev level
options.description:
Surround Mix Level. The amount of gain the decoder should apply to the surround
channel(s) when downmixing to stereo. This field will only be written to the
bitstream if one or more surround channels are present. The value is specified
as a scale factor.  There are 3 valid values:

options.name: -mixing_level number
options.description:
Mixing Level. Specifies peak sound pressure level (SPL) in the production
environment when the mix was mastered. Valid values are 80 to 111, or -1 for
unknown or not indicated. The default value is -1, but that value cannot be
used if the Audio Production Information is written to the bitstream. Therefore,
if the room_type option is not the default value, the mixing_level option must not be -1.

options.name: -room_type type
options.description:
Room Type. Describes the equalization used during the final mixing session at
the studio or on the dubbing stage. A large room is a dubbing stage with the
industry standard X-curve equalization; a small room has flat equalization.
This field will not be written to the bitstream if both the mixing_level option and the room_type option have the default values.

options.name: -copyright boolean
options.description: Copyright Indicator. Specifies whether a copyright exists for this audio.

options.name: -dialnorm value
options.description:
Dialogue Normalization. Indicates how far the average dialogue level of the
program is below digital 100% full scale (0 dBFS). This parameter determines a
level shift during audio reproduction that sets the average volume of the
dialogue to a preset level. The goal is to match volume level between program
sources. A value of -31dB will result in no volume level change, relative to
the source volume, during audio reproduction. Valid values are whole numbers in
the range -31 to -1, with -31 being the default.

options.name: -dsur_mode mode
options.description:
Dolby Surround Mode. Specifies whether the stereo signal uses Dolby Surround
(Pro Logic). This field will only be written to the bitstream if the audio
stream is stereo. Using this option does NOT mean the encoder will actually
apply Dolby Surround processing.

options.name: -original boolean
options.description:
Original Bit Stream Indicator. Specifies whether this audio is from the
original source and not a copy.

options.name: -dmix_mode mode
options.description:
Preferred Stereo Downmix Mode. Allows the user to select either Lt/Rt
(Dolby Surround) or Lo/Ro (normal stereo) as the preferred stereo downmix mode.

options.name: -ltrt_cmixlev level
options.description:
Lt/Rt Center Mix Level. The amount of gain the decoder should apply to the
center channel when downmixing to stereo in Lt/Rt mode.
