options.name: -ltrt_surmixlev level
options.description:
Lt/Rt Surround Mix Level. The amount of gain the decoder should apply to the
surround channel(s) when downmixing to stereo in Lt/Rt mode.

options.name: -loro_cmixlev level
options.description:
Lo/Ro Center Mix Level. The amount of gain the decoder should apply to the
center channel when downmixing to stereo in Lo/Ro mode.

options.name: -loro_surmixlev level
options.description:
Lo/Ro Surround Mix Level. The amount of gain the decoder should apply to the
surround channel(s) when downmixing to stereo in Lo/Ro mode.

options.name: -dsurex_mode mode
options.description:
Dolby Surround EX Mode. Indicates whether the stream uses Dolby Surround EX
(7.1 matrixed to 5.1). Using this option does NOT mean the encoder will actually
apply Dolby Surround EX processing.

options.name: -dheadphone_mode mode
options.description:
Dolby Headphone Mode. Indicates whether the stream uses Dolby Headphone
encoding (multi-channel matrixed to 2.0 for use with headphones). Using this
option does NOT mean the encoder will actually apply Dolby Headphone
processing.

options.name: -ad_conv_type type
options.description:
A/D Converter Type. Indicates whether the audio has passed through HDCD A/D
conversion.

options.name: -stereo_rematrixing boolean
options.description:
Stereo Rematrixing. Enables/Disables use of rematrixing for stereo input. This
is an optional AC-3 feature that increases quality by selectively encoding
the left/right channels as mid/side. This option is enabled by default, and it
is highly recommended that it be left as enabled except for testing purposes.

options.name: cutoff frequency
options.description:
Set lowpass cutoff frequency. If unspecified, the encoder selects a default
determined by various other encoding parameters.

options.name: -channel_coupling boolean
options.description:
Enables/Disables use of channel coupling, which is an optional AC-3 feature
that increases quality by combining high frequency information from multiple
channels into a single channel. The per-channel high frequency information is
sent with less accuracy in both the frequency and time domains. This allows
more bits to be used for lower frequencies while preserving enough information
to reconstruct the high frequencies. This option is enabled by default for the
floating-point encoder and should generally be left as enabled except for
testing purposes or to increase encoding speed.

options.name: -cpl_start_band number
options.description:
Coupling Start Band. Sets the channel coupling start band, from 1 to 15. If a
value higher than the bandwidth is used, it will be reduced to 1 less than the
coupling end band. If auto is used, the start band will be determined by
the encoder based on the bit rate, sample rate, and channel layout. This option
has no effect if channel coupling is disabled.

options.name: compression_level
options.description:
Sets the compression level, which chooses defaults for many other options
if they are not set explicitly. Valid values are from 0 to 12, 5 is the
default.

options.name: frame_size
options.description: Sets the size of the frames in samples per channel.

options.name: lpc_coeff_precision
options.description:
Sets the LPC coefficient precision, valid values are from 1 to 15, 15 is the
default.

options.name: lpc_type
options.description: Sets the first stage LPC algorithm

options.name: lpc_passes
options.description: Number of passes to use for Cholesky factorization during LPC analysis

options.name: min_partition_order
options.description: The minimum partition order

options.name: max_partition_order
options.description: The maximum partition order

options.name: ch_mode
options.description: Channel mode

possible_values.description: The mode is chosen automatically for each frame

options.name: exact_rice_parameters
options.description:
Chooses if rice parameters are calculated exactly or approximately.
if set to 1 then they are chosen exactly, which slows the code down slightly and
improves compression slightly.

options.name: multi_dim_quant
options.description:
Multi Dimensional Quantization. If set to 1 then a 2nd stage LPC algorithm is
applied after the first stage to finetune the coefficients. This is quite slow
and slightly improves compression.

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bit rate in bits/s. If unspecified it uses the number of channels and the layout
to make a good guess.

options.name: opus_delay
options.description:
Sets the maximum delay in milliseconds. Lower delays than 20ms will very quickly
decrease quality.

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bit rate in bits/s. If the bitrate is not explicitly specified, it
is automatically set to a suitable value depending on the selected
profile.
In case VBR mode is enabled the option is ignored.

options.name: ar
options.description: Set audio sampling rate (in Hz).

options.name: channels
options.description: Set the number of audio channels.

options.name: +qscale
options.description:
Enable fixed quality, VBR (Variable Bit Rate) mode.
Note that VBR is implicitly enabled when the vbr value is
positive.

options.name: cutoff
options.description:
Set cutoff frequency. If not specified (or explicitly set to 0) it
will use a value automatically computed by the library. Default value
is 0.

options.name: profile
options.description:
Set audio profile.
The following profiles are recognized:
If not specified it is set to ‘ aac_low ’.

options.name: afterburner
options.description:
Enable afterburner feature if set to 1, disabled if set to 0. This
improves the quality but also the required processing power.
Default value is 1.

options.name: eld_sbr
options.description:
Enable SBR (Spectral Band Replication) for ELD if set to 1, disabled
if set to 0.
Default value is 0.

options.name: eld_v2
options.description:
Enable ELDv2 (LD-MPS extension for ELD stereo signals) for ELDv2 if set to 1,
disabled if set to 0.
Note that option is available when fdk-aac version (AACENCODER_LIB_VL0.AACENCODER_LIB_VL1.AACENCODER_LIB_VL2) > (4.0.0).
Default value is 0.

options.name: signaling
options.description:
Set SBR/PS signaling style.
It can assume one of the following values:
Default value is ‘ default ’.

possible_values.description:
choose signaling implicitly (explicit hierarchical by default,
implicit if global header is disabled)

options.name: latm
options.description:
Output LATM/LOAS encapsulated data if set to 1, disabled if set to 0.
Default value is 0.

options.name: header_period
options.description:
Set StreamMuxConfig and PCE repetition period (in frames) for sending
in-band configuration buffers within LATM/LOAS transport layer.
Must be a 16-bits non-negative integer.
Default value is 0.

options.name: vbr
options.description:
Set VBR mode, from 1 to 5. 1 is lowest quality (though still pretty
good) and 5 is highest quality. A value of 0 will disable VBR, and CBR
(Constant Bit Rate) is enabled.
Currently only the ‘ aac_low ’ profile supports VBR encoding.
VBR modes 1-5 correspond to roughly the following average bit rates:
Default value is 0.

options.name: frame_length
options.description:
Set the audio frame length in samples. Default value is the internal
default of the library. Refer to the library’s documentation for information
about supported values.

options.name: b bitrate
options.description:
Set the bit rate in bits/s. This will determine the fixed size of the encoded
frames, for a selected frame duration.

options.name: ar frequency
options.description: Set the audio sampling rate (in Hz).

options.name: channels
options.description: Set the number of audio channels.

options.name: frame_duration
options.description:
Set the audio frame duration in milliseconds. Default value is 10ms.
Allowed frame durations are 2.5ms, 5ms, 7.5ms and 10ms.
LC3 (Bluetooth LE Audio), allows 7.5ms and 10ms; and LC3plus 2.5ms, 5ms
and 10ms.
The 10ms frame duration is available in LC3 and LC3 plus standard.
In this mode, the produced bitstream can be referenced either as LC3 or LC3plus.

options.name: high_resolution
options.description:
Enable the high-resolution mode if set to 1. The high-resolution mode is
available with all LC3plus frame durations and for a sampling rate of 48 KHz,
and 96 KHz.
The encoder automatically turns off this mode at lower sampling rates and
activates it at 96 KHz.
This mode should be preferred at high bitrates. In this mode, the audio
bandwidth is always up to the Nyquist frequency, compared to LC3 at 48 KHz,
which limits the bandwidth to 20 KHz.

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bitrate expressed in bits/s for CBR or ABR. LAME bitrate is
expressed in kilobits/s.

options.name: q
options.description:
Set constant quality setting for VBR. This option is valid only
using the ffmpeg command-line tool. For library interface
users, use global_quality .

options.name: compression_level
options.description:
Set algorithm quality. Valid arguments are integers in the 0-9 range,
with 0 meaning highest quality but slowest, and 9 meaning fastest
while producing the worst quality.

options.name: cutoff
options.description:
Set lowpass cutoff frequency. If unspecified, the encoder dynamically
adjusts the cutoff.

options.name: reservoir
options.description:
Enable use of bit reservoir when set to 1. Default value is 1. LAME
has this enabled by default, but can be overridden by use --nores option.

options.name: joint_stereo
options.description:
Enable the encoder to use (on a frame by frame basis) either L/R
stereo or mid/side stereo. Default value is 1.

options.name: abr
options.description:
Enable the encoder to use ABR when set to 1. The lame --abr sets the target bitrate, while this options only
tells FFmpeg to use ABR still relies on b to set bitrate.

options.name: copyright
options.description:
Set MPEG audio copyright flag when set to 1. The default value is 0
(disabled).

options.name: original
options.description:
Set MPEG audio original flag when set to 1. The default value is 1
(enabled).

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bitrate in bits per second. Only the following bitrates are supported,
otherwise libavcodec will round to the nearest valid bitrate.

options.name: dtx
options.description:
Allow discontinuous transmission (generate comfort noise) when set to 1. The
default value is 0 (disabled).

options.name: b
options.description:
Set the bit rate in bits/s.  FFmpeg’s b option is
expressed in bits/s, while opusenc ’s bitrate in
kilobits/s.

options.name: vbr
options.description:
Set VBR mode. The FFmpeg vbr option has the following
valid arguments, with the opusenc equivalent options
in parentheses:

possible_values.description: Use variable bit rate encoding (the default).
Use constrained variable bit rate encoding.

options.name: compression_level
options.description:
Set encoding algorithm complexity. Valid options are integers in
the 0-10 range. 0 gives the fastest encodes but lower quality, while 10
gives the highest quality but slowest encoding. The default is 10.

options.name: frame_duration
options.description:
Set maximum frame size, or duration of a frame in milliseconds. The
argument must be exactly the following: 2.5, 5, 10, 20, 40, 60. Smaller
frame sizes achieve lower latency but less quality at a given bitrate.
Sizes greater than 20ms are only interesting at fairly low bitrates.
The default is 20ms.

options.name: packet_loss
options.description: Set expected packet loss percentage. The default is 0.

options.name: fec
options.description:
Enable inband forward error correction. packet_loss must be non-zero
to take advantage - frequency of FEC ’side-data’ is proportional to expected packet loss.
Default is disabled.

options.name: application
options.description: Set intended application type. Valid options are listed below:

possible_values.description: Favor faithfulness to the input (the default).

Restrict to only the lowest delay modes by disabling voice-optimized
modes.

options.name: cutoff
options.description:
Set cutoff bandwidth in Hz. The argument must be exactly one of the
following: 4000, 6000, 8000, 12000, or 20000, corresponding to
narrowband, mediumband, wideband, super wideband, and fullband
respectively. The default is 0 (cutoff disabled). Note that libopus
forces a wideband cutoff for bitrates < 15 kbps, unless CELT-only
( application set to ‘ lowdelay ’) mode is used.

options.name: mapping_family
options.description:
Set channel mapping family to be used by the encoder. The default value of -1
uses mapping family 0 for mono and stereo inputs, and mapping family 1
otherwise. The default also disables the surround masking and LFE bandwidth
optimizations in libopus, and requires that the input contains 8 channels or
fewer.
Other values include 0 for mono and stereo, 1 for surround sound with masking
and LFE bandwidth optimizations, and 255 for independent streams with an
unspecified channel layout.

options.name: dtx
options.description:
Allow discontinuous transmission when set to 1. The default value is 0
(disabled).

options.name: apply_phase_inv (N.A.)
options.description:
If set to 0, disables the use of phase inversion for intensity stereo,
improving the quality of mono downmixes, but slightly reducing normal stereo
quality. The default is 1 (phase inversion enabled).

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bitrate expressed in bits/s for CBR. shineenc -b option
is expressed in kilobits/s.

options.name: b
options.description: Set bitrate expressed in bits/s for CBR. twolame b option is expressed in kilobits/s. Default value is 128k.

options.name: q
options.description:
Set quality for experimental VBR support. Maximum value range is
from -50 to 50, useful range is from -10 to 10. The higher the
value, the better the quality. This option is valid only using the ffmpeg command-line tool. For library interface users,
use global_quality .

options.name: mode
options.description: Set the mode of the resulting audio. Possible values:

possible_values.description: Choose mode automatically based on the input. This is the default.

options.name: psymodel
options.description:
Set psychoacoustic model to use in encoding. The argument must be
an integer between -1 and 4, inclusive. The higher the value, the
better the quality. The default value is 3.

options.name: energy_levels
options.description:
Enable energy levels extensions when set to 1. The default value is
0 (disabled).

options.name: error_protection
options.description:
Enable CRC error protection when set to 1. The default value is 0
(disabled).

options.name: copyright
options.description:
Set MPEG audio copyright flag when set to 1. The default value is 0
(disabled).

options.name: original
options.description:
Set MPEG audio original flag when set to 1. The default value is 0
(disabled).

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bitrate in bits/s. Only the following bitrates are supported, otherwise
libavcodec will round to the nearest valid bitrate.

options.name: dtx
options.description:
Allow discontinuous transmission (generate comfort noise) when set to 1. The
default value is 0 (disabled).

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bitrate expressed in bits/s for ABR. oggenc -b is
expressed in kilobits/s.

options.name: q
options.description:
Set constant quality setting for VBR. The value should be a float
number in the range of -1.0 to 10.0. The higher the value, the better
the quality. The default value is ‘ 3.0 ’.
This option is valid only using the ffmpeg command-line tool.
For library interface users, use global_quality .

options.name: cutoff
options.description:
Set cutoff bandwidth in Hz, a value of 0 disables cutoff. oggenc ’s
related option is expressed in kHz. The default value is ‘ 0 ’ (cutoff
disabled).

options.name: minrate
options.description:
Set minimum bitrate expressed in bits/s. oggenc -m is
expressed in kilobits/s.

options.name: maxrate
options.description:
Set maximum bitrate expressed in bits/s. oggenc -M is
expressed in kilobits/s. This only has effect on ABR mode.

options.name: iblock
options.description:
Set noise floor bias for impulse blocks. The value is a float number from
-15.0 to 0.0. A negative bias instructs the encoder to pay special attention
to the crispness of transients in the encoded audio. The tradeoff for better
transient response is a higher bitrate.

options.name: huffman
options.description: Set the huffman encoding strategy. Possible values:

possible_values.description: Use the default huffman tables. This is the default strategy.
Compute and use optimal huffman tables.

options.name: frame_size
options.description:
For this encoder, the range for this option is between 128 and 131072. Default
is automatically decided based on sample rate and number of channel.
For the complete formula of calculating default, see libavcodec/wavpackenc.c .
options.default: automatically decided based on sample rate and number of channel

options.name: joint_stereo
options.description: Set whether to enable joint stereo. Valid values are:

options.name: optimize_mono
options.description:
Set whether to enable optimization for mono. This option is only effective for
non-mono streams. Available values:

options.name: g
options.description:
Keyframe interval.
A keyframe is inserted at least every -g frames, sometimes sooner.

options.name: q:v
options.description:
Quality factor. Lower is better. Higher gives lower bitrate.
The following table lists bitrates when encoding akiyo_cif.y4m for various values of -q:v with -g 100 :

options.name: max_extra_cb_iterations
options.description: Max extra codebook recalculation passes, more is better and slower.

options.name: skip_empty_cb
options.description: Avoid wasting bytes, ignore vintage MacOS decoder.

options.name: max_strips
options.description:
The minimum and maximum number of strips to use.
Wider range sometimes improves quality.
More strips is generally better quality but costs more bits.
Fewer strips tend to yield more keyframes.
Vintage compatible is 1..3.

options.name: strip_number_adaptivity
options.description:
How much number of strips is allowed to change between frames.
Higher is better but slower.

options.name: context
options.description: Sets the context size, 0 (default) is small, 1 is big.

options.name: coder
options.description: Set the coder,

options.name: slicecrc
options.description: -1 (default, automatic), 1 use crc with zero initial and final state, 2 use crc with non zero initial and final state

options.name: remap_optimizer
options.description: 0 - 5, default 3, how much effort the encoder puts into optimizing the remap table.

options.name: gifflags
options.description: Sets the flags used for GIF encoding.

possible_values.description:
Enables picture offsetting.
Default is enabled.

Enables transparency detection between frames.
Default is enabled.

options.name: gifimage
options.description:
Enables encoding one full GIF image per frame, rather than an animated GIF.
Default value is 0 .

options.name: global_palette
options.description:
Writes a palette to the global GIF header where feasible.
If disabled, every frame will always have a palette written, even if there
is a global palette supplied.
Default value is 1 .

options.name: format
options.description:
Specifies the Hap format to encode.
Default value is hap .

options.name: chunks
options.description:
Specifies the number of chunks to split frames into, between 1 and 64. This
permits multithreaded decoding of large frames, potentially at the cost of
data-rate. The encoder may modify this value to divide frames evenly.
Default value is 1 .

options.name: compressor
options.description:
Specifies the second-stage compressor to use. If set to none , chunks will be limited to 1, as chunked uncompressed frames offer no
benefit.
Default value is snappy .

options.name: format
options.description:
Can be set to either j2k or jp2 (the default) that
makes it possible to store non-rgb pix_fmts.

options.name: tile_width
options.description: Sets tile width. Range is 1 to 1073741824. Default is 256.

options.name: tile_height
options.description: Sets tile height. Range is 1 to 1073741824. Default is 256.

options.name: pred
options.description:
Allows setting the discrete wavelet transform (DWT) type
Default is dwt97int

options.name: sop
options.description: Enable this to add SOP marker at the start of each packet. Disabled by default.

options.name: eph
options.description: Enable this to add EPH marker at the end of each packet header. Disabled by default.

options.name: prog
options.description:
Sets the progression order to be used by the encoder.
Possible values are:
Set to lrcp by default.

options.name: layer_rates
options.description:
By default, when this option is not used, compression is done using the quality metric.
This option allows for compression using compression ratio. The compression ratio for each
level could be specified. The compression ratio of a layer l species the what ratio of
total file size is contained in the first l layers.
Example usage:
This would compress the image to contain 3 layers, where the data contained in the
first layer would be compressed by 1000 times, compressed by 100 in the first two layers,
and shall contain all data while using all 3 layers.

options.name: qmax
options.description: Sets the maximum quantizer to use when using bitrate mode.

options.name: qmin
options.description: Sets the minimum quantizer to use when using bitrate mode.

options.name: qp
options.description: Uses quantizer mode to encode at the given quantizer (0-255).

options.name: speed
options.description: Selects the speed preset (0-10) to encode with.

options.name: tiles
options.description: Selects how many tiles to encode with.

options.name: tile-rows
options.description: Selects how many rows of tiles to encode with.

options.name: tile-columns
options.description: Selects how many columns of tiles to encode with.

options.name: rav1e-params
options.description:
Set rav1e options using a list of key = value pairs separated
by ":". See rav1e --help for a list of options.
For example to specify librav1e encoding options with -rav1e-params :

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bitrate target in bits/second.  By default this will use
variable-bitrate mode.  If maxrate and minrate are
also set to the same value then it will use constant-bitrate mode,
otherwise if crf is set as well then it will use
constrained-quality mode.

options.name: g keyint_min
options.description:
Set key frame placement.  The GOP size sets the maximum distance between
key frames; if zero the output stream will be intra-only.  The minimum
distance is ignored unless it is the same as the GOP size, in which case
key frames will always appear at a fixed interval.  Not set by default,
so without this option the library has completely free choice about
where to place key frames.

options.name: qmin qmax
options.description:
Set minimum/maximum quantisation values.  Valid range is from 0 to 63
(warning: this does not match the quantiser values actually used by AV1
- divide by four to map real quantiser values to this range).  Defaults
to min/max (no constraint).

options.name: minrate maxrate bufsize rc_init_occupancy
options.description:
Set rate control buffering parameters.  Not used if not set - defaults
to unconstrained variable bitrate.

options.name: threads
options.description:
Set the number of threads to use while encoding.  This may require the tiles or row-mt options to also be set to actually
use the specified number of threads fully. Defaults to the number of
hardware threads supported by the host machine.

options.name: profile
options.description:
Set the encoding profile.  Defaults to using the profile which matches
the bit depth and chroma subsampling of the input.

options.name: cpu-used
options.description:
Set the quality/encoding speed tradeoff.  Valid range is from 0 to 8,
higher numbers indicating greater speed and lower quality.  The default
value is 1, which will be slow and high quality.
options.default: 1, which will be slow and high quality

options.name: auto-alt-ref
options.description:
Enable use of alternate reference frames.  Defaults to the internal
default of the library.

options.name: arnr-max-frames
options.description: Set altref noise reduction max frame count. Default is -1.

options.name: arnr-strength
options.description: Set altref noise reduction filter strength. Range is -1 to 6. Default is -1.

options.name: aq-mode
options.description: Set adaptive quantization mode. Possible values:

options.name: tune
options.description: Set the distortion metric the encoder is tuned with. Default is psnr .

options.name: lag-in-frames
options.description:
Set the maximum number of frames which the encoder may keep in flight
at any one time for lookahead purposes.  Defaults to the internal
default of the library.

options.name: error-resilience
options.description:
Enable error resilience features:
Not enabled by default.

possible_values.description: Improve resilience against losses of whole frames.

options.name: crf
options.description:
Set the quality/size tradeoff for constant-quality (no bitrate target)
and constrained-quality (with maximum bitrate target) modes. Valid
range is 0 to 63, higher numbers indicating lower quality and smaller
output size.  Only used if set; by default only the bitrate target is
used.

options.name: static-thresh
options.description:
Set a change threshold on blocks below which they will be skipped by
the encoder.  Defined in arbitrary units as a nonnegative integer,
defaulting to zero (no blocks are skipped).

options.name: drop-threshold
options.description:
Set a threshold for dropping frames when close to rate control bounds.
Defined as a percentage of the target buffer - when the rate control
buffer falls below this percentage, frames will be dropped until it
has refilled above the threshold.  Defaults to zero (no frames are
dropped).

options.name: denoise-noise-level
options.description:
Amount of noise to be removed for grain synthesis. Grain synthesis is disabled if
this option is not set or set to 0.

options.name: denoise-block-size
options.description:
Block size used for denoising for grain synthesis. If not set, AV1 codec
uses the default value of 32.

options.name: undershoot-pct
options.description:
Set datarate undershoot (min) percentage of the target bitrate. Range is -1 to 100.
Default is -1.

options.name: overshoot-pct
options.description:
Set datarate overshoot (max) percentage of the target bitrate. Range is -1 to 1000.
Default is -1.

options.name: minsection-pct
options.description:
Minimum percentage variation of the GOP bitrate from the target bitrate. If minsection-pct
is not set, the libaomenc wrapper computes it as follows: (minrate * 100 / bitrate) .
Range is -1 to 100. Default is -1 (unset).

options.name: maxsection-pct
options.description:
Maximum percentage variation of the GOP bitrate from the target bitrate. If maxsection-pct
is not set, the libaomenc wrapper computes it as follows: (maxrate * 100 / bitrate) .
Range is -1 to 5000. Default is -1 (unset).

options.name: frame-parallel
options.description: Enable frame parallel decodability features. Default is true.

options.name: tiles
options.description:
Set the number of tiles to encode the input video with, as columns x
rows.  Larger numbers allow greater parallelism in both encoding and
decoding, but may decrease coding efficiency.  Defaults to the minimum
number of tiles required by the size of the input video (this is 1x1
(that is, a single tile) for sizes up to and including 4K).

options.name: tile-columns tile-rows
options.description:
Set the number of tiles as log2 of the number of tile rows and columns.
Provided for compatibility with libvpx/VP9.

options.name: row-mt
options.description: Enable row based multi-threading. Disabled by default.

options.name: enable-cdef
options.description:
Enable Constrained Directional Enhancement Filter. The libaom-av1
encoder enables CDEF by default.

options.name: enable-restoration
options.description: Enable Loop Restoration Filter. Default is true for libaom-av1.

options.name: enable-global-motion
options.description: Enable the use of global motion for block prediction. Default is true.

options.name: enable-intrabc
options.description:
Enable block copy mode for intra block prediction. This mode is
useful for screen content. Default is true.

options.name: enable-rect-partitions ( )
options.description: Enable rectangular partitions. Default is true.

options.name: enable-1to4-partitions ( )
options.description: Enable 1:4/4:1 partitions. Default is true.

options.name: enable-ab-partitions ( )
options.description: Enable AB shape partitions. Default is true.

options.name: enable-angle-delta ( )
options.description: Enable angle delta intra prediction. Default is true.

options.name: enable-cfl-intra ( )
options.description: Enable chroma predicted from luma intra prediction. Default is true.

options.name: enable-filter-intra ( )
options.description: Enable filter intra predictor. Default is true.

options.name: enable-intra-edge-filter ( )
options.description: Enable intra edge filter. Default is true.

options.name: enable-smooth-intra ( )
options.description: Enable smooth intra prediction mode. Default is true.

options.name: enable-paeth-intra ( )
options.description: Enable paeth predictor in intra prediction. Default is true.

options.name: enable-palette ( )
options.description: Enable palette prediction mode. Default is true.

options.name: enable-flip-idtx ( )
options.description:
Enable extended transform type, including FLIPADST_DCT, DCT_FLIPADST,
FLIPADST_FLIPADST, ADST_FLIPADST, FLIPADST_ADST, IDTX, V_DCT, H_DCT,
V_ADST, H_ADST, V_FLIPADST, H_FLIPADST. Default is true.

options.name: enable-tx64 ( )
options.description: Enable 64-pt transform. Default is true.

options.name: reduced-tx-type-set ( )
options.description: Use reduced set of transform types. Default is false.

options.name: use-intra-dct-only ( )
options.description: Use DCT only for INTRA modes. Default is false.

options.name: use-inter-dct-only ( )
options.description: Use DCT only for INTER modes. Default is false.

options.name: use-intra-default-tx-only ( )
options.description: Use Default-transform only for INTRA modes. Default is false.

options.name: enable-ref-frame-mvs ( )
options.description: Enable temporal mv prediction. Default is true.

options.name: enable-reduced-reference-set ( )
options.description: Use reduced set of single and compound references. Default is false.

options.name: enable-obmc ( )
options.description: Enable obmc. Default is true.

options.name: enable-dual-filter ( )
options.description: Enable dual filter. Default is true.

options.name: enable-diff-wtd-comp ( )
options.description: Enable difference-weighted compound. Default is true.

options.name: enable-dist-wtd-comp ( )
options.description: Enable distance-weighted compound. Default is true.

options.name: enable-onesided-comp ( )
options.description: Enable one sided compound. Default is true.

options.name: enable-interinter-wedge ( )
options.description: Enable interinter wedge compound. Default is true.

options.name: enable-interintra-wedge ( )
options.description: Enable interintra wedge compound. Default is true.

options.name: enable-masked-comp ( )
options.description: Enable masked compound. Default is true.

options.name: enable-interintra-comp ( )
options.description: Enable interintra compound. Default is true.

options.name: enable-smooth-interintra ( )
options.description: Enable smooth interintra mode. Default is true.

options.name: aom-params
options.description:
Set libaom options using a list of key = value pairs separated
by ":". For a list of supported options, see aomenc --help under the
section "AV1 Specific Options".
For example to specify libaom encoding options with -aom-params :

options.name: preset
options.description: Set the quality-speed tradeoff [fastest, fast, medium, slow, placebo, default]

options.name: qp
options.description: Set the quantization parameter value for CQP rate control mode.

options.name: oapv-params
options.description:
Set liboapvenc options using a list of key = value pairs separated
by ":". See the liboapv encoder user guide for a list of accepted parameters.

options.name: profile
options.description: Set the encoding profile.

options.name: level
options.description: Set the operating point level. For example: ’4.0’

options.name: hielevel
options.description: Set the Hierarchical prediction levels.

options.name: tier
options.description: Set the operating point tier.

options.name: qmax
options.description: Set the maximum quantizer to use when using a bitrate mode.

options.name: qmin
options.description: Set the minimum quantizer to use when using a bitrate mode.

options.name: crf
options.description: Constant rate factor value used in crf rate control mode (0-63).

options.name: qp
options.description: Set the quantizer used in cqp rate control mode (0-63).

options.name: sc_detection
options.description: Enable scene change detection.

options.name: la_depth
options.description: Set number of frames to look ahead (0-120).

options.name: preset
options.description:
Set the quality-speed tradeoff, in the range 0 to 13.  Higher values are
faster but lower quality.

options.name: tile_rows
options.description: Set log2 of the number of rows of tiles to use (0-6).

options.name: tile_columns
options.description: Set log2 of the number of columns of tiles to use (0-4).

options.name: svtav1-params
options.description:
Set SVT-AV1 options using a list of key = value pairs separated
by ":". See the SVT-AV1 encoder user guide for a list of accepted parameters.

options.name: decomp_v
options.description: Set vertical decomposition level

options.name: decomp_h
options.description: Set horizontal decomposition level

options.name: quantization
options.description: Set the quantization algorithm.

options.name: coding-signs
options.description: Enable Signs handling strategy

options.name: coding-sigf
options.description: Enable Significance coding

options.name: coding-vpred
options.description: Enable Vertical Prediction coding

options.name: distance
options.description:
Set the target Butteraugli distance. This is a quality setting: lower
distance yields higher quality, with distance=1.0 roughly comparable to
libjpeg Quality 90 for photographic content. Setting distance=0.0 yields
true lossless encoding. Valid values range between 0.0 and 15.0, and sane
values rarely exceed 5.0. Setting distance=0.1 usually attains
transparency for most input. The default is 1.0.

options.name: effort
options.description:
Set the encoding effort used. Higher effort values produce more consistent
quality and usually produces a better quality/bpp curve, at the cost of
more CPU time required. Valid values range from 1 to 9, and the default is 7.

options.name: modular
options.description:
Force the encoder to use Modular mode instead of choosing automatically. The
default is to use VarDCT for lossy encoding and Modular for lossless. VarDCT
is generally superior to Modular for lossy encoding but does not support
lossless encoding.
options.default: to use VarDCT for lossy encoding and Modular for lossless

options.name: b
options.description: Set target video bitrate in bit/s and enable rate control.

options.name: kvazaar-params
options.description:
Set kvazaar parameters as a list of name = value pairs separated
by commas (,). See kvazaar documentation for a list of options.

options.name: b
options.description: Set the bitrate (as a number of bits per second).

options.name: g
options.description: Set the GOP size.

options.name: maxrate
options.description: Set the max bitrate (as a number of bits per second).

options.name: +global_header
options.description: Set global header in the bitstream.

options.name: slices
options.description:
Set the number of slices, used in parallelized encoding. Default value
is 0. This is only used when slice_mode is set to
‘ fixed ’.

options.name: loopfilter
options.description:
Enable loop filter, if set to 1 (automatically enabled). To disable
set a value of 0.

options.name: profile
options.description:
Set profile restrictions. If set to the value of ‘ main ’ enable
CABAC (set the SEncParamExt.iEntropyCodingModeFlag flag to 1).

options.name: max_nal_size
options.description: Set maximum NAL size in bytes.

options.name: allow_skip_frames
options.description: Allow skipping frames to hit the target bitrate if set to 1.

options.name: b
options.description:
Set the video bitrate in bit/s for CBR (Constant Bit Rate) mode.  In
case VBR (Variable Bit Rate) mode is enabled this option is ignored.

options.name: flags
options.description: Used to enable constant quality mode (VBR) encoding through the qscale flag, and to enable the pass1 and pass2 modes.

options.name: g
options.description: Set the GOP size.

options.name: global_quality
options.description:
Set the global quality as an integer in lambda units.
Only relevant when VBR mode is enabled with flags +qscale . The
value is converted to QP units by dividing it by FF_QP2LAMBDA ,
clipped in the [0 - 10] range, and then multiplied by 6.3 to get a
value in the native libtheora range [0-63]. A higher value corresponds
to a higher quality.

options.name: q
options.description:
Enable VBR mode when set to a non-negative value, and set constant
quality value as a double floating point value in QP units.
The value is clipped in the [0-10] range, and then multiplied by 6.3
to get a value in the native libtheora range [0-63].
This option is valid only using the ffmpeg command-line
tool. For library interface users, use global_quality .

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bitrate in bits/s. Note that FFmpeg’s b option is
expressed in bits/s, while vpxenc ’s target-bitrate is in
kilobits/s.

options.name: g
options.description: Minimum (Best Quality) Quantizer.

options.name: qmax
options.description:
Maximum (Worst Quality) Quantizer.
Can be changed per-frame.

options.name: bufsize
options.description:
Set ratecontrol buffer size (in bits). Note vpxenc ’s options are
specified in milliseconds, the libvpx wrapper converts this value as follows: buf-sz = bufsize * 1000 / bitrate , buf-optimal-sz = bufsize * 1000 / bitrate * 5 / 6 .

options.name: rc_init_occupancy
options.description:
Set number of bits which should be loaded into the rc buffer before decoding
starts. Note vpxenc ’s option is specified in milliseconds, the libvpx
wrapper converts this value as follows: rc_init_occupancy * 1000 / bitrate .

options.name: undershoot-pct
options.description: Set datarate undershoot (min) percentage of the target bitrate.

options.name: overshoot-pct
options.description: Set datarate overshoot (max) percentage of the target bitrate.

options.name: skip_threshold
options.description:
Set GOP max bitrate in bits/s. Note vpxenc ’s option is specified as a
percentage of the target bitrate, the libvpx wrapper converts this value as
follows: (maxrate * 100 / bitrate) .

options.name: minrate
options.description:
Set GOP min bitrate in bits/s. Note vpxenc ’s option is specified as a
percentage of the target bitrate, the libvpx wrapper converts this value as
follows: (minrate * 100 / bitrate) .

options.name: minrate
options.description: (minrate == maxrate == bitrate) .

possible_values.description:
Use best quality deadline. Poorly named and quite slow, this option should be
avoided as it may give worse quality output than good.

Use good quality deadline. This is a good trade-off between speed and quality
when used with the cpu-used option.

options.name: speed
options.description:
Set quality/speed ratio modifier. Higher values speed up the encode at the cost
of quality.

options.name: nr
options.description:
Set a change threshold on blocks below which they will be skipped by the
encoder.

options.name: slices
options.description:
Note that FFmpeg’s slices option gives the total number of partitions,
while vpxenc ’s token-parts is given as log2(partitions) .

options.name: max-intra-rate
options.description:
Set maximum I-frame bitrate as a percentage of the target bitrate. A value of 0
means unlimited.

options.name: force_key_frames
options.description: VPX_EFLAG_FORCE_KF

possible_values.description:
Enable use of alternate reference frames (2-pass only).
Values greater than 1 enable multi-layer alternate reference frames (VP9 only).
Set altref noise reduction max frame count.
Set altref noise reduction filter type: backward, forward, centered.
Set altref noise reduction filter strength.
Set number of frames to look ahead for frametype and ratecontrol.
Set minimum golden/alternate reference frame interval (VP9 only).

options.name: error-resilient
options.description: Enable error resiliency features.

options.name: sharpness
options.description:
Increase sharpness at the expense of lower PSNR.
The valid range is [0, 7].

options.name: ts-parameters
options.description:
Sets the temporal scalability configuration using a :-separated list of
key=value pairs. For example, to specify temporal scalability parameters
with ffmpeg :
Below is a brief explanation of each of the parameters, please
refer to struct vpx_codec_enc_cfg in vpx/vpx_encoder.h for more
details.

possible_values.description:
Target bitrate for each temporal layer (in kbps).
(bitrate should be inclusive of the lower temporal layer).
Frame rate decimation factor for each temporal layer.
Length of the sequence defining frame temporal layer membership.
Template defining the membership of frames to temporal layers.

(optional) Selecting the temporal structure from a set of pre-defined temporal layering modes.
Currently supports the following options.
Screen content mode, one of: 0 (off), 1 (screen), 2 (screen with more aggressive rate control).

Set number of tile columns to use. Note this is given as log2(tile_columns) . For example, 8 tile columns would be requested by
setting the tile-columns option to 3.

Set number of tile rows to use. Note this is given as log2(tile_rows) .
For example, 4 tile rows would be requested by setting the tile-rows option to 2.

Set adaptive quantization mode (0: off (default), 1: variance 2: complexity, 3:
cyclic refresh, 4: equator360).

Set input color space. The VP9 bitstream supports signaling the following
colorspaces:
Set content type: default (0), screen (1), film (2).

Corpus VBR mode is a variant of standard VBR where the complexity distribution
midpoint is passed in rather than calculated for a specific clip or chunk.
The valid range is [0, 10000]. 0 (default) uses standard VBR.

Using per-frame metadata, set members of the structure vpx_svc_ref_frame_config_t in vpx/vp8cx.h to fine-control referencing schemes and frame buffer management. Use a :-separated list of key=value pairs.
For example,

options.name: b
options.description: Sets target video bitrate.

options.name: g
options.description: Set the GOP size. Currently support for g=1 (Intra only) or default.

options.name: preset
options.description: Set the VVenC preset.

options.name: levelidc
options.description: Set level idc.

options.name: tier
options.description: Set vvc tier.

options.name: qp
options.description: Set constant quantization parameter.

options.name: subopt
options.description: Set subjective (perceptually motivated) optimization. Default is 1 (on).

options.name: bitdepth8
options.description: Set 8bit coding mode instead of using 10bit. Default is 0 (off).

options.name: period
options.description: set (intra) refresh period in seconds.

options.name: vvenc-params
options.description:
Set vvenc options using a list of key = value couples separated
by ":". See vvencapp --fullhelp or vvencFFapp --fullhelp for a list of options.
For example, the options might be provided as:
For example the encoding options might be provided with -vvenc-params :

options.name: -lossless boolean
options.description: Enables/Disables use of lossless mode. Default is 0.

options.name: -compression_level integer
options.description:
For lossy, this is a quality/speed tradeoff. Higher values give better quality
for a given size at the cost of increased encoding time. For lossless, this is
a size/speed tradeoff. Higher values give smaller size at the cost of increased
encoding time. More specifically, it controls the number of extra algorithms
and compression tools used, and varies the combination of these tools. This
maps to the method option in libwebp. The valid range is 0 to 6.
Default is 4.

options.name: -quality float
options.description:
For lossy encoding, this controls image quality. For lossless encoding, this
controls the effort and time spent in compression.
Range is 0 to 100. Default is 75.

options.name: -preset type
options.description:
Configuration preset. This does some automatic settings based on the general
type of the image.

possible_values.description: Digital picture, like portrait, inner shot
Hand or line drawing, with high-contrast details

options.name: b
options.description:
Set bitrate in bits/s. Note that FFmpeg’s b option is
expressed in bits/s, while x264 ’s bitrate is in
kilobits/s.

options.name: bf
options.description: Number of B-frames between I and P-frames

options.name: g
options.description: Maximum GOP size

options.name: qmin
options.description: Minimum quantizer scale

options.name: qmax
options.description: Maximum quantizer scale

options.name: qdiff
options.description: Maximum difference between quantizer scales

options.name: qblur
options.description: Quantizer curve blur

options.name: qcomp
options.description: Quantizer curve compression factor

options.name: refs
options.description: Number of reference frames each P-frame can use. The range is 0-16 .

options.name: level
options.description:
Set the x264_param_t.i_level_idc value in case the value is
positive, it is ignored otherwise.
This value can be set using the AVCodecContext API (e.g. by
setting the AVCodecContext value directly), and is specified as
an integer mapped on a corresponding level (e.g. the value 31 maps
to H.264 level IDC "3.1", as defined in the x264_levels table). It is ignored when set to a non positive value.
Alternatively it can be set as a private option, overriding the value
set in AVCodecContext , and in this case must be specified as
the level IDC identifier (e.g. "3.1"), as defined by H.264 Annex A.

options.name: sc_threshold
options.description: Sets the threshold for the scene change detection.

options.name: trellis
options.description: Performs Trellis quantization to increase efficiency. Enabled by default.

options.name: nr
options.description: Noise reduction

options.name: me_range
options.description: Maximum range of the motion search in pixels.

options.name: me_method
options.description:
Set motion estimation method. Possible values in the decreasing order
of speed:

possible_values.description:
Diamond search with radius 1 (fastest). ‘ epzs ’ is an alias for
‘ dia ’.

options.name: forced-idr
options.description:
Normally, when forcing a I-frame type, the encoder can select any type
of I-frame. This option forces it to choose an IDR-frame.

options.name: subq
options.description: Sub-pixel motion estimation method.

options.name: b_strategy
options.description: Adaptive B-frame placement decision algorithm. Use only on first-pass.

options.name: keyint_min
options.description: Minimum GOP size.

options.name: coder
options.description: Set entropy encoder. Possible values:

possible_values.description: Enable CAVLC and disable CABAC. It generates the same effect as x264 ’s --no-cabac option.

options.name: cmp
options.description: Set full pixel motion estimation comparison algorithm. Possible values:

possible_values.description: Ignore chroma in motion estimation. It generates the same effect as x264 ’s --no-chroma-me option.

options.name: threads
options.description: Number of encoding threads.

options.name: thread_type
options.description: Set multithreading technique. Possible values:

possible_values.description: Slice-based multithreading. It generates the same effect as x264 ’s --sliced-threads option.

options.name: flags
options.description:
Set encoding flags. It can be used to disable closed GOP and enable
open GOP by setting it to -cgop . The result is similar to
the behavior of x264 ’s --open-gop option.

options.name: rc_init_occupancy
options.description: Initial VBV buffer occupancy

options.name: preset
options.description: Set the encoding preset.

options.name: tune
options.description: Set tuning of the encoding params.

options.name: profile
options.description: Set profile restrictions.

options.name: fastfirstpass
options.description:
Enable fast settings when encoding first pass, when set to 1. When set
to 0, it has the same effect of x264 ’s --slow-firstpass option.

options.name: crf
options.description: Set the quality for constant quality mode.

options.name: crf_max
options.description: In CRF mode, prevents VBV from lowering quality beyond this point.

options.name: qp
options.description: Set constant quantization rate control method parameter.

options.name: aq-mode
options.description: Set AQ method. Possible values:

options.name: aq-strength
options.description: Set AQ strength, reduce blocking and blurring in flat and textured areas.

options.name: psy
options.description:
Use psychovisual optimizations when set to 1. When set to 0, it has the
same effect as x264 ’s --no-psy option.

options.name: psy-rd
options.description: Set strength of psychovisual optimization, in psy-rd : psy-trellis format.

options.name: rc-lookahead
options.description: Set number of frames to look ahead for frametype and ratecontrol.

options.name: weightb
options.description:
Enable weighted prediction for B-frames when set to 1. When set to 0,
it has the same effect as x264 ’s --no-weightb option.

options.name: weightp
options.description: Set weighted prediction method for P-frames. Possible values:

possible_values.description: Enable both weighted refs and duplicates

options.name: ssim
options.description: Enable calculation and printing SSIM stats after the encoding.

options.name: intra-refresh
options.description:
Enable the use of Periodic Intra Refresh instead of IDR frames when set
to 1.

options.name: avcintra-class
options.description:
Configure the encoder to generate AVC-Intra.
Valid values are 50, 100 and 200

options.name: bluray-compat
options.description:
Configure the encoder to be compatible with the bluray standard.
It is a shorthand for setting "bluray-compat=1 force-cfr=1".

options.name: b-bias
options.description: Set the influence on how often B-frames are used.

options.name: b-pyramid
options.description: Set method for keeping of some B-frames as references. Possible values:

options.name: mixed-refs
options.description:
Enable the use of one reference per partition, as opposed to one
reference per macroblock when set to 1. When set to 0, it has the
same effect as x264 ’s --no-mixed-refs option.

options.name: 8x8dct
options.description:
Enable adaptive spatial transform (high profile 8x8 transform)
when set to 1. When set to 0, it has the same effect as x264 ’s --no-8x8dct option.

options.name: fast-pskip
options.description:
Enable early SKIP detection on P-frames when set to 1. When set
to 0, it has the same effect as x264 ’s --no-fast-pskip option.

options.name: aud
options.description: Enable use of access unit delimiters when set to 1.

options.name: mbtree
options.description:
Enable use macroblock tree ratecontrol when set to 1. When set
to 0, it has the same effect as x264 ’s --no-mbtree option.

options.name: deblock
options.description: Set loop filter parameters, in alpha : beta form.

options.name: cplxblur
options.description: Set fluctuations reduction in QP (before curve compression).

options.name: partitions
options.description:
Set partitions to consider as a comma-separated list of values.
Possible values in the list:

possible_values.description:
4x4 I-frame partition.
(Enabling ‘ p4x4 ’ requires ‘ p8x8 ’ to be enabled. Enabling
‘ i8x8 ’ requires adaptive spatial transform ( 8x8dct option) to be enabled.)

options.name: direct-pred
options.description: Set direct MV prediction mode. Possible values:

options.name: slice-max-size
options.description:
Set the limit of the size of each slice in bytes. If not specified
but RTP payload size ( ps ) is specified, that is used.

options.name: stats
options.description: Set the file name for multi-pass stats.

options.name: nal-hrd
options.description:
Set signal HRD information (requires vbv-bufsize to be set).
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Constant bit rate (not allowed in MP4 container).

Override the x264 configuration using a :-separated list of key=value

The argument for both options is a list of key = value couples separated by ":". With x264opts the value can be
omitted, and the value 1 is assumed in that case.
For filter and psy-rd options values that use ":" as a

Normally, when forcing a I-frame type, the encoder can select any type

options.name: x265-params
options.description:
Set x265 options using a list of key = value couples separated
by ":". See x265 --help for a list of options.
For example to specify libx265 encoding options with -x265-params :

options.name: lcu_row_threads
options.description: Set the number of parallel threads for rows from 1 to 8 (default 5).

options.name: initial_qp
options.description:
Set the xavs2 quantization parameter from 1 to 63 (default 34). This is
used to set the initial qp for the first frame.

options.name: qp
options.description:
Set the xavs2 quantization parameter from 1 to 63 (default 34). This is
used to set the qp value under constant-QP mode.

options.name: max_qp
options.description: Set the max qp for rate control from 1 to 63 (default 55).

options.name: min_qp
options.description: Set the min qp for rate control from 1 to 63 (default 20).

options.name: speed_level
options.description: Set the Speed level from 0 to 9 (default 0). Higher is better but slower.

options.name: log_level
options.description:
Set the log level from -1 to 3 (default 0). -1: none, 0: error,
1: warning, 2: info, 3: debug.

options.name: xavs2-params
options.description:
Set xavs2 options using a list of key = value couples separated
by ":".
For example to specify libxavs2 encoding options with -xavs2-params :

options.name: b
options.description:
Set target video bitrate in bits/s.
Note that FFmpeg’s b option is expressed in bits/s, while xeve’s bitrate is in kilobits/s.

options.name: bf
options.description: Set the maximum number of B frames (1,3,7,15).

options.name: g
options.description: Set the GOP size (I-picture period).

options.name: preset
options.description:
Set the xeve preset.
Set the encoder preset value to determine encoding speed [fast, medium, slow, placebo]

options.name: tune
options.description: Set the encoder tune parameter [psnr, zerolatency]

options.name: profile
options.description: Set the encoder profile [0: baseline; 1: main]

options.name: crf
options.description:
Set the quality for constant quality mode.
Constant rate factor <10..49> [default: 32]

options.name: qp
options.description:
Set constant quantization rate control method parameter.
Quantization parameter qp <0..51> [default: 32]

options.name: threads
options.description: Force to use a specific number of threads

options.name: b
options.description: Set specific encoding flags. Possible values:

possible_values.description: Use four motion vector by macroblock.
Place global headers in extradata instead of every keyframe.

options.name: gmc
options.description:
Enable the use of global motion compensation (GMC).  Default is 0
(disabled).

options.name: me_quality
options.description:
Set motion estimation quality level. Possible values in decreasing order of
speed and increasing order of quality:

possible_values.description:
Enable advanced diamond zonal search for 16x16 blocks and half-pixel
refinement for 16x16 blocks.

Enable all of the things described above, plus advanced diamond zonal
search for 8x8 blocks and half-pixel refinement for 8x8 blocks, also
enable motion estimation on chroma planes for P and B-frames.

Enable all of the things described above, plus extended 16x16 and 8x8
blocks search.

options.name: mbd
options.description:
Set macroblock decision algorithm. Possible values in the increasing
order of quality:

possible_values.description:
Enable rate distortion-based half pixel and quarter pixel refinement for
16x16 blocks.

Enable all of the things described above, plus rate distortion-based
half pixel and quarter pixel refinement for 8x8 blocks, and rate
distortion-based search using square pattern.

options.name: lumi_aq
options.description:
Enable lumi masking adaptive quantization when set to 1. Default is 0
(disabled).

options.name: variance_aq
options.description:
Enable variance adaptive quantization when set to 1. Default is 0
(disabled).
When combined with lumi_aq , the resulting quality will not
be better than any of the two specified individually. In other
words, the resulting quality will be the worse one of the two
effects.

options.name: trellis
options.description: Set rate-distortion optimal quantization.

options.name: ssim
options.description: Set structural similarity (SSIM) displaying method. Possible values:

possible_values.description:
Output average SSIM at the end of encoding to stdout. The format of
showing the average SSIM is:
For users who are not familiar with C, %f means a float number, or
a decimal (e.g. 0.939232).

Output both per-frame SSIM data during encoding and average SSIM at
the end of encoding to stdout. The format of per-frame information
is:
For users who are not familiar with C, %1.3f means a float number
rounded to 3 digits after the dot (e.g. 0.932).

options.name: ssim_acc
options.description:
Set SSIM accuracy. Valid options are integers within the range of
0-4, while 0 gives the most accurate result and 4 computes the
fastest.

options.name: ndk_codec
options.description:
Use the NDK-based MediaCodec API instead of the Java API. Enabled by default
if av_jni_get_java_vm() return NULL.

options.name: ndk_async
options.description:
Use NDK MediaCodec in async mode. Async mode has less overhead than poll in a
loop in sync mode. The drawback of async mode is AV_CODEC_FLAG_GLOBAL_HEADER
doesn’t work (use extract_extradata bsf when necessary). It doesn’t work and
will be disabled automatically on devices below Android 8.0.

options.name: codec_name
options.description:
A codec type can have multiple implementations on a single device, this option
specify which backend to use (via MediaCodec createCodecByName API). It’s NULL
by default, and encoder is created by createEncoderByType.

options.name: bitrate_mode
options.description: Possible values:

possible_values.description: Constant bitrate mode with frame drops

options.name: pts_as_dts
options.description:
Use PTS as DTS. This is a workaround since MediaCodec API doesn’t provide
decoding timestamp. It is enabled automatically if B frame is 0.

options.name: operating_rate
options.description:
The desired operating rate that the codec will need to operate at, zero for
unspecified. This is used for cases like high-speed/slow-motion video capture,
where the video encoder format contains the target playback rate (e.g. 30fps),
but the component must be able to handle the high operating capture rate (e.g.
240fps). This rate will be used by codec for resource planning and setting the
operating points.

options.name: qp_i_min
options.description: Minimum quantization parameter for I frame.

options.name: qp_p_min
options.description: Minimum quantization parameter for P frame.

options.name: qp_b_min
options.description: Minimum quantization parameter for B frame.

options.name: qp_i_max
options.description: Maximum quantization parameter for I frame.

options.name: qp_p_max
options.description: Maximum quantization parameter for P frame.

options.name: qp_b_max
options.description: Maximum quantization parameter for B frame.

options.name: rate_control
options.description: Select rate control mode. Available modes:

possible_values.description: Global low delay VBR mode (requires Windows 8+)

options.name: scenario
options.description: Select usage scenario. Available scenarios:

possible_values.description: Display remoting with feature map scenario

options.name: quality
options.description: Set encoding quality (0-100). -1 means default quality.

options.name: hw_encoding
options.description: Force hardware encoding (0-1). Default is 0 (disabled).

options.name: g
options.description:
Keyframe interval.
A keyframe is inserted at least every -g frames, sometimes sooner.

options.name: profile
options.description: Select the mpeg2 profile to encode:

options.name: level
options.description: Select the mpeg2 level to encode:

options.name: seq_disp_ext
options.description:
Specifies if the encoder should write a sequence_display_extension to the
output.

possible_values.description:
Decide automatically to write it or not (this is the default) by checking if
the data to be written is different from the default or unspecified values.

options.name: video_format
options.description:
Specifies the video_format written into the sequence display extension
indicating the source of the video pictures. The default is ‘ unspecified ’,
can be ‘ component ’, ‘ pal ’, ‘ ntsc ’, ‘ secam ’ or ‘ mac ’.
For maximum compatibility, use ‘ component ’.

options.name: a53cc
options.description:
Import closed captions (which must be ATSC compatible format) into output.
Default is 1 (on).

options.name: compression_level
options.description: Sets the compression level, from 0 to 9(default)

options.name: dpi
options.description: Set physical density of pixels, in dots per inch, unset by default

options.name: dpm
options.description: Set physical density of pixels, in dots per meter, unset by default

options.name: pred method
options.description: Set prediction method (none, sub, up, avg, paeth, mixed), default is paeth

options.name: profile
options.description: Select the ProRes profile to encode

options.name: quant_mat
options.description:
Select quantization matrix.
If set to auto , the matrix matching the profile will be picked.
If not set, the matrix providing the highest quality, default , will be
picked.

options.name: bits_per_mb
options.description:
How many bits to allot for coding one macroblock. Different profiles use
between 200 and 2400 bits per macroblock, the maximum is 8000.

options.name: mbs_per_slice
options.description:
Number of macroblocks in each slice (1-8); the default value (8)
should be good in almost all situations.

options.name: vendor
options.description:
Override the 4-byte vendor ID.
A custom vendor ID like apl0 would claim the stream was produced by
the Apple encoder.

options.name: alpha_bits
options.description:
Specify number of bits for alpha component.
Possible values are 0 , 8 and 16 .
Use 0 to disable alpha plane coding.

options.name: async_depth
options.description:
Specifies how many asynchronous operations an application performs
before the application explicitly synchronizes the result. If zero,
the value is not specified.

options.name: preset
options.description:
This option itemizes a range of choices from veryfast (best speed) to veryslow
(best quality).

options.name: forced_idr
options.description: Forcing I frames as IDR frames.

options.name: low_power
options.description: For encoders set this flag to ON to reduce power consumption and GPU usage.

options.name: global_quality
options.description:
Supported in h264_qsv and hevc_qsv.
Change these value to reset qsv codec’s qp configuration.

options.name: max_frame_size
options.description:
Supported in h264_qsv and hevc_qsv.
Change this value to reset qsv codec’s MaxFrameSize configuration.

options.name: gop_size
options.description: Change this value to reset qsv codec’s gop configuration.

options.name: int_ref_type
options.description:
Supported in h264_qsv and hevc_qsv.
Change these value to reset qsv codec’s Intra Refresh configuration.

options.name: qmax
options.description:
Supported in h264_qsv.
Change these value to reset qsv codec’s max/min qp configuration.

options.name: low_delay_brc
options.description:
Supported in h264_qsv, hevc_qsv and av1_qsv.
Change this value to reset qsv codec’s low_delay_brc configuration.

options.name: framerate
options.description: Change this value to reset qsv codec’s framerate configuration.

options.name: bit_rate
options.description: Change these value to reset qsv codec’s bitrate control configuration.

options.name: pic_timing_sei
options.description:
Supported in h264_qsv and hevc_qsv.
Change this value to reset qsv codec’s pic_timing_sei configuration.

options.name: qsv_params
options.description:
Set QSV encoder parameters as a colon-separated list of key-value pairs.
The qsv_params should be formatted as key1=value1:key2=value2:... .
These parameters are passed directly to the underlying Intel Quick Sync Video (QSV) encoder using the MFXSetParameter function.
Example:
This option allows fine-grained control over various encoder-specific settings provided by the QSV encoder.

options.name: extbrc
options.description: Extended bitrate control.

options.name: recovery_point_sei
options.description:
Set this flag to insert the recovery point SEI message at the beginning of every
intra refresh cycle.

options.name: rdo
options.description: Enable rate distortion optimization.

options.name: max_frame_size
options.description: Maximum encoded frame size in bytes.

options.name: max_frame_size_i
options.description:
Maximum encoded frame size for I frames in bytes. If this value is set as larger
than zero, then for I frames the value set by max_frame_size is ignored.

options.name: max_frame_size_p
options.description:
Maximum encoded frame size for P frames in bytes. If this value is set as larger
than zero, then for P frames the value set by max_frame_size is ignored.

options.name: max_slice_size
options.description: Maximum encoded slice size in bytes.

options.name: bitrate_limit
options.description:
Toggle bitrate limitations.
Modifies bitrate to be in the range imposed by the QSV encoder. Setting this
flag off may lead to violation of HRD conformance. Mind that specifying bitrate
below the QSV encoder range might significantly affect quality. If on this
option takes effect in non CQP modes: if bitrate is not in the range imposed
by the QSV encoder, it will be changed to be in the range.

options.name: mbbrc
options.description:
Setting this flag enables macroblock level bitrate control that generally
improves subjective visual quality. Enabling this flag may have negative impact
on performance and objective visual quality metric.

options.name: low_delay_brc
options.description:
Setting this flag turns on or off LowDelayBRC feature in qsv plugin, which provides
more accurate bitrate control to minimize the variance of bitstream size frame
by frame. Value: -1-default 0-off 1-on

options.name: adaptive_i
options.description:
This flag controls insertion of I frames by the QSV encoder. Turn ON this flag
to allow changing of frame type from P and B to I.

options.name: adaptive_b
options.description: This flag controls changing of frame type from B to P.

options.name: p_strategy
options.description: Enable P-pyramid: 0-default 1-simple 2-pyramid(bf need to be set to 0).

options.name: b_strategy
options.description: This option controls usage of B frames as reference.

options.name: dblk_idc
options.description: This option disable deblocking. It has value in range 0~2.

options.name: cavlc
options.description: If set, CAVLC is used; if unset, CABAC is used for encoding.

options.name: vcm
options.description: Video conferencing mode, please see ratecontrol method.

options.name: idr_interval
options.description: Distance (in I-frames) between IDR frames.

options.name: pic_timing_sei
options.description: Insert picture timing SEI with pic_struct_syntax element.

options.name: single_sei_nal_unit
options.description: Put all the SEI messages into one NALU.

options.name: max_dec_frame_buffering
options.description: Maximum number of frames buffered in the DPB.

options.name: look_ahead
options.description: Use VBR algorithm with look ahead.

options.name: look_ahead_depth
options.description: Depth of look ahead in number frames.

options.name: look_ahead_downsampling
options.description: Downscaling factor for the frames saved for the lookahead analysis.

options.name: int_ref_type
options.description:
Specifies intra refresh type. The major goal of intra refresh is improvement of
error resilience without significant impact on encoded bitstream size caused by
I frames. The SDK encoder achieves this by encoding part of each frame in
refresh cycle using intra MBs. none means no refresh. vertical means
vertical refresh, by column of MBs. horizontal means horizontal refresh,
by rows of MBs. slice means horizontal refresh by slices without
overlapping. In case of slice , in_ref_cycle_size is ignored. To enable
intra refresh, B frame should be set to 0.

options.name: int_ref_cycle_size
options.description:
Specifies number of pictures within refresh cycle starting from 2. 0 and 1 are
invalid values.

options.name: int_ref_qp_delta
options.description:
Specifies QP difference for inserted intra MBs. This is signed value in
[-51, 51] range if target encoding bit-depth for luma samples is 8 and this
range is [-63, 63] for 10 bit-depth or [-75, 75] for 12 bit-depth respectively.

options.name: int_ref_cycle_dist
options.description: Distance between the beginnings of the intra-refresh cycles in frames.

options.name: a53cc
options.description: Use A53 Closed Captions (if available).

options.name: aud
options.description: Insert the Access Unit Delimiter NAL.

options.name: mfmode
options.description: Multi-Frame Mode.

options.name: repeat_pps
options.description: Repeat pps for every frame.

options.name: max_qp_i
options.description: Maximum video quantizer scale for I frame.

options.name: min_qp_i
options.description: Minimum video quantizer scale for I frame.

options.name: max_qp_p
options.description: Maximum video quantizer scale for P frame.

options.name: min_qp_p
options.description: Minimum video quantizer scale for P frame.

options.name: max_qp_b
options.description: Maximum video quantizer scale for B frame.

options.name: min_qp_b
options.description: Minimum video quantizer scale for B frame.

options.name: scenario
options.description: Provides a hint to encoder about the scenario for the encoding session.

options.name: avbr_accuracy
options.description: Accuracy of the AVBR ratecontrol (unit of tenth of percent).

options.name: avbr_convergence
options.description:
Convergence of the AVBR ratecontrol (unit of 100 frames)
The parameters avbr_accuracy and avbr_convergence are for the
average variable bitrate control (AVBR) algorithm.
The algorithm focuses on overall encoding quality while meeting the specified
bitrate, target_bitrate , within the accuracy range avbr_accuracy ,
after a avbr_Convergence period. This method does not follow HRD and the
instant bitrate is not capped or padded.

options.name: skip_frame
options.description:
Use per-frame metadata "qsv_skip_frame" to skip frame when encoding. This option
defines the usage of this metadata.

possible_values.description:
Encoder inserts into bitstream frame where all macroblocks are encoded as
skipped.

Similar to insert_dummy, but encoder inserts nothing into bitstream. The skipped
frames are still used in brc. For example, gop still include skipped frames, and
the frames after skipped frames will be larger in size.

skip_frame metadata indicates the number of missed frames before the current
frame.

options.name: extbrc
options.description: Extended bitrate control.

options.description:
Set this flag to insert the recovery point SEI message at the beginning of every
intra refresh cycle.

options.description: Enable rate distortion optimization.

options.name: max_frame_size_i
options.description:

options.name: max_frame_size_p
options.description:

options.name: max_slice_size
options.description: Maximum encoded slice size in bytes.

options.description: Enable P-pyramid: 0-default 1-simple 2-pyramid(bf need to be set to 0).

options.name: dblk_idc

options.name: idr_interval
options.description: Distance (in I-frames) between IDR frames.

possible_values.description: Output an IDR-frame only at the beginning of the stream.

options.name: load_plugin
options.description: A user plugin to load in an internal session.

options.name: load_plugins
options.description:
A :-separate list of hexadecimal plugin UIDs to load in
an internal session.

options.name: look_ahead_depth
options.description: Depth of look ahead in number frames, available when extbrc option is enabled.

options.name: profile
options.description: Set the encoding profile (scc requires libmfx >= 1.32).

options.name: tier
options.description:
Set the encoding tier (only level >= 4 can support high tier).
This option only takes effect when the level option is specified.

options.name: gpb
options.description:
1: GPB (generalized P/B frame)
0: regular P frame.

options.name: tile_cols
options.description: Number of columns for tiled encoding.

options.name: tile_rows
options.description: Number of rows for tiled encoding.

options.name: aud
options.description: Insert the Access Unit Delimiter NAL.

options.name: pic_timing_sei
options.description: Insert picture timing SEI with pic_struct_syntax element.

options.name: transform_skip
options.description:
Turn this option ON to enable transformskip. It is supported on platform equal
or newer than ICL.

Specifies intra refresh type. The major goal of intra refresh is improvement of

I frames. The SDK encoder achieves this by encoding part of each frame in

Specifies number of pictures within refresh cycle starting from 2. 0 and 1 are

options.description: Distance between the beginnings of the intra-refresh cycles in frames.

skipped.

Similar to insert_dummy, but encoder inserts nothing into bitstream. The skipped

options.name: tile_cols
options.description: Number of columns for tiled encoding (requires libmfx >= 1.29).

options.name: tile_rows
options.description: Number of rows for tiled encoding (requires libmfx  >= 1.29).

options.name: tile_cols
options.description: Number of columns for tiled encoding.

options.name: tile_rows
options.description: Number of rows for tiled encoding.

options.name: adaptive_i
options.description:
This flag controls insertion of I frames by the QSV encoder. Turn ON this flag
to allow changing of frame type from P and B to I.

options.name: adaptive_b
options.description: This flag controls changing of frame type from B to P.

options.name: b_strategy
options.description: This option controls usage of B frames as reference.

options.name: extbrc
options.description: Extended bitrate control.

options.name: look_ahead_depth
options.description: Depth of look ahead in number frames, available when extbrc option is enabled.

options.name: low_delay_brc
options.description:
Setting this flag turns on or off LowDelayBRC feature in qsv plugin, which provides
more accurate bitrate control to minimize the variance of bitstream size frame
by frame. Value: -1-default 0-off 1-on

options.name: max_frame_size
options.description:
Set the allowed max size in bytes for each frame. If the frame size exceeds
the limitation, encoder will adjust the QP value to control the frame size.
Invalid in CQP rate control mode.

options.name: max_frame_size_i
options.description:
Maximum encoded frame size for I frames in bytes. If this value is set as larger
than zero, then for I frames the value set by max_frame_size is ignored.

options.name: max_frame_size_p
options.description:
Maximum encoded frame size for P frames in bytes. If this value is set as larger
than zero, then for P frames the value set by max_frame_size is ignored.

options.name: iterative_dia_size
options.description: dia size for the iterative motion estimation

options.name: low_power
options.description:
Some drivers/platforms offer a second encoder for some codecs intended to use
less power than the default encoder; setting this option will attempt to use
that encoder.  Note that it may support a reduced feature set, so some other
options may not be available in this mode.

options.name: idr_interval
options.description:
Set the number of normal intra frames between full-refresh (IDR) frames in
open-GOP mode.  The intra frames are still IRAPs, but will not include global
headers and may have non-decodable leading pictures.

options.name: b_depth
options.description:
Set the B-frame reference depth.  When set to one (the default), all B-frames
will refer only to P- or I-frames.  When set to greater values multiple layers
of B-frames will be present, frames in each layer only referring to frames in
higher layers.

options.name: async_depth
options.description:
Maximum processing parallelism. Increase this to improve single channel
performance. This option doesn’t work if driver doesn’t implement vaSyncBuffer
function. Please make sure there are enough hw_frames allocated if a large
number of async_depth is used.

options.name: max_frame_size
options.description:
Set the allowed max size in bytes for each frame. If the frame size exceeds
the limitation, encoder will adjust the QP value to control the frame size.
Invalid in CQP rate control mode.

options.name: rc_mode
options.description:
Set the rate control mode to use.  A given driver may only support a subset of
modes.
Possible modes:

possible_values.description:
Choose the mode automatically based on driver support and the other options.
This is the default.

options.name: blbrc
options.description:
Enable block level rate control, which assigns different bitrate block by block.
Invalid for CQP mode.

options.name: av1_vaapi
options.description: profile sets the value of seq_profile . tier sets the value of seq_tier . level sets the value of seq_level_idx .

possible_values.description:
Set the number of tiles to encode the input video with, as columns x rows.
(default is auto, which means use minimal tile column/row number).

Set tile groups number. All the tiles will be distributed as evenly as possible to
each tile group. (default is 1).

options.name: h264_vaapi
options.description: profile sets the value of profile_idc and the constraint_set*_flag s. level sets the value of level_idc .

possible_values.description: Set entropy encoder (default is cabac ).  Possible values:
Include access unit delimiters in the stream (not included by default).

Set SEI message types to include.
Some combination of the following values:

options.name: hevc_vaapi
options.description: profile and level set the values of general_profile_idc and general_level_idc respectively.

possible_values.description: Include access unit delimiters in the stream (not included by default).

Set general_tier_flag .  This may affect the level chosen for the stream
if it is not explicitly specified.

Set SEI message types to include.
Some combination of the following values:

Set the number of tiles to encode the input video with, as columns x rows.
Larger numbers allow greater parallelism in both encoding and decoding, but
may decrease coding efficiency.

options.name: mjpeg_vaapi
options.description:
Only baseline DCT encoding is supported.  The encoder always uses the standard
