quantisation and huffman tables - global_quality scales the standard
quantisation table (range 1-100).
For YUV, 4:2:0, 4:2:2 and 4:4:4 subsampling modes are supported.  RGB is also
supported, and will create an RGB JPEG.

possible_values.description: Include JFIF header in each frame (not included by default).

Include standard huffman tables (on by default).  Turning this off will save
a few hundred bytes in each output frame, but may lose compatibility with some
JPEG decoders which don’t fully handle MJPEG.

options.name: mpeg2_vaapi
options.description: profile and level set the value of profile_and_level_indication .

options.name: vp8_vaapi
options.description:
B-frames are not supported.
global_quality sets the q_idx used for non-key frames (range 0-127).

possible_values.description: Manually set the loop filter parameters.

options.name: vp9_vaapi
options.description:
global_quality sets the q_idx used for P-frames (range 0-255).
B-frames are supported, but the output stream is always in encode order rather than display
order.  If B-frames are enabled, it may be necessary to use the vp9_raw_reorder bitstream filter to modify the output stream to display frames in the correct order.
Only normal frames are produced - the vp9_superframe bitstream filter may be
required to produce a stream usable with all decoders.

possible_values.description: Manually set the loop filter parameters.

options.name: format
options.description: Sets the texture compression used by the VBN file. Can be dxt1 , dxt5 or raw . Default is dxt5 .

options.name: b
options.description:
Sets target video bitrate. Usually that’s around 1:6 of the uncompressed
video bitrate (e.g. for 1920x1080 50fps yuv422p10 that’s around 400Mbps). Higher
values (close to the uncompressed bitrate) turn on lossless compression mode.

options.name: field_order
options.description:
Enables field coding when set (e.g. to tt - top field first) for interlaced
inputs. Should increase compression with interlaced content as it splits the
fields and encodes each separately.

options.name: wavelet_depth
options.description:
Sets the total amount of wavelet transforms to apply, between 1 and 5 (default).
Lower values reduce compression and quality. Less capable decoders may not be
able to handle values of wavelet_depth over 3.

options.name: wavelet_type
options.description:
Sets the transform type. Currently only 5_3 (LeGall) and 9_7 (Deslauriers-Dubuc)
are implemented, with 9_7 being the one with better compression and thus
is the default.

options.name: slice_width
options.description:
Sets the slice size for each slice. Larger values result in better compression.
For compatibility with other more limited decoders use slice_width of
32 and slice_height of 8.

options.name: tolerance
options.description:
Sets the undershoot tolerance of the rate control system in percent. This is
to prevent an expensive search from being run.

options.name: qm
options.description: Sets the quantization matrix preset to use by default or when wavelet_depth is set to 5

options.name: min_bpp
options.description:
Set a minimum bits-per-pixel value for the subtitle color lookup tables.
DVB supports 2, 4, and 8 bits-per-pixel color lookup tables.  This
option enables forcing a particular bits-per-pixel value regardless of
the number of colors.  Since not all players support or properly
support 2 bits-per-pixel, this value defaults to 4.

options.name: palette
options.description:
Specify the global palette used by the bitmaps.
The format for this option is a string containing 16 24-bits hexadecimal
numbers (without 0x prefix) separated by commas, for example 0d00ee,
ee450d, 101010, eaeaea, 0ce60b, ec14ed, ebff0b, 0d617a, 7b7b7b, d1d1d1,
7b2a0e, 0d950c, 0f007b, cf0dec, cfa80c, 7c127b .

options.name: even_rows_fix
options.description:
When set to 1, enable a work-around that makes the number of pixel rows
even in all subtitles.  This fixes a problem with some players that
cut off the bottom row if the number is odd.  The work-around just adds
a fully transparent row if needed.  The overhead is low, typically
one byte per subtitle on average.
By default, this work-around is disabled.

options.name: precision
options.description:
Specify the precision of the fractional part of the timestamp. Time base is
determined based on this value.
Defaults to 2 for centiseconds.

options.name: td
options.description:
Insert or remove temporal delimiter OBUs in all temporal units of the
stream.

possible_values.description: Insert a TD at the beginning of every TU which does not already have one.
Remove the TD from the beginning of every TU which has one.

options.name: color_primaries
options.description: Set the color description fields in the stream (see AV1 section 6.4.2).

options.name: color_range
options.description:
Set the color range in the stream (see AV1 section 6.4.2; note that
this cannot be set for streams using BT.709 primaries, sRGB transfer
characteristic and identity (RGB) matrix coefficients).

options.name: chroma_sample_position
options.description:
Set the chroma sample location in the stream (see AV1 section 6.4.2).
This can only be set for 4:2:0 streams.

possible_values.description: Left position (matching the default in MPEG-2 and H.264).

options.name: tick_rate
options.description:
Set the tick rate ( time_scale / num_units_in_display_tick ) in
the timing info in the sequence header.

options.name: num_ticks_per_picture
options.description:
Set the number of ticks in each picture, to indicate that the stream
has a fixed framerate.  Ignored if tick_rate is not also set.

options.name: delete_padding
options.description: Deletes Padding OBUs.

options.name: strip
options.description:
If enabled, strip all Dolby Vision metadata (configuration record + RPU data
blocks) from the stream.

options.name: compression
options.description: Which compression level to enable.

possible_values.description:
Limited metadata compression scheme. Should be compatible with most devices.
This is the default.

Extended metadata compression. Devices are not required to support this. Note
that this level currently behaves the same as ‘ limited ’ in libavcodec.

options.name: freq
options.description:
The additional argument specifies which packets should be filtered.
It accepts the values:

possible_values.description: add extradata to all key packets

options.name: color
options.description: The color to replace damaged blocks by

options.name: sta
options.description:
A 16 bit mask which specifies which of the 16 possible error status values are
to be replaced by colored blocks. 0xFFFE is the default which replaces all non 0
error status values.
see page 44-46 or section 5.5 of http://web.archive.org/web/20060927044735/http://www.smpte.org/smpte_store/standards/pdf/s314m.pdf

possible_values.value: ‘ Aa, Ba, Ca, Ab, Bb, Cb, A, B, C, a, b, erri, erru ’

options.name: line_number
options.description: Choose which line number the generated VANC packets should go on. You generally want either line 9 (the default) or 11.

options.name: wrapping_type
options.description:
Choose the SMPTE 436M wrapping type, defaults to ‘ vanc_frame ’.
It accepts the values:

possible_values.description: VANC frame (interlaced or segmented progressive frame)

options.name: sample_coding
options.description:
Choose the SMPTE 436M sample coding, defaults to ‘ 8bit_luma ’.
It accepts the values:

possible_values.description: 8-bit component luma and color difference samples
10-bit component luma and color difference samples
8-bit component luma samples with parity error
8-bit component color difference samples with parity error
8-bit component luma and color difference samples with parity error

options.name: initial_cdp_sequence_cntr
options.description: The initial value of the CDP’s 16-bit unsigned integer cdp_hdr_sequence_cntr and cdp_ftr_sequence_cntr fields. Defaults to 0.

options.name: cdp_frame_rate
options.description: Set the CDP’s cdp_frame_rate field. This doesn’t actually change the timing of the data stream, it just changes the values inserted in that field in the generated CDP packets. Defaults to ‘ 30000/1001 ’.

options.name: remove
options.description:
When this option is enabled, the long-term headers are removed from the
bitstream after extraction.

options.name: pass_types
options.description:
List of unit types or ranges of unit types to pass through while removing
all others.  This is specified as a ’|’-separated list of unit type values
or ranges of values with ’-’.

options.name: remove_types
options.description:
Identical to pass_types , except the units in the given set
removed and all others passed through.

options.name: texture
options.description: Specifies the texture to keep.

options.name: aud
options.description:
Insert or remove AUD NAL units in all access units of the stream.
Default is pass.

options.name: sample_aspect_ratio
options.description:
Set the sample aspect ratio of the stream in the VUI parameters.
See H.264 table E-1.

options.name: overscan_appropriate_flag
options.description:
Set whether the stream is suitable for display using overscan
or not (see H.264 section E.2.1).

options.name: video_format
options.description:
Set the video format in the stream (see H.264 section E.2.1 and
table E-2).

options.name: colour_primaries
options.description:
Set the colour description in the stream (see H.264 section E.2.1
and tables E-3, E-4 and E-5).

options.name: chroma_sample_loc_type
options.description:
Set the chroma sample location in the stream (see H.264 section
E.2.1 and figure E-1).

options.name: tick_rate
options.description:
Set the tick rate (time_scale / num_units_in_tick) in the VUI
parameters.  This is the smallest time unit representable in the
stream, and in many cases represents the field rate of the stream
(double the frame rate).

options.name: fixed_frame_rate_flag
options.description:
Set whether the stream has fixed framerate - typically this indicates
that the framerate is exactly half the tick rate, but the exact
meaning is dependent on interlacing and the picture structure (see
H.264 section E.2.1 and table E-6).

options.name: zero_new_constraint_set_flags
options.description:
Zero constraint_set4_flag and constraint_set5_flag in the SPS. These
bits were reserved in a previous version of the H.264 spec, and thus
some hardware decoders require these to be zero. The result of zeroing
this is still a valid bitstream.

options.name: crop_left
options.description:
Set the frame cropping offsets in the SPS.  These values will replace
the current ones if the stream is already cropped.
These fields are set in pixels.  Note that some sizes may not be
representable if the chroma is subsampled or the stream is interlaced
(see H.264 section 7.4.2.1.1).

options.name: sei_user_data
options.description:
Insert a string as SEI unregistered user data.  The argument must
be of the form UUID+string , where the UUID is as hex digits
possibly separated by hyphens, and the string can be anything.
For example, ‘ 086f3693-b7b3-4f2c-9653-21492feee5b8+hello ’ will
insert the string “hello” associated with the given UUID.

options.name: delete_filler
options.description: Deletes both filler NAL units and filler SEI messages.

options.name: display_orientation
options.description:
Insert, extract or remove Display orientation SEI messages.
See H.264 section D.1.27 and D.2.27 for syntax and semantics.
Default is pass.
Insert mode works in conjunction with rotate and flip options.
Any pre-existing Display orientation messages will be removed in insert or remove mode.
Extract mode attaches the display matrix to the packet as side data.

options.name: rotate
options.description:
Set rotation in display orientation SEI (anticlockwise angle in degrees).
Range is -360 to +360. Default is NaN.

options.name: flip
options.description:
Set flip in display orientation SEI.
Default is unset.

options.name: level
options.description:
Set the level in the SPS.  Refer to H.264 section A.3 and tables A-1
to A-5.
The argument must be the name of a level (for example, ‘ 4.2 ’), a
level_idc value (for example, ‘ 42 ’), or the special name ‘ auto ’
indicating that the filter should attempt to guess the level from the
input stream properties.

options.name: aud
options.description: Insert or remove AUD NAL units in all access units of the stream.

options.name: sample_aspect_ratio
options.description: Set the sample aspect ratio in the stream in the VUI parameters.

options.name: video_format
options.description:
Set the video format in the stream (see H.265 section E.3.1 and
table E.2).

options.name: colour_primaries
options.description:
Set the colour description in the stream (see H.265 section E.3.1
and tables E.3, E.4 and E.5).

options.name: chroma_sample_loc_type
options.description:
Set the chroma sample location in the stream (see H.265 section
E.3.1 and figure E.1).

options.name: tick_rate
options.description:
Set the tick rate in the VPS and VUI parameters (time_scale /
num_units_in_tick). Combined with num_ticks_poc_diff_one , this can
set a constant framerate in the stream.  Note that it is likely to be
overridden by container parameters when the stream is in a container.

options.name: num_ticks_poc_diff_one
options.description:
Set poc_proportional_to_timing_flag in VPS and VUI and use this value
to set num_ticks_poc_diff_one_minus1 (see H.265 sections 7.4.3.1 and
E.3.1).  Ignored if tick_rate is not also set.

options.name: crop_left
options.description:
Set the conformance window cropping offsets in the SPS.  These values
will replace the current ones if the stream is already cropped.
These fields are set in pixels.  Note that some sizes may not be
representable if the chroma is subsampled (H.265 section 7.4.3.2.1).

options.name: width
options.description: Set width and height after crop.

options.name: level
options.description:
Set the level in the VPS and SPS.  See H.265 section A.4 and tables
A.6 and A.7.
The argument must be the name of a level (for example, ‘ 5.1 ’), a general_level_idc value (for example, ‘ 153 ’ for level 5.1),
or the special name ‘ auto ’ indicating that the filter should
attempt to guess the level from the input stream properties.

options.name: display_aspect_ratio
options.description:
Set the display aspect ratio in the stream.
The following fixed values are supported:
Any other value will result in square pixels being signalled instead
(see H.262 section 6.3.3 and table 6-3).

options.name: frame_rate
options.description:
Set the frame rate in the stream.  This is constructed from a table
of known values combined with a small multiplier and divisor - if
the supplied value is not exactly representable, the nearest
representable value will be used instead (see H.262 section 6.3.3
and table 6-4).

options.name: video_format
options.description:
Set the video format in the stream (see H.262 section 6.3.6 and
table 6-6).

options.name: colour_primaries
options.description:
Set the colour description in the stream (see H.262 section 6.3.6
and tables 6-7, 6-8 and 6-9).

options.name: amount
options.description:
Accepts an expression whose evaluation per-packet determines how often bytes in that
packet will be modified. A value below 0 will result in a variable frequency.
Default is 0 which results in no modification. However, if neither amount nor drop is specified,
amount will be set to -1 . See below for accepted variables.

options.name: drop
options.description:
Accepts an expression evaluated per-packet whose value determines whether that packet is dropped.
Evaluation to a positive value results in the packet being dropped. Evaluation to a negative
value results in a variable chance of it being dropped, roughly inverse in proportion to the magnitude
of the value. Default is 0 which results in no drops. See below for accepted variables.

options.name: dropamount
options.description:
Accepts a non-negative integer, which assigns a variable chance of it being dropped, roughly inverse
in proportion to the value. Default is 0 which results in no drops. This option is kept for backwards
compatibility and is equivalent to setting drop to a negative value with the same magnitude
i.e. dropamount=4 is the same as drop=-4 . Ignored if drop is also specified.

options.name: n
options.description: The index of the packet, starting from zero.

options.name: tb
options.description: The timebase for packet timestamps.

options.name: pts
options.description: Packet presentation timestamp.

options.name: dts
options.description: Packet decoding timestamp.

options.name: nopts
options.description: Constant representing AV_NOPTS_VALUE.

options.name: startpts
options.description: First non-AV_NOPTS_VALUE PTS seen in the stream.

options.name: startdts
options.description: First non-AV_NOPTS_VALUE DTS seen in the stream.

options.name: duration
options.description: Packet duration, in timebase units.

options.name: pos
options.description: Packet position in input; may be -1 when unknown or not set.

options.name: size
options.description: Packet size, in bytes.

options.name: key
options.description: Whether packet is marked as a keyframe.

options.name: state
options.description: A pseudo random integer, primarily derived from the content of packet payload.

options.name: nb_out_samples
options.description:
Set the number of samples per each output audio packet. The number is intended
as the number of samples per each channel . Default value is 1024.

options.name: pad
options.description:
If set to 1, the filter will pad the last audio packet with silence, so that it
will contain the same number of samples (or roughly the same number of samples,
see frame_rate ) as the previous ones. Default value is 1.

options.name: frame_rate
options.description:
This option makes the filter output a fixed number of packets per second instead
of a fixed number of samples per packet. If the audio sample rate is not
divisible by the frame rate then the number of samples will not be constant but
will vary slightly so that each packet will start as close to the frame
boundary as possible. Using this option has precedence over nb_out_samples .

options.name: color_primaries
options.description:
Set the color primaries.
Available values are:

possible_values.description: Keep the same color primaries property (default).

options.name: transfer_characteristics
options.description:
Set the color transfer.
Available values are:

possible_values.description: Keep the same transfer characteristics property (default).

options.name: matrix_coefficients
options.description:
Set the matrix coefficient.
Available values are:

options.name: freq
options.description: Set which frame types to remove extradata from.

options.name: ts
options.description: Set expressions for PTS, DTS or both.

options.name: duration
options.description: Set expression for duration.

options.name: time_base
options.description: Set output time base.

options.name: prescale
options.description: Set whether to convert time fields to user-set output time base before evaluation of expressions. Defaults to 0.

options.name: N
options.description: The count of the input packet. Starting from 0.

options.name: TS
options.description:
The demux timestamp in input in case of ts or dts option or presentation
timestamp in case of pts option.

options.name: POS
options.description:
The original position in the file of the packet, or undefined if undefined
for the current packet

options.name: DTS
options.description: The demux timestamp in input.

options.name: PTS
options.description: The presentation timestamp in input.

options.name: DURATION
options.description: The duration in input.

options.name: STARTDTS
options.description: The DTS of the first packet.

options.name: STARTPTS
options.description: The PTS of the first packet.

options.name: PREV_INDTS
options.description: The previous input DTS.

options.name: PREV_INPTS
options.description: The previous input PTS.

options.name: PREV_INDURATION
options.description: The previous input duration.

options.name: PREV_OUTDTS
options.description: The previous output DTS.

options.name: PREV_OUTPTS
options.description: The previous output PTS.

options.name: PREV_OUTDURATION
options.description: The previous output duration.

options.name: NEXT_DTS
options.description: The next input DTS.

options.name: NEXT_PTS
options.description: The next input PTS.

options.name: NEXT_DURATION
options.description: The next input duration.

options.name: TB
options.description: The timebase in which time fields are denominated. The user-set output timebase if prescale is enabled, else the input timebase.

options.name: TB_OUT
options.description: The output timebase.

options.name: SR
options.description: The sample rate of stream packet belongs.

options.name: NOPTS
options.description: The AV_NOPTS_VALUE constant.

options.name: color_space
options.description:
Set the color space value in the frame header.  Note that any frame
set to RGB will be implicitly set to PC range and that RGB is
incompatible with profiles 0 and 2.

options.name: color_range
options.description:
Set the color range value in the frame header.  Note that any value
imposed by the color space will take precedence over this value.

options.name: avioflags
options.description: Possible values:

options.name: probesize
options.description:
Set probing size in bytes, i.e. the size of the data to analyze to get
stream information. A higher value will enable detecting more
information in case it is dispersed into the stream, but will increase
latency. Must be an integer not lesser than 32. It is 5000000 by default.

options.name: max_probe_packets
options.description:
Set the maximum number of buffered packets when probing a codec.
Default is 2500 packets.

options.name: packetsize
options.description: Set packet size.

options.name: fflags
options.description:
Set format flags. Some are implemented for a limited number of formats.
Possible values for input files:
Possible values for output files:

possible_values.description: Enable fast, but inaccurate seeks for some formats.
Generate missing PTS if DTS is present.

Ignore DTS if PTS is also set. In case the PTS is set, the DTS value
is set to NOPTS. This is ignored when the nofillin flag is set.
Reduce the latency introduced by buffering during initial input streams analysis.
Do not fill in missing values in packet fields that can be exactly calculated.
Try to interleave output packets by DTS. At present, available only for AVIs with an index.

options.name: seek2any
options.description:
Allow seeking to non-keyframes on demuxer level when supported if set to 1.
Default is 0.

options.name: analyzeduration
options.description:
Specify how many microseconds are analyzed to probe the input. A
higher value will enable detecting more accurate information, but will
increase latency. It defaults to 5,000,000 microseconds = 5 seconds.

options.name: cryptokey hexadecimal
options.description: Set decryption key.

options.name: indexmem
options.description: Set max memory used for timestamp index (per stream).

options.name: rtbufsize
options.description: Set max memory used for buffering real-time frames.

options.name: fdebug
options.description:
Print specific debug info.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Print ID3v2 frame data when demuxing files containing ID3v2 tags.

options.name: max_delay
options.description: Set maximum muxing or demuxing delay in microseconds.

options.name: fpsprobesize
options.description: Set number of frames used to probe fps.

options.name: audio_preload
options.description: Set microseconds by which audio packets should be interleaved earlier.

options.name: chunk_duration
options.description: Set microseconds for each chunk.

options.name: chunk_size
options.description: Set size in bytes for each chunk.

options.name: err_detect
options.description:
Set error detection flags. f_err_detect is deprecated and
should be used only via the ffmpeg tool.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Abort decoding on minor error detection.

Consider things that violate the spec and have not been seen in the
wild as errors.
Consider all spec non compliancies as errors.
Consider things that a sane encoder should not do as an error.

options.name: max_interleave_delta
options.description:
Set maximum buffering duration for interleaving. The duration is
expressed in microseconds, and defaults to 10000000 (10 seconds).
To ensure all the streams are interleaved correctly, libavformat will
wait until it has at least one packet for each stream before actually
writing any packets to the output file. When some streams are
"sparse" (i.e. there are large gaps between successive packets), this
can result in excessive buffering.
This field specifies the maximum difference between the timestamps of the
first and the last packet in the muxing queue, above which libavformat
will output a packet regardless of whether it has queued a packet for all
the streams.
If set to 0, libavformat will continue buffering packets until it has
a packet for each stream, regardless of the maximum timestamp
difference between the buffered packets.

options.name: use_wallclock_as_timestamps
options.description: Use wallclock as timestamps if set to 1. Default is 0.

options.name: avoid_negative_ts
options.description:
Possible values:
When shifting is enabled, all output timestamps are shifted by the
same amount. Audio, video, and subtitles desynching and relative
timestamp differences are preserved compared to how they would have
been without shifting.

possible_values.description:
Shift timestamps to make them non-negative.
Also note that this affects only leading negative timestamps, and not
non-monotonic negative timestamps.
Shift timestamps so that the first timestamp is 0.
Enables shifting when required by the target format.

options.name: skip_initial_bytes
options.description:
Set number of bytes to skip before reading header and frames if set to 1.
Default is 0.

options.name: correct_ts_overflow
options.description: Correct single timestamp overflows if set to 1. Default is 1.

options.name: flush_packets
options.description:
Flush the underlying I/O stream after each packet. Default is -1 (auto), which
means that the underlying protocol will decide, 1 enables it, and has the
effect of reducing the latency, 0 disables it and may increase IO throughput in
some cases.

options.name: output_ts_offset offset
options.description:
Set the output time offset.
offset must be a time duration specification,
see (ffmpeg-utils)the Time duration section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual .
The offset is added by the muxer to the output timestamps.
Specifying a positive offset means that the corresponding streams are
delayed bt the time duration specified in offset . Default value
is 0 (meaning that no offset is applied).

options.name: format_whitelist
options.description: "," separated list of allowed demuxers. By default all are allowed.

options.name: dump_separator
options.description:
Separator used to separate the fields printed on the command line about the
Stream parameters.
For example, to separate the fields with newlines and indentation:

options.name: max_streams
options.description:
Specifies the maximum number of streams. This can be used to reject files that
would require too many resources due to a large number of streams.

options.name: skip_estimate_duration_from_pts bool
options.description:
Skip estimation of input duration if it requires an additional probing for PTS at end of file.
At present, applicable for MPEG-PS and MPEG-TS.

options.name: duration_probesize
options.description:
Set probing size, in bytes, for input duration estimation when it actually requires
an additional probing for PTS at end of file (at present: MPEG-PS and MPEG-TS).
It is aimed at users interested in better durations probing for itself, or indirectly
because using the concat demuxer, for example.
The typical use case is an MPEG-TS CBR with a high bitrate, high video buffering and
ending cleaning with similar PTS for video and audio: in such a scenario, the large
physical gap between the last video packet and the last audio packet makes it necessary
to read many bytes in order to get the video stream duration.
Another use case is where the default probing behaviour only reaches a single video frame which is
not the last one of the stream due to frame reordering, so the duration is not accurate.
Setting this option has a performance impact even for small files because the probing
size is fixed.
Default behaviour is a general purpose trade-off, largely adaptive, but the probing size
will not be extended to get streams durations at all costs.
Must be an integer not lesser than 1, or 0 for default behaviour.

options.name: strict
options.description:
Specify how strictly to follow the standards. f_strict is deprecated and
should be used only via the ffmpeg tool.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: strictly conform to an older more strict version of the spec or reference software
strictly conform to all the things in the spec no matter what consequences

allow non standardized experimental things, experimental
(unfinished/work in progress/not well tested) decoders and encoders.
Note: experimental decoders can pose a security risk, do not use this for
decoding untrusted input.

options.name: -ignore_loop bool
options.description: Ignore the loop variable in the file if set. Default is enabled.

options.name: -max_fps int
options.description: Maximum framerate in frames per second. Default of 0 imposes no limit.

options.name: -default_fps int
options.description:
Default framerate in frames per second when none is specified in the file
(0 meaning as fast as possible). Default is 15.

options.name: -no_resync_search bool
options.description: Do not try to resynchronize by looking for a certain optional start code.

options.name: file path
options.description:
Path to a file to read; special characters and spaces must be escaped with
backslash or single quotes.
All subsequent file-related directives apply to that file.

options.name: ffconcat version 1.0
options.description:
Identify the script type and version.
To make FFmpeg recognize the format automatically, this directive must
appear exactly as is (no extra space or byte-order-mark) on the very first
line of the script.

options.name: dur
options.description:
Duration of the file. This information can be specified from the file;
specifying it here may be more efficient or help if the information from the
file is not available or accurate.
If the duration is set for all files, then it is possible to seek in the
whole concatenated video.

options.name: inpoint timestamp
options.description:
In point of the file. When the demuxer opens the file it instantly seeks to the
specified timestamp. Seeking is done so that all streams can be presented
successfully at In point.
This directive works best with intra frame codecs, because for non-intra frame
ones you will usually get extra packets before the actual In point and the
decoded content will most likely contain frames before In point too.
For each file, packets before the file In point will have timestamps less than
the calculated start timestamp of the file (negative in case of the first
file), and the duration of the files (if not specified by the duration directive) will be reduced based on their specified In point.
Because of potential packets before the specified In point, packet timestamps
may overlap between two concatenated files.

options.name: outpoint timestamp
options.description:
Out point of the file. When the demuxer reaches the specified decoding
timestamp in any of the streams, it handles it as an end of file condition and
skips the current and all the remaining packets from all streams.
Out point is exclusive, which means that the demuxer will not output packets
with a decoding timestamp greater or equal to Out point.
This directive works best with intra frame codecs and formats where all streams
are tightly interleaved. For non-intra frame codecs you will usually get
additional packets with presentation timestamp after Out point therefore the
decoded content will most likely contain frames after Out point too. If your
streams are not tightly interleaved you may not get all the packets from all
streams before Out point and you may only will be able to decode the earliest
stream until Out point.
The duration of the files (if not specified by the duration directive) will be reduced based on their specified Out point.

options.name: file_packet_metadata key=value
options.description:
Metadata of the packets of the file. The specified metadata will be set for
each file packet. You can specify this directive multiple times to add multiple
metadata entries.
This directive is deprecated, use file_packet_meta instead.

options.name: file_packet_meta key value
options.description:
Metadata of the packets of the file. The specified metadata will be set for
each file packet. You can specify this directive multiple times to add multiple
metadata entries.

options.name: option key value
options.description:
Option to access, open and probe the file.
Can be present multiple times.

options.name: stream
options.description:
Introduce a stream in the virtual file.
All subsequent stream-related directives apply to the last introduced
stream.
Some streams properties must be set in order to allow identifying the
matching streams in the subfiles.
If no streams are defined in the script, the streams from the first file are
copied.

options.name: exact_stream_id id
options.description:
Set the id of the stream.
If this directive is given, the string with the corresponding id in the
subfiles will be used.
This is especially useful for MPEG-PS (VOB) files, where the order of the
streams is not reliable.

options.name: stream_meta key value
options.description:
Metadata for the stream.
Can be present multiple times.

options.name: stream_codec value
options.description: Codec for the stream.

options.name: stream_extradata hex_string
options.description: Extradata for the string, encoded in hexadecimal.

options.name: chapter id start end
options.description:
Add a chapter. id is an unique identifier, possibly small and
consecutive.

options.name: safe
options.description:
If set to 1, reject unsafe file paths and directives.
A file path is considered safe if it
does not contain a protocol specification and is relative and all components
only contain characters from the portable character set (letters, digits,
period, underscore and hyphen) and have no period at the beginning of a
component.
If set to 0, any file name is accepted.
The default is 1.

options.name: auto_convert
options.description:
If set to 1, try to perform automatic conversions on packet data to make the
streams concatenable.
The default is 1.
Currently, the only conversion is adding the h264_mp4toannexb bitstream
filter to H.264 streams in MP4 format. This is necessary in particular if
there are resolution changes.

options.name: segment_time_metadata
options.description:
If set to 1, every packet will contain the lavf.concat.start_time and the lavf.concat.duration packet metadata values which are the start_time and
the duration of the respective file segments in the concatenated output
expressed in microseconds. The duration metadata is only set if it is known
based on the concat file.
The default is 0.

options.name: cenc_decryption_key
options.description: Default 16-byte key, in hex, to decrypt files encrypted using ISO Common Encryption (CENC/AES-128 CTR; ISO/IEC 23001-7).

options.name: cenc_decryption_keys
options.description:
Dictionary of 16-byte key ID => 16-byte key, both in hex, to decrypt files encrypted using ISO Common Encryption
(CENC/AES-128 CTR; ISO/IEC 23001-7).

options.name: title int
options.description:
The title number to play. Must be set if pgc and pg are not set.
Not applicable to menus.
Default is 0 (auto), which currently only selects the first available title (title 1)
and notifies the user about the implications.
options.default: 0 (auto), which currently only selects the first available title (title 1)

options.name: chapter_start int
options.description:
The chapter, or PTT (part-of-title), number to start at. Not applicable to menus.
Default is 1.

options.name: chapter_end int
options.description:
The chapter, or PTT (part-of-title), number to end at. Not applicable to menus.
Default is 0, which is a special value to signal end at the last possible chapter.
options.default: 0, which is a special value to signal end at the last possible chapter

options.name: angle int
options.description:
The video angle number, referring to what is essentially an additional
video stream that is composed from alternate frames interleaved in the VOBs.
Not applicable to menus.
Default is 1.

options.name: region int
options.description:
The region code to use for playback. Some discs may use this to default playback
at a particular angle in different regions. This option will not affect the region code
of a real DVD drive, if used as an input. Not applicable to menus.
Default is 0, "world".

options.name: menu bool
options.description:
Demux menu assets instead of navigating a title. Requires exact coordinates
of the menu ( menu_lu , menu_vts , pgc , pg ).
Default is false.

options.name: menu_lu int
options.description:
The menu language to demux. In DVD, menus are grouped by language.
Default is 1, the first language unit.

options.name: menu_vts int
options.description:
The VTS where the menu lives, or 0 if it is a VMG menu (root-level).
Default is 1, menu of the first VTS.

options.name: pgc int
options.description:
The entry PGC to start playback, in conjunction with pg .
Alternative to setting title .
Chapter markers are not supported at this time.
Must be explicitly set for menus.
Default is 0, automatically resolve from value of title .
options.default: 0, automatically resolve from value of title

options.name: pg int
options.description:
The entry PG to start playback, in conjunction with pgc .
Alternative to setting title .
Chapter markers are not supported at this time.
Default is 1, the first PG of the PGC.
options.default: 1, the first PG of the PGC

options.name: preindex bool
options.description:
Enable this to have accurate chapter (PTT) markers and duration measurement,
which requires a slow second pass read in order to index the chapter marker
timestamps from NAV packets. This is non-ideal extra work for real optical drives.
It is recommended and faster to use this option with a backup of the DVD structure
stored on a hard drive. Not compatible with pgc and pg .
Default is 0, false.

options.name: trim bool
options.description:
Skip padding cells (i.e. cells shorter than 1 second) from the beginning.
There exist many discs with filler segments at the beginning of the PGC,
often with junk data intended for controlling a real DVD player’s
buffering speed and with no other material data value.
Not applicable to menus.
Default is 1, true.

options.name: merge_alpha bool
options.description:
Normally the VP6 alpha channel (if exists) is returned as a secondary video
stream, by setting this option you can make the demuxer return a single video
stream which contains the alpha channel in addition to the ordinary video.

options.name: -flv_metadata bool
options.description: Allocate the streams according to the onMetaData array content.

options.name: -flv_ignore_prevtag bool
options.description: Ignore the size of previous tag value.

options.name: -flv_full_metadata bool
options.description: Output all context of the onMetadata.

options.name: min_delay
options.description:
Set the minimum valid delay between frames in hundredths of seconds.
Range is 0 to 6000. Default value is 2.

options.name: max_gif_delay
options.description:
Set the maximum valid delay between frames in hundredth of seconds.
Range is 0 to 65535. Default value is 65535 (nearly eleven minutes),
the maximum value allowed by the specification.

options.name: default_delay
options.description:
Set the default delay between frames in hundredths of seconds.
Range is 0 to 6000. Default value is 10.

options.name: ignore_loop
options.description:
GIF files can contain information to loop a certain number of times (or
infinitely). If ignore_loop is set to 1, then the loop setting
from the input will be ignored and looping will not occur. If set to 0,
then looping will occur and will cycle the number of times according to
the GIF. Default value is 1.

options.name: live_start_index
options.description: segment index to start live streams at (negative values are from the end).

options.name: prefer_x_start
options.description: prefer to use #EXT-X-START if it’s in playlist instead of live_start_index.

options.name: allowed_extensions
options.description: ’,’ separated list of file extensions that hls is allowed to access.

options.name: extension_picky
options.description:
This blocks disallowed extensions from probing
It also requires all available segments to have matching extensions to the format
except mpegts, which is always allowed.
It is recommended to set the whitelists correctly instead of depending on extensions
Enabled by default.

options.name: max_reload
options.description:
Maximum number of times a insufficient list is attempted to be reloaded.
Default value is 1000.

options.name: m3u8_hold_counters
options.description:
The maximum number of times to load m3u8 when it refreshes without new segments.
Default value is 1000.

options.name: http_persistent
options.description:
Use persistent HTTP connections. Applicable only for HTTP streams.
Enabled by default.

options.name: http_multiple
options.description:
Use multiple HTTP connections for downloading HTTP segments.
Enabled by default for HTTP/1.1 servers.

options.name: http_seekable
options.description:
Use HTTP partial requests for downloading HTTP segments.
0 = disable, 1 = enable, -1 = auto, Default is auto.

options.name: seg_format_options
options.description: Set options for the demuxer of media segments using a list of key=value pairs separated by : .

options.name: seg_max_retry
options.description:
Maximum number of times to reload a segment on error, useful when segment skip on network error is not desired.
Default value is 0.

options.name: framerate
options.description: Set the frame rate for the video stream. It defaults to 25.

options.name: loop
options.description: If set to 1, loop over the input. Default value is 0.

options.name: pattern_type
options.description:
Select the pattern type used to interpret the provided filename.
pattern_type accepts one of the following values.
Default value is sequence .

possible_values.description:
Disable pattern matching, therefore the video will only contain the specified
image. You should use this option if you do not want to create sequences from
multiple images and your filenames may contain special pattern characters.

Select a sequence pattern type, used to specify a sequence of files
indexed by sequential numbers.
A sequence pattern may contain the string "%d" or "%0 N d", which
specifies the position of the characters representing a sequential
number in each filename matched by the pattern. If the form
"%d0 N d" is used, the string representing the number in each
filename is 0-padded and N is the total number of 0-padded
digits representing the number. The literal character ’%’ can be
specified in the pattern with the string "%%".
If the sequence pattern contains "%d" or "%0 N d", the first filename of
the file list specified by the pattern must contain a number
inclusively contained between start_number and start_number + start_number_range -1, and all the following
numbers must be sequential.
For example the pattern "img-%03d.bmp" will match a sequence of
filenames of the form img-001.bmp , img-002.bmp , ..., img-010.bmp , etc.; the pattern "i%%m%%g-%d.jpg" will match a
sequence of filenames of the form i%m%g-1.jpg , i%m%g-2.jpg , ..., i%m%g-10.jpg , etc.
Note that the pattern must not necessarily contain "%d" or
"%0 N d", for example to convert a single image file img.jpeg you can employ the command:

Select a glob wildcard pattern type.
The pattern is interpreted like a glob() pattern. This is only
selectable if libavformat was compiled with globbing support.

options.name: pixel_format
options.description:
Set the pixel format of the images to read. If not specified the pixel
format is guessed from the first image file in the sequence.

options.name: start_number
options.description:
Set the index of the file matched by the image file pattern to start
to read from. Default value is 0.

options.name: start_number_range
options.description:
Set the index interval range to check when looking for the first image
file in the sequence, starting from start_number . Default value
is 5.

options.name: ts_from_file
options.description:
If set to 1, will set frame timestamp to modification time of image file. Note
that monotonity of timestamps is not provided: images go in the same order as
without this option. Default value is 0.
If set to 2, will set frame timestamp to the modification time of the image file in
nanosecond precision.

options.name: video_size
options.description:
Set the video size of the images to read. If not specified the video
size is guessed from the first image file in the sequence.

options.name: export_path_metadata
options.description:
If set to 1, will add two extra fields to the metadata found in input, making them
also available for other filters (see drawtext filter for examples). Default
value is 0. The extra fields are described below:

possible_values.description: Corresponds to the full path to the input file being read.
Corresponds to the name of the file being read.

options.name: track_index
options.description:
Set the index of which track to demux. The demuxer can only export one track.
Track indexes start at 0. Default is to pick the first track. Number of tracks
is exported as tracks metadata entry.

options.name: sample_rate
options.description: Set the sampling rate of the exported track. Range is 1000 to 999999. Default is 44100.

options.name: max_size
options.description:
The demuxer buffers the entire file into memory. Adjust this value to set the maximum buffer size,
which in turn, acts as a ceiling for the size of files that can be read.
Default is 50 MiB.

options.name: noise_reduction
options.description: Apply a simple low-pass filter. Can be 1 (on) or 0 (off). Default is 0.

options.name: reverb_depth
options.description: Set amount of reverb. Range 0-100. Default is 0.

options.name: reverb_delay
options.description: Set delay in ms, clamped to 40-250 ms. Default is 0.

options.name: bass_amount
options.description: Apply bass expansion a.k.a. XBass or megabass. Range is 0 (quiet) to 100 (loud). Default is 0.

options.name: bass_range
options.description: Set cutoff i.e. upper-bound for bass frequencies. Range is 10-100 Hz. Default is 0.

options.name: surround_depth
options.description: Apply a Dolby Pro-Logic surround effect. Range is 0 (quiet) to 100 (heavy). Default is 0.

options.name: surround_delay
options.description: Set surround delay in ms, clamped to 5-40 ms. Default is 0.

options.name: max_size
options.description:
The demuxer buffers the entire file into memory. Adjust this value to set the maximum buffer size,
which in turn, acts as a ceiling for the size of files that can be read. Range is 0 to 100 MiB.
0 removes buffer size limit (not recommended). Default is 5 MiB.

options.name: video_stream_expr
options.description:
String which is evaluated using the eval API to assign colors to the generated video stream.
Variables which can be used are x , y , w , h , t , speed , tempo , order , pattern and row .

options.name: video_stream
options.description: Generate video stream. Can be 1 (on) or 0 (off). Default is 0.

options.name: video_stream_w
options.description: Set video frame width in ’chars’ where one char indicates 8 pixels. Range is 20-512. Default is 30.

options.name: video_stream_h
options.description: Set video frame height in ’chars’ where one char indicates 8 pixels. Range is 20-512. Default is 30.

options.name: video_stream_ptxt
options.description: Print metadata on video stream. Includes speed , tempo , order , pattern , row and ts (time in ms). Can be 1 (on) or 0 (off). Default is 1.

options.name: subsong
options.description:
Set the subsong index. This can be either  ’all’, ’auto’, or the index of the
subsong. Subsong indexes start at 0. The default is ’auto’.
The default value is to let libopenmpt choose.

options.name: layout
options.description:
Set the channel layout. Valid values are 1, 2, and 4 channel layouts.
The default value is STEREO.

options.name: sample_rate
options.description:
Set the sample rate for libopenmpt to output.
Range is from 1000 to INT_MAX. The value default is 48000.

options.name: enable_drefs
options.description:
Enable loading of external tracks, disabled by default.
Enabling this can theoretically leak information in some use cases.

options.name: use_absolute_path
options.description:
Allows loading of external tracks via absolute paths, disabled by default.
Enabling this poses a security risk. It should only be enabled if the source
is known to be non-malicious.

options.name: seek_streams_individually
options.description:
When seeking, identify the closest point in each stream individually and demux packets in
that stream from identified point. This can lead to a different sequence of packets compared
to demuxing linearly from the beginning. Default is true.

options.name: ignore_editlist
options.description:
Ignore any edit list atoms. The demuxer, by default, modifies the stream index to reflect the
timeline described by the edit list. Default is false.

options.name: advanced_editlist
options.description:
Modify the stream index to reflect the timeline described by the edit list. ignore_editlist must be set to false for this option to be effective.
If both ignore_editlist and this option are set to false, then only the
start of the stream index is modified to reflect initial dwell time or starting timestamp
described by the edit list. Default is true.

options.name: ignore_chapters
options.description:
Don’t parse chapters. This includes GoPro ’HiLight’ tags/moments. Note that chapters are
only parsed when input is seekable. Default is false.

options.name: use_mfra_for
options.description:
For seekable fragmented input, set fragment’s starting timestamp from media fragment random access box, if present.
Following options are available:

possible_values.description: Auto-detect whether to set mfra timestamps as PTS or DTS (default)
Don’t use mfra box to set timestamps

options.name: use_tfdt
options.description:
For fragmented input, set fragment’s starting timestamp to baseMediaDecodeTime from the tfdt box.
Default is enabled, which will prefer to use the tfdt box to set DTS. Disable to use the earliest_presentation_time from the sidx box.
In either case, the timestamp from the mfra box will be used if it’s available and use_mfra_for is
set to pts or dts.
options.default: enabled, which will prefer to use the tfdt box to set DTS

options.name: export_all
options.description:
Export unrecognized boxes within the udta box as metadata entries. The first four
characters of the box type are set as the key. Default is false.

options.name: export_xmp
options.description:
Export entire contents of XMP_ box and uuid box as a string with key xmp . Note that
if export_all is set and this option isn’t, the contents of XMP_ box are still exported
but with key XMP_ . Default is false.

options.name: activation_bytes
options.description: 4-byte key required to decrypt Audible AAX and AAX+ files. See Audible AAX subsection below.

options.name: audible_fixed_key
options.description:
Fixed key used for handling Audible AAX/AAX+ files. It has been pre-set so should not be necessary to
specify.

options.name: decryption_key
options.description: Default 16-byte key, in hex, to decrypt files encrypted using ISO Common Encryption (CENC/AES-128 CTR; ISO/IEC 23001-7).

options.name: decryption_keys
options.description:
Dictionary of 16-byte key ID => 16-byte key, both in hex, to decrypt files encrypted using ISO Common Encryption
(CENC/AES-128 CTR; ISO/IEC 23001-7).

options.name: max_stts_delta
options.description:
Very high sample deltas written in a trak’s stts box may occasionally be intended but usually they are written in
error or used to store a negative value for dts correction when treated as signed 32-bit integers. This option lets
the user set an upper limit, beyond which the delta is clamped to 1. Values greater than the limit if negative when
cast to int32 are used to adjust onward dts.
Unit is the track time scale. Range is 0 to UINT_MAX. Default is UINT_MAX - 48000*10 which allows up to
a 10 second dts correction for 48 kHz audio streams while accommodating 99.9% of uint32 range.

options.name: interleaved_read
options.description:
Interleave packets from multiple tracks at demuxer level. For badly interleaved files, this prevents playback issues
caused by large gaps between packets in different tracks, as MOV/MP4 do not have packet placement requirements.
However, this can cause excessive seeking on very badly interleaved files, due to seeking between tracks, so disabling
it may prevent I/O issues, at the expense of playback.

options.name: resync_size
options.description:
Set size limit for looking up a new synchronization. Default value is
65536.

options.name: skip_unknown_pmt
options.description: Skip PMTs for programs not defined in the PAT. Default value is 0.

options.name: fix_teletext_pts
options.description:
Override teletext packet PTS and DTS values with the timestamps calculated
from the PCR of the first program which the teletext stream is part of and is
not discarded. Default value is 1, set this option to 0 if you want your
teletext packet PTS and DTS values untouched.
options.default: 1, set this option to 0 if you want your

options.name: ts_packetsize
options.description:
Output option carrying the raw packet size in bytes.
Show the detected raw packet size, cannot be set by the user.

options.name: scan_all_pmts
options.description:
Scan and combine all PMTs. The value is an integer with value from -1
to 1 (-1 means automatic setting, 1 means enabled, 0 means
disabled). Default value is -1.

options.name: merge_pmt_versions
options.description:
Reuse existing streams when a PMT’s version is updated and elementary
streams move to different PIDs. Default value is 0.

options.name: max_packet_size
options.description:
Set maximum size, in bytes, of packet emitted by the demuxer. Payloads above this size
are split across multiple packets. Range is 1 to INT_MAX/2. Default is 204800 bytes.

options.name: strict_mime_boundary
options.description:
Default implementation applies a relaxed standard to multi-part MIME boundary detection,
to prevent regression with numerous existing endpoints not generating a proper MIME
MJPEG stream. Turning this option on by setting it to 1 will result in a stricter check
of the boundary value.

options.name: framerate
options.description: Set input video frame rate. Default value is 25.

options.name: pixel_format
options.description: Set the input video pixel format. Default value is yuv420p .

options.name: stride
options.description:
Set frame line size in bytes, only required if there are extra padding.
For multiplane formats, stride is a list of line size for each
plane.

options.name: video_size
options.description: Set the input video size. This value must be specified explicitly.

options.name: start_time
options.description:
Set the start time of the TED talk, in milliseconds. The default is 15000
(15s). It is used to sync the captions with the downloadable videos, because
they include a 15s intro.

options.name: max_script_size
options.description:
The demuxer buffers the entire script into memory. Adjust this value to set the maximum buffer size,
which in turn, acts as a ceiling for the size of scripts that can be read.
Default is 1 MiB.

options.name: max_size
options.description: See the same option for the wav demuxer.

options.name: max_size
options.description:
Specify the maximum packet size in bytes for the demuxed packets. By default
this is set to 0, which means that a sensible value is chosen based on the
input format.

options.name: -min_delay int
options.description:
Set the minimum valid delay between frames in milliseconds.
Range is 0 to 60000. Default value is 10.

options.name: -max_webp_delay int
options.description:
Set the maximum valid delay between frames in milliseconds.
Range is 0 to 16777215. Default value is 16777215 (over four hours),
the maximum value allowed by the specification.

options.name: -default_delay int
options.description:
Set the default delay between frames in milliseconds.
Range is 0 to 60000. Default value is 100.

options.name: -ignore_loop bool
options.description:
WebP files can contain information to loop a certain number of times
(or infinitely). If ignore_loop is set to true, then the loop
setting from the input will be ignored and looping will not occur.
If set to false, then looping will occur and will cycle the number
of times according to the WebP. Default value is true.

options.name: usebgcolor bool
options.description:
WebP files contain a background color hint in the ANIM chunk, but the
WebP specification says that viewer applications are not required to
use it. If usebgcolor is set to true, then the background
color hint will be used, otherwise transparent black will be used for
the background. Default value is false.

options.name: ac3 audio
options.description: Dolby Digital, also known as AC-3.

options.name: adx audio
options.description:
CRI Middleware ADX audio.
This muxer will write out the total sample count near the start of the
first packet when the output is seekable and the count can be stored
in 32 bits.

options.name: aptx audio
options.description: aptX (Audio Processing Technology for Bluetooth)

options.name: aptx_hd audio
options.description: aptX HD (Audio Processing Technology for Bluetooth) audio

options.name: avs2 video
options.description:
AVS2-P2 (Audio Video Standard - Second generation - Part 2) /
IEEE 1857.4 video

options.name: avs3 video
options.description:
AVS3-P2 (Audio Video Standard - Third generation - Part 2) /
IEEE 1857.10 video

options.name: cavsvideo video
options.description: Chinese AVS (Audio Video Standard - First generation)

options.name: codec2raw audio
options.description:
Codec 2 audio.
No extension is registered so format name has to be supplied e.g. with
the ffmpeg CLI tool -f codec2raw .

options.name: data any
options.description:
Generic data muxer.
This muxer accepts a single stream with any codec of any type. The
input stream has to be selected using the -map option with the ffmpeg CLI tool.
No extension is registered so format name has to be supplied e.g. with
the ffmpeg CLI tool -f data .

options.name: dfpwm audio
options.description: Raw DFPWM1a (Dynamic Filter Pulse With Modulation) audio muxer.

options.name: dirac video
options.description:
BBC Dirac video.
The Dirac Pro codec is a subset and is standardized as SMPTE VC-2.

options.name: dnxhd video
options.description:
Avid DNxHD video.
It is standardized as SMPTE VC-3. Accepts DNxHR streams.

options.name: dts audio
options.description: DTS Coherent Acoustics (DCA) audio

options.name: eac3 audio
options.description: Dolby Digital Plus, also known as Enhanced AC-3

options.name: evc video
options.description: MPEG-5 Essential Video Coding (EVC) / EVC / MPEG-5 Part 1 EVC video

options.name: g722 audio
options.description: ITU-T G.722 audio

options.name: g723_1 audio
options.description: ITU-T G.723.1 audio

options.name: g726 audio
options.description:
ITU-T G.726 big-endian ("left-justified") audio.
No extension is registered so format name has to be supplied e.g. with
the ffmpeg CLI tool -f g726 .

options.name: g726le audio
options.description:
ITU-T G.726 little-endian ("right-justified") audio.
No extension is registered so format name has to be supplied e.g. with
the ffmpeg CLI tool -f g726le .

options.name: gsm audio
options.description: Global System for Mobile Communications audio

options.name: h261 video
options.description: ITU-T H.261 video

options.name: h264 video
options.description:
ITU-T H.264 / MPEG-4 Part 10 AVC video. Bitstream shall be converted
to Annex B syntax if it’s in length-prefixed mode.

options.name: hevc video
options.description:
ITU-T H.265 / MPEG-H Part 2 HEVC video. Bitstream shall be converted
to Annex B syntax if it’s in length-prefixed mode.

options.name: m4v video
options.description: MPEG-4 Part 2 video

options.name: mjpeg video
options.description: Motion JPEG video

options.name: mlp audio
options.description: Meridian Lossless Packing, also known as Packed PCM

options.name: mp2 audio
options.description: MPEG-1 Audio Layer II audio

options.name: mpeg1video video
options.description: MPEG-1 Part 2 video.

options.name: mpeg2video video
options.description: ITU-T H.262 / MPEG-2 Part 2 video

options.name: obu video
options.description:
AV1 low overhead Open Bitstream Units muxer.
Temporal delimiter OBUs will be inserted in all temporal units of the
stream.

options.name: rawvideo video
options.description: Raw uncompressed video.

options.name: sbc audio
options.description: Bluetooth SIG low-complexity subband codec audio

options.name: truehd audio
options.description: Dolby TrueHD audio

options.name: vc1 video
options.description: SMPTE 421M / VC-1 video

options.name: alaw
options.description: PCM A-law

options.name: f32be
options.description: PCM 32-bit floating-point big-endian

options.name: f32le
options.description: PCM 32-bit floating-point little-endian

options.name: f64be
options.description: PCM 64-bit floating-point big-endian

options.name: f64le
options.description: PCM 64-bit floating-point little-endian

options.name: mulaw
options.description: PCM mu-law

options.name: s16be
options.description: PCM signed 16-bit big-endian

options.name: s16le
options.description: PCM signed 16-bit little-endian

options.name: s24be
options.description: PCM signed 24-bit big-endian

options.name: s24le
options.description: PCM signed 24-bit little-endian

options.name: s32be
options.description: PCM signed 32-bit big-endian

options.name: s32le
options.description: PCM signed 32-bit little-endian

options.name: s8
options.description: PCM signed 8-bit

options.name: u16be
options.description: PCM unsigned 16-bit big-endian

options.name: u16le
options.description: PCM unsigned 16-bit little-endian

options.name: u24be
options.description: PCM unsigned 24-bit big-endian

options.name: u24le
options.description: PCM unsigned 24-bit little-endian

options.name: u32be
options.description: PCM unsigned 32-bit big-endian

options.name: u32le
options.description: PCM unsigned 32-bit little-endian

options.name: u8
options.description: PCM unsigned 8-bit

options.name: vidc
options.description: PCM Archimedes VIDC

options.name: mpeg
options.description: MPEG-1 Systems / MPEG-1 program stream muxer.

options.name: vcd
options.description:
MPEG-1 Systems / MPEG-1 program stream (VCD) muxer.
This muxer can be used to generate tracks in the format accepted by
the VCD (Video Compact Disc) storage devices.
It is the same as the ‘ mpeg ’ muxer with a few differences.

options.name: vob
options.description: MPEG-2 program stream (VOB) muxer.

options.name: dvd
options.description:
MPEG-2 program stream (DVD VOB) muxer.
This muxer can be used to generate tracks in the format accepted by
the DVD (Digital Versatile Disc) storage devices.
This is the same as the ‘ vob ’ muxer with a few differences.

options.name: svcd
options.description:
MPEG-2 program stream (SVCD VOB) muxer.
This muxer can be used to generate tracks in the format accepted by
the SVCD (Super Video Compact Disc) storage devices.
This is the same as the ‘ vob ’ muxer with a few differences.

options.name: muxrate rate
options.description:
Set user-defined mux rate expressed as a number of bits/s. If not
specified the automatically computed mux rate is employed. Default value
is 0 .

options.name: preload delay
options.description: Set initial demux-decode delay in microseconds. Default value is 500000 .

options.name: 3gp
options.description:
Third Generation Partnership Project (3GPP) format for 3G UMTS
multimedia services

options.name: 3g2
options.description:
Third Generation Partnership Project 2 (3GP2 or 3GPP2) format for 3G
CDMA2000 multimedia services, similar to ‘ 3gp ’ with extensions
and limitations

options.name: f4v
options.description: Adobe Flash Video format

options.name: ipod
options.description:
MPEG-4 audio file format, as MOV/MP4 but limited to contain only audio
streams, typically played with the Apple ipod device

options.name: ismv
options.description:
Microsoft IIS (Internet Information Services) Smooth Streaming
Audio/Video (ISMV or ISMA) format. This is based on MPEG-4 Part 14
format with a few incompatible variants, used to stream media files
for the Microsoft IIS server.

options.name: mov
options.description: QuickTime player format identified by the .mov extension

options.name: mp4
options.description: MP4 or MPEG-4 Part 14 format

options.name: psp
options.description:
PlayStation Portable MP4/MPEG-4 Part 14 format variant. This is based
on MPEG-4 Part 14 format with a few incompatible variants, used to
play files on PlayStation devices.

options.name: brand brand_string
options.description: Override major brand.

options.name: empty_hdlr_name bool
options.description:
Enable to skip writing the name inside a hdlr box.
Default is false .

options.name: encryption_key key
options.description: set the media encryption key in hexadecimal format

options.name: encryption_kid kid
options.description: set the media encryption key identifier in hexadecimal format

options.name: encryption_scheme scheme
options.description:
configure the encryption scheme, allowed values are ‘ none ’, and
‘ cenc-aes-ctr ’

options.name: frag_duration
options.description: Create fragments that are duration microseconds long.

options.name: frag_interleave number
options.description:
Interleave samples within fragments (max number of consecutive
samples, lower is tighter interleaving, but with more overhead. It is
set to 0 by default.

options.name: frag_size size
options.description: create fragments that contain up to size bytes of payload data

options.name: iods_audio_profile profile
options.description:
specify iods number for the audio profile atom (from -1 to 255),
default is -1

options.name: iods_video_profile profile
options.description:
specify iods number for the video profile atom (from -1 to 255),
default is -1

options.name: ism_lookahead num_entries
options.description:
specify number of lookahead entries for ISM files (from 0 to 255),
default is 0

options.name: min_frag_duration
options.description: do not create fragments that are shorter than duration microseconds long

options.name: moov_size bytes
options.description:
Reserves space for the moov atom at the beginning of the file instead of placing the
moov atom at the end. If the space reserved is insufficient, muxing will fail.

options.name: mov_gamma gamma
options.description:
specify gamma value for gama atom (as a decimal number from 0 to 10),
default is 0.0 , must be set together with + movflags

options.name: movflags
options.description: Set various muxing switches. The following flags can be used:

possible_values.description:
write CMAF (Common Media Application Format) compatible fragmented
MP4 output

write DASH (Dynamic Adaptive Streaming over HTTP) compatible fragmented
MP4 output

Similarly to the ‘ omit_tfhd_offset ’ flag, this flag avoids
writing the absolute base_data_offset field in tfhd atoms, but does so
by using the new default-base-is-moof flag instead. This flag is new
from 14496-12:2012. This may make the fragments easier to parse in
certain circumstances (avoiding basing track fragment location
calculations on the implicit end of the previous track fragment).

delay writing the initial moov until the first fragment is cut, or
until the first fragment flush

Disable Nero chapter markers (chpl atom). Normally, both Nero chapters
and a QuickTime chapter track are written to the file. With this
option set, only the QuickTime chapter track will be written. Nero
chapters can cause failures when the file is reprocessed with certain
tagging programs, like mp3Tag 2.61a and iTunes 11.3, most likely other
versions are affected as well.

Run a second pass moving the index (moov atom) to the beginning of the
file. This operation can take a while, and will not work in various
situations such as fragmented output, thus it is not enabled by
default.

Allow the caller to manually choose when to cut fragments, by calling av_write_frame(ctx, NULL) to write a fragment with the packets
written so far. (This is only useful with other applications
integrating libavformat, not from ffmpeg .)
signal that the next fragment is discontinuous from earlier ones
start a new fragment at each video keyframe
write a global sidx index at the start of the file
create a live smooth streaming feed (for pushing to a publishing point)

Enables utilization of version 1 of the CTTS box, in which the CTS offsets can
be negative. This enables the initial sample to have DTS/CTS of zero, and
reduces the need for edit lists for some cases such as video tracks with
B-frames. Additionally, eases conformance with the DASH-IF interoperability
guidelines.
This option is implicitly set when writing ‘ ismv ’ (Smooth
Streaming) files.

Do not write any absolute base_data_offset in tfhd atoms. This avoids
tying fragments to absolute byte positions in the file/streams.

If writing colr atom prioritise usage of ICC profile if it exists in
stream packet side data.
add RTP hinting tracks to the output file

Write a separate moof (movie fragment) atom for each track. Normally,
packets for all tracks are written in a moof atom (which is slightly
more efficient), but with this option set, the muxer writes one
moof/mdat pair for each track, making it easier to separate tracks.

Skip writing of sidx atom. When bitrate overhead due to sidx atom is
high, this option could be used for cases where sidx atom is not
mandatory. When the ‘ global_sidx ’ flag is enabled, this option
is ignored.
skip writing the mfra/tfra/mfro trailer for fragmented files

write colr atom even if the color info is unspecified. This flag is
experimental, may be renamed or changed, do not use from scripts.

For recoverability - write the output file as a fragmented file.
This allows the intermediate file to be read while being written
(in particular, if the writing process is aborted uncleanly). When
writing is finished, the file is converted to a regular, non-fragmented
file, which is more compatible and allows easier and quicker seeking.
If writing is aborted, the intermediate file can manually be
remuxed to get a regular, non-fragmented file of what had been
written into the unfinished file.

options.name: movie_timescale scale
options.description:
Set the timescale written in the movie header box ( mvhd ).
Range is 1 to INT_MAX. Default is 1000 .

options.name: rtpflags
options.description:
Add RTP hinting tracks to the output file.
The following flags can be used:

possible_values.description: use mode 0 for H.264 in RTP
use MP4A-LATM packetization instead of MPEG4-GENERIC for AAC
use RFC 2190 packetization instead of RFC 4629 for H.263
send RTCP BYE packets when finishing
do not send RTCP sender reports

options.name: skip_iods bool
options.description: skip writing iods atom (default value is true )

options.name: use_editlist bool
options.description: use edit list (default value is auto )

options.name: use_stream_ids_as_track_ids bool
options.description: use stream ids as track ids (default value is false )

options.name: video_track_timescale scale
options.description:
Set the timescale used for video tracks. Range is 0 to INT_MAX. If
set to 0 , the timescale is automatically set based on the
native stream time base. Default is 0 .

options.name: write_btrt bool
options.description:
Force or disable writing bitrate box inside stsd box of a track. The
box contains decoding buffer size (in bytes), maximum bitrate and
average bitrate for the track. The box will be skipped if none of
these values can be computed.  Default is -1 or auto ,
which will write the box only in MP4 mode.

options.name: write_prft option
options.description:
Write producer time reference box (PRFT) with a specified time source for the
NTP field in the PRFT box. Set value as ‘ wallclock ’ to specify timesource
as wallclock time and ‘ pts ’ to specify timesource as input packets’ PTS
values.

options.name: write_tmcd bool
options.description:
Specify on to force writing a timecode track, off to disable it
and auto to write a timecode track only for mov and mp4 output (default).
Setting value to ‘ pts ’ is applicable only for a live encoding use case,
where PTS values are set as as wallclock time at the source. For example, an
encoding use case with decklink capture source where video_pts and audio_pts are set to ‘ abs_wallclock ’.

options.name: write_crc bool
options.description:
when enabled, write a CRC checksum for each packet to the output,
default is false

options.name: write_id3v2 bool
options.description:
Enable to write ID3v2.4 tags at the start of the stream. Default is
disabled.

options.name: write_apetag bool
options.description:
Enable to write APE tags at the end of the stream. Default is
disabled.

options.name: write_mpeg2 bool
options.description:
Enable to set MPEG version bit in the ADTS frame header to 1 which
indicates MPEG-2. Default is 0, which indicates MPEG-4.

options.name: write_id3v2 bool
options.description: Enable ID3v2 tags writing when set to 1. Default is 0 (disabled).

options.name: id3v2_version bool
options.description:
Select ID3v2 version to write. Currently only version 3 and 4 (aka.
ID3v2.3 and ID3v2.4) are supported. The default is version 4.

options.name: type type
options.description:
Set file type.
type accepts the following values:

possible_values.description: Set file type as music. Must have a sample rate of 22050 Hz.

Set file type as per output file extension. .pcm results in
type pcm else type tun is set. (default)

options.name: final_delay delay
options.description:
Force a delay expressed in seconds after the last frame of each
repetition. Default value is 0.0 .

options.name: plays repetitions
options.description:
specify how many times to play the content, 0 causes an infinite
loop, with 1 there is no loop

options.name: version_major version
options.description: override file major version, specified as an integer, default value is 2

options.name: version_minor version
options.description: override file minor version, specified as an integer, default value is 1

options.name: name name
options.description:
Embed file name into file, if not specified use the output file
name. The name is truncated to 8 characters.

options.name: skip_rate_check bool
options.description: skip sample rate check (default is false )

options.name: loop bool
options.description: set loop flag (default is false )

options.name: reverb
options.description: set reverb flag (default is true )

options.name: packet_size size
options.description:
Set the muxer packet size as a number of bytes. By tuning this setting
you may reduce data fragmentation or muxer overhead depending on your
source. Default value is 3200 , minimum is 100 , maximum
is 64Ki .

options.name: ignore_readorder bool
options.description:
Write dialogue events immediately, even if they are out-of-order,
default is false , otherwise they are cached until the expected
time event is found.
options.default: false , otherwise they are cached until the expected

options.name: loopstart start
options.description:
Specify loop start position expressesd in milliseconds, from -1 to INT_MAX , in case -1 is set then no loop is specified
(default -1) and the loopend value is ignored.

options.name: loopend end
options.description:
Specify loop end position expressed in milliseconds, from 0 to INT_MAX , default is 0 , in case 0 is set it
assumes the total stream duration.

options.name: flipped_raw_rgb bool
options.description:
If set to true , store positive height for raw RGB bitmaps, which
indicates bitmap is stored bottom-up. Note that this option does not flip the
bitmap which has to be done manually beforehand, e.g. by using the ‘ vflip ’
filter. Default is false and indicates bitmap is stored top down.
options.default: false and indicates bitmap is stored top down

options.name: reserve_index_space size
options.description:
Reserve the specified amount of bytes for the OpenDML master index of each
stream within the file header. By default additional master indexes are
embedded within the data packets if there is no space left in the first master
index and are linked together as a chain of indexes. This index structure can
cause problems for some use cases, e.g. third-party software strictly relying
on the OpenDML index specification or when file seeking is slow. Reserving
enough index space in the file header avoids these problems.
The required index space depends on the output file size and should be about 16
bytes per gigabyte. When this option is omitted or set to zero the necessary
index space is guessed.
Default value is 0 .

options.name: write_channel_mask bool
options.description:
Write the channel layout mask into the audio stream header.
This option is enabled by default. Disabling the channel mask can be useful in
specific scenarios, e.g. when merging multiple audio streams into one for
compatibility with software that only supports a single audio stream in AVI
(see (ffmpeg-filters)the "amerge" section in the ffmpeg-filters manual ).

options.name: loop count
options.description:
number of times to loop an animated AVIF, 0 specify an infinite
loop, default is 0

options.name: movie_timescale timescale
options.description:
Set the timescale written in the movie header box ( mvhd ).
Range is 1 to INT_MAX. Default is 1000 .

options.name: algorithm version
options.description: Select version of algorithm to fingerprint with. Range is 0 to 4 . Version 3 enables silence detection. Default is 1 .

options.name: fp_format format
options.description: Format to output the fingerprint as. Accepts the following options:

options.name: silence_threshold threshold
options.description:
Threshold for detecting silence. Range is from -1 to 32767 , where -1 disables silence detection. Silence
detection can only be used with version 3 of the algorithm.
Silence detection must be disabled for use with the AcoustID
service. Default is -1 .

options.name: adaptation_sets adaptation_sets
options.description:
Assign streams to adaptation sets, specified in the MPD manifest AdaptationSets section.
An adaptation set contains a set of one or more streams accessed as a
single subset, e.g. corresponding streams encoded at different size
selectable by the user depending on the available bandwidth, or to
different audio streams with a different language.
Each adaptation set is specified with the syntax:
where index must be a numerical index, and streams is a
sequence of , -separated stream indices. Multiple adaptation
sets can be specified, separated by spaces.
