To map all video (or audio) streams to an adaptation set, v (or a ) can be used as stream identifier instead of IDs.
When no assignment is defined, this defaults to an adaptation set for
each stream.
The following optional fields can also be specified:
A few examples of possible values for the adaptation_sets option follow:

possible_values.description:
Define the descriptor as defined by ISO/IEC 23009-1:2014/Amd.2:2015.
For example:
The descriptor string should be a self-closing XML tag.
Override the global fragment duration specified with the frag_duration option.
Override the global fragment type specified with the frag_type option.
Override the global segment duration specified with the seg_duration option.

Mark an adaptation set as containing streams meant to be used for
Trick Mode for the referenced adaptation set.

options.name: dash_segment_type type
options.description:
Set DASH segment files type.
Possible values:

possible_values.description:
The dash segment files format will be selected based on the stream
codec. This is the default mode.
the dash segment files will be in ISOBMFF/MP4 format
the dash segment files will be in WebM format

options.name: extra_window_size size
options.description:
Set the maximum number of segments kept outside of the manifest before
removing from disk.

options.name: format_options options_list
options.description:
Set container format (mp4/webm) options using a : -separated list of
key=value parameters. Values containing : special characters must be
escaped.

options.name: frag_duration
options.description:
Set the length in seconds of fragments within segments, fractional
value can also be set.

options.name: frag_type type
options.description:
Set the type of interval for fragmentation.
Possible values:

possible_values.description:
fragment at keyframes and following P-Frame reordering (Video only,
experimental)

options.name: global_sidx bool
options.description:
Write global SIDX atom. Applicable only for single file, mp4
output, non-streaming mode.

options.name: hls_master_name file_name
options.description: HLS master playlist name. Default is master.m3u8 .

options.name: hls_playlist bool
options.description:
Generate HLS playlist files. The master playlist is generated with
filename specified by the hls_master_name option. One media
playlist file is generated for each stream with filenames media_0.m3u8 , media_1.m3u8 , etc.

options.name: http_opts http_opts
options.description:
Specify a list of : -separated key=value options to pass to the
underlying HTTP protocol. Applicable only for HTTP output.

options.name: http_persistent bool
options.description: Use persistent HTTP connections. Applicable only for HTTP output.

options.name: http_user_agent user_agent
options.description:
Override User-Agent field in HTTP header. Applicable only for HTTP
output.

options.name: ignore_io_errors bool
options.description:
Ignore IO errors during open and write. Useful for long-duration runs
with network output. This is disabled by default.

options.name: index_correction bool
options.description:
Enable or disable segment index correction logic. Applicable only when use_template is enabled and use_timeline is
disabled. This is disabled by default.
When enabled, the logic monitors the flow of segment indexes. If a
streams’s segment index value is not at the expected real time
position, then the logic corrects that index value.
Typically this logic is needed in live streaming use cases. The
network bandwidth fluctuations are common during long run
streaming. Each fluctuation can cause the segment indexes fall behind
the expected real time position.

options.name: init_seg_name init_name
options.description:
DASH-templated name to use for the initialization segment. Default is init-stream$RepresentationID$.$ext$ . $ext$ is replaced
with the file name extension specific for the segment format.

options.name: ldash bool
options.description:
Enable Low-latency Dash by constraining the presence and values of
some elements. This is disabled by default.

options.name: lhls bool
options.description:
Enable Low-latency HLS (LHLS). Add #EXT-X-PREFETCH tag with
current segment’s URI. hls.js player folks are trying to standardize
an open LHLS spec. The draft spec is available at https://github.com/video-dev/hlsjs-rfcs/blob/lhls-spec/proposals/0001-lhls.md .
This option tries to comply with the above open spec. It enables streaming and hls_playlist options automatically.
This is an experimental feature.
Note: This is not Apple’s version LHLS. See https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-pantos-hls-rfc8216bis

options.name: master_m3u8_publish_rate segment_intervals_count
options.description:
Publish master playlist repeatedly every after specified number of
segment intervals.

options.name: max_playback_rate rate
options.description:
Set the maximum playback rate indicated as appropriate for the
purposes of automatically adjusting playback latency and buffer
occupancy during normal playback by clients.

options.name: media_seg_name segment_name
options.description:
DASH-templated name to use for the media segments. Default is chunk-stream$RepresentationID$-$Number%05d$.$ext$ . $ext$ is replaced with the file name extension specific for the segment
format.

options.name: method method
options.description: Use the given HTTP method to create output files. Generally set to PUT or POST .

options.name: min_playback_rate rate
options.description:
Set the minimum playback rate indicated as appropriate for the
purposes of automatically adjusting playback latency and buffer
occupancy during normal playback by clients.

options.name: mpd_profile
options.description:
Set one or more MPD manifest profiles.
Possible values:
Default value is dash .

possible_values.description: MPEG-DASH ISO Base media file format live profile

options.name: remove_at_exit bool
options.description:
Enable or disable removal of all segments when finished. This is
disabled by default.

options.name: seg_duration
options.description:
Set the segment length in seconds (fractional value can be set). The
value is treated as average segment duration when the use_template option is enabled and the use_timeline option is disabled and as minimum segment duration for all the other
use cases.
Default value is 5 .

options.name: single_file bool
options.description:
Enable or disable storing all segments in one file, accessed using
byte ranges. This is disabled by default.
The name of the single file can be specified with the single_file_name option, if not specified assume the basename
of the manifest file with the output format extension.

options.name: single_file_name file_name
options.description:
DASH-templated name to use for the manifest baseURL element. Imply that the single_file option is set to true . In the template, $ext$ is replaced with the file
name extension specific for the segment format.

options.name: availability_start_time_ms milliseconds
options.description:
Set the MPD availabilityStartTime attribute as Unix epoch time in
milliseconds. If set to 0 , the wall clock is used when the first
packet is written. This is useful for live outputs that need a stable
availability start time across restarts.

options.name: streaming bool
options.description:
Enable or disable chunk streaming mode of output. In chunk streaming
mode, each frame will be a moof fragment which forms a
chunk. This is disabled by default.

options.name: suggested_presentation_delay
options.description: Set the MPD suggestedPresentationDelay attribute. If set to 0 , the last segment duration is used.

options.name: target_latency target_latency
options.description:
Set an intended target latency in seconds for serving (fractional
value can be set). Applicable only when the streaming and write_prft options are enabled. This is an informative fields
clients can use to measure the latency of the service.

options.name: timeout timeout
options.description:
Set timeout for socket I/O operations expressed in seconds (fractional
value can be set). Applicable only for HTTP output.

options.name: update_period period
options.description:
Set the MPD update period, for dynamic content. The unit is
second. If set to 0 , the period is automatically computed.
Default value is 0 .

options.name: use_template bool
options.description: Enable or disable use of SegmentTemplate instead of SegmentList in the manifest. This is enabled by default.

options.name: use_timeline bool
options.description: Enable or disable use of SegmentTimeline within the SegmentTemplate manifest section. This is enabled by default.

options.name: utc_timing_url url
options.description:
URL of the page that will return the UTC timestamp in ISO
format, for example https://time.akamai.com/?iso

options.name: window_size size
options.description:
Set the maximum number of segments kept in the manifest, discard the
oldest one. This is useful for live streaming.
If the value is 0 , all segments are kept in the
manifest. Default value is 0 .

options.name: write_prft write_prft
options.description:
Write Producer Reference Time elements on supported streams. This also
enables writing prft boxes in the underlying muxer. Applicable only
when the utc_url option is enabled. It is set to auto by
default, in which case the muxer will attempt to enable it only in
modes that require it.

options.name: attempt_recovery bool
options.description:
If failure occurs, attempt to recover the output. This is especially
useful when used with network output, since it makes it possible to
restart streaming transparently. By default this option is set to false .

options.name: drop_pkts_on_overflow bool
options.description:
If set to true , in case the fifo queue fills up, packets will
be dropped rather than blocking the encoder. This makes it possible to
continue streaming without delaying the input, at the cost of omitting
part of the stream. By default this option is set to false , so in
such cases the encoder will be blocked until the muxer processes some
of the packets and none of them is lost.

options.name: fifo_format format_name
options.description:
Specify the format name. Useful if it cannot be guessed from the
output name suffix.

options.name: format_opts options
options.description:
Specify format options for the underlying muxer. Muxer options can be
specified as a list of key = value pairs separated by ’:’.

options.name: max_recovery_attempts count
options.description:
Set maximum number of successive unsuccessful recovery attempts after
which the output fails permanently. By default this option is set to 0 (unlimited).

options.name: queue_size size
options.description: Specify size of the queue as a number of packets. Default value is 60 .

options.name: recover_any_error bool
options.description:
If set to true , recovery will be attempted regardless of type
of the error causing the failure. By default this option is set to false and in case of certain (usually permanent) errors the
recovery is not attempted even when the attempt_recovery option is set to true .

options.name: recovery_wait_streamtime bool
options.description:
If set to false , the real time is used when waiting for the
recovery attempt (i.e. the recovery will be attempted after the time
specified by the recovery_wait_time option).
If set to true , the time of the processed stream is taken into
account instead (i.e. the recovery will be attempted after discarding
the packets corresponding to the recovery_wait_time option).
By default this option is set to false .

options.name: recovery_wait_time
options.description:
Specify waiting time in seconds before the next recovery attempt after
previous unsuccessful recovery attempt. Default value is 5 .

options.name: restart_with_keyframe bool
options.description:
Specify whether to wait for the keyframe after recovering from
queue overflow or failure. This option is set to false by default.

options.name: timeshift
options.description:
Buffer the specified amount of packets and delay writing the
output. Note that the value of the queue_size option must be
big enough to store the packets for timeshift. At the end of the input
the fifo buffer is flushed at realtime speed.

options.name: write_header bool
options.description: write the file header if set to true , default is true

options.name: flvflags
options.description: Possible values:

possible_values.description: Place AAC sequence header based on audio stream data.

Disable duration and filesize in metadata when they are equal to zero
at the end of stream. (Be used to non-seekable living stream).
Used to facilitate seeking; particularly for HTTP pseudo streaming.

options.name: hash algorithm
options.description:
Use the cryptographic hash function specified by the string algorithm .
Supported values include MD5 , murmur3 , RIPEMD128 , RIPEMD160 , RIPEMD256 , RIPEMD320 , SHA160 , SHA224 , SHA256 (default), SHA512/224 , SHA512/256 , SHA384 , SHA512 , CRC32 and adler32 .

options.name: loop bool
options.description: Set the number of times to loop the output. Use -1 for no loop, 0 for looping indefinitely (default).

options.name: final_delay delay
options.description:
Force the delay (expressed in centiseconds) after the last frame. Each frame
ends with a delay until the next frame. The default is -1 , which is a
special value to tell the muxer to reuse the previous delay. In case of a
loop, you might want to customize this value to mark a pause for instance.

options.name: hash algorithm
options.description:
Use the cryptographic hash function specified by the string algorithm .
Supported values include MD5 , murmur3 , RIPEMD128 , RIPEMD160 , RIPEMD256 , RIPEMD320 , SHA160 , SHA224 , SHA256 (default), SHA512/224 , SHA512/256 , SHA384 , SHA512 , CRC32 and adler32 .

options.name: extra_window_size int
options.description: number of fragments kept outside of the manifest before removing from disk

options.name: min_frag_duration microseconds
options.description:
minimum fragment duration (in microseconds), default value is 1 second
( 10000000 )

options.name: remove_at_exit bool
options.description: remove all fragments when finished when set to true

options.name: window_size int
options.description: number of fragments kept in the manifest, if set to a value different from 0 . By default all segments are kept in the output directory.

options.name: hls_init_time
options.description:
Set the initial target segment length. Default value is 0 .
duration must be a time duration specification,
see (ffmpeg-utils)the Time duration section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual .
Segment will be cut on the next key frame after this time has passed on the
first m3u8 list. After the initial playlist is filled, ffmpeg will cut
segments at duration equal to hls_time .

options.name: hls_time
options.description:
Set the target segment length. Default value is 2.
duration must be a time duration specification,
see (ffmpeg-utils)the Time duration section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual .
Segment will be cut on the next key frame after this time has passed.

options.name: hls_list_size size
options.description:
Set the maximum number of playlist entries. If set to 0 the list file
will contain all the segments. Default value is 5.

options.name: hls_delete_threshold size
options.description:
Set the number of unreferenced segments to keep on disk before hls_flags delete_segments deletes them. Increase this to allow continue clients to download segments which
were recently referenced in the playlist. Default value is 1, meaning segments older than hls_list_size+1 will be deleted.
options.default: 1, meaning segments older than hls_list_size+1 will be deleted

options.name: hls_start_number_source source
options.description:
Start the playlist sequence number ( #EXT-X-MEDIA-SEQUENCE ) according to the specified source.
Unless hls_flags single_file is set, it also specifies source of starting sequence numbers of
segment and subtitle filenames. In any case, if hls_flags append_list is set and read playlist sequence number is greater than the specified start sequence number,
then that value will be used as start value.
It accepts the following values:

possible_values.description: Set the start numbers according to the start_number option value.
Set the start number as the seconds since epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00).
Set the start number as the microseconds since epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00).
Set the start number based on the current date/time as YYYYmmddHHMMSS. e.g. 20161231235759.

options.name: start_number number
options.description:
Start the playlist sequence number ( #EXT-X-MEDIA-SEQUENCE ) from the specified number when hls_start_number_source value is generic . (This is the default case.)
Unless hls_flags single_file is set, it also specifies starting sequence numbers of segment and subtitle filenames.
Default value is 0.

options.name: hls_allow_cache bool
options.description: Explicitly set whether the client MAY (1) or MUST NOT (0) cache media segments.

options.name: hls_base_url baseurl
options.description:
Append baseurl to every entry in the playlist.
Useful to generate playlists with absolute paths.
Note that the playlist sequence number must be unique for each segment
and it is not to be confused with the segment filename sequence number
which can be cyclic, for example if the wrap option is
specified.

options.name: hls_segment_filename filename
options.description:
Set the segment filename. Unless the hls_flags option is set with
‘ single_file ’, filename is used as a string format with the
segment number appended.
For example:
will produce the playlist, out.m3u8 , and segment files: file000.ts , file001.ts , file002.ts , etc.
filename may contain a full path or relative path specification,
but only the file name part without any path will be contained in the m3u8 segment list.
Should a relative path be specified, the path of the created segment
files will be relative to the current working directory.
When strftime_mkdir is set, the whole expanded value of filename will be written into the m3u8 segment list.
When var_stream_map is set with two or more variant streams, the filename pattern must contain the string "%v", and this string will be
expanded to the position of variant stream index in the generated segment file
names.
For example:
will produce the playlists segment file sets: file_0_000.ts , file_0_001.ts , file_0_002.ts , etc. and file_1_000.ts , file_1_001.ts , file_1_002.ts , etc.
The string "%v" may be present in the filename or in the last directory name
containing the file, but only in one of them. (Additionally, %v may appear multiple times in the last
sub-directory or filename.) If the string %v is present in the directory name, then
sub-directories are created after expanding the directory name pattern. This
enables creation of segments corresponding to different variant streams in
subdirectories.
For example:
will produce the playlists segment file sets: vs0/file_000.ts , vs0/file_001.ts , vs0/file_002.ts , etc. and vs1/file_000.ts , vs1/file_001.ts , vs1/file_002.ts , etc.

options.name: strftime bool
options.description:
Use strftime() on filename to expand the segment filename with
localtime. The segment number is also available in this mode, but to use it,
you need to set ‘ second_level_segment_index ’ in the hls_flag and
%%d will be the specifier.
For example:
will produce the playlist, out.m3u8 , and segment files: file-20160215-1455569023.ts , file-20160215-1455569024.ts , etc.
Note: On some systems/environments, the %s specifier is not
available. See strftime() documentation.
For example:
will produce the playlist, out.m3u8 , and segment files: file-20160215-0001.ts , file-20160215-0002.ts , etc.

options.name: strftime_mkdir bool
options.description:
Used together with strftime , it will create all subdirectories which
are present in the expanded values of option hls_segment_filename .
For example:
will create a directory 201560215 (if it does not exist), and then
produce the playlist, out.m3u8 , and segment files: 20160215/file-20160215-1455569023.ts , 20160215/file-20160215-1455569024.ts , etc.
For example:
will create a directory hierarchy 2016/02/15 (if any of them do not
exist), and then produce the playlist, out.m3u8 , and segment files: 2016/02/15/file-20160215-1455569023.ts , 2016/02/15/file-20160215-1455569024.ts , etc.

options.name: hls_segment_options options_list
options.description:
Set output format options using a :-separated list of key=value
parameters. Values containing : special characters must be
escaped.

options.name: hls_key_info_file key_info_file
options.description:
Use the information in key_info_file for segment encryption. The first
line of key_info_file specifies the key URI written to the playlist. The
key URL is used to access the encryption key during playback. The second line
specifies the path to the key file used to obtain the key during the encryption
process. The key file is read as a single packed array of 16 octets in binary
format. The optional third line specifies the initialization vector (IV) as a
hexadecimal string to be used instead of the segment sequence number (default)
for encryption. Changes to key_info_file will result in segment
encryption with the new key/IV and an entry in the playlist for the new key
URI/IV if hls_flags periodic_rekey is enabled.
Key info file format:
Example key URIs:
Example key file paths:
Example IV:
Key info file example:
Example shell script:

options.name: hls_enc bool
options.description:
Enable (1) or disable (0) the AES128 encryption.
When enabled every segment generated is encrypted and the encryption key
is saved as playlist name .key.

options.name: hls_enc_key key
options.description:
Specify a 16-octet key to encrypt the segments, by default it is randomly
generated.

options.name: hls_enc_key_url keyurl
options.description:
If set, keyurl is prepended instead of baseurl to the key filename
in the playlist.

options.name: hls_enc_iv iv
options.description:
Specify the 16-octet initialization vector for every segment instead of the
autogenerated ones.

options.name: hls_segment_type
options.description: Possible values:

possible_values.description:
Output segment files in MPEG-2 Transport Stream format. This is
compatible with all HLS versions.

Output segment files in fragmented MP4 format, similar to MPEG-DASH.
fmp4 files may be used in HLS version 7 and above.

options.name: hls_fmp4_init_filename filename
options.description:
Set filename for the fragment files header file, default filename is init.mp4 .
When strftime is enabled, filename is expanded to the segment filename with localtime.
For example:
will produce init like this 1602678741_init.mp4 .

options.name: hls_fmp4_init_resend bool
options.description:
Resend init file after m3u8 file refresh every time, default is 0 .
When var_stream_map is set with two or more variant streams, the filename pattern must contain the string "%v", this string specifies
the position of variant stream index in the generated init file names.
The string "%v" may be present in the filename or in the last directory name
containing the file. If the string is present in the directory name, then
sub-directories are created after expanding the directory name pattern. This
enables creation of init files corresponding to different variant streams in
subdirectories.

options.name: hls_flags
options.description: Possible values:

possible_values.description:
If this flag is set, the muxer will store all segments in a single MPEG-TS
file, and will use byte ranges in the playlist. HLS playlists generated with
this way will have the version number 4.
For example:
will produce the playlist, out.m3u8 , and a single segment file, out.ts .

Segment files removed from the playlist are deleted after a period of time
equal to the duration of the segment plus the duration of the playlist.

Append new segments into the end of old segment list,
and remove the #EXT-X-ENDLIST from the old segment list.

Round the duration info in the playlist file segment info to integer
values, instead of using floating point.
If there are no other features requiring higher HLS versions be used,
then this will allow ffmpeg to output a HLS version 2 m3u8.

Add the #EXT-X-DISCONTINUITY tag to the playlist, before the
first segment’s information.
Do not append the EXT-X-ENDLIST tag at the end of the playlist.

The file specified by hls_key_info_file will be checked periodically and
detect updates to the encryption info. Be sure to replace this file atomically,
including the file containing the AES encryption key.

Add the #EXT-X-INDEPENDENT-SEGMENTS tag to playlists that has video segments
and when all the segments of that playlist are guaranteed to start with a key frame.

Add the #EXT-X-I-FRAMES-ONLY tag to playlists that has video segments
and can play only I-frames in the #EXT-X-BYTERANGE mode.

Allow segments to start on frames other than key frames. This improves
behavior on some players when the time between key frames is inconsistent,
but may make things worse on others, and can cause some oddities during
seeking. This flag should be used with the hls_time option.

Make it possible to use segment indexes as %%d in the hls_segment_filename option expression besides date/time values when strftime option is on. To get fixed width numbers with trailing zeroes, %%0xd format
is available where x is the required width.

Make it possible to use segment sizes (counted in bytes) as %%s in hls_segment_filename option expression besides date/time values when
strftime is on. To get fixed width numbers with trailing zeroes, %%0xs format
is available where x is the required width.

Make it possible to use segment duration (calculated in microseconds) as %%t in hls_segment_filename option expression besides date/time values when
strftime is on. To get fixed width numbers with trailing zeroes, %%0xt format
is available where x is the required width.
For example:
will produce segments like this: segment_20170102194334_0003_00122200_0000003000000.ts , segment_20170102194334_0004_00120072_0000003000000.ts etc.

Write segment data to filename.tmp and rename to filename only once the
segment is complete.
A webserver serving up segments can be configured to reject requests to *.tmp to
prevent access to in-progress segments before they have been added to the m3u8
playlist.
This flag also affects how m3u8 playlist files are created. If this flag is set,
all playlist files will be written into a temporary file and renamed after they
are complete, similarly as segments are handled. But playlists with file protocol and with hls_playlist_type type other than ‘ vod ’ are
always written into a temporary file regardless of this flag.
Master playlist files specified with master_pl_name , if any, with file protocol, are always written into temporary file regardless of this
flag if master_pl_publish_rate value is other than zero.

options.name: hls_playlist_type type
options.description:
If type is ‘ event ’, emit #EXT-X-PLAYLIST-TYPE:EVENT in the m3u8
header. This forces hls_list_size to 0; the playlist can only be
appended to.
If type is ‘ vod ’, emit #EXT-X-PLAYLIST-TYPE:VOD in the m3u8
header. This forces hls_list_size to 0; the playlist must not change.

options.name: method method
options.description:
Use the given HTTP method to create the hls files.
For example:
will upload all the mpegts segment files to the HTTP server using the HTTP PUT
method, and update the m3u8 files every refresh times using the same
method. Note that the HTTP server must support the given method for uploading
files.

options.name: http_user_agent agent
options.description: Override User-Agent field in HTTP header. Applicable only for HTTP output.

options.name: var_stream_map stream_map
options.description:
Specify a map string defining how to group the audio, video and subtitle streams
into different variant streams. The variant stream groups are separated by
space.
Expected string format is like this "a:0,v:0 a:1,v:1 ....". Here a:, v:, s: are
the keys to specify audio, video and subtitle streams respectively.
Allowed values are 0 to 9 (limited just based on practical usage).
When there are two or more variant streams, the output filename pattern must
contain the string "%v": this string specifies the position of variant stream
index in the output media playlist filenames. The string "%v" may be present in
the filename or in the last directory name containing the file. If the string is
present in the directory name, then sub-directories are created after expanding
the directory name pattern. This enables creation of variant streams in
subdirectories.
A few examples follow.

options.name: cc_stream_map cc_stream_map
options.description:
Map string which specifies different closed captions groups and their
attributes. The closed captions stream groups are separated by space.
Expected string format is like this
"ccgroup:<group name>,instreamid:<INSTREAM-ID>,language:<language code> ....".
’ccgroup’ and ’instreamid’ are mandatory attributes. ’language’ is an optional
attribute.
The closed captions groups configured using this option are mapped to different
variant streams by providing the same ’ccgroup’ name in the var_stream_map string.
For example:
will add two #EXT-X-MEDIA tags with TYPE=CLOSED-CAPTIONS in the
master playlist for the INSTREAM-IDs ’CC1’ and ’CC2’. Also, it will add CLOSED-CAPTIONS attribute with group name ’cc’ for the two output variant
streams.
If var_stream_map is not set, then the first available ccgroup in cc_stream_map is mapped to the output variant stream.
For example:
this will add #EXT-X-MEDIA tag with TYPE=CLOSED-CAPTIONS in the
master playlist with group name ’cc’, language ’en’ (english) and INSTREAM-ID
’CC1’. Also, it will add CLOSED-CAPTIONS attribute with group name ’cc’
for the output variant stream.

options.name: master_pl_name name
options.description:
Create HLS master playlist with the given name.
For example:
creates an HLS master playlist with name master.m3u8 which is published
at http://rabzelj.com/live/ .

options.name: master_pl_publish_rate count
options.description:
Publish master play list repeatedly every after specified number of segment intervals.
For example:
creates an HLS master playlist with name master.m3u8 and keeps
publishing it repeatedly every after 30 segments i.e. every after 60s.

options.name: http_persistent bool
options.description: Use persistent HTTP connections. Applicable only for HTTP output.

options.name: timeout timeout
options.description: Set timeout for socket I/O operations. Applicable only for HTTP output.

options.name: ignore_io_errors bool
options.description: Ignore IO errors during open, write and delete. Useful for long-duration runs with network output.

options.name: headers headers
options.description: Set custom HTTP headers, can override built in default headers. Applicable only for HTTP output.

options.name: frame_pts bool
options.description:
If set to 1, expand the filename with the packet PTS (presentation time stamp).
Default value is 0.

options.name: start_number count
options.description: Start the sequence from the specified number. Default value is 1.

options.name: update bool
options.description:
If set to 1, the filename will always be interpreted as just a
filename, not a pattern, and the corresponding file will be continuously
overwritten with new images. Default value is 0.

options.name: strftime bool
options.description: If set to 1, expand the filename with date and time information from strftime() . Default value is 0.

options.name: atomic_writing bool
options.description:
Write output to a temporary file, which is renamed to target filename once
writing is completed. Default is disabled.

options.name: protocol_opts options_list
options.description:
Set protocol options as a :-separated list of key=value parameters. Values
containing the : special character must be escaped.

options.name: title
options.description:
Set title name provided to a single track. This gets mapped to
the FileDescription element for a stream written as attachment.

options.name: language
options.description:
Specify the language of the track in the Matroska languages form.
The language can be either the 3 letters bibliographic ISO-639-2 (ISO
639-2/B) form (like "fre" for French), or a language code mixed with a
country code for specialities in languages (like "fre-ca" for Canadian
French).

options.name: stereo_mode
options.description:
Set stereo 3D video layout of two views in a single video track.
The following values are recognized:

possible_values.description: Both views are arranged side by side, Left-eye view is on the left
Both views are arranged in top-bottom orientation, Left-eye view is at bottom
Both views are arranged in top-bottom orientation, Left-eye view is on top
Each view is arranged in a checkerboard interleaved pattern, Left-eye view being first
Each view is arranged in a checkerboard interleaved pattern, Right-eye view being first
Each view is constituted by a row based interleaving, Right-eye view is first row
Each view is constituted by a row based interleaving, Left-eye view is first row
Both views are arranged in a column based interleaving manner, Right-eye view is first column
Both views are arranged in a column based interleaving manner, Left-eye view is first column
All frames are in anaglyph format viewable through red-cyan filters
Both views are arranged side by side, Right-eye view is on the left
All frames are in anaglyph format viewable through green-magenta filters
Both eyes laced in one Block, Left-eye view is first
Both eyes laced in one Block, Right-eye view is first

options.name: reserve_index_space size
options.description:
By default, this muxer writes the index for seeking (called cues in Matroska
terms) at the end of the file, because it cannot know in advance how much space
to leave for the index at the beginning of the file. However for some use cases
– e.g.  streaming where seeking is possible but slow – it is useful to put the
index at the beginning of the file.
If this option is set to a non-zero value, the muxer will reserve size bytes
of space in the file header and then try to write the cues there when the muxing
finishes. If the reserved space does not suffice, no Cues will be written, the
file will be finalized and writing the trailer will return an error.
A safe size for most use cases should be about 50kB per hour of video.
Note that cues are only written if the output is seekable and this option will
have no effect if it is not.

options.name: cues_to_front bool
options.description:
If set, the muxer will write the index at the beginning of the file
by shifting the main data if necessary. This can be combined with
reserve_index_space in which case the data is only shifted if
the initially reserved space turns out to be insufficient.
This option is ignored if the output is unseekable.

options.name: cluster_size_limit size
options.description:
Store at most the provided amount of bytes in a cluster.
If not specified, the limit is set automatically to a sensible
hardcoded fixed value.

options.name: cluster_time_limit
options.description:
Store at most the provided number of milliseconds in a cluster.
If not specified, the limit is set automatically to a sensible
hardcoded fixed value.

options.name: dash bool
options.description:
Create a WebM file conforming to WebM DASH specification. By default
it is set to false .

options.name: dash_track_number index
options.description: Track number for the DASH stream. By default it is set to 1 .

options.name: live bool
options.description: Write files assuming it is a live stream. By default it is set to false .

options.name: allow_raw_vfw bool
options.description: Allow raw VFW mode. By default it is set to false .

options.name: flipped_raw_rgb bool
options.description:
If set to true , store positive height for raw RGB bitmaps, which indicates
bitmap is stored bottom-up. Note that this option does not flip the bitmap
which has to be done manually beforehand, e.g. by using the ‘ vflip ’ filter.
Default is false and indicates bitmap is stored top down.
options.default: false and indicates bitmap is stored top down

options.name: write_crc32 bool
options.description:
Write a CRC32 element inside every Level 1 element. By default it is
set to true . This option is ignored for WebM.

options.name: default_mode mode
options.description:
Control how the FlagDefault of the output tracks will be set.
It influences which tracks players should play by default. The default mode
is ‘ passthrough ’.

possible_values.description:
Every track with disposition default will have the FlagDefault set.
Additionally, for each type of track (audio, video or subtitle), if no track
with disposition default of this type exists, then the first track of this type
will be marked as default (if existing). This ensures that the default flag
is set in a sensible way even if the input originated from containers that
lack the concept of default tracks.

This mode is the same as infer except that if no subtitle track with
disposition default exists, no subtitle track will be marked as default.

In this mode the FlagDefault is set if and only if the AV_DISPOSITION_DEFAULT
flag is set in the disposition of the corresponding stream.

options.name: override_time_code_rate
options.description: Override the Time Code Rate value in the output. Defaults to trying to deduce from the stream’s time_base , which often doesn’t work.

options.name: use_u_alias
options.description:
Use the U alias for the byte sequence E1h 00h 00h 00h .
Disabled by default because some .mcc files disagree on whether it has 2 or 3 zero bytes.

options.name: mcc_version
options.description: The MCC file format version. Must be either 1 or 2, defaults to 2.

options.name: creation_program
options.description: The creation program. Defaults to this version of FFmpeg.

options.name: creation_time
options.description: The creation time. Defaults to the current time.

options.name: mpegts_transport_stream_id
options.description:
Set the ‘ transport_stream_id ’. This identifies a transponder in DVB.
Default is 0x0001 .

options.name: mpegts_original_network_id
options.description:
Set the ‘ original_network_id ’. This is unique identifier of a
network in DVB. Its main use is in the unique identification of a service
through the path ‘ Original_Network_ID, Transport_Stream_ID ’. Default
is 0xff01 .

options.name: mpegts_service_id
options.description: Set the ‘ service_id ’, also known as program in DVB. Default is 0x0001 .

options.name: mpegts_service_type
options.description:
Set the program ‘ service_type ’. Default is digital_tv .
Accepts the following options:

possible_values.description:
Any hexadecimal value between 0x01 and 0xff as defined in
ETSI 300 468.

options.name: mpegts_pmt_start_pid
options.description:
Set the first PID for PMTs. Default is 0x1000 , minimum is 0x0020 ,
maximum is 0x1ffa . This option has no effect in m2ts mode where the PMT
PID is fixed 0x0100 .

options.name: mpegts_start_pid
options.description:
Set the first PID for elementary streams. Default is 0x0100 , minimum is 0x0020 , maximum is 0x1ffa . This option has no effect in m2ts mode
where the elementary stream PIDs are fixed.

options.name: mpegts_m2ts_mode
options.description:
Enable m2ts mode if set to 1 . Default value is -1 which
disables m2ts mode.

options.name: muxrate
options.description: Set a constant muxrate. Default is VBR.

options.name: pes_payload_size
options.description: Set minimum PES packet payload in bytes. Default is 2930 .

options.name: mpegts_flags
options.description: Set mpegts flags. Accepts the following options:

possible_values.description: Reemit PAT/PMT before writing the next packet.
Reemit PAT and PMT at each video frame.
Conform to System B (DVB) instead of System A (ATSC).
Mark the initial packet of each stream as discontinuity.
Disable writing of random access indicator.

options.name: mpegts_copyts
options.description:
Preserve original timestamps, if value is set to 1 . Default value
is -1 , which results in shifting timestamps so that they start from 0.
options.default: -1 , which results in shifting timestamps so that they start from 0

options.name: omit_video_pes_length
options.description: Omit the PES packet length for video packets. Default is 1 (true).

options.name: pcr_period
options.description:
Override the default PCR retransmission time in milliseconds. Default is -1 which means that the PCR interval will be determined automatically:
20 ms is used for CBR streams, the highest multiple of the frame duration which
is less than 100 ms is used for VBR streams.
options.default: -1 which means that the PCR interval will be determined automatically:

options.name: pat_period
options.description: Maximum time in seconds between PAT/PMT tables. Default is 0.1 .

options.name: sdt_period
options.description: Maximum time in seconds between SDT tables. Default is 0.5 .

options.name: nit_period
options.description: Maximum time in seconds between NIT tables. Default is 0.5 .

options.name: tables_version
options.description:
Set PAT, PMT, SDT and NIT version (default 0 , valid values are from 0 to 31, inclusively).
This option allows updating stream structure so that standard consumer may
detect the change. To do so, reopen output AVFormatContext (in case of API
usage) or restart ffmpeg instance, cyclically changing tables_version value:

options.name: store_user_comments bool
options.description:
Set if user comments should be stored if available or never.
IRT D-10 does not allow user comments. The default is thus to write them for
mxf and mxf_opatom but not for mxf_d10

options.name: -syncpoints flags
options.description:
Change the syncpoint usage in nut:
The none and timestamped flags are experimental.

possible_values.value: default use the normal low-overhead seeking aids.
none do not use the syncpoints at all, reducing the overhead but making the stream non-seekable;
possible_values.description:
Use of this option is not recommended, as the resulting files are very damage
    sensitive and seeking is not possible. Also in general the overhead from
    syncpoints is negligible. Note, - write_index 0 can be used to disable
    all growing data tables, allowing to mux endless streams with limited memory
    and without these disadvantages.

possible_values.value: timestamped extend the syncpoint with a wallclock field.

options.name: -write_index bool
options.description: Write index at the end, the default is to write an index.

options.name: -page_duration duration
options.description:
Preferred page duration, in microseconds. The muxer will attempt to create
pages that are approximately duration microseconds long. This allows the
user to compromise between seek granularity and container overhead. The default
is 1 second. A value of 0 will fill all segments, making pages as large as
possible. A value of 1 will effectively use 1 packet-per-page in most
situations, giving a small seek granularity at the cost of additional container
overhead.

options.name: -serial_offset value
options.description:
Serial value from which to set the streams serial number.
Setting it to different and sufficiently large values ensures that the produced
ogg files can be safely chained.

options.name: max_frames frames
options.description:
Reserve space for at most frames video frames in the file header. This
option is mandatory.

options.name: increment_tc 1|0
options.description:
if set to 1 , increment timecode between each segment
If this is selected, the input need to have
a timecode in the first video stream. Default value is 0 .

options.name: reference_stream specifier
options.description:
Set the reference stream, as specified by the string specifier .
If specifier is set to auto , the reference is chosen
automatically. Otherwise it must be a stream specifier (see the “Stream
specifiers” chapter in the ffmpeg manual) which specifies the
reference stream. The default value is auto .

options.name: segment_format format
options.description:
Override the inner container format, by default it is guessed by the filename
extension.

options.name: segment_format_options options_list
options.description:
Set output format options using a :-separated list of key=value
parameters. Values containing the : special character must be
escaped.

options.name: segment_list name
options.description:
Generate also a listfile named name . If not specified no
listfile is generated.

options.name: segment_list_flags
options.description:
Set flags affecting the segment list generation.
It currently supports the following flags:

possible_values.description: Allow caching (only affects M3U8 list files).

options.name: segment_list_size size
options.description:
Update the list file so that it contains at most size segments. If 0 the list file will contain all the segments. Default
value is 0.

options.name: segment_list_entry_prefix prefix
options.description:
Prepend prefix to each entry. Useful to generate absolute paths.
By default no prefix is applied.

options.name: segment_list_type type
options.description:
Select the listing format.
The following values are recognized:
If not specified the type is guessed from the list file name suffix.

possible_values.description: Generate a flat list for the created segments, one segment per line.

Generate a list for the created segments, one segment per line,
each line matching the format (comma-separated values):
segment_filename is the name of the output file generated by the
muxer according to the provided pattern. CSV escaping (according to
RFC4180) is applied if required.
segment_start_time and segment_end_time specify
the segment start and end time expressed in seconds.
A list file with the suffix ".csv" or ".ext" will
auto-select this format.
‘ ext ’ is deprecated in favor or ‘ csv ’.

Generate an ffconcat file for the created segments. The resulting file
can be read using the FFmpeg concat demuxer.
A list file with the suffix ".ffcat" or ".ffconcat" will
auto-select this format.

Generate an extended M3U8 file, version 3, compliant with http://tools.ietf.org/id/draft-pantos-http-live-streaming .
A list file with the suffix ".m3u8" will auto-select this format.

options.name: segment_time time
options.description:
Set segment duration to time , the value must be a duration
specification. Default value is "2". See also the segment_times option.
Note that splitting may not be accurate, unless you force the
reference stream key-frames at the given time. See the introductory
notice and the examples below.

options.name: min_seg_duration time
options.description:
Set minimum segment duration to time , the value must be a duration
specification. This prevents the muxer ending segments at a duration below
this value. Only effective with segment_time . Default value is "0".

options.name: segment_atclocktime 1|0
options.description:
If set to "1" split at regular clock time intervals starting from 00:00
o’clock. The time value specified in segment_time is
used for setting the length of the splitting interval.
For example with segment_time set to "900" this makes it possible
to create files at 12:00 o’clock, 12:15, 12:30, etc.
Default value is "0".

options.name: segment_clocktime_offset
options.description:
Delay the segment splitting times with the specified duration when using segment_atclocktime .
For example with segment_time set to "900" and segment_clocktime_offset set to "300" this makes it possible to
create files at 12:05, 12:20, 12:35, etc.
Default value is "0".

options.name: segment_clocktime_wrap_duration
options.description:
Force the segmenter to only start a new segment if a packet reaches the muxer
within the specified duration after the segmenting clock time. This way you
can make the segmenter more resilient to backward local time jumps, such as
leap seconds or transition to standard time from daylight savings time.
Default is the maximum possible duration which means starting a new segment
regardless of the elapsed time since the last clock time.
options.default: the maximum possible duration which means starting a new segment

options.name: segment_time_delta delta
options.description:
Specify the accuracy time when selecting the start time for a
segment, expressed as a duration specification. Default value is "0".
When delta is specified a key-frame will start a new segment if its
PTS satisfies the relation:
This option is useful when splitting video content, which is always
split at GOP boundaries, in case a key frame is found just before the
specified split time.
In particular may be used in combination with the ffmpeg option force_key_frames . The key frame times specified by force_key_frames may not be set accurately because of rounding
issues, with the consequence that a key frame time may result set just
before the specified time. For constant frame rate videos a value of
1/(2* frame_rate ) should address the worst case mismatch between
the specified time and the time set by force_key_frames .

options.name: segment_times times
options.description:
Specify a list of split points. times contains a list of comma
separated duration specifications, in increasing order. See also
the segment_time option.

options.name: segment_frames frames
options.description:
Specify a list of split video frame numbers. frames contains a
list of comma separated integer numbers, in increasing order.
This option specifies to start a new segment whenever a reference
stream key frame is found and the sequential number (starting from 0)
of the frame is greater or equal to the next value in the list.

options.name: segment_wrap limit
options.description: Wrap around segment index once it reaches limit .

options.name: segment_start_number number
options.description: Set the sequence number of the first segment. Defaults to 0 .

options.name: strftime 1|0
options.description:
Use the strftime function to define the name of the new
segments to write. If this is selected, the output segment name must
contain a strftime function template. Default value is 0 .

options.name: break_non_keyframes 1|0
options.description:
If enabled, allow segments to start on frames other than keyframes. This
improves behavior on some players when the time between keyframes is
inconsistent, but may make things worse on others, and can cause some oddities
during seeking. Defaults to 0 .

options.name: reset_timestamps 1|0
options.description:
Reset timestamps at the beginning of each segment, so that each segment
will start with near-zero timestamps. It is meant to ease the playback
of the generated segments. May not work with some combinations of
muxers/codecs. It is set to 0 by default.

options.name: initial_offset offset
options.description:
Specify timestamp offset to apply to the output packet timestamps. The
argument must be a time duration specification, and defaults to 0.

options.name: write_empty_segments 1|0
options.description:
If enabled, write an empty segment if there are no packets during the period a
segment would usually span. Otherwise, the segment will be filled with the next
packet written. Defaults to 0 .

options.name: window_size
options.description: Specify the number of fragments kept in the manifest. Default 0 (keep all).

options.name: extra_window_size
options.description: Specify the number of fragments kept outside of the manifest before removing from disk. Default 5.

options.name: lookahead_count
options.description: Specify the number of lookahead fragments. Default 2.

options.name: min_frag_duration
options.description: Specify the minimum fragment duration (in microseconds). Default 5000000.

options.name: remove_at_exit
options.description: Specify whether to remove all fragments when finished. Default 0 (do not remove).

options.name: hash algorithm
options.description:
Use the cryptographic hash function specified by the string algorithm .
Supported values include MD5 , murmur3 , RIPEMD128 , RIPEMD160 , RIPEMD256 , RIPEMD320 , SHA160 , SHA224 , SHA256 (default), SHA512/224 , SHA512/256 , SHA384 , SHA512 , CRC32 and adler32 .

options.name: use_fifo bool
options.description:
If set to 1, slave outputs will be processed in separate threads using the fifo muxer. This allows to compensate for different speed/latency/reliability of
outputs and setup transparent recovery. By default this feature is turned off.

options.name: fifo_options
options.description: Options to pass to fifo pseudo-muxer instances. See fifo .

options.name: f
options.description:
Specify the format name. Required if it cannot be guessed from the
output URL.

options.name: bsfs
options.description:
Specify a list of bitstream filters to apply to the specified
output.
It is possible to specify to which streams a given bitstream filter
applies, by appending a stream specifier to the option separated by / . spec must be a stream specifier (see Format stream specifiers ).
If the stream specifier is not specified, the bitstream filters will be
applied to all streams in the output. This will cause that output operation
to fail if the output contains streams to which the bitstream filter cannot
be applied e.g. h264_mp4toannexb being applied to an output containing an audio stream.
Options for a bitstream filter must be specified in the form of opt=value .
Several bitstream filters can be specified, separated by ",".

options.name: use_fifo bool
options.description: This allows to override tee muxer use_fifo option for individual slave muxer.

options.name: fifo_options
options.description:
This allows to override tee muxer fifo_options for individual slave muxer.
See fifo .

options.name: select
options.description:
Select the streams that should be mapped to the slave output,
specified by a stream specifier. If not specified, this defaults to
all the mapped streams. This will cause that output operation to fail
if the output format does not accept all mapped streams.
You may use multiple stream specifiers separated by commas ( , ) e.g.: a:0,v

options.name: onfail
options.description:
Specify behaviour on output failure. This can be set to either abort (which is
default) or ignore . abort will cause whole process to fail in case of failure
on this slave output. ignore will ignore failure on this output, so other outputs
will continue without being affected.

options.name: chunk_start_index
options.description: Index of the first chunk (defaults to 0).

options.name: header
options.description: Filename of the header where the initialization data will be written.

options.name: audio_chunk_duration
options.description: Duration of each audio chunk in milliseconds (defaults to 5000).

options.name: adaptation_sets
options.description:
This option has the following syntax: "id=x,streams=a,b,c id=y,streams=d,e" where x and y are the
unique identifiers of the adaptation sets and a,b,c,d and e are the indices of the corresponding
audio and video streams. Any number of adaptation sets can be added using this option.

options.name: live
options.description: Set this to 1 to create a live stream DASH Manifest. Default: 0.

options.name: chunk_start_index
options.description:
Start index of the first chunk. This will go in the ‘ startNumber ’ attribute
of the ‘ SegmentTemplate ’ element in the manifest. Default: 0.

options.name: chunk_duration_ms
options.description:
Duration of each chunk in milliseconds. This will go in the ‘ duration ’
attribute of the ‘ SegmentTemplate ’ element in the manifest. Default: 1000.

options.name: utc_timing_url
options.description:
URL of the page that will return the UTC timestamp in ISO format. This will go
in the ‘ value ’ attribute of the ‘ UTCTiming ’ element in the manifest.
Default: None.

options.name: time_shift_buffer_depth
options.description:
Smallest time (in seconds) shifting buffer for which any Representation is
guaranteed to be available. This will go in the ‘ timeShiftBufferDepth ’
attribute of the ‘ MPD ’ element. Default: 60.

options.name: minimum_update_period
options.description:
Minimum update period (in seconds) of the manifest. This will go in the
‘ minimumUpdatePeriod ’ attribute of the ‘ MPD ’ element. Default: 0.

options.name: handshake_timeout
options.description:
Set the timeout in milliseconds for ICE and DTLS handshake.
Default value is 5000.

options.name: timeout
options.description:
Set timeout in seconds for socket I/O operations. Applicable only for HTTP output.
Default value is -1.

options.name: pkt_size
options.description:
Set the maximum size, in bytes, of RTP packets that send out.
Default value is 1200.

options.name: ts_buffer_size
options.description:
Set the buffer size, in bytes, of underlying protocol.
Default value is -1(auto). The UDP auto selects a reasonable value.

options.name: whip_flags
options.description: Possible values:

possible_values.description: The muxer will try to set dtls active role and send the first client hello.

options.name: rtp_history
options.description:
Set the number of RTP history items to store.
Default value is 512.

options.name: authorization
options.description: The optional Bearer token for WHIP Authorization.

options.name: cert_file
options.description: The optional certificate file path for DTLS.

options.name: key_file
options.description: The optional private key file path for DTLS.

options.name: protocol_whitelist
options.description:
Set a ","-separated list of allowed protocols. "ALL" matches all protocols. Protocols
prefixed by "-" are disabled.
All protocols are allowed by default but protocols used by an another
protocol (nested protocols) are restricted to a per protocol subset.

options.name: rw_timeout
options.description:
Maximum time to wait for (network) read/write operations to complete,
in microseconds.

options.name: exchange
options.description:
Sets the exchange to use on the broker. RabbitMQ has several predefined
exchanges: "amq.direct" is the default exchange, where the publisher and
subscriber must have a matching routing_key; "amq.fanout" is the same as a
broadcast operation (i.e. the data is forwarded to all queues on the fanout
exchange independent of the routing_key); and "amq.topic" is similar to
"amq.direct", but allows for more complex pattern matching (refer to the RabbitMQ
documentation).

options.name: routing_key
options.description:
Sets the routing key. The default value is "amqp". The routing key is used on
the "amq.direct" and "amq.topic" exchanges to decide whether packets are written
to the queue of a subscriber.

options.name: pkt_size
options.description:
Maximum size of each packet sent/received to the broker. Default is 131072.
Minimum is 4096 and max is any large value (representable by an int). When
receiving packets, this sets an internal buffer size in FFmpeg. It should be
equal to or greater than the size of the published packets to the broker. Otherwise
the received message may be truncated causing decoding errors.

options.name: connection_timeout
options.description:
The timeout in seconds during the initial connection to the broker. The
default value is rw_timeout, or 5 seconds if rw_timeout is not set.
options.default: rw_timeout, or 5 seconds if rw_timeout is not set

options.name: delivery_mode mode
options.description:
Sets the delivery mode of each message sent to broker.
The following values are accepted:

possible_values.description:
Delivery mode set to "persistent" (2). This is the default value.
Messages may be written to the broker’s disk depending on its setup.

Delivery mode set to "non-persistent" (1).
Messages will stay in broker’s memory unless the broker is under memory
pressure.

options.name: angle
options.description: BluRay angle

options.name: chapter
options.description: Start chapter (1...N)

options.name: playlist
options.description: Playlist to read (BDMV/PLAYLIST/?????.mpls)

options.name: read_ahead_limit
options.description:
Amount in bytes that may be read ahead when seeking isn’t supported. Range is -1 to INT_MAX.
-1 for unlimited. Default is 65536.

options.name: key
options.description: Set the AES decryption key binary block from given hexadecimal representation.

options.name: iv
options.description: Set the AES decryption initialization vector binary block from given hexadecimal representation.

options.name: blocksize
options.description:
Set I/O operation maximum block size, in bytes. Default value is INT_MAX , which results in not limiting the requested block size.
Setting this value reasonably low improves user termination request reaction
time, which is valuable if data transmission is slow.
options.default: INT_MAX , which results in not limiting the requested block size

options.name: fd
options.description: Set file descriptor.

options.name: truncate
options.description:
Truncate existing files on write, if set to 1. A value of 0 prevents
truncating. Default value is 1.

options.name: blocksize
options.description:
Set I/O operation maximum block size, in bytes. Default value is INT_MAX , which results in not limiting the requested block size.
Setting this value reasonably low improves user termination request reaction
time, which is valuable for files on slow medium.
options.default: INT_MAX , which results in not limiting the requested block size

options.name: follow
options.description:
If set to 1, the protocol will retry reading at the end of the file, allowing
reading files that still are being written. In order for this to terminate,
you either need to use the rw_timeout option, or use the interrupt callback
(for API users).

options.name: seekable
options.description:
Controls if seekability is advertised on the file. 0 means non-seekable, -1
means auto (seekable for normal files, non-seekable for named pipes).
Many demuxers handle seekable and non-seekable resources differently,
overriding this might speed up opening certain files at the cost of losing some
features (e.g. accurate seeking).

options.name: pkt_size
options.description:
Set the maximum packet size used for file I/O. A smaller value may reduce
memory usage. A higher value may increase throughput especially with networked
filesystems.
For reading, if explicitly set, it overrides the default internal buffer size
(32 KB) and limits the maximum amount of data read per operation.
For writing, this sets the size of each write operation. The default is 256 KB
for regular files, 32 KB otherwise.

options.name: timeout
options.description:
Set timeout in microseconds of socket I/O operations used by the underlying low level
operation. By default it is set to -1, which means that the timeout is
not specified.

options.name: ftp-user
options.description:
Set a user to be used for authenticating to the FTP server. This is overridden by the
user in the FTP URL.

options.name: ftp-password
options.description:
Set a password to be used for authenticating to the FTP server. This is overridden by
the password in the FTP URL, or by ftp-anonymous-password if no user is set.

options.name: ftp-anonymous-password
options.description:
Password used when login as anonymous user. Typically an e-mail address
should be used.

options.name: ftp-write-seekable
options.description:
Control seekability of connection during encoding. If set to 1 the
resource is supposed to be seekable, if set to 0 it is assumed not
to be seekable. Default value is 0.

options.name: seekable
options.description:
Control seekability of connection. If set to 1 the resource is
supposed to be seekable, if set to 0 it is assumed not to be seekable,
if set to -1 it will try to autodetect if it is seekable. Default
value is -1.

options.name: chunked_post
options.description: If set to 1 use chunked Transfer-Encoding for posts, default is 1.

options.name: http_proxy
options.description: set HTTP proxy to tunnel through e.g. http://rabzelj.com:1234

options.name: headers
options.description:
Set custom HTTP headers, can override built in default headers. The
value must be a string encoding the headers.

options.name: content_type
options.description: Set a specific content type for the POST messages or for listen mode.

options.name: user_agent
options.description:
Override the User-Agent header. If not specified the protocol will use a
string describing the libavformat build. ("Lavf/<version>")

options.name: referer
options.description: Set the Referer header. Include ’Referer: URL’ header in HTTP request.

options.name: multiple_requests
options.description: Use persistent connections if set to 1, default is 0.

options.name: request_size
options.description:
Limit the size of requests made. This is useful for some pathological servers
that throttle unbounded range requests, as well as when expecting to seek
frequently. Disabled (set to 0) by default.
Note that if enabling this option, it’s strongly recommended to also enable
the multiple_requests option, as well as setting short_seek_size to the same value or higher. Doing so allows FFmpeg
to reuse a single HTTP connection wherever possible.

options.name: initial_request_size
options.description:
Limit the size of initial requests. Similar to request_size , but only
used during initial format parsing. Useful for formats like MXF or MOV that
require frequent seeks during header parsing. Lasts until the demuxer makes a
read request larger than this size (without a seek in between), after which
the implementation will continue using requests as usual. Disabled (set to 0)
by default.
Note that if enabling this option, it’s strongly recommended to also enable
the multiple_requests option, as well as setting short_seek_size to the same value or higher.

options.name: post_data
options.description: Set custom HTTP post data.

options.name: mime_type
options.description: Export the MIME type.

options.name: http_version
options.description: Exports the HTTP response version number. Usually "1.0" or "1.1".

options.name: cookies
options.description:
Set the cookies to be sent in future requests. The format of each cookie is the
same as the value of a Set-Cookie HTTP response field. Multiple cookies can be
delimited by a newline character.

options.name: icy
options.description:
If set to 1 request ICY (SHOUTcast) metadata from the server. If the server
supports this, the metadata has to be retrieved by the application by reading
the icy_metadata_headers and icy_metadata_packet options.
The default is 1.

options.name: icy_metadata_headers
options.description:
If the server supports ICY metadata, this contains the ICY-specific HTTP reply
headers, separated by newline characters.

options.name: icy_metadata_packet
options.description:
If the server supports ICY metadata, and icy was set to 1, this
contains the last non-empty metadata packet sent by the server. It should be
polled in regular intervals by applications interested in mid-stream metadata
updates.

options.name: metadata
options.description:
Set an exported dictionary containing Icecast metadata from the bitstream, if present.
Only useful with the C API.

options.name: auth_type
options.description:
Set HTTP authentication type. No option for Digest, since this method requires
getting nonce parameters from the server first and can’t be used straight away like
Basic.

possible_values.description: Choose the HTTP authentication type automatically. This is the default.

Choose the HTTP basic authentication.
Basic authentication sends a Base64-encoded string that contains a user name and password
for the client. Base64 is not a form of encryption and should be considered the same as
sending the user name and password in clear text (Base64 is a reversible encoding).
If a resource needs to be protected, strongly consider using an authentication scheme
other than basic authentication. HTTPS/TLS should be used with basic authentication.
Without these additional security enhancements, basic authentication should not be used
to protect sensitive or valuable information.

options.name: send_expect_100
options.description:
Send an Expect: 100-continue header for POST. If set to 1 it will send, if set
to 0 it won’t, if set to -1 it will try to send if it is applicable. Default
value is -1.

options.name: location
options.description:
An exported dictionary containing the content location. Only useful with the C
API.

options.name: offset
options.description: Set initial byte offset.

options.name: end_offset
options.description: Try to limit the request to bytes preceding this offset.

options.name: method
options.description:
When used as a client option it sets the HTTP method for the request.
When used as a server option it sets the HTTP method that is going to be
expected from the client(s).
If the expected and the received HTTP method do not match the client will
be given a Bad Request response.
When unset the HTTP method is not checked for now. This will be replaced by
autodetection in the future.

options.name: reconnect
options.description: Reconnect automatically when disconnected before EOF is hit.

options.name: reconnect_at_eof
options.description:
If set then eof is treated like an error and causes reconnection, this is useful
for live / endless streams.

options.name: reconnect_on_network_error
options.description: Reconnect automatically in case of TCP/TLS errors during connect.

options.name: reconnect_on_http_error
options.description:
A comma separated list of HTTP status codes to reconnect on. The list can
include specific status codes (e.g. ’503’) or the strings ’4xx’ / ’5xx’.

options.name: reconnect_streamed
options.description: If set then even streamed/non seekable streams will be reconnected on errors.

options.name: reconnect_delay_max
options.description: Set the maximum delay in seconds after which to give up reconnecting.

options.name: reconnect_max_retries
options.description: Set the maximum number of times to retry a connection. Default unset.

options.name: reconnect_delay_total_max
options.description: Set the maximum total delay in seconds after which to give up reconnecting.

options.name: respect_retry_after
options.description:
If enabled, and a Retry-After header is encountered, its requested reconnection
delay will be honored, rather than using exponential backoff. Useful for 429 and
503 errors. Default enabled.

options.name: listen
options.description:
If set to 1 enables experimental HTTP server. This can be used to send data when
used as an output option, or read data from a client with HTTP POST when used as
an input option.
If set to 2 enables experimental multi-client HTTP server. This is not yet implemented
in ffmpeg.c and thus must not be used as a command line option.

options.name: resource
options.description: The resource requested by a client, when the experimental HTTP server is in use.

options.name: reply_code
options.description: The HTTP code returned to the client, when the experimental HTTP server is in use.

options.name: short_seek_size
options.description:
Set the threshold, in bytes, for when a readahead should be preferred over a seek and
new HTTP request. This is useful, for example, to make sure the same connection
is used for reading large video packets with small audio packets in between.

options.name: ice_genre
options.description: Set the stream genre.

options.name: ice_name
options.description: Set the stream name.

options.name: ice_description
options.description: Set the stream description.

options.name: ice_url
options.description: Set the stream website URL.

options.name: ice_public
options.description:
Set if the stream should be public.
The default is 0 (not public).

options.name: user_agent
options.description:
Override the User-Agent header. If not specified a string of the form
"Lavf/<version>" will be used.

options.name: password
options.description: Set the Icecast mountpoint password.

options.name: content_type
options.description:
Set the stream content type. This must be set if it is different from
audio/mpeg.

options.name: legacy_icecast
options.description:
This enables support for Icecast versions < 2.4.0, that do not support the
HTTP PUT method but the SOURCE method.

options.name: tls
options.description: Establish a TLS (HTTPS) connection to Icecast.

options.name: gateway
options.description:
Defines the gateway to use. When not set, the protocol will first try
locating the local gateway by looking at $IPFS_GATEWAY , $IPFS_PATH and $HOME/.ipfs/ , in that order.

options.name: blocksize
options.description:
Set I/O operation maximum block size, in bytes. Default value is INT_MAX , which results in not limiting the requested block size.
Setting this value reasonably low improves user termination request reaction
time, which is valuable if data transmission is slow.
options.default: INT_MAX , which results in not limiting the requested block size

options.name: fd
options.description: Set file descriptor.

options.name: l= n
options.description: The number of columns (4-20, LxD <= 100)

options.name: d= n
options.description: The number of rows (4-20, LxD <= 100)

options.name: rist_profile
options.description: Supported values:

options.name: buffer_size
options.description:
Set internal RIST buffer size in milliseconds for retransmission of data.
Default value is 0 which means the librist default (1 sec). Maximum value is 30
seconds.
options.default: 0 which means the librist default (1 sec)

options.name: fifo_size
options.description:
Size of the librist receiver output fifo in number of packets. This must be a
power of 2.
Defaults to 8192 (vs the librist default of 1024).

options.name: overrun_nonfatal= 1|0
options.description: Survive in case of librist fifo buffer overrun. Default value is 0.

options.name: pkt_size
options.description: Set maximum packet size for sending data. 1316 by default.

options.name: log_level
options.description:
Set loglevel for RIST logging messages. You only need to set this if you
explicitly want to enable debug level messages or packet loss simulation,
otherwise the regular loglevel is respected.

options.name: secret
options.description: Set override of encryption secret, by default is unset.

options.name: encryption
options.description:
Set encryption type, by default is disabled.
Acceptable values are 128 and 256.

options.name: username
options.description: An optional username (mostly for publishing).

options.name: password
options.description: An optional password (mostly for publishing).

options.name: server
options.description: The address of the RTMP server.

options.name: port
options.description: The number of the TCP port to use (by default is 1935).

options.name: app
options.description:
It is the name of the application to access. It usually corresponds to
the path where the application is installed on the RTMP server
(e.g. /ondemand/ , /flash/live/ , etc.). You can override
the value parsed from the URI through the rtmp_app option, too.

options.name: playpath
options.description:
It is the path or name of the resource to play with reference to the
application specified in app , may be prefixed by "mp4:". You
can override the value parsed from the URI through the rtmp_playpath option, too.

options.name: listen
options.description: Act as a server, listening for an incoming connection.

options.name: timeout
options.description: Maximum time to wait for the incoming connection. Implies listen.

options.name: rtmp_app
options.description:
Name of application to connect on the RTMP server. This option
overrides the parameter specified in the URI.

options.name: rtmp_buffer
options.description: Set the client buffer time in milliseconds. The default is 3000.

options.name: rtmp_conn
options.description:
Extra arbitrary AMF connection parameters, parsed from a string,
e.g. like B:1 S:authMe O:1 NN:code:1.23 NS:flag:ok O:0 .
Each value is prefixed by a single character denoting the type,
B for Boolean, N for number, S for string, O for object, or Z for null,
followed by a colon. For Booleans the data must be either 0 or 1 for
FALSE or TRUE, respectively.  Likewise for Objects the data must be 0 or
1 to end or begin an object, respectively. Data items in subobjects may
be named, by prefixing the type with ’N’ and specifying the name before
the value (i.e. NB:myFlag:1 ). This option may be used multiple
times to construct arbitrary AMF sequences.

options.name: rtmp_enhanced_codecs
options.description:
Specify the list of codecs the client advertises to support in an
enhanced RTMP stream. This option should be set to a comma separated
list of fourcc values, like hvc1,av01,vp09 for multiple codecs
or hvc1 for only one codec. The specified list will be presented
in the "fourCcLive" property of the Connect Command Message.

options.name: rtmp_flashver
rtmp_flush_interval
options.description:
Number of packets flushed in the same request (RTMPT only). The default
is 10.

options.name: rtmp_live
options.description:
Specify that the media is a live stream. No resuming or seeking in
live streams is possible. The default value is any , which means the
subscriber first tries to play the live stream specified in the
playpath. If a live stream of that name is not found, it plays the
recorded stream. The other possible values are live and recorded .

options.name: rtmp_pageurl
options.description:
URL of the web page in which the media was embedded. By default no
value will be sent.

options.name: rtmp_playpath
options.description:
Stream identifier to play or to publish. This option overrides the
parameter specified in the URI.

options.name: rtmp_subscribe
options.description:
Name of live stream to subscribe to. By default no value will be sent.
It is only sent if the option is specified or if rtmp_live
is set to live.

options.name: rtmp_swfhash
options.description: SHA256 hash of the decompressed SWF file (32 bytes).

options.name: rtmp_swfsize
options.description: Size of the decompressed SWF file, required for SWFVerification.

options.name: rtmp_swfurl
options.description: URL of the SWF player for the media. By default no value will be sent.

options.name: rtmp_swfverify
options.description: URL to player swf file, compute hash/size automatically.

options.name: rtmp_tcurl
options.description: URL of the target stream. Defaults to proto://host[:port]/app.

options.name: tcp_nodelay= 1|0
options.description:
Set TCP_NODELAY to disable Nagle’s algorithm. Default value is 0.
Remark: Writing to the socket is currently not optimized to minimize system calls and reduces the efficiency / effect of TCP_NODELAY.

options.name: tcp_keepalive= 1|0
options.description:
Enable the TCP keepalive mechanism to detect dead peers and help maintain long-lived idle connections. Default value is 0.
Only the basic keepalive option (SO_KEEPALIVE) can be enabled or disabled. Platform-specific tuning parameters such as TCP_KEEPIDLE, TCP_KEEPINTVL, or TCP_KEEPCNT are not configurable and will use the operating system’s default values.

options.name: timeout
options.description:
Set timeout in milliseconds of socket I/O operations used by the underlying
low level operation. By default it is set to -1, which means that the timeout
is not specified.

options.name: truncate
options.description:
Truncate existing files on write, if set to 1. A value of 0 prevents
truncating. Default value is 1.

options.name: workgroup
options.description: Set the workgroup used for making connections. By default workgroup is not specified.

options.name: timeout
options.description:
Set timeout of socket I/O operations used by the underlying low level
operation. By default it is set to -1, which means that the timeout
is not specified.

options.name: truncate
options.description:
