Truncate existing files on write, if set to 1. A value of 0 prevents
truncating. Default value is 1.

options.name: private_key
options.description:
Specify the path of the file containing private key to use during authorization.
By default libssh searches for keys in the ~/.ssh/ directory.

options.name: ttl= n
options.description: Set the TTL (Time-To-Live) value (for multicast only).

options.name: rtcpport= n
options.description: Set the remote RTCP port to n .

options.name: localport
options.description:
Set the local RTP port to n .
Using the localport option name is deprecated and should not be used.

options.name: local_rtcpport
options.description: Set the local RTCP port to n .

options.name: pkt_size= n
options.description: Set max packet size (in bytes) to n .

options.name: buffer_size= size
options.description: Set the maximum UDP socket buffer size in bytes.

options.name: connect=0|1
options.description:
Do a connect() on the UDP socket (if set to 1) or not (if set
to 0).

options.name: sources= ip
options.description: List allowed source IP addresses.

options.name: block= ip
options.description: List disallowed (blocked) source IP addresses.

options.name: write_to_source=0|1
options.description:
Send packets to the source address of the latest received packet (if
set to 1) or to a default remote address (if set to 0).

options.name: localaddr= addr
options.description:
Local IP address of a network interface used for sending packets or joining
multicast groups.

options.name: timeout= n
options.description: Set timeout (in microseconds) of socket I/O operations to n .

options.name: rtsp_transport
options.description:
Set RTSP transport protocols.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is ‘ 0 ’.

possible_values.description: Use UDP as lower transport protocol.

Use TCP (interleaving within the RTSP control channel) as lower
transport protocol.

options.name: rtsp_flags
options.description:
Set RTSP flags.
The following values are accepted:
Default value is ‘ 0 ’.

possible_values.description: Use MP4A-LATM packetization instead of MPEG4-GENERIC for AAC.
Use RFC 2190 packetization instead of RFC 4629 for H.263.
Use mode 0 for H.264 in RTP.
Send RTCP BYE packets when finishing.

options.name: min_port
options.description: Set minimum local UDP port. Default value is 5000.

options.name: max_port
options.description: Set maximum local UDP port. Default value is 65000.

options.name: buffer_size
options.description: Set the maximum socket buffer size in bytes.

options.name: pkt_size
options.description: Set max send packet size (in bytes). Default value is 1472.

options.name: initial_pause
options.description:
Do not start playing the stream immediately if set to 1. Default value
is 0.

options.name: rtsp_transport
options.description:
Set RTSP transport protocols.
It accepts the following values:
Multiple lower transport protocols may be specified, in that case they are
tried one at a time (if the setup of one fails, the next one is tried).
For the muxer, only the ‘ tcp ’ and ‘ udp ’ options are supported.

possible_values.description: Use UDP as lower transport protocol.

Use TCP (interleaving within the RTSP control channel) as lower
transport protocol.
Use UDP multicast as lower transport protocol.

Use HTTP tunneling as lower transport protocol, which is useful for
passing proxies.

Use HTTPs tunneling as lower transport protocol, which is useful for
passing proxies and widely used for security consideration.

options.name: rtsp_flags
options.description:
Set RTSP flags.
The following values are accepted:
Default value is ‘ none ’.

possible_values.description: Accept packets only from negotiated peer address and port.
Act as a server, listening for an incoming connection.
Try TCP for RTP transport first, if TCP is available as RTSP RTP transport.

Export raw MPEG-TS stream instead of demuxing. The flag will simply write out
the raw stream, with the original PAT/PMT/PIDs intact.

options.name: allowed_media_types
options.description:
Set media types to accept from the server.
The following flags are accepted:
By default it accepts all media types.

options.name: min_port
options.description: Set minimum local UDP port. Default value is 5000.

options.name: max_port
options.description: Set maximum local UDP port. Default value is 65000.

options.name: listen_timeout
options.description:
Set maximum timeout (in seconds) to establish an initial connection. Setting listen_timeout > 0 sets rtsp_flags to ‘ listen ’. Default is -1
which means an infinite timeout when ‘ listen ’ mode is set.

options.name: reorder_queue_size
options.description: Set number of packets to buffer for handling of reordered packets.

options.name: timeout
options.description: Set socket TCP I/O timeout in microseconds.

options.name: user_agent
options.description:
Override User-Agent header. If not specified, it defaults to the
libavformat identifier string.

options.name: buffer_size
options.description: Set the maximum socket buffer size in bytes.

options.name: announce_addr= address
options.description:
Specify the destination IP address for sending the announcements to.
If omitted, the announcements are sent to the commonly used SAP
announcement multicast address 224.2.127.254 (sap.mcast.net), or
ff0e::2:7ffe if destination is an IPv6 address.

options.name: announce_port= port
options.description:
Specify the port to send the announcements on, defaults to
9875 if not specified.

options.name: ttl= ttl
options.description:
Specify the time to live value for the announcements and RTP packets,
defaults to 255.

options.name: same_port= 0|1
options.description:
If set to 1, send all RTP streams on the same port pair. If zero (the
default), all streams are sent on unique ports, with each stream on a
port 2 numbers higher than the previous.
VLC/Live555 requires this to be set to 1, to be able to receive the stream.
The RTP stack in libavformat for receiving requires all streams to be sent
on unique ports.

options.name: listen
options.description: If set to any value, listen for an incoming connection. Outgoing connection is done by default.

options.name: max_streams
options.description: Set the maximum number of streams. By default no limit is set.

options.name: connect_timeout= milliseconds
options.description:
Connection timeout; SRT cannot connect for RTT > 1500 msec
(2 handshake exchanges) with the default connect timeout of
3 seconds. This option applies to the caller and rendezvous
connection modes. The connect timeout is 10 times the value
set for the rendezvous mode (which can be used as a
workaround for this connection problem with earlier versions).

options.name: ffs= bytes
options.description:
Flight Flag Size (Window Size), in bytes. FFS is actually an
internal parameter and you should set it to not less than recv_buffer_size and mss . The default value
is relatively large, therefore unless you set a very large receiver buffer,
you do not need to change this option. Default value is 25600.
options.default: relatively large, therefore unless you set a very large receiver buffer,

options.name: inputbw= bytes/seconds
options.description:
Sender nominal input rate, in bytes per seconds. Used along with oheadbw , when maxbw is set to relative (0), to
calculate maximum sending rate when recovery packets are sent
along with the main media stream: inputbw * (100 + oheadbw ) / 100
if inputbw is not set while maxbw is set to
relative (0), the actual input rate is evaluated inside
the library. Default value is 0.

options.name: iptos= tos
options.description: IP Type of Service. Applies to sender only. Default value is 0xB8.

options.name: ipttl= ttl
options.description: IP Time To Live. Applies to sender only. Default value is 64.

options.name: latency= microseconds
options.description:
Timestamp-based Packet Delivery Delay.
Used to absorb bursts of missed packet retransmissions.
This flag sets both rcvlatency and peerlatency to the same value. Note that prior to version 1.3.0
this is the only flag to set the latency, however
this is effectively equivalent to setting peerlatency ,
when side is sender and rcvlatency when side is receiver, and the bidirectional stream
sending is not supported.

options.name: listen_timeout= microseconds
options.description: Set socket listen timeout.

options.name: maxbw= bytes/seconds
options.description:
Maximum sending bandwidth, in bytes per seconds.
-1 infinite (CSRTCC limit is 30mbps)
0 relative to input rate (see inputbw )
>0 absolute limit value
Default value is 0 (relative)

options.name: mode= caller|listener|rendezvous
options.description:
Connection mode. caller opens client connection. listener starts server to listen for incoming connections. rendezvous use Rendez-Vous connection mode.
Default value is caller.

options.name: mss= bytes
options.description:
Maximum Segment Size, in bytes. Used for buffer allocation
and rate calculation using a packet counter assuming fully
filled packets. The smallest MSS between the peers is
used. This is 1500 by default in the overall internet.
This is the maximum size of the UDP packet and can be
only decreased, unless you have some unusual dedicated
network settings. Default value is 1500.

options.name: nakreport= 1|0
options.description:
If set to 1, Receiver will send ‘UMSG_LOSSREPORT‘ messages
periodically until a lost packet is retransmitted or
intentionally dropped. Default value is 1.

options.name: oheadbw= percents
options.description:
Recovery bandwidth overhead above input rate, in percents.
See inputbw . Default value is 25%.

options.name: passphrase=
options.description:
HaiCrypt Encryption/Decryption Passphrase string, length
from 10 to 79 characters. The passphrase is the shared
secret between the sender and the receiver. It is used
to generate the Key Encrypting Key using PBKDF2
(Password-Based Key Derivation Function). It is used
only if pbkeylen is non-zero. It is used on
the receiver only if the received data is encrypted.
The configured passphrase cannot be recovered (write-only).

options.name: enforced_encryption= 1|0
options.description:
If true, both connection parties must have the same password
set (including empty, that is, with no encryption). If the
password doesn’t match or only one side is unencrypted,
the connection is rejected. Default is true.

options.name: kmrefreshrate= packets
options.description:
The number of packets to be transmitted after which the
encryption key is switched to a new key. Default is -1.
-1 means auto (0x1000000 in srt library). The range for
this option is integers in the 0 - INT_MAX .

options.name: kmpreannounce= packets
options.description:
The interval between when a new encryption key is sent and
when switchover occurs. This value also applies to the
subsequent interval between when switchover occurs and
when the old encryption key is decommissioned. Default is -1.
-1 means auto (0x1000 in srt library). The range for
this option is integers in the 0 - INT_MAX .

options.name: snddropdelay= microseconds
options.description:
The sender’s extra delay before dropping packets. This delay is
added to the default drop delay time interval value.
Special value -1: Do not drop packets on the sender at all.

options.name: payload_size= bytes
options.description:
Sets the maximum declared size of a packet transferred
during the single call to the sending function in Live
mode. Use 0 if this value isn’t used (which is default in
file mode).
Default is -1 (automatic), which typically means MPEG-TS;
if you are going to use SRT
to send any different kind of payload, such as, for example,
wrapping a live stream in very small frames, then you can
use a bigger maximum frame size, though not greater than
1456 bytes.

options.name: pkt_size= bytes
options.description: Alias for ‘ payload_size ’.

options.name: peerlatency= microseconds
options.description:
The latency value (as described in rcvlatency ) that is
set by the sender side as a minimum value for the receiver.

options.name: pbkeylen= bytes
options.description:
Sender encryption key length, in bytes.
Only can be set to 0, 16, 24 and 32.
Enable sender encryption if not 0.
Not required on receiver (set to 0),
key size obtained from sender in HaiCrypt handshake.
Default value is 0.

options.name: rcvlatency= microseconds
options.description:
The time that should elapse since the moment when the
packet was sent and the moment when it’s delivered to
the receiver application in the receiving function.
This time should be a buffer time large enough to cover
the time spent for sending, unexpectedly extended RTT
time, and the time needed to retransmit the lost UDP
packet. The effective latency value will be the maximum
of this options’ value and the value of peerlatency set by the peer side. Before version 1.3.0 this option
is only available as latency .

options.name: recv_buffer_size= bytes
options.description: Set UDP receive buffer size, expressed in bytes.

options.name: send_buffer_size= bytes
options.description: Set UDP send buffer size, expressed in bytes.

options.name: timeout= microseconds
options.description:
Set raise error timeouts for read, write and connect operations. Note that the
SRT library has internal timeouts which can be controlled separately, the
value set here is only a cap on those.

options.name: tlpktdrop= 1|0
options.description:
Too-late Packet Drop. When enabled on receiver, it skips
missing packets that have not been delivered in time and
delivers the following packets to the application when
their time-to-play has come. It also sends a fake ACK to
the sender. When enabled on sender and enabled on the
receiving peer, the sender drops the older packets that
have no chance of being delivered in time. It was
automatically enabled in the sender if the receiver
supports it.

options.name: sndbuf= bytes
options.description: Set send buffer size, expressed in bytes.

options.name: rcvbuf= bytes
options.description:
Set receive buffer size, expressed in bytes.
Receive buffer must not be greater than ffs .

options.name: lossmaxttl= packets
options.description:
The value up to which the Reorder Tolerance may grow. When
Reorder Tolerance is > 0, then packet loss report is delayed
until that number of packets come in. Reorder Tolerance
increases every time a "belated" packet has come, but it
wasn’t due to retransmission (that is, when UDP packets tend
to come out of order), with the difference between the latest
sequence and this packet’s sequence, and not more than the
value of this option. By default it’s 0, which means that this
mechanism is turned off, and the loss report is always sent
immediately upon experiencing a "gap" in sequences.

options.name: minversion
options.description:
The minimum SRT version that is required from the peer. A connection
to a peer that does not satisfy the minimum version requirement
will be rejected.
The version format in hex is 0xXXYYZZ for x.y.z in human readable
form.

options.name: streamid=
options.description:
A string limited to 512 characters that can be set on the socket prior
to connecting. This stream ID will be able to be retrieved by the
listener side from the socket that is returned from srt_accept and
was connected by a socket with that set stream ID. SRT does not enforce
any special interpretation of the contents of this string.
This option doesn’t make sense in Rendezvous connection; the result
might be that simply one side will override the value from the other
side and it’s the matter of luck which one would win

options.name: srt_streamid=
options.description: Alias for ‘ streamid ’ to avoid conflict with ffmpeg command line option.

options.name: smoother= live|file
options.description:
The type of Smoother used for the transmission for that socket, which
is responsible for the transmission and congestion control. The Smoother
type must be exactly the same on both connecting parties, otherwise
the connection is rejected.

options.name: messageapi= 1|0
options.description:
When set, this socket uses the Message API, otherwise it uses Buffer
API. Note that in live mode (see transtype ) there’s only
message API available. In File mode you can chose to use one of two modes:
Stream API (default, when this option is false). In this mode you may
send as many data as you wish with one sending instruction, or even use
dedicated functions that read directly from a file. The internal facility
will take care of any speed and congestion control. When receiving, you
can also receive as many data as desired, the data not extracted will be
waiting for the next call. There is no boundary between data portions in
the Stream mode.
Message API. In this mode your single sending instruction passes exactly
one piece of data that has boundaries (a message). Contrary to Live mode,
this message may span across multiple UDP packets and the only size
limitation is that it shall fit as a whole in the sending buffer. The
receiver shall use as large buffer as necessary to receive the message,
otherwise the message will not be given up. When the message is not
complete (not all packets received or there was a packet loss) it will
not be given up.

options.name: transtype= live|file
options.description:
Sets the transmission type for the socket, in particular, setting this
option sets multiple other parameters to their default values as required
for a particular transmission type.
live: Set options as for live transmission. In this mode, you should
send by one sending instruction only so many data that fit in one UDP packet,
and limited to the value defined first in payload_size (1316 is
default in this mode). There is no speed control in this mode, only the
bandwidth control, if configured, in order to not exceed the bandwidth with
the overhead transmission (retransmitted and control packets).
file: Set options as for non-live transmission. See messageapi for further explanations

options.name: linger= seconds
options.description:
The number of seconds that the socket waits for unsent data when closing.
Default is -1. -1 means auto (off with 0 seconds in live mode, on with 180
seconds in file mode). The range for this option is integers in the
0 - INT_MAX .

options.name: tsbpd= 1|0
options.description:
When true, use Timestamp-based Packet Delivery mode. The default behavior
depends on the transmission type: enabled in live mode, disabled in file
mode.

options.name: ipv6only= 1|0
options.description:
Accept IPv4 or not while using the IPv6 wildcard address. This should be set
when listening on a IPv6 wildcard address.

options.name: srtp_in_suite
options.description:
Select input and output encoding suites.
Supported values:

options.name: srtp_in_params
options.description:
Set input and output encoding parameters, which are expressed by a
base64-encoded representation of a binary block. The first 16 bytes of
this binary block are used as master key, the following 14 bytes are
used as master salt.

options.name: start
options.description: Start offset of the extracted segment, in bytes.

options.name: end
options.description:
End offset of the extracted segment, in bytes.
If set to 0, extract till end of file.

options.name: listen= 2|1|0
options.description:
Listen for an incoming connection. 0 disables listen, 1 enables listen in
single client mode, 2 enables listen in multi-client mode. Default value is 0.

options.name: local_addr= addr
options.description: Local IP address of a network interface used for tcp socket connect.

options.name: local_port= port
options.description: Local port used for tcp socket connect.

options.name: timeout= microseconds
options.description:
Set raise error timeout, expressed in microseconds.
This option is only relevant in read mode: if no data arrived in more
than this time interval, raise error.

options.name: listen_timeout= milliseconds
options.description: Set listen timeout, expressed in milliseconds.

options.name: recv_buffer_size= bytes
options.description: Set receive buffer size, expressed bytes.

options.name: send_buffer_size= bytes
options.description: Set send buffer size, expressed bytes.

options.name: tcp_nodelay= 1|0
options.description:
Set TCP_NODELAY to disable Nagle’s algorithm. Default value is 0.
Remark: Writing to the socket is currently not optimized to minimize system calls and reduces the efficiency / effect of TCP_NODELAY.

options.name: tcp_mss= bytes
options.description: Set maximum segment size for outgoing TCP packets, expressed in bytes.

options.name: ca_file
options.description:
A file containing certificate authority (CA) root certificates to treat
as trusted. If the linked TLS library contains a default this might not
need to be specified for verification to work, but not all libraries and
setups have defaults built in.
The file must be in OpenSSL PEM format.

options.name: tls_verify
options.description:
If enabled, try to verify the peer that we are communicating with.
Note, if using OpenSSL, this currently only makes sure that the
peer certificate is signed by one of the root certificates in the CA
database, but it does not validate that the certificate actually
matches the host name we are trying to connect to. (With other backends,
the host name is validated as well.)
This is disabled by default since it requires a CA database to be
provided by the caller in many cases.

options.name: cert_file
options.description:
A file containing a certificate to use in the handshake with the peer.
(When operating as server, in listen mode, this is more often required
by the peer, while client certificates only are mandated in certain
setups.)

options.name: key_file
options.description: A file containing the private key for the certificate.

options.name: listen= 1|0
options.description:
If enabled, listen for connections on the provided port, and assume
the server role in the handshake instead of the client role.

options.name: http_proxy
options.description:
The HTTP proxy to tunnel through, e.g. http://rabzelj.com:1234 .
The proxy must support the CONNECT method.

options.name: ca_file
options.description:
A file containing certificate authority (CA) root certificates to treat
as trusted. If the linked TLS library contains a default this might not
need to be specified for verification to work, but not all libraries and
setups have defaults built in.
The file must be in OpenSSL PEM format.

options.name: tls_verify
options.description:
If enabled, try to verify the peer that we are communicating with.
Note, if using OpenSSL, this currently only makes sure that the
peer certificate is signed by one of the root certificates in the CA
database, but it does not validate that the certificate actually
matches the host name we are trying to connect to.
This is disabled by default since it requires a CA database to be
provided by the caller in many cases.

options.name: cert_file
options.description:
A file containing a certificate to use in the handshake with the peer.
(When operating as server, in listen mode, this is more often required
by the peer, while client certificates only are mandated in certain
setups.)

options.name: key_file
options.description: A file containing the private key for the certificate.

options.name: cert_pem=
options.description: Certificate PEM string

options.name: key_pem=
options.description: Private key PEM string

options.name: listen= 1|0
options.description:
If enabled, listen for connections on the provided port, and assume
the server role in the handshake instead of the client role.

options.name: mtu= size
options.description: Set the Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) for DTLS packets.

options.name: use_srtp= 1|0
options.description:
Enable the use_srtp DTLS extension.
This is used in WebRTC applications to establish SRTP encryption keys
through the DTLS handshake. Default is disabled.

options.name: external_sock= 1|0
options.description:
Use an external socket instead of creating a new one.
This option only makes sense to pass when interacting with the code via
API, enabling this from CLI will cause immediate failure.
Default is disabled.

options.name: buffer_size= size
options.description:
Set the UDP maximum socket buffer size in bytes. This is used to set either
the receive or send buffer size, depending on what the socket is used for.
Default is 32 KB for output, 384 KB for input.  See also fifo_size .
options.default: 32 KB for output, 384 KB for input

options.name: bitrate= bitrate
options.description:
If set to nonzero, the output will have the specified constant bitrate if the
input has enough packets to sustain it.

options.name: burst_bits= bits
options.description:
When using bitrate this specifies the maximum number of bits in
packet bursts.

options.name: localport= port
options.description: Override the local UDP port to bind with.

options.name: localaddr= addr
options.description:
Local IP address of a network interface used for sending packets or joining
multicast groups.

options.name: pkt_size= size
options.description: Set the size in bytes of UDP packets.

options.name: reuse= 1|0
options.description: Explicitly allow or disallow reusing UDP sockets.

options.name: ttl= ttl
options.description: Set the time to live value (for multicast only).

options.name: dscp= dscp
options.description: Set the 6-bit DSCP field for outgoing packets.

options.name: connect= 1|0
options.description:
Initialize the UDP socket with connect() . In this case, the
destination address can’t be changed with ff_udp_set_remote_url later.
If the destination address isn’t known at the start, this option can
be specified in ff_udp_set_remote_url, too.
This allows finding out the source address for the packets with getsockname,
and makes writes return with AVERROR(ECONNREFUSED) if "destination
unreachable" is received.
For receiving, this gives the benefit of only receiving packets from
the specified peer address/port.

options.name: sources= address
options.description:
Only receive packets sent from the specified addresses. In case of multicast,
also subscribe to multicast traffic coming from these addresses only.

options.name: block= address
options.description:
Ignore packets sent from the specified addresses. In case of multicast, also
exclude the source addresses in the multicast subscription.

options.name: fifo_size= units
options.description:
Set the UDP receiving circular buffer size, expressed as a number of
packets with size of 188 bytes. If not specified defaults to 7*4096.

options.name: overrun_nonfatal= 1|0
options.description:
Survive in case of UDP receiving circular buffer overrun. Default
value is 0.

options.name: timeout= microseconds
options.description:
Set raise error timeout, expressed in microseconds.
This option is only relevant in read mode: if no data arrived in more
than this time interval, raise error.

options.name: broadcast= 1|0
options.description:
Explicitly allow or disallow UDP broadcasting.
Note that broadcasting may not work properly on networks having
a broadcast storm protection.

options.name: timeout
options.description: Timeout in ms.

options.name: listen
options.description: Create the Unix socket in listening mode.

options.name: type
options.description: Select socket type.

options.name: pkt_size
options.description:
Maximum packet size for packet-oriented sockets (SOCK_DGRAM and
SOCK_SEQPACKET). If greater than zero, this value is used as max_packet_size . Ignored for SOCK_STREAM. Default is 0 .

options.name: pkt_size
options.description:
Forces the maximum packet size for sending/receiving data. The default value is
131,072 bytes. On the server side, this sets the maximum size of sent packets
via ZeroMQ. On the clients, it sets an internal buffer size for receiving
packets. Note that pkt_size on the clients should be equal to or greater than
pkt_size on the server. Otherwise the received message may be truncated causing
decoding errors.

options.name: sample_rate
options.description: Set the sample rate in Hz. Default is 48000.

options.name: channels
options.description: Set the number of channels. Default is 2.

options.name: video_size
options.description:
Set the video size given as a string such as 640x480 or hd720.
Falls back to the first available configuration reported by
Android if requested video size is not available or by default.

options.name: framerate
options.description:
Set the video framerate.
Falls back to the first available configuration reported by
Android if requested framerate is not available or by default (-1).

options.name: camera_index
options.description: Set the index of the camera to use. Default is 0.

options.name: input_queue_size
options.description: Set the maximum number of frames to buffer. Default is 5.

options.name: -list_devices <TRUE|FALSE>
options.description:
If set to true, a list of all available input devices is given showing all
device names and indices.

options.name: -video_device_index <INDEX>
options.description: Specify the video device by its index. Overrides anything given in the input filename.

options.name: -audio_device_index <INDEX>
options.description: Specify the audio device by its index. Overrides anything given in the input filename.

options.name: -pixel_format <FORMAT>
options.description:
Request the video device to use a specific pixel format.
If the specified format is not supported, a list of available formats is given
and the first one in this list is used instead. Available pixel formats are: monob, rgb555be, rgb555le, rgb565be, rgb565le, rgb24, bgr24, 0rgb, bgr0, 0bgr, rgb0,
 bgr48be, uyvy422, yuva444p, yuva444p16le, yuv444p, yuv422p16, yuv422p10, yuv444p10,
 yuv420p, nv12, yuyv422, gray

options.name: -framerate
options.description:
Set the grabbing frame rate. Default is ntsc , corresponding to a
frame rate of 30000/1001 .

options.name: -video_size
options.description: Set the video frame size.

options.name: -capture_cursor
options.description: Capture the mouse pointer. Default is 0.

options.name: -capture_mouse_clicks
options.description: Capture the screen mouse clicks. Default is 0.

options.name: -capture_raw_data
options.description:
Capture the raw device data. Default is 0.
Using this option may result in receiving the underlying data delivered to the AVFoundation framework. E.g. for muxed devices that sends raw DV data to the framework (like tape-based camcorders), setting this option to false results in extracted video frames captured in the designated pixel format only. Setting this option to true results in receiving the raw DV stream untouched.

options.name: list_devices
options.description:
If set to true , print a list of devices and exit.
Defaults to false . This option is deprecated, please use the -sources option of ffmpeg to list the available input devices.

options.name: list_formats
options.description:
If set to true , print a list of supported formats and exit.
Defaults to false .

options.name: format_code <FourCC>
options.description:
This sets the input video format to the format given by the FourCC. To see
the supported values of your device(s) use list_formats .
Note that there is a FourCC 'pal ' that can also be used
as pal (3 letters).
Default behavior is autodetection of the input video format, if the hardware
supports it.

options.name: raw_format
options.description:
Set the pixel format of the captured video.
Available values are:

possible_values.description:
This is the default which means 8-bit YUV 422 or 8-bit ARGB if format
autodetection is used, 8-bit YUV 422 otherwise.

options.name: teletext_lines
options.description:
If set to nonzero, an additional teletext stream will be captured from the
vertical ancillary data. Both SD PAL (576i) and HD (1080i or 1080p)
sources are supported. In case of HD sources, OP47 packets are decoded.
This option is a bitmask of the SD PAL VBI lines captured, specifically lines 6
to 22, and lines 318 to 335. Line 6 is the LSB in the mask. Selected lines
which do not contain teletext information will be ignored. You can use the
special all constant to select all possible lines, or standard to skip lines 6, 318 and 319, which are not compatible with
all receivers.
For SD sources, ffmpeg needs to be compiled with --enable-libzvbi . For
HD sources, on older (pre-4K) DeckLink card models you have to capture in 10
bit mode.

options.name: channels
options.description:
Defines number of audio channels to capture. Must be ‘ 2 ’, ‘ 8 ’ or ‘ 16 ’.
Defaults to ‘ 2 ’.

options.name: duplex_mode
options.description:
Sets the decklink device duplex/profile mode. Must be ‘ unset ’, ‘ half ’, ‘ full ’,
‘ one_sub_device_full ’, ‘ one_sub_device_half ’, ‘ two_sub_device_full ’,
‘ four_sub_device_half ’
Defaults to ‘ unset ’.
Note: DeckLink SDK 11.0 have replaced the duplex property by a profile property.
For the DeckLink Duo 2 and DeckLink Quad 2, a profile is shared between any 2
sub-devices that utilize the same connectors. For the DeckLink 8K Pro, a profile
is shared between all 4 sub-devices. So DeckLink 8K Pro support four profiles.
Valid profile modes for DeckLink 8K Pro(with DeckLink SDK >= 11.0):
‘ one_sub_device_full ’, ‘ one_sub_device_half ’, ‘ two_sub_device_full ’,
‘ four_sub_device_half ’
Valid profile modes for DeckLink Quad 2 and DeckLink Duo 2:
‘ half ’, ‘ full ’

options.name: timecode_format
options.description:
Timecode type to include in the frame and video stream metadata. Must be
‘ none ’, ‘ rp188vitc ’, ‘ rp188vitc2 ’, ‘ rp188ltc ’,
‘ rp188hfr ’, ‘ rp188any ’, ‘ vitc ’, ‘ vitc2 ’, or ‘ serial ’.
Defaults to ‘ none ’ (not included).
In order to properly support 50/60 fps timecodes, the ordering of the queried
timecode types for ‘ rp188any ’ is HFR, VITC1, VITC2 and LTC for >30 fps
content. Note that this is slightly different to the ordering used by the
DeckLink API, which is HFR, VITC1, LTC, VITC2.

options.name: video_input
options.description:
Sets the video input source. Must be ‘ unset ’, ‘ sdi ’, ‘ hdmi ’,
‘ optical_sdi ’, ‘ component ’, ‘ composite ’ or ‘ s_video ’.
Defaults to ‘ unset ’.

options.name: audio_input
options.description:
Sets the audio input source. Must be ‘ unset ’, ‘ embedded ’,
‘ aes_ebu ’, ‘ analog ’, ‘ analog_xlr ’, ‘ analog_rca ’ or
‘ microphone ’. Defaults to ‘ unset ’.

options.name: video_pts
options.description:
Sets the video packet timestamp source. Must be ‘ video ’, ‘ audio ’,
‘ reference ’, ‘ wallclock ’ or ‘ abs_wallclock ’.
Defaults to ‘ video ’.

options.name: audio_pts
options.description:
Sets the audio packet timestamp source. Must be ‘ video ’, ‘ audio ’,
‘ reference ’, ‘ wallclock ’ or ‘ abs_wallclock ’.
Defaults to ‘ audio ’.

options.name: draw_bars
options.description:
If set to ‘ true ’, color bars are drawn in the event of a signal loss.
Defaults to ‘ true ’.
This option is deprecated, please use the signal_loss_action option.

options.name: signal_loss_action
options.description:
Sets the action to take in the event of a signal loss. Accepts one of the
following values:
Defaults to ‘ bars ’.

possible_values.description: Do nothing on signal loss. This usually results in black frames.
Draw color bars on signal loss. Only supported for 8-bit input signals.
Repeat the last video frame on signal loss.

options.name: queue_size
options.description:
Sets maximum input buffer size in bytes. If the buffering reaches this value,
incoming frames will be dropped.
Defaults to ‘ 1073741824 ’.

options.name: audio_depth
options.description:
Sets the audio sample bit depth. Must be ‘ 16 ’ or ‘ 32 ’.
Defaults to ‘ 16 ’.

options.name: decklink_copyts
options.description:
If set to true , timestamps are forwarded as they are without removing
the initial offset.
Defaults to false .

options.name: timestamp_align
options.description:
Capture start time alignment in seconds. If set to nonzero, input frames are
dropped till the system timestamp aligns with configured value.
Alignment difference of up to one frame duration is tolerated.
This is useful for maintaining input synchronization across N different
hardware devices deployed for ’N-way’ redundancy. The system time of different
hardware devices should be synchronized with protocols such as NTP or PTP,
before using this option.
Note that this method is not foolproof. In some border cases input
synchronization may not happen due to thread scheduling jitters in the OS.
Either sync could go wrong by 1 frame or in a rarer case timestamp_align seconds.
Defaults to ‘ 0 ’.

options.name: wait_for_tc
options.description:
Drop frames till a frame with timecode is received. Sometimes serial timecode
isn’t received with the first input frame. If that happens, the stored stream
timecode will be inaccurate. If this option is set to true , input frames
are dropped till a frame with timecode is received.
Option timecode_format must be specified.
Defaults to false .

options.name: enable_klv
options.description:
If set to true , extracts KLV data from VANC and outputs KLV packets.
KLV VANC packets are joined based on MID and PSC fields and aggregated into
one KLV packet.
Defaults to false .

options.name: video_size
options.description: Set the video size in the captured video.

options.name: framerate
options.description: Set the frame rate in the captured video.

options.name: sample_rate
options.description: Set the sample rate (in Hz) of the captured audio.

options.name: sample_size
options.description: Set the sample size (in bits) of the captured audio.

options.name: channels
options.description: Set the number of channels in the captured audio.

options.name: list_devices
options.description: If set to true , print a list of devices and exit.

options.name: list_options
options.description:
If set to true , print a list of selected device’s options
and exit.

options.name: video_device_number
options.description:
Set video device number for devices with the same name (starts at 0,
defaults to 0).

options.name: audio_device_number
options.description:
Set audio device number for devices with the same name (starts at 0,
defaults to 0).

options.name: pixel_format
options.description:
Select pixel format to be used by DirectShow. This may only be set when
the video codec is not set or set to rawvideo.

options.name: audio_buffer_size
options.description:
Set audio device buffer size in milliseconds (which can directly
impact latency, depending on the device).
Defaults to using the audio device’s
default buffer size (typically some multiple of 500ms).
Setting this value too low can degrade performance.
See also http://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/dd377582(v=vs.85).aspx

options.name: video_pin_name
options.description: Select video capture pin to use by name or alternative name.

options.name: audio_pin_name
options.description: Select audio capture pin to use by name or alternative name.

options.name: crossbar_video_input_pin_number
options.description:
Select video input pin number for crossbar device. This will be
routed to the crossbar device’s Video Decoder output pin.
Note that changing this value can affect future invocations
(sets a new default) until system reboot occurs.

options.name: crossbar_audio_input_pin_number
options.description:
Select audio input pin number for crossbar device. This will be
routed to the crossbar device’s Audio Decoder output pin.
Note that changing this value can affect future invocations
(sets a new default) until system reboot occurs.

options.name: show_video_device_dialog
options.description:
If set to true , before capture starts, popup a display dialog
to the end user, allowing them to change video filter properties
and configurations manually.
Note that for crossbar devices, adjusting values in this dialog
may be needed at times to toggle between PAL (25 fps) and NTSC (29.97)
input frame rates, sizes, interlacing, etc.  Changing these values can
enable different scan rates/frame rates and avoiding green bars at
the bottom, flickering scan lines, etc.
Note that with some devices, changing these properties can also affect future
invocations (sets new defaults) until system reboot occurs.

options.name: show_audio_device_dialog
options.description:
If set to true , before capture starts, popup a display dialog
to the end user, allowing them to change audio filter properties
and configurations manually.

options.name: show_video_crossbar_connection_dialog
options.description:
If set to true , before capture starts, popup a display
dialog to the end user, allowing them to manually
modify crossbar pin routings, when it opens a video device.

options.name: show_audio_crossbar_connection_dialog
options.description:
If set to true , before capture starts, popup a display
dialog to the end user, allowing them to manually
modify crossbar pin routings, when it opens an audio device.

options.name: show_analog_tv_tuner_dialog
options.description:
If set to true , before capture starts, popup a display
dialog to the end user, allowing them to manually
modify TV channels and frequencies.

options.name: show_analog_tv_tuner_audio_dialog
options.description:
If set to true , before capture starts, popup a display
dialog to the end user, allowing them to manually
modify TV audio (like mono vs. stereo, Language A,B or C).

options.name: audio_device_load
options.description:
Load an audio capture filter device from file instead of searching
it by name. It may load additional parameters too, if the filter
supports the serialization of its properties to.
To use this an audio capture source has to be specified, but it can
be anything even fake one.

options.name: audio_device_save
options.description:
Save the currently used audio capture filter device and its
parameters (if the filter supports it) to a file.
If a file with the same name exists it will be overwritten.

options.name: video_device_load
options.description:
Load a video capture filter device from file instead of searching
it by name. It may load additional parameters too, if the filter
supports the serialization of its properties to.
To use this a video capture source has to be specified, but it can
be anything even fake one.

options.name: video_device_save
options.description:
Save the currently used video capture filter device and its
parameters (if the filter supports it) to a file.
If a file with the same name exists it will be overwritten.

options.name: use_video_device_timestamps
options.description:
If set to false , the timestamp for video frames will be
derived from the wallclock instead of the timestamp provided by
the capture device. This allows working around devices that
provide unreliable timestamps.

options.name: framerate
options.description: Set the frame rate. Default is 25.

options.name: draw_mouse
options.description:
Specify whether to draw the mouse pointer. Use the value 0 to
not draw the pointer. Default value is 1 .

options.name: framerate
options.description:
Set the grabbing frame rate. Default value is ntsc ,
corresponding to a frame rate of 30000/1001 .

options.name: show_region
options.description:
Show grabbed region on screen.
If show_region is specified with 1 , then the grabbing
region will be indicated on screen. With this option, it is easy to
know what is being grabbed if only a portion of the screen is grabbed.
Note that show_region is incompatible with grabbing the contents
of a single window.
For example:

options.name: video_size
options.description: Set the video frame size. The default is to capture the full screen if desktop is selected, or the full window size if title= window_title is selected.
options.default: to capture the full screen if desktop is selected, or the full window size if title= window_title is selected

options.name: offset_x
options.description:
When capturing a region with video_size , set the distance from the left edge of the screen or desktop.
Note that the offset calculation is from the top left corner of the primary monitor on Windows. If you have a monitor positioned to the left of your primary monitor, you will need to use a negative offset_x value to move the region to that monitor.

options.name: offset_y
options.description:
When capturing a region with video_size , set the distance from the top edge of the screen or desktop.
Note that the offset calculation is from the top left corner of the primary monitor on Windows. If you have a monitor positioned above your primary monitor, you will need to use a negative offset_y value to move the region to that monitor.

options.name: dvtype
options.description:
Override autodetection of DV/HDV. This should only be used if auto
detection does not work, or if usage of a different device type
should be prohibited. Treating a DV device as HDV (or vice versa) will
not work and result in undefined behavior.
The values auto , dv and hdv are supported.

options.name: dvbuffer
options.description:
Set maximum size of buffer for incoming data, in frames. For DV, this
is an exact value. For HDV, it is not frame exact, since HDV does
not have a fixed frame size.

options.name: dvguid
options.description:
Select the capture device by specifying its GUID. Capturing will only
be performed from the specified device and fails if no device with the
given GUID is found. This is useful to select the input if multiple
devices are connected at the same time.
Look at /sys/bus/firewire/devices to find out the GUIDs.

options.name: channels
options.description: Set the number of channels. Default is 2.

options.name: device
options.description: DRM device to capture on.  Defaults to /dev/dri/card0 .

options.name: format
options.description:
Pixel format of the framebuffer.  This can be autodetected if you are running Linux 5.7
or later, but needs to be provided for earlier versions.  Defaults to bgr0 ,
which is the most common format used by the Linux console and Xorg X server.

options.name: format_modifier
options.description:
Format modifier to signal on output frames.  This is necessary to import correctly into
some APIs.  It can be autodetected if you are running Linux 5.7 or later, but will need
to be provided explicitly when needed in earlier versions.  See the libdrm documentation
for possible values.

options.name: crtc_id
options.description:
KMS CRTC ID to define the capture source.  The first active plane on the given CRTC
will be used.

options.name: plane_id
options.description:
KMS plane ID to define the capture source.  Defaults to the first active plane found if
neither crtc_id nor plane_id are specified.

options.name: framerate
options.description:
Framerate to capture at.  This is not synchronised to any page flipping or framebuffer
changes - it just defines the interval at which the framebuffer is sampled.  Sampling
faster than the framebuffer update rate will generate independent frames with the same
content.  Defaults to 30 .

options.name: graph
options.description:
Specify the filtergraph to use as input. Each video open output must be
labelled by a unique string of the form "out N ", where N is a
number starting from 0 corresponding to the mapped input stream
generated by the device.
The first unlabelled output is automatically assigned to the "out0"
label, but all the others need to be specified explicitly.
The suffix "+subcc" can be appended to the output label to create an extra
stream with the closed captions packets attached to that output
(experimental; only for EIA-608 / CEA-708 for now).
The subcc streams are created after all the normal streams, in the order of
the corresponding stream.
For example, if there is "out19+subcc", "out7+subcc" and up to "out42", the
stream #43 is subcc for stream #7 and stream #44 is subcc for stream #19.
If not specified defaults to the filename specified for the input
device.

options.name: graph_file
options.description:
Set the filename of the filtergraph to be read and sent to the other
filters. Syntax of the filtergraph is the same as the one specified by
the option graph .

options.name: dumpgraph
options.description: Dump graph to stderr.

options.name: speed
options.description:
Set drive reading speed. Default value is 0.
The speed is specified CD-ROM speed units. The speed is set through
the libcdio cdio_cddap_speed_set function. On many CD-ROM
drives, specifying a value too large will result in using the fastest
speed.

options.name: paranoia_mode
options.description:
Set paranoia recovery mode flags. It accepts one of the following values:
Default value is ‘ disable ’.
For more information about the available recovery modes, consult the
paranoia project documentation.

options.name: framerate
options.description:
Set the frame rate. Default is ntsc , corresponding to a frame
rate of 30000/1001 .

options.name: pixel_format
options.description: Select the pixel format. Default is uyvy422 .

options.name: video_size
options.description:
Set the video size given as a string such as 640x480 or hd720 .
Default is qvga .

options.name: channels
options.description:
Set the number of channels in the captured audio. Only the values 1 (monaural) and 2 (stereo) are currently supported.
Defaults to 2 .

options.name: sample_size
options.description: Set the sample size (in bits) of the captured audio. Only the values 8 and 16 are currently supported. Defaults to 16 .

options.name: sample_rate
options.description:
Set the sample rate (in Hz) of the captured audio.
Defaults to 44.1k .

options.name: list_devices
options.description:
If set to true , print a list of devices and exit.
Defaults to false .

options.name: sample_rate
options.description: Set the sample rate in Hz. Default is 48000.

options.name: channels
options.description: Set the number of channels. Default is 2.

options.name: server
options.description:
Connect to a specific PulseAudio server, specified by an IP address.
Default server is used when not provided.

options.name: name
options.description:
Specify the application name PulseAudio will use when showing active clients,
by default it is the LIBAVFORMAT_IDENT string.

options.name: stream_name
options.description:
Specify the stream name PulseAudio will use when showing active streams,
by default it is "record".

options.name: sample_rate
options.description: Specify the samplerate in Hz, by default 48kHz is used.

options.name: channels
options.description: Specify the channels in use, by default 2 (stereo) is set.

options.name: frame_size
options.description: This option does nothing and is deprecated.

options.name: fragment_size
options.description:
Specify the size in bytes of the minimal buffering fragment in PulseAudio, it
will affect the audio latency. By default it is set to 50 ms amount of data.

options.name: wallclock
options.description: Set the initial PTS using the current time. Default is 1.

options.name: sample_rate
options.description: Set the sample rate in Hz. Default is 48000.

options.name: channels
options.description: Set the number of channels. Default is 2.

options.name: standard
options.description:
Set the standard. Must be the name of a supported standard. To get a
list of the supported standards, use the list_standards option.

options.name: channel
options.description:
Set the input channel number. Default to -1, which means using the
previously selected channel.

options.name: video_size
options.description: Set the video frame size. The argument must be a string in the form WIDTH x HEIGHT or a valid size abbreviation.

options.name: pixel_format
options.description: Select the pixel format (only valid for raw video input).

options.name: input_format
options.description:
Set the preferred pixel format (for raw video) or a codec name.
This option allows one to select the input format, when several are
available.

options.name: framerate
options.description: Set the preferred video frame rate.

options.name: list_formats
options.description:
List available formats (supported pixel formats, codecs, and frame
sizes) and exit.
Available values are:

possible_values.description: Show all available (compressed and non-compressed) formats.

options.name: list_standards
options.description:
List supported standards and exit.
Available values are:

options.name: timestamps
options.description:
Set type of timestamps for grabbed frames.
Available values are:
Default value is default .

possible_values.description: Force conversion from monotonic to absolute timestamps.

options.name: use_libv4l2
options.description: Use libv4l2 (v4l-utils) conversion functions. Default is 0.

options.name: video_size
options.description: Set the video frame size.

options.name: framerate
options.description:
Set the grabbing frame rate. Default value is ntsc ,
corresponding to a frame rate of 30000/1001 .

options.name: select_region
options.description:
Specify whether to select the grabbing area graphically using the pointer.
A value of 1 prompts the user to select the grabbing area graphically
by clicking and dragging. A single click with no dragging will select the
whole screen. A region with zero width or height will also select the whole
screen. This option overwrites the video_size , grab_x , and grab_y options. Default value is 0 .

options.name: draw_mouse
options.description:
Specify whether to draw the mouse pointer. A value of 0 specifies
not to draw the pointer. Default value is 1 .

options.name: follow_mouse
options.description:
Make the grabbed area follow the mouse. The argument can be centered or a number of pixels PIXELS .
When it is specified with "centered", the grabbing region follows the mouse
pointer and keeps the pointer at the center of region; otherwise, the region
follows only when the mouse pointer reaches within PIXELS (greater than
zero) to the edge of region.
For example:
To follow only when the mouse pointer reaches within 100 pixels to edge:

options.name: framerate
options.description:
Set the grabbing frame rate. Default value is ntsc ,
corresponding to a frame rate of 30000/1001 .

options.name: show_region
options.description:
Show grabbed region on screen.
If show_region is specified with 1 , then the grabbing
region will be indicated on screen. With this option, it is easy to
know what is being grabbed if only a portion of the screen is grabbed.

options.name: region_border
options.description:
Set the region border thickness if -show_region 1 is used.
Range is 1 to 128 and default is 3 (XCB-based x11grab only).
For example:
With follow_mouse :

options.name: window_id
options.description:
Grab this window, instead of the whole screen. Default value is 0, which maps to
the whole screen (root window).
The id of a window can be found using the xwininfo program, possibly with options -tree and
-root.
If the window is later enlarged, the new area is not recorded. Video ends when
the window is closed, unmapped (i.e., iconified) or shrunk beyond the video
size (which defaults to the initial window size).
This option disables options follow_mouse and select_region .

options.name: video_size
options.description: Set the video frame size. Default is the full desktop or window.

options.name: grab_x
options.description:
Set the grabbing region coordinates. They are expressed as offset from
the top left corner of the X11 window and correspond to the x_offset and y_offset parameters in the device name. The
default value for both options is 0.

options.name: -audio_device_index <INDEX>
options.description: Specify the audio device by its index. Overrides anything given in the output filename.

options.name: window_title
options.description:
Set the CACA window title, if not specified default to the filename
specified for the output device.

options.name: window_size
options.description:
Set the CACA window size, can be a string of the form width x height or a video size abbreviation.
If not specified it defaults to the size of the input video.

options.name: driver
options.description: Set display driver.

options.name: algorithm
options.description:
Set dithering algorithm. Dithering is necessary
because the picture being rendered has usually far more colours than
the available palette.
The accepted values are listed with -list_dither algorithms .

options.name: antialias
options.description:
Set antialias method. Antialiasing smoothens the rendered
image and avoids the commonly seen staircase effect.
The accepted values are listed with -list_dither antialiases .

options.name: charset
options.description:
Set which characters are going to be used when rendering text.
The accepted values are listed with -list_dither charsets .

options.name: color
options.description:
Set color to be used when rendering text.
The accepted values are listed with -list_dither colors .

options.name: list_drivers
options.description: If set to true , print a list of available drivers and exit.

options.name: list_dither
options.description:
List available dither options related to the argument.
The argument must be one of algorithms , antialiases , charsets , colors .

options.name: list_devices
options.description:
If set to true , print a list of devices and exit.
Defaults to false . This option is deprecated, please use the -sinks option of ffmpeg to list the available output devices.

options.name: list_formats
options.description:
If set to true , print a list of supported formats and exit.
Defaults to false .

options.name: preroll
options.description:
Amount of time to preroll video in seconds.
Defaults to 0.5 .

options.name: duplex_mode
options.description:
Sets the decklink device duplex/profile mode. Must be ‘ unset ’, ‘ half ’, ‘ full ’,
‘ one_sub_device_full ’, ‘ one_sub_device_half ’, ‘ two_sub_device_full ’,
‘ four_sub_device_half ’
Defaults to ‘ unset ’.
Note: DeckLink SDK 11.0 have replaced the duplex property by a profile property.
For the DeckLink Duo 2 and DeckLink Quad 2, a profile is shared between any 2
sub-devices that utilize the same connectors. For the DeckLink 8K Pro, a profile
is shared between all 4 sub-devices. So DeckLink 8K Pro support four profiles.
Valid profile modes for DeckLink 8K Pro(with DeckLink SDK >= 11.0):
‘ one_sub_device_full ’, ‘ one_sub_device_half ’, ‘ two_sub_device_full ’,
‘ four_sub_device_half ’
Valid profile modes for DeckLink Quad 2 and DeckLink Duo 2:
‘ half ’, ‘ full ’

options.name: timing_offset
options.description:
Sets the genlock timing pixel offset on the used output.
Defaults to ‘ unset ’.

options.name: link
options.description:
Sets the SDI video link configuration on the used output. Must be
‘ unset ’, ‘ single ’ link SDI, ‘ dual ’ link SDI or ‘ quad ’ link
SDI.
Defaults to ‘ unset ’.

options.name: sqd
options.description:
Enable Square Division Quad Split mode for Quad-link SDI output.
Must be ‘ unset ’, ‘ true ’ or ‘ false ’.
Defaults to unset .

options.name: level_a
options.description:
Enable SMPTE Level A mode on the used output.
Must be ‘ unset ’, ‘ true ’ or ‘ false ’.
Defaults to unset .

options.name: vanc_queue_size
options.description:
Sets maximum output buffer size in bytes for VANC data. If the buffering reaches this value,
outgoing VANC data will be dropped.
Defaults to ‘ 1048576 ’.

options.name: xoffset
options.description: Set x/y coordinate of top left corner. Default is 0.

options.name: server
options.description:
Connect to a specific PulseAudio server, specified by an IP address.
Default server is used when not provided.

options.name: name
options.description:
Specify the application name PulseAudio will use when showing active clients,
by default it is the LIBAVFORMAT_IDENT string.

options.name: stream_name
options.description:
Specify the stream name PulseAudio will use when showing active streams,
by default it is set to the specified output name.

options.name: device
options.description:
Specify the device to use. Default device is used when not provided.
List of output devices can be obtained with command pactl list sinks .

options.name: buffer_size
options.description:
Control the size and duration of the PulseAudio buffer. A small buffer
gives more control, but requires more frequent updates.
buffer_size specifies size in bytes while buffer_duration specifies duration in milliseconds.
When both options are provided then the highest value is used
(duration is recalculated to bytes using stream parameters). If they
are set to 0 (which is default), the device will use the default
PulseAudio duration value. By default PulseAudio set buffer duration
to around 2 seconds.

options.name: prebuf
options.description:
Specify pre-buffering size in bytes. The server does not start with
playback before at least prebuf bytes are available in the
buffer. By default this option is initialized to the same value as buffer_size or buffer_duration (whichever is bigger).

options.name: minreq
options.description:
Specify minimum request size in bytes. The server does not request less
than minreq bytes from the client, instead waits until the buffer
is free enough to request more bytes at once. It is recommended to not set
this option, which will initialize this to a value that is deemed sensible
by the server.

options.name: display_name
options.description:
Specify the hardware display name, which determines the display and
communications domain to be used.
The display name or DISPLAY environment variable can be a string in
the format hostname [: number [. screen_number ]].
hostname specifies the name of the host machine on which the
display is physically attached. number specifies the number of
the display server on that host machine. screen_number specifies
the screen to be used on that server.
If unspecified, it defaults to the value of the DISPLAY environment
variable.
For example, dual-headed:0.1 would specify screen 1 of display
0 on the machine named “dual-headed”.
Check the X11 specification for more detailed information about the
display name format.

options.name: window_id
options.description:
When set to non-zero value then device doesn’t create new window,
but uses existing one with provided window_id . By default
this options is set to zero and device creates its own window.

options.name: window_size
options.description:
Set the created window size, can be a string of the form width x height or a video size abbreviation. If not
specified it defaults to the size of the input video.
Ignored when window_id is set.

options.name: window_x
options.description:
Set the X and Y window offsets for the created window. They are both
set to 0 by default. The values may be ignored by the window manager.
Ignored when window_id is set.

options.name: window_title
options.description:
Set the window title, if not specified default to the filename
specified for the output device. Ignored when window_id is set.

options.name: t
options.description: timestamp expressed in seconds, NAN if the input timestamp is unknown

options.name: n
options.description: sequential number of the input frame, starting from 0

options.name: pos
options.description:
the position in the file of the input frame, NAN if unknown; deprecated, do
not use

options.name: w
options.description: width and height of the input frame if video

options.name: eof_action
options.description:
The action to take when EOF is encountered on the secondary input; it accepts
one of the following values:
options.section: 7 Options for filters with several inputs (framesync)

options.name: shortest
options.description:
If set to 1, force the output to terminate when the shortest input
terminates. Default value is 0.
options.section: 7 Options for filters with several inputs (framesync)

options.name: repeatlast
options.description:
If set to 1, force the filter to extend the last frame of secondary streams
until the end of the primary stream. A value of 0 disables this behavior.
Default value is 1.
options.section: 7 Options for filters with several inputs (framesync)

options.name: ts_sync_mode
options.description:
How strictly to sync streams based on secondary input timestamps; it accepts
one of the following values:

possible_values.description:
Frame from secondary input with the nearest lower or equal timestamp to the
primary input frame.

Frame from secondary input with the absolute nearest timestamp to the primary
input frame.

options.section: 7 Options for filters with several inputs (framesync)

options.name: order
options.description: Set the filter order.

options.name: projection
options.description: Set the projection order.

options.name: mu
options.description: Set the filter mu.

options.name: delta
options.description: Set the coefficient to initialize internal covariance matrix.

options.name: out_mode
options.description: Set the filter output samples. It accepts the following values:

possible_values.description: Pass difference between desired, 2nd input and error signal estimate.
Pass difference between input, 1st input and error signal estimate.

Pass error signal estimated samples.
Default value is o .

options.name: precision
options.description: Set which precision to use when processing samples.

possible_values.description: Auto pick internal sample format depending on other filters.
Always use single-floating point precision sample format.
Always use double-floating point precision sample format.

options.name: level_in
options.description: Set input gain. Default is 1. Range is between 0.015625 and 64.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set mode of compressor operation. Can be upward or downward .
Default is downward .

options.name: threshold
options.description:
If a signal of stream rises above this level it will affect the gain
reduction.
By default it is 0.125. Range is between 0.00097563 and 1.

options.name: ratio
options.description:
Set a ratio by which the signal is reduced. 1:2 means that if the level
rose 4dB above the threshold, it will be only 2dB above after the reduction.
Default is 2. Range is between 1 and 20.

options.name: attack
options.description:
Amount of milliseconds the signal has to rise above the threshold before gain
reduction starts. Default is 20. Range is between 0.01 and 2000.

options.name: release
options.description:
Amount of milliseconds the signal has to fall below the threshold before
reduction is decreased again. Default is 250. Range is between 0.01 and 9000.

options.name: makeup
options.description:
Set the amount by how much signal will be amplified after processing.
Default is 1. Range is from 1 to 64.

options.name: knee
options.description:
Curve the sharp knee around the threshold to enter gain reduction more softly.
Default is 2.82843. Range is between 1 and 8.

options.name: link
options.description:
Choose if the average level between all channels of input stream
or the louder( maximum ) channel of input stream affects the
reduction. Default is average .

options.name: detection
options.description:
Should the exact signal be taken in case of peak or an RMS one in case
of rms . Default is rms which is mostly smoother.

options.name: mix
options.description:
How much to use compressed signal in output. Default is 1.
Range is between 0 and 1.

options.name: contrast
options.description: Set contrast. Default is 33. Allowed range is between 0 and 100.

options.name: inputs
options.description:
Specify the number of inputs to crossfade. When crossfading multiple inputs,
each input will be concatenated and crossfaded in sequence, similar to the concat filter . Default is 2.

options.name: nb_samples
options.description:
Specify the number of samples for which the cross fade effect has to last.
At the end of the cross fade effect the first input audio will be completely
silent. Default is 44100.

options.name: duration
options.description:
Specify the duration of the cross fade effect. See (ffmpeg-utils)the Time duration section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual for the accepted syntax.
By default the duration is determined by nb_samples .
If set this option is used instead of nb_samples .

options.name: overlap
options.description: Should first stream end overlap with second stream start. Default is enabled.

options.name: curve1
options.description: Set curve for cross fade transition for first stream.

options.name: curve2
options.description:
Set curve for cross fade transition for second stream.
For description of available curve types see afade filter description.

options.name: split
options.description: Set split frequencies. Those must be positive and increasing.

options.name: order
options.description:
Set filter order for each band split. This controls filter roll-off or steepness
of filter transfer function.
Available values are:
Default is 4th .

options.name: level
options.description: Set input gain level. Allowed range is from 0 to 1. Default value is 1.

options.name: gains
options.description: Set output gain for each band. Default value is 1 for all bands.

options.name: precision
options.description:
Set which precision to use when processing samples.
Default value is auto .

possible_values.description: Auto pick internal sample format depending on other filters.
Always use single-floating point precision sample format.
Always use double-floating point precision sample format.

options.name: level_in
options.description: Set level in.

options.name: level_out
options.description: Set level out.

options.name: bits
options.description: Set bit reduction.

options.name: mix
options.description: Set mixing amount.

options.name: mode
options.description: Can be linear: lin or logarithmic: log .

options.name: dc
options.description: Set DC.

options.name: aa
options.description: Set anti-aliasing.

options.name: samples
options.description: Set sample reduction.

options.name: lfo
options.description: Enable LFO. By default disabled.

options.name: lforange
options.description: Set LFO range.

options.name: lforate
options.description: Set LFO rate.

options.name: window
options.description:
Set window size, in milliseconds. Allowed range is from 10 to 100 . Default value is 55 milliseconds.
This sets size of window which will be processed at once.

options.name: overlap
options.description:
Set window overlap, in percentage of window size. Allowed range is from 50 to 95 . Default value is 75 percent.
Setting this to a very high value increases impulsive noise removal but makes
whole process much slower.

options.name: arorder
options.description:
Set autoregression order, in percentage of window size. Allowed range is from 0 to 25 . Default value is 2 percent. This option also
controls quality of interpolated samples using neighbour good samples.

options.name: threshold
options.description:
Set threshold value. Allowed range is from 1 to 100 .
Default value is 2 .
This controls the strength of impulsive noise which is going to be removed.
The lower value, the more samples will be detected as impulsive noise.

options.name: burst
options.description:
Set burst fusion, in percentage of window size. Allowed range is 0 to 10 . Default value is 2 .
If any two samples detected as noise are spaced less than this value then any
sample between those two samples will be also detected as noise.

options.name: method
options.description:
Set overlap method.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is a .

possible_values.description:
Select overlap-add method. Even not interpolated samples are slightly
changed with this method.
Select overlap-save method. Not interpolated samples remain unchanged.

options.name: window
options.description:
Set window size, in milliseconds. Allowed range is from 10 to 100 .
Default value is 55 milliseconds.
This sets size of window which will be processed at once.

options.name: overlap
options.description: Set window overlap, in percentage of window size. Allowed range is from 50 to 95 . Default value is 75 percent.

options.name: arorder
options.description:
Set autoregression order, in percentage of window size. Allowed range is from 0 to 25 . Default value is 8 percent. This option also controls
quality of interpolated samples using neighbour good samples.

options.name: threshold
options.description:
Set threshold value. Allowed range is from 1 to 100 .
Default value is 10 . Higher values make clip detection less aggressive.

options.name: hsize
options.description:
Set size of histogram used to detect clips. Allowed range is from 100 to 9999 .
Default value is 1000 . Higher values make clip detection less aggressive.

options.name: method
options.description:
Set overlap method.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is a .

possible_values.description:
Select overlap-add method. Even not interpolated samples are slightly changed
with this method.
Select overlap-save method. Not interpolated samples remain unchanged.

options.name: stages
options.description:
Set decorrelation stages of filtering. Allowed
range is from 1 to 16. Default value is 6.

options.name: seed
options.description: Set random seed used for setting delay in samples across channels.

options.name: delays
options.description:
Set list of delays in milliseconds for each channel separated by ’|’.
Unused delays will be silently ignored. If number of given delays is
smaller than number of channels all remaining channels will not be delayed.
If you want to delay exact number of samples, append ’S’ to number.
If you want instead to delay in seconds, append ’s’ to number.

options.name: all
options.description:
Use last set delay for all remaining channels. By default is disabled.
This option if enabled changes how option delays is interpreted.

options.name: level
options.description:
Set level of added noise in dB. Default is -351 .
Allowed range is from -451 to -90.

options.name: type
options.description:
Set type of added noise.
Default is dc .

options.name: transfer
options.description:
Set the transfer expression.
The expression can contain the following constants:
Default value is p .

possible_values.description: current frequency power value, in dB

options.name: attack
options.description:
Set the attack in milliseconds. Default is 50 milliseconds.
Allowed range is from 1 to 1000 milliseconds.

options.name: release
options.description:
Set the release in milliseconds. Default is 100 milliseconds.
Allowed range is from 5 to 2000 milliseconds.

options.name: channels
options.description: Set which channels to filter, by default all channels in audio stream are filtered.

options.name: threshold
options.description:
Set the detection threshold used to trigger equalization.
Threshold detection is using detection filter.
Default value is 0. Allowed range is from 0 to 100.

options.name: dfrequency
options.description:
Set the detection frequency in Hz used for detection filter used to trigger equalization.
Default value is 1000 Hz. Allowed range is between 2 and 1000000 Hz.

options.name: dqfactor
options.description:
Set the detection resonance factor for detection filter used to trigger equalization.
Default value is 1. Allowed range is from 0.001 to 1000.

options.name: tfrequency
options.description:
Set the target frequency of equalization filter.
Default value is 1000 Hz. Allowed range is between 2 and 1000000 Hz.

options.name: tqfactor
options.description:
Set the target resonance factor for target equalization filter.
Default value is 1. Allowed range is from 0.001 to 1000.

options.name: attack
options.description:
Set the amount of milliseconds the signal from detection has to rise above
the detection threshold before equalization starts.
Default is 20. Allowed range is between 1 and 2000.

options.name: release
options.description:
Set the amount of milliseconds the signal from detection has to fall below the
detection threshold before equalization ends.
Default is 200. Allowed range is between 1 and 2000.

options.name: ratio
options.description:
Set the ratio by which the equalization gain is raised.
Default is 1. Allowed range is between 0 and 30.

options.name: makeup
options.description:
Set the makeup offset by which the equalization gain is raised.
Default is 0. Allowed range is between 0 and 100.

options.name: range
options.description:
Set the max allowed cut/boost amount. Default is 50.
Allowed range is from 1 to 200.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set the mode of filter operation, can be one of the following:
Default mode is ‘ cutbelow ’.

options.name: dftype
options.description:
Set the type of detection filter, can be one of the following:
Default type is ‘ bandpass ’.

options.name: tftype
options.description:
Set the type of target filter, can be one of the following:
Default type is ‘ bell ’.

options.name: auto
options.description:
Automatically gather threshold from detection filter. By default
is ‘ disabled ’.
This option is useful to detect threshold in certain time frame of
input audio stream, in such case option value is changed at runtime.
Available values are:

possible_values.description: Disable using automatically gathered threshold value.
Adaptively pick threshold value, by calculating sliding window entropy.

options.name: precision
options.description: Set which precision to use when processing samples.

possible_values.description: Auto pick internal sample format depending on other filters.
Always use single-floating point precision sample format.
Always use double-floating point precision sample format.

options.name: sensitivity
options.description:
Set an amount of sensitivity to frequency fluctations. Default is 2.
Allowed range is from 0 to 1e+06.

options.name: basefreq
options.description:
Set a base frequency for smoothing. Default value is 22050.
Allowed range is from 2 to 1e+06.

options.name: in_gain
options.description: Set input gain of reflected signal. Default is 0.6 .

options.name: out_gain
options.description: Set output gain of reflected signal. Default is 0.3 .

options.name: delays
options.description:
Set list of time intervals in milliseconds between original signal and reflections
separated by ’|’. Allowed range for each delay is (0 - 90000.0] .
Default is 1000 .

options.name: decays
options.description:
Set list of loudness of reflected signals separated by ’|’.
Allowed range for each decay is (0 - 1.0] .
Default is 0.5 .

options.name: level_in
options.description: Set input gain.

options.name: level_out
options.description: Set output gain.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set filter mode. For restoring material use reproduction mode, otherwise
use production mode. Default is reproduction mode.

options.name: type
options.description: Set filter type. Selects medium. Can be one of the following:

options.name: exprs
options.description:
Set the ’|’-separated expressions list for each separate channel. If
the number of input channels is greater than the number of
expressions, the last specified expression is used for the remaining
output channels.

options.name: channel_layout
options.description:
Set output channel layout. If not specified, the channel layout is
specified by the number of expressions. If set to ‘ same ’, it will
use by default the same input channel layout.

options.name: ch
options.description: channel number of the current expression

options.name: n
options.description: number of the evaluated sample, starting from 0

options.name: s
options.description: sample rate

options.name: t
options.description: time of the evaluated sample expressed in seconds

options.name: nb_in_channels
options.description: input and output number of channels

options.name: val
options.description: the value of input channel with number CH

options.name: level_in
options.description:
Set input level prior processing of signal.
Allowed range is from 0 to 64.
Default value is 1.

options.name: level_out
options.description:
Set output level after processing of signal.
Allowed range is from 0 to 64.
Default value is 1.

options.name: amount
options.description:
Set the amount of harmonics added to original signal.
Allowed range is from 0 to 64.
Default value is 1.
