Set bias for each plane. This value is added to the result of the multiplication.
Useful for making the overall image brighter or darker. Default is 0.0.

options.name: 0mode
options.description:
Set matrix mode for each plane. Can be square , row or column .
Default is square .

options.name: planes
options.description: Set which planes to process.

options.name: impulse
options.description: Set which impulse video frames will be processed, can be first or all . Default is all .

options.name: list_filters
options.description:
List all available filters and generators along with all their respective
options as well as possible minimum and maximum values along with the default
values.

options.name: filter
options.description:
Specify all filters by their respective name and options.
Use list_filters to determine all valid filter names and options.
Numerical options are specified by a float value and are automatically clamped
to their respective value range.  Vector and color options have to be specified
by a list of space separated float values. Character escaping has to be done.
A special option name default is available to use default options for a
filter.
It is required to specify either default or at least one of the filter options.
All omitted options are used with their default values.
The syntax of the filter string is as follows:
options.default: available to use default options for a

options.name: output_rect
options.description:
Specify a rectangle where the output of the filter chain is copied into the
input image. It is given by a list of space separated float values:
If not given, the output rectangle equals the dimensions of the input image.
The output rectangle is automatically cropped at the borders of the input
image. Negative values are valid for each component.

options.name: cover
options.description: Filepath of the optional cover image, needs to be in yuv420.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set covering mode.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is blur .

possible_values.description: cover it by the supplied image
cover it by interpolating the surrounding pixels

options.name: w
options.description:
The width of the output video. It defaults to iw .
This expression is evaluated only once during the filter
configuration, or when the ‘ w ’ or ‘ out_w ’ command is sent.

options.name: h
options.description:
The height of the output video. It defaults to ih .
This expression is evaluated only once during the filter
configuration, or when the ‘ h ’ or ‘ out_h ’ command is sent.

options.name: x
options.description:
The horizontal position, in the input video, of the left edge of the output
video. It defaults to (in_w-out_w)/2 .
This expression is evaluated per-frame.

options.name: y
options.description:
The vertical position, in the input video, of the top edge of the output video.
It defaults to (in_h-out_h)/2 .
This expression is evaluated per-frame.

options.name: keep_aspect
options.description:
If set to 1 will force the output display aspect ratio
to be the same of the input, by changing the output sample aspect
ratio. It defaults to 0.

options.name: exact
options.description:
Enable exact cropping. If enabled, subsampled videos will be cropped at exact
width/height/x/y as specified and will not be rounded to nearest smaller value.
It defaults to 0.

options.name: x
options.description:
The computed values for x and y . They are evaluated for
each new frame.

options.name: in_w
options.description: The input width and height.

options.name: iw
options.description: These are the same as in_w and in_h .

options.name: out_w
options.description: The output (cropped) width and height.

options.name: ow
options.description: These are the same as out_w and out_h .

options.name: a
options.description: same as iw / ih

options.name: sar
options.description: input sample aspect ratio

options.name: dar
options.description: input display aspect ratio, it is the same as ( iw / ih ) * sar

options.name: hsub
options.description:
horizontal and vertical chroma subsample values. For example for the
pixel format "yuv422p" hsub is 2 and vsub is 1.

options.name: n
options.description: The number of the input frame, starting from 0.

options.name: pos
options.description:
the position in the file of the input frame, NAN if unknown; deprecated,
do not use

options.name: t
options.description: The timestamp expressed in seconds. It’s NAN if the input timestamp is unknown.

options.name: w
options.description:
Set width/height of the output video and the horizontal/vertical position
in the input video.
The command accepts the same syntax of the corresponding option.
If the specified expression is not valid, it is kept at its current
value.

options.name: mode
options.description: Depending on mode crop detection is based on either the mere black value of surrounding pixels or a combination of motion vectors and edge pixels.

possible_values.description: Detect black pixels surrounding the playing video. For fine control use option limit .
Detect the playing video by the motion vectors inside the video and scanning for edge pixels typically forming the border of a playing video.

options.name: limit
options.description:
Set higher black value threshold, which can be optionally specified
from nothing (0) to everything (255 for 8-bit based formats). An intensity
value greater to the set value is considered non-black. It defaults to 24.
You can also specify a value between 0.0 and 1.0 which will be scaled depending
on the bitdepth of the pixel format.

options.name: round
options.description:
The value which the width/height should be divisible by. It defaults to
16. The offset is automatically adjusted to center the video. Use 2 to
get only even dimensions (needed for 4:2:2 video). 16 is best when
encoding to most video codecs.

options.name: skip
options.description:
Set the number of initial frames for which evaluation is skipped.
Default is 2. Range is 0 to INT_MAX.

options.name: reset_count
options.description:
Set the counter that determines after how many frames cropdetect will
reset the previously detected largest video area and start over to
detect the current optimal crop area. Default value is 0.
This can be useful when channel logos distort the video area. 0
indicates ’never reset’, and returns the largest area encountered during
playback.

options.name: mv_threshold
options.description: Set motion in pixel units as threshold for motion detection. It defaults to 8.

options.name: low
options.description:
Set low and high threshold values used by the Canny thresholding
algorithm.
The high threshold selects the "strong" edge pixels, which are then
connected through 8-connectivity with the "weak" edge pixels selected
by the low threshold.
low and high threshold values must be chosen in the range
[0,1], and low should be lesser or equal to high .
Default value for low is 5/255 , and default value for high is 15/255 .

options.name: limit
options.description:
The command accepts the same syntax of the corresponding option.
If the specified expression is not valid, it is kept at its current value.

options.name: cue
options.description: The cue timestamp expressed in a UNIX timestamp in microseconds. Default is 0.

options.name: preroll
options.description: The duration of content to pass on as preroll expressed in seconds. Default is 0.

options.name: buffer
options.description:
The maximum duration of content to buffer before waiting for the cue expressed
in seconds. Default is 0.

options.name: preset
options.description:
Select one of the available color presets. This option can be used in addition
to the r , g , b parameters; in this case, the later
options takes priority on the preset values.
Available presets are:
Default is none .

options.name: master
options.description:
Set the master key points. These points will define a second pass mapping. It
is sometimes called a "luminance" or "value" mapping. It can be used with r , g , b or all since it acts like a
post-processing LUT.

options.name: red
options.description: Set the key points for the red component.

options.name: green
options.description: Set the key points for the green component.

options.name: blue
options.description: Set the key points for the blue component.

options.name: all
options.description:
Set the key points for all components (not including master).
Can be used in addition to the other key points component
options. In this case, the unset component(s) will fallback on this all setting.

options.name: psfile
options.description: Specify a Photoshop curves file ( .acv ) to import the settings from.

options.name: plot
options.description: Save Gnuplot script of the curves in specified file.

options.name: interp
options.description: Specify the kind of interpolation. Available algorithms are:

possible_values.description: Natural cubic spline using a piece-wise cubic polynomial that is twice continuously differentiable.
Monotonic cubic spline using a piecewise cubic Hermite interpolating polynomial (PCHIP).

options.name: size
options.description: Set output video size.

options.name: x
options.description: Set x offset from where to pick pixels.

options.name: y
options.description: Set y offset from where to pick pixels.

options.name: mode
options.description: Set scope mode, can be one of the following:

possible_values.description: Draw hexadecimal pixel values with white color on black background.

Draw hexadecimal pixel values with input video pixel color on black
background.

Draw hexadecimal pixel values on color background picked from input video,
the text color is picked in such way so its always visible.

options.name: axis
options.description: Draw rows and columns numbers on left and top of video.

options.name: opacity
options.description: Set background opacity.

options.name: format
options.description: Set display number format. Can be hex , or dec . Default is hex .

options.name: components
options.description: Set pixel components to display. By default all pixel components are displayed.

options.name: angle
options.description: Set angle of directional blur. Default is 45 .

options.name: radius
options.description: Set radius of directional blur. Default is 5 .

options.name: planes
options.description: Set which planes to filter. By default all planes are filtered.

options.name: sigma
options.description:
Set the noise sigma constant.
This sigma defines a hard threshold of 3 * sigma ; every DCT
coefficient (absolute value) below this threshold with be dropped.
If you need a more advanced filtering, see expr .
Default is 0 .

options.name: overlap
options.description:
Set number overlapping pixels for each block. Since the filter can be slow, you
may want to reduce this value, at the cost of a less effective filter and the
risk of various artefacts.
If the overlapping value doesn’t permit processing the whole input width or
height, a warning will be displayed and according borders won’t be denoised.
Default value is blocksize -1, which is the best possible setting.
options.default: blocksize -1, which is the best possible setting

options.name: expr
options.description:
Set the coefficient factor expression.
For each coefficient of a DCT block, this expression will be evaluated as a
multiplier value for the coefficient.
If this is option is set, the sigma option will be ignored.
The absolute value of the coefficient can be accessed through the c variable.

options.name: n
options.description:
Set the blocksize using the number of bits. 1<< n defines the blocksize , which is the width and height of the processed blocks.
The default value is 3 (8x8) and can be raised to 4 for a blocksize of 16x16. Note that changing this setting has huge consequences
on the speed processing. Also, a larger block size does not necessarily means a
better de-noising.
options.default: 3 (8x8) and can be raised to 4 for a blocksize of 16x16

options.name: 1thr
options.description:
Set banding detection threshold for each plane. Default is 0.02.
Valid range is 0.00003 to 0.5.
If difference between current pixel and reference pixel is less than threshold,
it will be considered as banded.

options.name: range
options.description:
Banding detection range in pixels. Default is 16. If positive, random number
in range 0 to set value will be used. If negative, exact absolute value
will be used.
The range defines square of four pixels around current pixel.

options.name: direction
options.description:
Set direction in radians from which four pixel will be compared. If positive,
random direction from 0 to set direction will be picked. If negative, exact of
absolute value will be picked. For example direction 0, -PI or -2*PI radians
will pick only pixels on same row and -PI/2 will pick only pixels on same
column.

options.name: blur
options.description:
If enabled, current pixel is compared with average value of all four
surrounding pixels. The default is enabled. If disabled current pixel is
compared with all four surrounding pixels. The pixel is considered banded
if only all four differences with surrounding pixels are less than threshold.

options.name: coupling
options.description:
If enabled, current pixel is changed if and only if all pixel components are banded,
e.g. banding detection threshold is triggered for all color components.
The default is disabled.

options.name: filter
options.description:
Set filter type, can be weak or strong . Default is strong .
This controls what kind of deblocking is applied.

options.name: block
options.description: Set size of block, allowed range is from 4 to 512. Default is 8 .

options.name: alpha
options.description:
Set blocking detection thresholds. Allowed range is 0 to 1.
Defaults are: 0.098 for alpha and 0.05 for the rest.
Using higher threshold gives more deblocking strength.
Setting alpha controls threshold detection at exact edge of block.
Remaining options controls threshold detection near the edge. Each one for
below/above or left/right. Setting any of those to 0 disables
deblocking.

options.name: planes
options.description: Set planes to filter. Default is to filter all available planes.

options.name: cycle
options.description:
Set the number of frames from which one will be dropped. Setting this to N means one frame in every batch of N frames will be dropped.
Default is 5 .

options.name: dupthresh
options.description:
Set the threshold for duplicate detection. If the difference metric for a frame
is less than or equal to this value, then it is declared as duplicate. Default
is 1.1

options.name: scthresh
options.description: Set scene change threshold. Default is 15 .

options.name: blockx
options.description:
Set the size of the x and y-axis blocks used during metric calculations.
Larger blocks give better noise suppression, but also give worse detection of
small movements. Must be a power of two. Default is 32 .

options.name: ppsrc
options.description:
Mark main input as a pre-processed input and activate clean source input
stream. This allows the input to be pre-processed with various filters to help
the metrics calculation while keeping the frame selection lossless. When set to 1 , the first stream is for the pre-processed input, and the second
stream is the clean source from where the kept frames are chosen. Default is 0 .

options.name: chroma
options.description: Set whether or not chroma is considered in the metric calculations. Default is 1 .

options.name: mixed
options.description:
Set whether or not the input only partially contains content to be decimated.
Default is false .
If enabled video output stream will be in variable frame rate.

options.name: planes
options.description: Set which planes to process.

options.name: impulse
options.description: Set which impulse video frames will be processed, can be first or all . Default is all .

options.name: noise
options.description:
Set noise when doing divisions. Default is 0.0000001 . Useful when width
and height are not same and not power of 2 or if stream prior to convolving
had noise.

options.name: m
options.description: Set mode of operation. Can be combination of dotcrawl for cross-luminance reduction and/or rainbows for cross-color reduction.

options.name: lt
options.description: Set spatial luma threshold. Lower values increases reduction of cross-luminance.

options.name: tl
options.description: Set tolerance for temporal luma. Higher values increases reduction of cross-luminance.

options.name: tc
options.description: Set tolerance for chroma temporal variation. Higher values increases reduction of cross-color.

options.name: ct
options.description: Set temporal chroma threshold. Lower values increases reduction of cross-color.

options.name: threshold0
options.description:
Limit the maximum change for each plane, default is 65535.
If 0, plane will remain unchanged.

options.name: size
options.description: Set moving-average filter size in frames. Default is 5. Allowed range is 2 - 129.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set averaging mode to smooth temporal luminance variations.
Available values are:

options.name: bypass
options.description: Do not actually modify frame. Useful when one only wants metadata.

options.name: method
options.description:
The deinterlacing method. It accepts one of the following values:
The default value is default .

possible_values.description:
Select the best deinterlacing method available on the hardware.
If the driver supports custom , that method is used; otherwise
falls back to bob . This is the default.

Bob deinterlacing. Each field is independently scaled to full
frame height. Simple and fast, but may produce visible bob artifacts
on moving content.

Driver-defined advanced deinterlacing. The exact algorithm is
hardware-specific and typically employs motion-adaptive techniques
with temporal reference frames for higher quality.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Specify the interlacing mode. It accepts one of the following values:
The default value is frame .

possible_values.description:
Send one frame for each frame. The output frame rate equals the input
frame rate. This is the default.

Send one frame for each field. The output frame rate is double the
input frame rate.

options.name: deint
options.description:
Specify which frames to deinterlace. It accepts one of the following values:
The default value is all .

possible_values.description: Deinterlace all frames. This is the default.

Only deinterlace frames marked as interlaced; progressive frames are
passed through unchanged.

options.name: cycle
options.description:
Specify the length of the window over which the judder repeats.
Accepts any integer greater than 1. Useful values are:
The default is ‘ 4 ’.

possible_values.description: If the original was telecined from 24 to 30 fps (Film to NTSC).
If the original was telecined from 25 to 30 fps (PAL to NTSC).
If a mixture of the two.

options.name: x
options.description:
Specify the top left corner coordinates of the logo. They must be
specified.

options.name: w
options.description:
Specify the width and height of the logo to clear. They must be
specified.

options.name: show
options.description:
When set to 1, a green rectangle is drawn on the screen to simplify
finding the right x , y , w , and h parameters.
The default value is 0.
The rectangle is drawn on the outermost pixels which will be (partly)
replaced with interpolated values. The values of the next pixels
immediately outside this rectangle in each direction will be used to
compute the interpolated pixel values inside the rectangle.

options.name: filter_type
options.description:
Specify which filter to use. This option accepts the following values:
Default value is ‘ derain ’.

possible_values.description: Derain filter. To conduct derain filter, you need to use a derain model.
Dehaze filter. To conduct dehaze filter, you need to use a dehaze model.

options.name: dnn_backend
options.description:
Specify which DNN backend to use for model loading and execution. This option accepts
the following values:

possible_values.description:
TensorFlow backend. To enable this backend you
need to install the TensorFlow for C library (see https://www.tensorflow.org/install/lang_c ) and configure FFmpeg with --enable-libtensorflow

options.name: model
options.description:
Set path to model file specifying network architecture and its parameters.
Note that different backends use different file formats. TensorFlow can load files for only its format.

options.name: x
options.description:
Specify a rectangular area where to limit the search for motion
vectors.
If desired the search for motion vectors can be limited to a
rectangular area of the frame defined by its top left corner, width
and height. These parameters have the same meaning as the drawbox
filter which can be used to visualise the position of the bounding
box.
This is useful when simultaneous movement of subjects within the frame
might be confused for camera motion by the motion vector search.
If any or all of x , y , w and h are set to -1
then the full frame is used. This allows later options to be set
without specifying the bounding box for the motion vector search.
Default - search the whole frame.

options.name: rx
options.description:
Specify the maximum extent of movement in x and y directions in the
range 0-64 pixels. Default 16.

options.name: edge
options.description:
Specify how to generate pixels to fill blanks at the edge of the
frame. Available values are:
Default value is ‘ mirror ’.

possible_values.description: Extruded edge value at blank locations

options.name: blocksize
options.description:
Specify the blocksize to use for motion search. Range 4-128 pixels,
default 8.

options.name: contrast
options.description:
Specify the contrast threshold for blocks. Only blocks with more than
the specified contrast (difference between darkest and lightest
pixels) will be considered. Range 1-255, default 125.

options.name: search
options.description:
Specify the search strategy. Available values are:
Default value is ‘ exhaustive ’.

options.name: filename
options.description:
If set then a detailed log of the motion search is written to the
specified file.

options.name: type
options.description: Set what type of despill to use.

options.name: mix
options.description: Set how spillmap will be generated.

options.name: expand
options.description: Set how much to get rid of still remaining spill.

options.name: red
options.description: Controls amount of red in spill area.

options.name: green
options.description:
Controls amount of green in spill area.
Should be -1 for greenscreen.

options.name: blue
options.description:
Controls amount of blue in spill area.
Should be -1 for bluescreen.

options.name: brightness
options.description: Controls brightness of spill area, preserving colors.

options.name: alpha
options.description: Modify alpha from generated spillmap.

possible_values.description:
bottom field first
The default value is top .

options.name: pattern
options.description:
A string of numbers representing the pulldown pattern you wish to apply.
The default value is 23 .

options.name: start_frame
options.description:
A number representing position of the first frame with respect to the telecine
pattern. This is to be used if the stream is cut. The default value is 0 .

options.name: threshold0
options.description:
Limit the maximum change for each plane, default is 65535.
If 0, plane will remain unchanged.

options.name: coordinates
options.description:
Flag which specifies the pixel to refer to. Default is 255 i.e. all eight
pixels are used.
Flags to local 3x3 coordinates maps like this:
1 2 3
    4   5
    6 7 8

options.name: edge
options.description:
Set displace behavior for pixels that are out of range.
Available values are:
Default is ‘ smear ’.

possible_values.description: Missing pixels are replaced by black pixels.
Adjacent pixels will spread out to replace missing pixels.
Out of range pixels are wrapped so they point to pixels of other side.
Out of range pixels will be replaced with mirrored pixels.

options.name: dnn_backend
options.description:
Specify which DNN backend to use for model loading and execution. This option accepts
only openvino now, tensorflow backends will be added.

options.name: model
options.description:
Set path to model file specifying network architecture and its parameters.
Note that different backends use different file formats.

options.name: input
options.description: Set the input name of the dnn network.

options.name: output
options.description: Set the output name of the dnn network.

options.name: confidence
options.description: Set the confidence threshold (default: 0.5).

options.name: labels
options.description:
Set path to label file specifying the mapping between label id and name.
Each label name is written in one line, tailing spaces and empty lines are skipped.
The first line is the name of label id 0,
and the second line is the name of label id 1, etc.
The label id is considered as name if the label file is not provided.

options.name: backend_configs
options.description:
Set the configs to be passed into backend
For tensorflow backend, you can set its configs with sess_config options,
please use tools/python/tf_sess_config.py to get the configs for your system.

options.name: dnn_backend
options.description:
Specify which DNN backend to use for model loading and execution. This option accepts
only openvino now, tensorflow backends will be added.

options.name: model
options.description:
Set path to model file specifying network architecture and its parameters.
Note that different backends use different file formats.

options.name: input
options.description: Set the input name of the dnn network.

options.name: output
options.description: Set the output name of the dnn network.

options.name: confidence
options.description: Set the confidence threshold (default: 0.5).

options.name: labels
options.description:
Set path to label file specifying the mapping between label id and name.
Each label name is written in one line, tailing spaces and empty lines are skipped.
The first line is the name of label id 0 (usually it is ’background’),
and the second line is the name of label id 1, etc.
The label id is considered as name if the label file is not provided.

options.name: backend_configs
options.description:
Set the configs to be passed into backend. To use async execution, set async (default: set).
Roll back to sync execution if the backend does not support async.

options.name: dnn_backend
options.description:
Specify which DNN backend to use for model loading and execution. This option accepts
the following values:

possible_values.description:
TensorFlow backend. To enable this backend you
need to install the TensorFlow for C library (see https://www.tensorflow.org/install/lang_c ) and configure FFmpeg with --enable-libtensorflow

OpenVINO backend. To enable this backend you
need to build and install the OpenVINO for C library (see https://github.com/openvinotoolkit/openvino/blob/master/build-instruction.md ) and configure FFmpeg with --enable-libopenvino (–extra-cflags=-I... –extra-ldflags=-L... might
be needed if the header files and libraries are not installed into system path)

Libtorch backend. To enable this backend you need to build and install Libtroch
for C++ library. Please download cxx11 ABI version (see https://pytorch.org/get-started/locally )
and configure FFmpeg with --enable-libtorch
--extra-cflags=-I/libtorch_root/libtorch/include
--extra-cflags=-I/libtorch_root/libtorch/include/torch/csrc/api/include
--extra-ldflags=-L/libtorch_root/libtorch/lib/

options.name: model
options.description:
Set path to model file specifying network architecture and its parameters.
Note that different backends use different file formats. TensorFlow, OpenVINO
and Libtorch backend can load files for only its format.

options.name: input
options.description: Set the input name of the dnn network.

options.name: output
options.description: Set the output name of the dnn network.

options.name: backend_configs
options.description:
Set the configs to be passed into backend. To use async execution, set async (default: set).
Roll back to sync execution if the backend does not support async.
For tensorflow backend, you can set its configs with sess_config options,
please use tools/python/tf_sess_config.py to get the configs of TensorFlow backend for your system.

options.name: x
options.description: The expressions which specify the top left corner coordinates of the box. It defaults to 0.

options.name: width
options.description:
The expressions which specify the width and height of the box; if 0 they are interpreted as
the input width and height. It defaults to 0.

options.name: color
options.description:
Specify the color of the box to write. For the general syntax of this option,
check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Color" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual . If the special
value invert is used, the box edge color is the same as the
video with inverted luma.

options.name: thickness
options.description:
The expression which sets the thickness of the box edge.
A value of fill will create a filled box. Default value is 3 .
See below for the list of accepted constants.

options.name: replace
options.description:
Applicable if the input has alpha. With value 1 , the pixels of the painted box
will overwrite the video’s color and alpha pixels.
Default is 0 , which composites the box onto the input, leaving the video’s alpha intact.
options.default: 0 , which composites the box onto the input, leaving the video’s alpha intact

options.name: dar
options.description: The input display aspect ratio, it is the same as ( w / h ) * sar .

options.name: hsub
options.description:
horizontal and vertical chroma subsample values. For example for the
pixel format "yuv422p" hsub is 2 and vsub is 1.

options.name: in_h
options.description: The input width and height.

options.name: sar
options.description: The input sample aspect ratio.

options.name: x
options.description: The x and y offset coordinates where the box is drawn.

options.name: w
options.description: The width and height of the drawn box.

options.name: box_source
options.description:
Box source can be set as side_data_detection_bboxes if you want to use box data in
detection bboxes of side data.
If box_source is set, the x , y , width and height will be ignored and
still use box data in detection bboxes of side data. So please do not use this parameter if you were
not sure about the box source.

options.name: t
options.description:
The thickness of the drawn box.
These constants allow the x , y , w , h and t expressions to refer to
each other, so you may for example specify y=x/dar or h=w/dar .

options.name: m1
options.description: Set 1st frame metadata key from which metadata values will be used to draw a graph.

options.name: fg1
options.description: Set 1st foreground color expression.

options.name: m2
options.description: Set 2nd frame metadata key from which metadata values will be used to draw a graph.

options.name: fg2
options.description: Set 2nd foreground color expression.

options.name: m3
options.description: Set 3rd frame metadata key from which metadata values will be used to draw a graph.

options.name: fg3
options.description: Set 3rd foreground color expression.

options.name: m4
options.description: Set 4th frame metadata key from which metadata values will be used to draw a graph.

options.name: fg4
options.description: Set 4th foreground color expression.

options.name: min
options.description: Set minimal value of metadata value.

options.name: max
options.description: Set maximal value of metadata value.

options.name: bg
options.description: Set graph background color. Default is white.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set graph mode.
Available values for mode is:
Default is line .

options.name: slide
options.description:
Set slide mode.
Available values for slide is:
Default is frame .

possible_values.description: Draw new frame when right border is reached.
Replace old columns with new ones.

options.name: size
options.description:
Set size of graph video. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
The default value is 900x256 .

options.name: rate
options.description:
Set the output frame rate. Default value is 25 .
The foreground color expressions can use the following variables:
The color is defined as 0xAABBGGRR.

options.name: x
options.description: The expressions which specify the coordinates of some point of grid intersection (meant to configure offset). Both default to 0.

options.name: width
options.description:
The expressions which specify the width and height of the grid cell, if 0 they are interpreted as the
input width and height, respectively, minus thickness , so image gets
framed. Default to 0.

options.name: color
options.description:
Specify the color of the grid. For the general syntax of this option,
check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Color" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual . If the special
value invert is used, the grid color is the same as the
video with inverted luma.

options.name: thickness
options.description:
The expression which sets the thickness of the grid line. Default value is 1 .
See below for the list of accepted constants.

options.name: replace
options.description:
Applicable if the input has alpha. With 1 the pixels of the painted grid
will overwrite the video’s color and alpha pixels.
Default is 0 , which composites the grid onto the input, leaving the video’s alpha intact.
options.default: 0 , which composites the grid onto the input, leaving the video’s alpha intact

options.name: dar
options.description: The input display aspect ratio, it is the same as ( w / h ) * sar .

options.name: hsub
options.description:
horizontal and vertical chroma subsample values. For example for the
pixel format "yuv422p" hsub is 2 and vsub is 1.

options.name: in_h
options.description: The input grid cell width and height.

options.name: sar
options.description: The input sample aspect ratio.

options.name: x
options.description: The x and y coordinates of some point of grid intersection (meant to configure offset).

options.name: w
options.description: The width and height of the drawn cell.

options.name: t
options.description:
The thickness of the drawn cell.
These constants allow the x , y , w , h and t expressions to refer to
each other, so you may for example specify y=x/dar or h=w/dar .

options.name: box
options.description:
Used to draw a box around text using the background color.
The value must be either 1 (enable) or 0 (disable).
The default value of box is 0.

options.name: boxborderw
options.description:
Set the width of the border to be drawn around the box using boxcolor .
The value must be specified using one of the following formats:
The default value of boxborderw is "0".

options.name: boxcolor
options.description:
The color to be used for drawing box around text. For the syntax of this
option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Color" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
The default value of boxcolor is "white".

options.name: line_spacing
options.description: Set the line spacing in pixels. The default value of line_spacing is 0.

options.name: text_align
options.description:
Set the vertical and horizontal alignment of the text with respect to the box boundaries.
The value is combination of flags, one for the vertical alignment (T=top,
M=middle, B=bottom) and one for the horizontal alignment (L=left, C=center, R=right).
Please note that tab characters are only supported with the left horizontal alignment.

options.name: y_align
options.description:
Specify what the y value is referred to. Possible values are:
The default value of y_align is "text" for backward compatibility.

options.name: borderw
options.description:
Set the width of the border to be drawn around the text using bordercolor .
The default value of borderw is 0.

options.name: bordercolor
options.description:
Set the color to be used for drawing border around text. For the syntax of this
option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Color" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
The default value of bordercolor is "black".

options.name: expansion
options.description: Select how the text is expanded. Can be either none , strftime (deprecated) or normal (default). See the Text expansion section below for details.

options.name: basetime
options.description:
Set a start time for the count. Value is in microseconds. Only applied
in the deprecated strftime expansion mode. To emulate in normal expansion
mode use the pts function, supplying the start time (in seconds)
as the second argument.

options.name: fix_bounds
options.description: If true, check and fix text coords to avoid clipping.

options.name: fontcolor
options.description:
The color to be used for drawing fonts. For the syntax of this option, check
the (ffmpeg-utils)"Color" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
The default value of fontcolor is "black".

options.name: fontcolor_expr
options.description:
String which is expanded the same way as text to obtain dynamic fontcolor value. By default this option has empty value and is not
processed. When this option is set, it overrides fontcolor option.

options.name: font
options.description: The font family to be used for drawing text. By default Sans.

options.name: fontfile
options.description:
The font file to be used for drawing text. The path must be included.
This parameter is mandatory if the fontconfig support is disabled.

options.name: alpha
options.description:
Draw the text applying alpha blending. The value can
be a number between 0.0 and 1.0.
The expression accepts the same variables x, y as well.
The default value is 1.
Please see fontcolor_expr .

options.name: fontsize
options.description:
The font size to be used for drawing text.
The default value of fontsize is 16.

options.name: text_shaping
options.description:
If set to 1, attempt to shape the text (for example, reverse the order of
right-to-left text and join Arabic characters) before drawing it.
Otherwise, just draw the text exactly as given.
By default 1 (if supported).

options.name: ft_load_flags
options.description:
The flags to be used for loading the fonts.
The flags map the corresponding flags supported by libfreetype, and are
a combination of the following values:
Default value is "default".
For more information consult the documentation for the FT_LOAD_*
libfreetype flags.

options.name: shadowcolor
options.description:
The color to be used for drawing a shadow behind the drawn text. For the
syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Color" section in the
ffmpeg-utils manual .
The default value of shadowcolor is "black".

options.name: boxw
options.description:
Set the width of the box to be drawn around text.
The default value of boxw is computed automatically to match the text width

options.name: boxh
options.description:
Set the height of the box to be drawn around text.
The default value of boxh is computed automatically to match the text height

options.name: shadowx
options.description:
The x and y offsets for the text shadow position with respect to the
position of the text. They can be either positive or negative
values. The default value for both is "0".

options.name: start_number
options.description:
The starting frame number for the n/frame_num variable. The default value
is "0".

options.name: tabsize
options.description:
The size in number of spaces to use for rendering the tab.
Default value is 4.

options.name: timecode
options.description:
Set the initial timecode representation in "hh:mm:ss[:;.]ff"
format. It can be used with or without text parameter. timecode_rate option must be specified.

options.name: timecode_rate
options.description:
Set the timecode frame rate (timecode only). Value will be rounded to nearest
integer. Minimum value is "1".
Drop-frame timecode is supported for frame rates 30 & 60.

options.name: tc24hmax
options.description:
If set to 1, the output of the timecode option will wrap around at 24 hours.
Default is 0 (disabled).

options.name: text
options.description:
The text string to be drawn. The text must be a sequence of UTF-8
encoded characters.
This parameter is mandatory if no file is specified with the parameter textfile .

options.name: textfile
options.description:
A text file containing text to be drawn. The text must be a sequence
of UTF-8 encoded characters.
This parameter is mandatory if no text string is specified with the
parameter text .
If both text and textfile are specified, an error is thrown.

options.name: text_source
options.description:
Text source should be set as side_data_detection_bboxes if you want to use text data in
detection bboxes of side data.
If text source is set, text and textfile will be ignored and still use
text data in detection bboxes of side data. So please do not use this parameter
if you are not sure about the text source.

options.name: reload
options.description:
The textfile will be reloaded at specified frame interval.
Be sure to update textfile atomically, or it may be read partially,
or even fail.
Range is 0 to INT_MAX. Default is 0.

options.name: x
options.description:
The expressions which specify the offsets where text will be drawn
within the video frame. They are relative to the top/left border of the
output image.
The default value of x and y is "0".
See below for the list of accepted constants and functions.

options.name: dar
options.description: input display aspect ratio, it is the same as ( w / h ) * sar

options.name: hsub
options.description:
horizontal and vertical chroma subsample values. For example for the
pixel format "yuv422p" hsub is 2 and vsub is 1.

options.name: line_h
options.description: the height of each text line

options.name: main_h
options.description: the input height

options.name: main_w
options.description: the input width

options.name: max_glyph_a
options.description:
the maximum distance from the baseline to the highest/upper grid
coordinate used to place a glyph outline point, for all the rendered
glyphs.
It is a positive value, due to the grid’s orientation with the Y axis
upwards.

options.name: max_glyph_d
options.description:
the maximum distance from the baseline to the lowest grid coordinate
used to place a glyph outline point, for all the rendered glyphs.
This is a negative value, due to the grid’s orientation, with the Y axis
upwards.

options.name: max_glyph_h
options.description:
maximum glyph height, that is the maximum height for all the glyphs
contained in the rendered text, it is equivalent to ascent - descent .

options.name: max_glyph_w
options.description:
maximum glyph width, that is the maximum width for all the glyphs
contained in the rendered text

options.name: font_a
options.description: the ascent size defined in the font metrics

options.name: font_d
options.description: the descent size defined in the font metrics

options.name: top_a
options.description: the maximum ascender of the glyphs of the first text line

options.name: bottom_d
options.description: the maximum descender of the glyphs of the last text line

options.name: n
options.description: the number of input frame, starting from 0

options.name: rand
options.description: return a random number included between min and max

options.name: sar
options.description: The input sample aspect ratio.

options.name: t
options.description: timestamp expressed in seconds, NAN if the input timestamp is unknown

options.name: text_h
options.description: the height of the rendered text

options.name: text_w
options.description: the width of the rendered text

options.name: x
options.description:
the x and y offset coordinates where the text is drawn.
These parameters allow the x and y expressions to refer
to each other, so you can for example specify y=x/dar .

options.name: pict_type
options.description: A one character description of the current frame’s picture type.

options.name: pkt_pos
options.description:
The current packet’s position in the input file or stream
(in bytes, from the start of the input). A value of -1 indicates
this info is not available.

options.name: duration
options.description: The current packet’s duration, in seconds.

options.name: pkt_size
options.description: The current packet’s size (in bytes).

options.name: reinit
options.description:
Alter existing filter parameters.
Syntax for the argument is the same as for filter invocation, e.g.
Full filter invocation with sendcmd would look like this:
If the entire argument can’t be parsed or applied as valid values then the filter will
continue with its existing parameters.

options.name: s
options.description: Script source to draw the graphics.

options.name: file
options.description: Path of the file to load the script source.

options.name: low
options.description:
Set low and high threshold values used by the Canny thresholding
algorithm.
The high threshold selects the "strong" edge pixels, which are then
connected through 8-connectivity with the "weak" edge pixels selected
by the low threshold.
low and high threshold values must be chosen in the range
[0,1], and low should be lesser or equal to high .
Default value for low is 20/255 , and default value for high is 50/255 .

options.name: mode
options.description:
Define the drawing mode.
Default value is wires .

possible_values.description: Draw white/gray wires on black background.
Mix the colors to create a paint/cartoon effect.
Apply Canny edge detector on all selected planes.

options.name: planes
options.description: Select planes for filtering. By default all available planes are filtered.

options.name: codebook_length
options.description:
Set codebook length. The value must be a positive integer, and
represents the number of distinct output colors. Default value is 256.

options.name: nb_steps
options.description:
Set the maximum number of iterations to apply for computing the optimal
mapping. The higher the value the better the result and the higher the
computation time. Default value is 1.

options.name: seed
options.description:
Set a random seed, must be an integer included between 0 and
UINT32_MAX. If not specified, or if explicitly set to -1, the filter
will try to use a good random seed on a best effort basis.

options.name: pal8
options.description:
Set pal8 output pixel format. This option does not work with codebook
length greater than 256. Default is disabled.

options.name: use_alpha
options.description:
Include alpha values in the quantization calculation. Allows creating
palettized output images (e.g. PNG8) with multiple alpha smooth blending.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Can be either normal or diff . Default is normal .
diff mode measures entropy of histogram delta values, absolute differences
between neighbour histogram values.

options.name: n
options.description:
Set the scaling dimension: 2 for 2xEPX , 3 for 3xEPX .
Default is 3 .

options.name: contrast
options.description: Set the contrast expression. The value must be a float value in range -1000.0 to 1000.0 . The default value is "1".

options.name: brightness
options.description:
Set the brightness expression. The value must be a float value in
range -1.0 to 1.0 . The default value is "0".

options.name: saturation
options.description:
Set the saturation expression. The value must be a float in
range 0.0 to 3.0 . The default value is "1".

options.name: gamma
options.description: Set the gamma expression. The value must be a float in range 0.1 to 10.0 .  The default value is "1".

options.name: gamma_r
options.description:
Set the gamma expression for red. The value must be a float in
range 0.1 to 10.0 . The default value is "1".

options.name: gamma_g
options.description: Set the gamma expression for green. The value must be a float in range 0.1 to 10.0 . The default value is "1".

options.name: gamma_b
options.description: Set the gamma expression for blue. The value must be a float in range 0.1 to 10.0 . The default value is "1".

options.name: gamma_weight
options.description:
Set the gamma weight expression. It can be used to reduce the effect
of a high gamma value on bright image areas, e.g. keep them from
getting overamplified and just plain white. The value must be a float
in range 0.0 to 1.0 . A value of 0.0 turns the
gamma correction all the way down while 1.0 leaves it at its
full strength. Default is "1".

options.name: eval
options.description:
Set when the expressions for brightness, contrast, saturation and
gamma expressions are evaluated.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is ‘ init ’.

possible_values.description:
only evaluate expressions once during the filter initialization or
when a command is processed
evaluate expressions for each incoming frame

options.name: n
options.description: frame count of the input frame starting from 0

options.name: pos
options.description:
byte position of the corresponding packet in the input file, NAN if
unspecified; deprecated, do not use

options.name: r
options.description: frame rate of the input video, NAN if the input frame rate is unknown

options.name: t
options.description: timestamp expressed in seconds, NAN if the input timestamp is unknown

options.name: contrast
options.description: Set the contrast expression.

options.name: brightness
options.description: Set the brightness expression.

options.name: saturation
options.description: Set the saturation expression.

options.name: gamma
options.description: Set the gamma expression.

options.name: gamma_r
options.description: Set the gamma_r expression.

options.name: gamma_g
options.description: Set gamma_g expression.

options.name: gamma_b
options.description: Set gamma_b expression.

options.name: gamma_weight
options.description:
Set gamma_weight expression.
The command accepts the same syntax of the corresponding option.
If the specified expression is not valid, it is kept at its current
value.

options.name: threshold0
options.description:
Limit the maximum change for each plane, default is 65535.
If 0, plane will remain unchanged.

options.name: coordinates
options.description:
Flag which specifies the pixel to refer to. Default is 255 i.e. all eight
pixels are used.
Flags to local 3x3 coordinates maps like this:
1 2 3
    4   5
    6 7 8

options.name: mode
options.description:
The interlacing mode to adopt. It accepts one of the following values:
The default value is field .

possible_values.description: Output one frame for each frame.
Output one frame for each field.

options.name: parity
options.description:
The picture field parity assumed for the input interlaced video. It accepts one
of the following values:
The default value is auto .
If the interlacing is unknown or the decoder does not export this information,
top field first will be assumed.

possible_values.description: Assume the top field is first.
Assume the bottom field is first.
Enable automatic detection of field parity.

options.name: deint
options.description:
Specify which frames to deinterlace. Accepts one of the following
values:
The default value is all .

possible_values.description: Only deinterlace frames marked as interlaced.

options.name: rslope
options.description:
Specify the search radius for edge slope tracing. Default value is 1.
Allowed range is from 1 to 15.

options.name: redge
options.description:
Specify the search radius for best edge matching. Default value is 2.
Allowed range is from 0 to 15.

options.name: ecost
options.description:
Specify the edge cost for edge matching. Default value is 2.
Allowed range is from 0 to 50.

options.name: mcost
options.description:
Specify the middle cost for edge matching. Default value is 1.
Allowed range is from 0 to 50.

options.name: dcost
options.description:
Specify the distance cost for edge matching. Default value is 1.
Allowed range is from 0 to 50.

options.name: interp
options.description:
Specify the interpolation used. Default is 4-point interpolation. It accepts one
of the following values:

options.name: exposure
options.description:
Set the exposure correction in EV. Allowed range is from -3.0 to 3.0 EV
Default value is 0 EV.

options.name: black
options.description:
Set the black level correction. Allowed range is from -1.0 to 1.0.
Default value is 0.

options.name: planes
options.description:
Set plane(s) to extract.
Available values for planes are:
Choosing planes not available in the input will result in an error.
That means you cannot select r , g , b planes
with y , u , v planes at same time.

options.name: type
options.description:
The effect type can be either "in" for a fade-in, or "out" for a fade-out
effect.
Default is in .

options.name: start_frame
options.description:
Specify the number of the frame to start applying the fade
effect at. Default is 0.

options.name: nb_frames
options.description:
The number of frames that the fade effect lasts. At the end of the
fade-in effect, the output video will have the same intensity as the input video.
At the end of the fade-out transition, the output video will be filled with the
selected color .
Default is 25.

options.name: alpha
options.description:
If set to 1, fade only alpha channel, if one exists on the input.
Default value is 0.

options.name: start_time
options.description:
Specify the timestamp (in seconds) of the frame to start to apply the fade
effect. If both start_frame and start_time are specified, the fade will start at
whichever comes last.  Default is 0.

options.name: duration
options.description:
The number of seconds for which the fade effect has to last. At the end of the
fade-in effect the output video will have the same intensity as the input video,
at the end of the fade-out transition the output video will be filled with the
selected color .
If both duration and nb_frames are specified, duration is used. Default is 0
(nb_frames is used by default).

options.name: color
options.description: Specify the color of the fade. Default is "black".

options.name: x
options.description: Set the top left crop position.

options.name: w
options.description: Set the crop size.

options.name: sigma
options.description:
Set the noise sigma constant. This sets denoising strength.
Default value is 1. Allowed range is from 0 to 30.
Using very high sigma with low overlap may give blocking artifacts.

options.name: amount
options.description:
Set amount of denoising. By default all detected noise is reduced.
Default value is 1. Allowed range is from 0 to 1.

options.name: block
options.description: Set size of block in pixels, Default is 32, can be 8 to 256.

options.name: overlap
options.description: Set block overlap. Default is 0.5. Allowed range is from 0.2 to 0.8.

options.name: method
options.description: Set denoising method. Default is wiener , can also be hard .

options.name: prev
options.description: Set number of previous frames to use for denoising. By default is set to 0.

options.name: next
options.description: Set number of next frames to to use for denoising. By default is set to 0.

options.name: planes
options.description:
Set planes which will be filtered, by default are all available filtered
except alpha.

options.name: dc_Y
options.description:
Adjust the dc value (gain) of the luma plane of the image. The filter
accepts an integer value in range 0 to 1000 . The default
value is set to 0 .

options.name: dc_U
options.description:
Adjust the dc value (gain) of the 1st chroma plane of the image. The
filter accepts an integer value in range 0 to 1000 . The
default value is set to 0 .

options.name: dc_V
options.description:
Adjust the dc value (gain) of the 2nd chroma plane of the image. The
filter accepts an integer value in range 0 to 1000 . The
default value is set to 0 .

options.name: weight_Y
options.description: Set the frequency domain weight expression for the luma plane.

options.name: weight_U
options.description: Set the frequency domain weight expression for the 1st chroma plane.

options.name: weight_V
options.description: Set the frequency domain weight expression for the 2nd chroma plane.

options.name: eval
options.description:
Set when the expressions are evaluated.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is ‘ init ’.
The filter accepts the following variables:

possible_values.description: Only evaluate expressions once during the filter initialization.
Evaluate expressions for each incoming frame.

options.name: X
options.description: The coordinates of the current sample.

options.name: W
options.description: The width and height of the image.

options.name: N
options.description: The number of input frame, starting from 0.

options.name: WS
options.description: The size of FFT array for horizontal and vertical processing.

options.name: type
options.description: Specify whether to extract the top (if the value is 0 or top ) or the bottom field (if the value is 1 or bottom ).

options.name: hint
options.description:
Set file containing hints: absolute/relative frame numbers.
There must be one line for each frame in a clip. Each line must contain two
numbers separated by the comma, optionally followed by - or + .
Numbers supplied on each line of file can not be out of [N-1,N+1] where N
is current frame number for absolute mode or out of [-1, 1] range
for relative mode. First number tells from which frame to pick up top
field and second number tells from which frame to pick up bottom field.
If optionally followed by + output frame will be marked as interlaced,
else if followed by - output frame will be marked as progressive, else
it will be marked same as input frame.
If optionally followed by t output frame will use only top field, or in
case of b it will use only bottom field.
If line starts with # or ; that line is skipped.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Can be item absolute or relative or pattern . Default is absolute .
The pattern mode is same as relative mode, except at last entry of file if there
are more frames to process than hint file is seek back to start.

options.name: order
options.description:
Specify the assumed field order of the input stream. Available values are:
Note that it is sometimes recommended not to trust the parity announced by the
stream.
Default value is auto .

possible_values.description: Auto detect parity (use FFmpeg’s internal parity value).

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set the matching mode or strategy to use. pc mode is the safest in the
sense that it won’t risk creating jerkiness due to duplicate frames when
possible, but if there are bad edits or blended fields it will end up
outputting combed frames when a good match might actually exist. On the other
hand, pcn_ub mode is the most risky in terms of creating jerkiness,
but will almost always find a good frame if there is one. The other values are
all somewhere in between pc and pcn_ub in terms of risking
jerkiness and creating duplicate frames versus finding good matches in sections
with bad edits, orphaned fields, blended fields, etc.
More details about p/c/n/u/b are available in p/c/n/u/b meaning section.
Available values are:
The parenthesis at the end indicate the matches that would be used for that
mode assuming order = tff (and field on auto or top ).
In terms of speed pc mode is by far the fastest and pcn_ub is
the slowest.
Default value is pc_n .

possible_values.description: 2-way matching, and trying 3rd match if still combed (p/c + n)
2-way matching, and trying 3rd match (same order) if still combed (p/c + u)

2-way matching, trying 3rd match if still combed, and trying 4th/5th matches if
still combed (p/c + n + u/b)

3-way matching, and trying 4th/5th matches if all 3 of the original matches are
detected as combed (p/c/n + u/b)

options.name: ppsrc
options.description:
Mark the main input stream as a pre-processed input, and enable the secondary
input stream as the clean source to pick the fields from. See the filter
introduction for more details. It is similar to the clip2 feature from
VFM/TFM.
Default value is 0 (disabled).

options.name: field
options.description:
Set the field to match from. It is recommended to set this to the same value as order unless you experience matching failures with that setting. In
certain circumstances changing the field that is used to match from can have a
large impact on matching performance. Available values are:
Default value is auto .

options.name: mchroma
options.description:
Set whether or not chroma is included during the match comparisons. In most
cases it is recommended to leave this enabled. You should set this to 0 only if your clip has bad chroma problems such as heavy rainbowing or other
artifacts. Setting this to 0 could also be used to speed things up at
the cost of some accuracy.
Default value is 1 .

options.name: y0
options.description:
These define an exclusion band which excludes the lines between y0 and y1 from being included in the field matching decision. An exclusion
band can be used to ignore subtitles, a logo, or other things that may
interfere with the matching. y0 sets the starting scan line and y1 sets the ending line; all lines in between y0 and y1 (including y0 and y1 ) will be ignored. Setting y0 and y1 to the same value will disable the feature. y0 and y1 defaults to 0 .

options.name: scthresh
options.description:
Set the scene change detection threshold as a percentage of maximum change on
the luma plane. Good values are in the [8.0, 14.0] range. Scene change
detection is only relevant in case combmatch = sc .  The range for scthresh is [0.0, 100.0] .
Default value is 12.0 .

options.name: combmatch
options.description:
When combatch is not none , fieldmatch will take into
account the combed scores of matches when deciding what match to use as the
final match. Available values are:
Default is sc .

possible_values.description: No final matching based on combed scores.
Combed scores are only used when a scene change is detected.
Use combed scores all the time.

options.name: combdbg
options.description:
Force fieldmatch to calculate the combed metrics for certain matches and
print them. This setting is known as micout in TFM/VFM vocabulary.
Available values are:
Default value is none .

options.name: cthresh
options.description:
This is the area combing threshold used for combed frame detection. This
essentially controls how "strong" or "visible" combing must be to be detected.
Larger values mean combing must be more visible and smaller values mean combing
can be less visible or strong and still be detected. Valid settings are from -1 (every pixel will be detected as combed) to 255 (no pixel will
be detected as combed). This is basically a pixel difference value. A good
range is [8, 12] .
Default value is 9 .

options.name: chroma
options.description:
Sets whether or not chroma is considered in the combed frame decision.  Only
disable this if your source has chroma problems (rainbowing, etc.) that are
causing problems for the combed frame detection with chroma enabled. Actually,
using chroma = 0 is usually more reliable, except for the case
where there is chroma only combing in the source.
Default value is 0 .

options.name: blockx
options.description:
Respectively set the x-axis and y-axis size of the window used during combed
frame detection. This has to do with the size of the area in which combpel pixels are required to be detected as combed for a frame to be
declared combed. See the combpel parameter description for more info.
Possible values are any number that is a power of 2 starting at 4 and going up
to 512.
Default value is 16 .

options.name: combpel
options.description:
The number of combed pixels inside any of the blocky by blockx size blocks on the frame for the frame to be detected as
combed. While cthresh controls how "visible" the combing must be, this
setting controls "how much" combing there must be in any localized area (a
window defined by the blockx and blocky settings) on the
frame. Minimum value is 0 and maximum is blocky x blockx (at
which point no frames will ever be detected as combed). This setting is known
as MI in TFM/VFM vocabulary.
Default value is 80 .

options.name: order
options.description: The output field order. Valid values are tff for top field first or bff for bottom field first.

options.name: left
options.description: Number of pixels to fill from left border.

options.name: right
options.description: Number of pixels to fill from right border.

options.name: top
options.description: Number of pixels to fill from top border.

options.name: bottom
options.description: Number of pixels to fill from bottom border.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set fill mode.
It accepts the following values:
Default is smear .

possible_values.description: fill pixels using mirroring (half sample symmetric)
fill pixels using reflecting (whole sample symmetric)
fill pixels at top and bottom with weighted averages pixels near borders

options.name: color
options.description: Set color for pixels in fixed or fade mode. Default is black .

options.name: lavfi.rect.w
options.description: width of object

options.name: lavfi.rect.h
options.description: height of object

options.name: lavfi.rect.x
options.description: x position of object

options.name: lavfi.rect.y
options.description: y position of object

options.name: lavfi.rect.score
options.description: match score of the found object

options.name: object
options.description: Filepath of the object image, needs to be in gray8.

options.name: threshold
options.description:
Detection threshold, expressed as a decimal number in the range 0-1.
A threshold value of 0.01 means only exact matches, a threshold of 0.99 means
almost everything matches.
Default value is 0.5.

options.name: mipmaps
options.description: Number of mipmaps, default is 3.

options.name: xmin
options.description: Specifies the rectangle in which to search.

options.name: discard
options.description: Discard frames where object is not detected. Default is disabled.

options.name: x
options.description: Set pixel x coordinate.

options.name: y
options.description: Set pixel y coordinate.

options.name: s0
options.description: Set source #0 component value.

options.name: s1
options.description: Set source #1 component value.

options.name: s2
options.description: Set source #2 component value.

options.name: s3
options.description: Set source #3 component value.

options.name: d0
options.description: Set destination #0 component value.

options.name: d1
options.description: Set destination #1 component value.

options.name: d2
options.description: Set destination #2 component value.

options.name: d3
options.description: Set destination #3 component value.

options.name: pix_fmts
options.description:
A ’|’-separated list of pixel format names, such as
"pix_fmts=yuv420p|monow|rgb24".

options.name: color_spaces
options.description:
A ’|’-separated list of color space names, such as
"color_spaces=bt709|bt470bg|bt2020nc".

options.name: color_ranges
options.description:
A ’|’-separated list of color range names, such as
"color_ranges=tv|pc".

options.name: alpha_modes
options.description:
A ’|’-separated list of color range names, such as
"alpha_modes=straight|premultiplied".

options.name: fps
options.description:
The desired output frame rate. It accepts expressions containing the following
constants:
The default is 25 .

options.name: start_time
options.description:
Assume the first PTS should be the given value, in seconds. This allows for
padding/trimming at the start of stream. By default, no assumption is made
about the first frame’s expected PTS, so no padding or trimming is done.
For example, this could be set to 0 to pad the beginning with duplicates of
the first frame if a video stream starts after the audio stream or to trim any
frames with a negative PTS.

options.name: round
options.description:
Timestamp (PTS) rounding method.
Possible values are:
The default is near .

options.name: eof_action
options.description:
Action performed when reading the last frame.
Possible values are:
The default is round .

possible_values.description: Use same timestamp rounding method as used for other frames.
Pass through last frame if input duration has not been reached yet.

options.name: format
options.description: The desired packing format. Supported values are:

possible_values.description: The views are next to each other (default).
The views are on top of each other.
The views are packed by line.
The views are packed by column.

options.name: fps
options.description:
Specify the output frames per second. This option can also be specified
as a value alone. The default is 50 .

options.name: interp_start
options.description:
Specify the start of a range where the output frame will be created as a
linear interpolation of two frames. The range is [ 0 - 255 ],
the default is 15 .

options.name: interp_end
options.description:
Specify the end of a range where the output frame will be created as a
linear interpolation of two frames. The range is [ 0 - 255 ],
the default is 240 .

options.name: scene
options.description:
Specify the level at which a scene change is detected as a value between
0 and 100 to indicate a new scene; a low value reflects a low
probability for the current frame to introduce a new scene, while a higher
value means the current frame is more likely to be one.
The default is 8.2 .

options.name: flags
options.description:
Specify flags influencing the filter process.
Available value for flags is:

possible_values.description:
Enable scene change detection using the value of the option scene .
This flag is enabled by default.

options.name: engine_type
options.description: Specify the engine used to run shaders.

options.name: enable
options.description:
Boolean value: enable/disable FRC. Dynamic value, can be altered at a runtime
without re-initializing the filter. (Default value: enabled).

options.name: fallback_mode
options.description: Fallback behavior in case of low interpolation confidence.

options.name: indicator
options.description:
Boolean value: show FRC indicator square in the top left corner of the video
(Default value: disabled).

options.name: profile
options.description: Level of hierarchical motion search.

possible_values.description:
Less levels of hierarchical motion search.
Only recommended for extremely low resolutions.
Recommended for any resolution up to 1440p. (Default value)

More levels of hierarchical motion search. Recommended for resolutions 1440p
or higher.

options.name: mv_search_mode
options.description: Performance mode of the motion search.

possible_values.description: Conduct motion search on the full resolution of source images.

Conduct motion search on the down scaled source images.
Recommended for APU or low end GPU for better performance.

options.name: use_future_frame
options.description:
Boolean value: enable dependency on future frame, improves quality for the cost
of latency (Default value: enabled).

options.name: step
options.description:
Select frame after every step frames.
Allowed values are positive integers higher than 0. Default value is 1 .

options.name: noise
options.description:
Set noise tolerance. Can be specified in dB (in case "dB" is appended to the
specified value) or as a difference ratio between 0 and 1. Default is -60dB, or
0.001.

options.name: duration
options.description: Set freeze duration until notification (default is 2 seconds).

options.name: first
options.description: Set number of first frame from which to start freeze.

options.name: last
options.description: Set number of last frame from which to end freeze.

options.name: replace
options.description: Set number of frame from 2nd input which will be used instead of replaced frames.

options.name: filter_name
options.description:
The name of the frei0r effect to load. If the environment variable FREI0R_PATH is defined, the frei0r effect is searched for in each of the
directories specified by the colon-separated list in FREI0R_PATH .
Otherwise, the standard frei0r paths are searched, in this order: HOME/.frei0r-1/lib/ , /usr/local/lib/frei0r-1/ , /usr/lib/frei0r-1/ .

options.name: filter_params
options.description: A ’|’-separated list of parameters to pass to the frei0r effect.

options.name: quality
options.description:
Set quality. This option defines the number of levels for averaging. It accepts
an integer in the range 4-5. Default value is 4 .

options.name: qp
options.description:
Force a constant quantization parameter. It accepts an integer in range 0-63.
If not set, the filter will use the QP from the video stream (if available).

options.name: strength
options.description:
Set filter strength. It accepts an integer in range -15 to 32. Lower values mean
more details but also more artifacts, while higher values make the image smoother
but also blurrier. Default value is 0 − PSNR optimal.

options.name: use_bframe_qp
options.description:
Enable the use of the QP from the B-Frames if set to 1 . Using this
option may cause flicker since the B-Frames have often larger QP. Default is 0 (not enabled).

options.name: file
options.description: The filename of the map file to be used.

options.name: sigma
options.description: Set horizontal sigma, standard deviation of Gaussian blur. Default is 0.5 .

options.name: steps
options.description: Set number of steps for Gaussian approximation. Default is 1 .

options.name: planes
options.description: Set which planes to filter. By default all planes are filtered.

options.name: sigmaV
options.description:
Set vertical sigma, if negative it will be same as sigma .
Default is -1 .

options.name: lum_expr
options.description: Set the luma expression.

options.name: cb_expr
options.description: Set the chrominance blue expression.

options.name: cr_expr
options.description: Set the chrominance red expression.

options.name: alpha_expr
options.description: Set the alpha expression.

options.name: red_expr
options.description: Set the red expression.

options.name: green_expr
options.description: Set the green expression.

options.name: blue_expr
options.description: Set the blue expression.

options.name: N
options.description: The sequential number of the filtered frame, starting from 0 .

options.name: X
options.description: The coordinates of the current sample.

options.name: W
options.description: The width and height of the image.

options.name: SW
options.description:
Width and height scale depending on the currently filtered plane. It is the
ratio between the corresponding luma plane number of pixels and the current
plane ones. E.g. for YUV4:2:0 the values are 1,1 for the luma plane, and 0.5,0.5 for chroma planes.

options.name: T
options.description: Time of the current frame, expressed in seconds.

options.name: p
options.description:
Return the value of the pixel at location ( x , y ) of the current
plane.

options.name: lum
options.description:
Return the value of the pixel at location ( x , y ) of the luma
plane.

options.name: cb
options.description:
Return the value of the pixel at location ( x , y ) of the
blue-difference chroma plane. Return 0 if there is no such plane.

options.name: cr
options.description:
Return the value of the pixel at location ( x , y ) of the
red-difference chroma plane. Return 0 if there is no such plane.

options.name: r
options.description:
Return the value of the pixel at location ( x , y ) of the
red/green/blue component. Return 0 if there is no such component.

options.name: alpha
options.description:
Return the value of the pixel at location ( x , y ) of the alpha
plane. Return 0 if there is no such plane.

options.name: psum(x
options.description:
Sum of sample values in the rectangle from (0,0) to (x,y), this allows obtaining
sums of samples within a rectangle. See the functions without the sum postfix.

options.name: interpolation
options.description:
Set one of interpolation methods:
Default is bilinear.

options.name: strength
options.description:
The maximum amount by which the filter will change any one pixel. This is also
the threshold for detecting nearly flat regions. Acceptable values range from
.51 to 64; the default value is 1.2. Out-of-range values will be clipped to the
valid range.

options.name: radius
options.description:
The neighborhood to fit the gradient to. A larger radius makes for smoother
gradients, but also prevents the filter from modifying the pixels near detailed
regions. Acceptable values are 8-32; the default value is 16. Out-of-range
values will be clipped to the valid range.

options.name: size
options.description: Set video output size. Default is hd720 .

options.name: opacity
options.description: Set video opacity. Default is 0.9 . Allowed range is from 0 to 1 .

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set output mode flags.
Available values for flags are:

possible_values.description: Show only filters with queued frames.
Show only filters with non-zero stats.
Show only filters with non-eof stat.
Show only filters that are enabled in timeline.

options.name: flags
options.description:
Set flags which enable which stats are shown in video.
Available values for flags are:

possible_values.description: Display number of queued frames in each link.
Display number of frames taken from filter.
Display number of frames given out from filter.
Display delta number of frames between above two values.
Display pts delta between current and previous frame.
Display time delta between current and previous frame.
Display time base for filter link.
Display used format for filter link.
Display video size or number of audio channels in case of audio used by filter link.
Display video frame rate or sample rate in case of audio used by filter link.
Display number of samples taken from filter.
Display number of samples given out from filter.
Display delta number of samples between above two values.

options.name: rate
options.description:
Set upper limit for video rate of output stream, Default value is 25 .
This guarantee that output video frame rate will not be higher than this value.

options.name: difford
options.description:
The order of differentiation to be applied on the scene. Must be chosen in the range
[0,2] and default value is 1.

options.name: minknorm
options.description:
The Minkowski parameter to be used for calculating the Minkowski distance. Must
be chosen in the range [0,20] and default value is 1. Set to 0 for getting
max value instead of calculating Minkowski distance.

options.name: sigma
options.description:
The standard deviation of Gaussian blur to be applied on the scene. Must be
chosen in the range [0,1024.0] and default value = 1. floor( sigma * break_off_sigma(3) )
can’t be equal to 0 if difford is greater than 0.

options.name: radius
options.description:
Set the box radius in pixels.
Allowed range is 1 to 20. Default is 3.

options.name: eps
options.description:
Set regularization parameter (with square).
Allowed range is 0 to 1. Default is 0.01.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set filter mode. Can be basic or fast .
Default is basic .

options.name: sub
options.description:
Set subsampling ratio for fast mode.
Range is 2 to 64. Default is 4.
No subsampling occurs in basic mode.

options.name: guidance
options.description:
Set guidance mode. Can be off or on . Default is off .
If off , single input is required.
If on , two inputs of the same resolution and pixel format are required.
The second input serves as the guidance.

options.name: planes
options.description: Set planes to filter. Default is first only.

options.name: clut
options.description:
Set which CLUT video frames will be processed from second input stream,
