options.name: filename
options.description:
Read the initial cellular automaton state, i.e. the starting row, from
the specified file.
In the file, each non-whitespace character is considered an alive
cell, a newline will terminate the row, and further characters in the
file will be ignored.

options.name: pattern
options.description:
Read the initial cellular automaton state, i.e. the starting row, from
the specified string.
Each non-whitespace character in the string is considered an alive
cell, a newline will terminate the row, and further characters in the
string will be ignored.

options.name: rate
options.description:
Set the video rate, that is the number of frames generated per second.
Default is 25.

options.name: random_fill_ratio
options.description:
Set the random fill ratio for the initial cellular automaton row. It
is a floating point number value ranging from 0 to 1, defaults to
1/PHI.
This option is ignored when a file or a pattern is specified.

options.name: random_seed
options.description:
Set the seed for filling randomly the initial row, must be an integer
included between 0 and UINT32_MAX. If not specified, or if explicitly
set to -1, the filter will try to use a good random seed on a best
effort basis.

options.name: rule
options.description:
Set the cellular automaton rule, it is a number ranging from 0 to 255.
Default value is 110.

options.name: size
options.description:
Set the size of the output video. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
If filename or pattern is specified, the size is set
by default to the width of the specified initial state row, and the
height is set to width * PHI.
If size is set, it must contain the width of the specified
pattern string, and the specified pattern will be centered in the
larger row.
If a filename or a pattern string is not specified, the size value
defaults to "320x518" (used for a randomly generated initial state).

options.name: scroll
options.description:
If set to 1, scroll the output upward when all the rows in the output
have been already filled. If set to 0, the new generated row will be
written over the top row just after the bottom row is filled.
Defaults to 1.

options.name: start_full
options.description:
If set to 1, completely fill the output with generated rows before
outputting the first frame.
This is the default behavior, for disabling set the value to 0.

options.name: stitch
options.description:
If set to 1, stitch the left and right row edges together.
This is the default behavior, for disabling set the value to 0.

options.name: list_generators
options.description:
List all available generators along with all their respective options as well as
possible minimum and maximum values along with the default values.

options.name: size
options.description:
Specify the size of the sourced video. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
The default value is 320x240 .

options.name: rate
options.description:
Specify the frame rate of the sourced video, as the number of frames
generated per second. It has to be a string in the format frame_rate_num / frame_rate_den , an integer number, a floating point
number or a valid video frame rate abbreviation. The default value is
"25".

options.name: sar
options.description: Set the sample aspect ratio of the sourced video.

options.name: duration
options.description:
Set the duration of the sourced video. See (ffmpeg-utils)the Time duration section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual for the accepted syntax.
If not specified, or the expressed duration is negative, the video is
supposed to be generated forever.

options.name: output_idx
options.description:
DXGI Output Index to capture.
Usually corresponds to the index Windows has given the screen minus one,
so it’s starting at 0.
Defaults to output 0.

options.name: draw_mouse
options.description:
Whether to draw the mouse cursor.
Defaults to true.
Only affects hardware cursors. If a game or application renders its own cursor,
it’ll always be captured.

options.name: framerate
options.description:
Maximum framerate at which the desktop will be captured - the interval between
successive frames will not be smaller than the inverse of the framerate. When dup_frames is true (the default) and the desktop is not being updated
often enough, the filter will duplicate a previous frame. Note that there is no
background buffering going on, so when the filter is not polled often enough
then the actual inter-frame interval may be significantly larger.
Defaults to 30 FPS.

options.name: video_size
options.description:
Specify the size of the captured video.
Defaults to the full size of the screen.
Cropped from the bottom/right if smaller than screen size.

options.name: offset_x
options.description: Horizontal offset of the captured video.

options.name: offset_y
options.description: Vertical offset of the captured video.

options.name: output_fmt
options.description:
Desired filter output format.
Defaults to 8 Bit BGRA.
It accepts the following values:

possible_values.description: Passes all supported output formats to DDA and returns what DDA decides to use.
8 Bit formats always work, and DDA will convert to them if necessary.
Filter initialization will fail if 10 bit format is requested but unavailable.

options.name: dup_frames
options.description:
When this option is set to true (the default), the filter will duplicate frames
when the desktop has not been updated in order to maintain approximately
constant target framerate. When this option is set to false, the filter will
wait for the desktop to be updated (inter-frame intervals may vary significantly
in this case).

options.name: window_title
options.description:
ECMAScript regular expression matched against the window title. Supports a
PCRE style (?i) prefix for case-insensitive matching.

options.name: window_class
options.description: As window_title , but matched against the window class name.

options.name: window_exe
options.description:
As window_title , but matched against the executable file name of the
window’s process.

options.name: monitor_idx
options.description:
Zero-based index of the monitor to capture.
Can also be set to window to capture the monitor the selected window
is displayed on at filter initialization time.

options.name: hwnd
options.description: Explicit native window handle (HWND).

options.name: hmonitor
options.description: Explicit native monitor handle (HMONITOR).

options.name: capture_cursor
options.description: Capture the mouse cursor. Enabled by default.

options.name: capture_border
options.description:
Capture the full area of the window, including its window decorarions/border.
Disabled by default.

options.name: display_border
options.description: Draw a yellow highlight border around the captured window. Disabled by default.

options.name: max_framerate
options.description:
Maximum capture frame rate. Accepts a video rate (e.g. 30 , 60/1 , 24000/1001 ). The default is 60 FPS.
The actual rate is the rate at which the compositor renders the window/monitor,
capped by this option.

options.name: width
options.description:
Force the output canvas width. If zero (default) the initial captured source
width is used. If a negative number is provided, the width will be rounded
down to the next multiple of that number.
See resize_mode .

options.name: height
options.description:
Force the output canvas height. If zero (default) the initial captured source
height is used. If a negative number is provided, the height will be rounded
down to the next multiple of that number.
See resize_mode .

options.name: crop_left
options.description: Crop this many pixels from the left side of the captured frames.

options.name: crop_top
options.description: Crop this many pixels from the left side of the captured frames.

options.name: crop_right
options.description: Crop this many pixels from the left side of the captured frames.

options.name: crop_bottom
options.description: Crop this many pixels from the left side of the captured frames.

options.name: premultiplied
options.description:
If set to 1, return frames with premultiplied alpha. Default is 0 (straight
alpha).

options.name: resize_mode
options.description:
Defines how the captured content is fitted into the output canvas size.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Crop (or pad with black) to the canvas size. (default)
Scale the source to fill the canvas, potentially altering aspect ratio.

Scale the source to fit inside the canvas while preserving aspect ratio.
Remaining area is filled with black.

options.name: scale_mode
options.description:
Scaling algorithm used when resizing is required.
Possible values:

possible_values.description: Bicubic filtering. Potentially more blurry, but fewer scaling artifacts depending on contents.

options.name: output_fmt
options.description:
Desired output pixel format inside the D3D11 hardware frames.
Possible values:

options.name: speed

options.name: start_y
options.description:
Set the initial y position. Must be a floating point value between
-100 and 100. Default value is -0.131825904205311970493132056385139.

options.name: rate

Specify the frame rate of the sourced video, as the number of frames

number or a valid video frame rate abbreviation. The default value is
"25".

options.name: duration

Set the duration of the sourced video. See (ffmpeg-utils)the Time duration section in the ffmpeg-utils(1) manual for the accepted syntax.
If not specified, or the expressed duration is negative, the video is
supposed to be generated forever.

options.name: test
options.description:
Set the number or the name of the test to perform. Supported tests are:
Default value is "all", which will cycle through the list of all tests.
options.default: "all", which will cycle through the list of all tests

possible_values.description: Set the maximum number of frames generated for each test, default value is 30.

options.name: size
options.description: The size of the video to generate. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .

options.name: framerate
options.description: The framerate of the generated video. It may be a string of the form num / den or a frame rate abbreviation.

options.name: filter_name
options.description:
The name to the frei0r source to load. For more information regarding frei0r and
how to set the parameters, read the frei0r section in the video filters
documentation.

options.name: filter_params
options.description: A ’|’-separated list of parameters to pass to the frei0r source.

options.name: filename
options.description:
Set the file from which to read the initial grid state. In the file,
each non-whitespace character is considered an alive cell, and newline
is used to delimit the end of each row.
If this option is not specified, the initial grid is generated
randomly.

options.name: rate
options.description:
Set the video rate, that is the number of frames generated per second.
Default is 25.

options.name: random_fill_ratio
options.description:
Set the random fill ratio for the initial random grid. It is a
floating point number value ranging from 0 to 1, defaults to 1/PHI.
It is ignored when a file is specified.

options.name: random_seed
options.description:
Set the seed for filling the initial random grid, must be an integer
included between 0 and UINT32_MAX. If not specified, or if explicitly
set to -1, the filter will try to use a good random seed on a best
effort basis.

options.name: rule
options.description:
Set the life rule.
A rule can be specified with a code of the kind "S NS /B NB ",
where NS and NB are sequences of numbers in the range 0-8, NS specifies the number of alive neighbor cells which make a
live cell stay alive, and NB the number of alive neighbor cells
which make a dead cell to become alive (i.e. to "born").
"s" and "b" can be used in place of "S" and "B", respectively.
Alternatively a rule can be specified by an 18-bits integer. The 9
high order bits are used to encode the next cell state if it is alive
for each number of neighbor alive cells, the low order bits specify
the rule for "borning" new cells. Higher order bits encode for an
higher number of neighbor cells.
For example the number 6153 = (12<<9)+9 specifies a stay alive
rule of 12 and a born rule of 9, which corresponds to "S23/B03".
Default value is "S23/B3", which is the original Conway’s game of life
rule, and will keep a cell alive if it has 2 or 3 neighbor alive
cells, and will born a new cell if there are three alive cells around
a dead cell.
options.default: "S23/B3", which is the original Conway’s game of life

options.name: size
options.description:
Set the size of the output video. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
If filename is specified, the size is set by default to the
same size of the input file. If size is set, it must contain
the size specified in the input file, and the initial grid defined in
that file is centered in the larger resulting area.
If a filename is not specified, the size value defaults to "320x240"
(used for a randomly generated initial grid).

options.name: stitch
options.description:
If set to 1, stitch the left and right grid edges together, and the
top and bottom edges also. Defaults to 1.

options.name: mold
options.description:
Set cell mold speed. If set, a dead cell will go from death_color to mold_color with a step of mold . mold can have a
value from 0 to 255.

options.name: life_color
options.description: Set the color of living (or new born) cells.

options.name: death_color
options.description:
Set the color of dead cells. If mold is set, this is the first color
used to represent a dead cell.

options.name: mold_color
options.description:
Set mold color, for definitely dead and moldy cells.
For the syntax of these 3 color options, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Color" section in the
ffmpeg-utils manual .

options.name: size
options.description:
Specify the size (width and height) of the buffered video frames. For the
syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is 320x240 .

options.name: rate
options.description:
Specify the frame rate expected for the video stream, expressed as a
number of frames per second. Default value is 25 .

options.name: octaves
options.description:
Specify the total number of components making up the noise, each one
with doubled frequency. Default value is 1 .

options.name: persistence
options.description:
Set the ratio used to compute the amplitude of the next octave
component with respect to the previous component amplitude. Default
value is 1 .

options.name: xscale
options.description:
Define a scale factor used to multiple the x, y coordinates. This can
be useful to define an effect with a pattern stretched along the x or
y axis. Default value is 1 .

options.name: tscale
options.description:
Define a scale factor used to multiple the time coordinate. This can
be useful to change the time variation speed. Default value is 1 .

options.name: random_mode
options.description:
Set random mode used to compute initial pattern.
Supported values are:
Default value is random .

possible_values.description:
Use the predefined initial pattern defined by Ken Perlin in the
original article, can be useful to compare the output with other
sources.
Use the value specified by random_seed option.

options.name: random_seed
options.description:
When random_mode is set to random_seed , use this value
to compute the initial pattern. Default value is 0 .

options.name: qrcode_width
options.description:
Specify an expression for the width of the rendered QR code, with and without
padding. The qrcode_width expression can reference the value set by the padded_qrcode_width expression, and vice versa.
By default padded_qrcode_width is set to qrcode_width , meaning that
there is no padding.
These expressions are evaluated only once, when initializing the source.
See the qrencode Expressions section for details.
Note that some of the constants are missing for the source (for example the x or t or  n ), since they only makes sense when evaluating the
expression for each frame rather than at initialization time.

options.name: rate
options.description:
Specify the frame rate of the sourced video, as the number of frames
generated per second. It has to be a string in the format frame_rate_num / frame_rate_den , an integer number, a floating point
number or a valid video frame rate abbreviation. The default value is
"25".

options.name: case_sensitive
options.description:
Instruct libqrencode to use case sensitive encoding. This is enabled by
default. This can be disabled to reduce the QR encoding size.

options.name: level
options.description:
Specify the QR encoding error correction level. With an higher correction level,
the encoding size will increase but the code will be more robust to corruption.
Lower level is L .
It accepts the following values:

options.name: expansion
options.description: Select how the input text is expanded. Can be either none , or normal (default). See the qrencode Text expansion section for details.

options.name: text
options.description:
Define the text to be rendered. In case neither is specified, no QR is encoded
(just an empty colored frame).
In case expansion is enabled, the text is treated as a text template, using the
qrencode expansion mechanism. See the qrencode
Text expansion section for details.

options.name: background_color
options.description:
Set the QR code and background color. The default value of foreground_color is "black", the default value of background_color is "white".
For the syntax of the color options, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Color"
section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .

options.name: level
options.description:
Specify the level of the Hald CLUT, only available in the haldclutsrc source. A level of N generates a picture of N*N*N by N*N*N pixels to be used as identity matrix for 3D lookup tables. Each component is
coded on a 1/(N*N) scale.

options.name: size
options.description:
Specify the video size for the output. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is hd720 .

options.name: scale
options.description:
Set display scale.
It accepts the following values:
Default is log .

options.name: ascale
options.description:
Set amplitude scale.
It accepts the following values:
Default is log .

options.name: acount
options.description:
Set how much frames to accumulate in histogram.
Default is 1. Setting this to -1 accumulates all frames.

options.name: rheight
options.description: Set histogram ratio of window height.

options.name: slide
options.description:
Set sonogram sliding.
It accepts the following values:
Default is replace .

possible_values.description: replace old rows with new ones.

options.name: hmode
options.description:
Set histogram mode.
It accepts the following values:
Default is abs .

options.name: rate
options.description: Set the output frame rate. Default value is 25 .

options.name: size
options.description:
Set the video size for the output. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is 800x400 .

options.name: rc
options.description:
Specify the red, green, blue contrast. Default values are 2 , 7 and 1 .
Allowed range is [0, 255] .

options.name: mpc
options.description: Set color which will be used for drawing median phase. If color is none which is default, no median phase value will be drawn.

options.name: video
options.description: Enable video output. Default is enabled.

options.name: phasing
options.description: Enable mono and out of phase detection. Default is disabled.

options.name: tolerance
options.description:
Set phase tolerance for mono detection, in amplitude ratio. Default is 0 .
Allowed range is [0, 1] .

options.name: angle
options.description:
Set angle threshold for out of phase detection, in degree. Default is 170 .
Allowed range is [90, 180] .

options.name: duration
options.description: Set mono or out of phase duration until notification, expressed in seconds. Default is 2 .

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set the vectorscope mode.
Available values are:
Default value is ‘ lissajous ’.

possible_values.description: Same as above but not rotated.

options.name: size
options.description:
Set the video size for the output. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is 400x400 .

options.name: rate
options.description: Set the output frame rate. Default value is 25 .

options.name: rc
options.description:
Specify the red, green, blue and alpha contrast. Default values are 40 , 160 , 80 and 255 .
Allowed range is [0, 255] .

options.name: rf
options.description:
Specify the red, green, blue and alpha fade. Default values are 15 , 10 , 5 and 5 .
Allowed range is [0, 255] .

options.name: zoom
options.description:
Set the zoom factor. Default value is 1 . Allowed range is [0, 10] .
Values lower than 1 will auto adjust zoom factor to maximal possible value.

options.name: draw
options.description:
Set the vectorscope drawing mode.
Available values are:
Default value is ‘ dot ’.

possible_values.description: Draw line between previous and current sample.
Draw anti-aliased line between previous and current sample.

options.name: scale
options.description:
Specify amplitude scale of audio samples.
Available values are:

options.name: swap
options.description: Swap left channel axis with right channel axis.

options.name: mirror
options.description: Mirror axis.

options.name: action
options.description:
Start or stop a timer.
Available values are:

possible_values.description: Get the current time, set it as frame metadata (using the key lavfi.bench.start_time ), and forward the frame to the next filter.

Get the current time and fetch the lavfi.bench.start_time metadata from
the input frame metadata to get the time difference. Time difference, average,
maximum and minimum time (respectively t , avg , max and min ) are then printed. The timestamps are expressed in seconds.

options.name: n
options.description: Set the number of segments. Default is 2.

options.name: v
options.description:
Set the number of output video streams, that is also the number of video
streams in each segment. Default is 1.

options.name: a
options.description:
Set the number of output audio streams, that is also the number of audio
streams in each segment. Default is 0.

options.name: unsafe
options.description: Activate unsafe mode: do not fail if segments have a different format.

options.name: next
options.description: Close the current segment and step to the next one

options.name: video
options.description:
Activate the video output. The audio stream is passed unchanged whether this
option is set or no. The video stream will be the first output stream if
activated. Default is 0 .

options.name: size
options.description:
Set the video size. This option is for video only. For the syntax of this
option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default and minimum resolution is 640x480 .

options.name: meter
options.description:
Set the EBU scale meter. Default is 9 . Common values are 9 and 18 , respectively for EBU scale meter +9 and EBU scale meter +18. Any
other integer value between this range is allowed.

options.name: metadata
options.description:
Set metadata injection. If set to 1 , the audio input will be segmented
into 100ms output frames, each of them containing various loudness information
in metadata.  All the metadata keys are prefixed with lavfi.r128. .
Default is 0 .

options.name: framelog
options.description:
Force the frame logging level.
Available values are:
By default, the logging level is set to info . If the video or
the metadata options are set, it switches to verbose .

options.name: peak
options.description:
Set peak mode(s).
Available modes can be cumulated (the option is a flag type). Possible
values are:

possible_values.description:
Enable sample-peak mode.
Simple peak mode looking for the higher sample value. It logs a message
for sample-peak (identified by SPK ).

Enable true-peak mode.
If enabled, the peak lookup is done on an over-sampled version of the input
stream for better peak accuracy. It logs a message for true-peak.
(identified by TPK ) and true-peak per frame (identified by FTPK ).
This mode requires a build with libswresample .

options.name: dualmono
options.description:
Treat mono input files as "dual mono". If a mono file is intended for playback
on a stereo system, its EBU R128 measurement will be perceptually incorrect.
If set to true , this option will compensate for this effect.
Multi-channel input files are not affected by this option.

options.name: panlaw
options.description:
Set a specific pan law to be used for the measurement of dual mono files.
This parameter is optional, and has a default value of -3.01dB.

options.name: target
options.description:
Set a specific target level (in LUFS) used as relative zero in the visualization.
This parameter is optional and has a default value of -23LUFS as specified
by EBU R128. However, material published online may prefer a level of -16LUFS
(e.g. for use with podcasts or video platforms).

options.name: gauge
options.description:
Set the value displayed by the gauge. Valid values are momentary and s shortterm . By default the momentary value will be used, but in certain
scenarios it may be more useful to observe the short term value instead (e.g.
live mixing).

options.name: scale
options.description:
Sets the display scale for the loudness. Valid parameters are absolute (in LUFS) or relative (LU) relative to the target. This only affects the
video output, not the summary or continuous log output.

options.name: integrated
options.description: Read-only exported value for measured integrated loudness, in LUFS.

options.name: range
options.description: Read-only exported value for measured loudness range, in LU.

options.name: lra_low
options.description: Read-only exported value for measured LRA low, in LUFS.

options.name: lra_high
options.description: Read-only exported value for measured LRA high, in LUFS.

options.name: sample_peak
options.description: Read-only exported value for measured sample peak, in dBFS.

options.name: true_peak
options.description: Read-only exported value for measured true peak, in dBFS.

options.name: nb_inputs
options.description: Set the number of different inputs, it is 2 by default.

options.name: duration
options.description: How to determine the end-of-stream.

possible_values.description: The duration of the longest input. (default)
The duration of the shortest input.
The duration of the first input.

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set mode of operation of the filter.
Can be one of the following:

possible_values.description:
If both value and key is set, select frames
which have such metadata. If only key is set, select
every frame that has such key in metadata.

Add new metadata key and value . If key is already available
do nothing.
Modify value of already present key.

If value is set, delete only keys that have such value.
Otherwise, delete key. If key is not set, delete all metadata values in
the frame.

Print key and its value if metadata was found. If key is not set print all
metadata values available in frame.

options.name: key
options.description: Set key used with all modes. Must be set for all modes except print and delete .

options.name: value
options.description: Set metadata value which will be used. This option is mandatory for modify and add mode.

options.name: function
options.description:
Which function to use when comparing metadata value and value .
Can be one of following:

possible_values.description: Values are interpreted as strings, returns true if metadata value is same as value .

Values are interpreted as strings, returns true if metadata value starts with
the value option string.
Values are interpreted as floats, returns true if metadata value is less than value .
Values are interpreted as floats, returns true if value is equal with metadata value.
Values are interpreted as floats, returns true if metadata value is greater than value .
Values are interpreted as floats, returns true if expression from option expr evaluates to true.

Values are interpreted as strings, returns true if metadata value ends with
the value option string.

options.name: expr
options.description:
Set expression which is used when function is set to expr .
The expression is evaluated through the eval API and can contain the following
constants:

possible_values.description: Float representation of value from metadata key.
Float representation of value as supplied by user in value option.

options.name: file
options.description:
If specified in print mode, output is written to the named file. Instead of
plain filename any writable url can be specified. Filename “-” is a shorthand
for standard output. If file option is not set, output is written to the log
with AV_LOG_INFO loglevel.

options.name: direct
options.description: Reduces buffering in print mode when output is written to a URL set using file .

options.name: mode
options.description:
Select the permissions mode.
It accepts the following values:

possible_values.description: Do nothing. This is the default.
Set all the output frames read-only.
Set all the output frames directly writable.
Make the frame read-only if writable, and writable if read-only.
Set each output frame read-only or writable randomly.

options.name: seed
options.description:
Set the seed for the random mode, must be an integer included between 0 and UINT32_MAX . If not specified, or if explicitly set to -1 , the filter will try to use a good random seed on a best effort
basis.

options.name: limit
options.description:
Time limit for the pauses. Any pause longer than that will be considered
a timestamp discontinuity and reset the timer. Default is 2 seconds.

options.name: speed
options.description:
Speed factor for processing. The value must be a float larger than zero.
Values larger than 1.0 will result in faster than realtime processing,
smaller will slow processing down. The limit is automatically adapted
accordingly. Default is 1.0.
A processing speed faster than what is possible without these filters cannot
be achieved.

options.name: timestamps
options.description:
Timestamps of output segments separated by ’|’. The first segment will run
from the beginning of the input stream. The last segment will run until
the end of the input stream

options.name: frames
options.description: Exact frame/sample count to split the segments.

options.name: expr
options.description:
Set expression, which is evaluated for each input frame.
If the expression is evaluated to zero, the frame is discarded.
If the evaluation result is negative or NaN, the frame is sent to the
first output; otherwise it is sent to the output with index ceil(val)-1 , assuming that the input index starts from 0.
For example a value of 1.2 corresponds to the output with index ceil(1.2)-1 = 2-1 = 1 , that is the second output.

options.name: outputs
options.description:
Set the number of outputs. The output to which to send the selected
frame is based on the result of the evaluation. Default value is 1.

options.name: n
options.description: The (sequential) number of the filtered frame, starting from 0.

options.name: selected_n
options.description: The (sequential) number of the selected frame, starting from 0.

options.name: prev_selected_n
options.description: The sequential number of the last selected frame. It’s NAN if undefined.

options.name: TB
options.description: The timebase of the input timestamps.

options.name: pts
options.description:
The PTS (Presentation TimeStamp) of the filtered frame,
expressed in TB units. It’s NAN if undefined.

options.name: t
options.description:
The PTS of the filtered frame,
expressed in seconds. It’s NAN if undefined.

options.name: prev_pts
options.description: The PTS of the previously filtered frame. It’s NAN if undefined.

options.name: prev_selected_pts
options.description: The PTS of the last previously filtered frame. It’s NAN if undefined.

options.name: prev_selected_t
options.description: The PTS of the last previously selected frame, expressed in seconds. It’s NAN if undefined.

options.name: start_pts
options.description: The first PTS in the stream which is not NAN. It remains NAN if not found.

options.name: start_t
options.description: The first PTS, in seconds, in the stream which is not NAN. It remains NAN if not found.

options.name: pict_type
options.description:
The type of the filtered frame. It can assume one of the following
values:

options.name: interlace_type
options.description: The frame interlace type. It can assume one of the following values:

options.name: consumed_sample_n
options.description: the number of selected samples before the current frame

options.name: samples_n
options.description: the number of samples in the current frame

options.name: sample_rate
options.description: the input sample rate

options.name: key
options.description: This is 1 if the filtered frame is a key-frame, 0 otherwise.

options.name: pos
options.description:
the position in the file of the filtered frame, -1 if the information
is not available (e.g. for synthetic video); deprecated, do not use

options.name: scene
options.description:
value between 0 and 1 to indicate a new scene; a low value reflects a low
probability for the current frame to introduce a new scene, while a higher
value means the current frame is more likely to be one (see the example below)

options.name: concatdec_select
options.description:
The concat demuxer can select only part of a concat input file by setting an
inpoint and an outpoint, but the output packets may not be entirely contained
in the selected interval. By using this variable, it is possible to skip frames
generated by the concat demuxer which are not exactly contained in the selected
interval.
This works by comparing the frame pts against the lavf.concat.start_time and the lavf.concat.duration packet metadata values which are also
present in the decoded frames.
The concatdec_select variable is -1 if the frame pts is at least
start_time and either the duration metadata is missing or the frame pts is less
than start_time + duration, 0 otherwise, and NaN if the start_time metadata is
missing.
That basically means that an input frame is selected if its pts is within the
interval set by the concat demuxer.

options.name: iw
options.description: Represents the width of the input video frame.

options.name: ih
options.description: Represents the height of the input video frame.

options.name: view
options.description: View ID for multi-view video.

options.name: commands
options.description: Set the commands to be read and sent to the other filters.

options.name: filename
options.description:
Set the filename of the commands to be read and sent to the other
filters.

options.name: enter
options.description:
The command is sent when the current frame timestamp enters the
specified interval. In other words, the command is sent when the
previous frame timestamp was not in the given interval, and the
current is.

options.name: leave
options.description:
The command is sent when the current frame timestamp leaves the
specified interval. In other words, the command is sent when the
previous frame timestamp was in the given interval, and the
current is not.

options.name: expr
options.description:
The command ARG is interpreted as expression and result of
expression is passed as ARG .
The expression is evaluated through the eval API and can contain the following
constants:

possible_values.description:
Original position in the file of the frame, or undefined if undefined
for the current frame. Deprecated, do not use.
The count of the input frame for video or audio, starting from 0.
The time in seconds of the current frame.
The start time in seconds of the current command interval.
The end time in seconds of the current command interval.
The interpolated time of the current command interval, TI = (T - TS) / (TE - TS).

options.name: expr
options.description: The expression which is evaluated for each frame to construct its timestamp.

options.name: strip_fps
options.description:
Boolean option which determines if the original framerate and frame duration
metadata is unset. If set to true, be advised that a sane frame rate should be
explicitly specified if output is sent to a constant frame rate muxer.
Default is false .

options.name: FRAME_RATE
options.description: frame rate, only defined for constant frame-rate video

options.name: PTS
options.description: The presentation timestamp in input

options.name: N
options.description:
The count of the input frame for video or the number of consumed samples,
not including the current frame for audio, starting from 0.

options.name: NB_CONSUMED_SAMPLES
options.description:
The number of consumed samples, not including the current frame (only
audio)

options.name: NB_SAMPLES
options.description: The number of samples in the current frame (only audio)

options.name: SAMPLE_RATE
options.description: The audio sample rate.

options.name: STARTPTS
options.description: The PTS of the first frame.

options.name: STARTT
options.description: the time in seconds of the first frame

options.name: INTERLACED
options.description: State whether the current frame is interlaced.

options.name: T
options.description: the time in seconds of the current frame

options.name: POS
options.description:
original position in the file of the frame, or undefined if undefined
for the current frame; deprecated, do not use

options.name: PREV_INPTS
options.description: The previous input PTS.

options.name: PREV_INT
options.description: previous input time in seconds

options.name: PREV_OUTPTS
options.description: The previous output PTS.

options.name: PREV_OUTT
options.description: previous output time in seconds

options.name: RTCTIME
options.description:
The wallclock (RTC) time in microseconds. This is deprecated, use time(0)
instead.

options.name: RTCSTART
options.description: The wallclock (RTC) time at the start of the movie in microseconds.

options.name: TB
options.description: The timebase of the input timestamps.

options.name: T_CHANGE
options.description: Time of the first frame after command was applied or time of the first frame if no commands.

options.name: range
options.description: Available values are:

possible_values.description: Keep the same color range property.
Set the color range as unspecified.
Set the color range as limited.
Set the color range as full.

options.name: expr
options.description: The expression which is evaluated into the output timebase.

options.name: size
options.description:
Specify the video size for the output. It must be even. For the syntax of this option,
check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is 1920x1080 .

options.name: fps
options.description: Set the output frame rate. Default value is 25 .

options.name: bar_h
options.description:
Set the bargraph height. It must be even. Default value is -1 which
computes the bargraph height automatically.

options.name: axis_h
options.description:
Set the axis height. It must be even. Default value is -1 which computes
the axis height automatically.

options.name: sono_h
options.description:
Set the sonogram height. It must be even. Default value is -1 which
computes the sonogram height automatically.

options.name: fullhd
options.description: Set the fullhd resolution. This option is deprecated, use size , s instead. Default value is 1 .

options.name: sono_v
options.description:
Specify the sonogram volume expression. It can contain variables:
and functions:
Default value is 16 .

possible_values.description: the frequency where it is evaluated

options.name: bar_v
options.description:
Specify the bargraph volume expression. It can contain variables:
and functions:
Default value is sono_v .

possible_values.description: the frequency where it is evaluated

options.name: sono_g
options.description:
Specify the sonogram gamma. Lower gamma makes the spectrum more contrast,
higher gamma makes the spectrum having more range. Default value is 3 .
Acceptable range is [1, 7] .

options.name: bar_g
options.description: Specify the bargraph gamma. Default value is 1 . Acceptable range is [1, 7] .

options.name: bar_t
options.description:
Specify the bargraph transparency level. Lower value makes the bargraph sharper.
Default value is 1 . Acceptable range is [0, 1] .

options.name: timeclamp
options.description:
Specify the transform timeclamp. At low frequency, there is trade-off between
accuracy in time domain and frequency domain. If timeclamp is lower,
event in time domain is represented more accurately (such as fast bass drum),
otherwise event in frequency domain is represented more accurately
(such as bass guitar). Acceptable range is [0.002, 1] . Default value is 0.17 .

options.name: attack
options.description:
Set attack time in seconds. The default is 0 (disabled). Otherwise, it
limits future samples by applying asymmetric windowing in time domain, useful
when low latency is required. Accepted range is [0, 1] .

options.name: basefreq
options.description:
Specify the transform base frequency. Default value is 20.01523126408007475 ,
which is frequency 50 cents below E0. Acceptable range is [10, 100000] .

options.name: endfreq
options.description:
Specify the transform end frequency. Default value is 20495.59681441799654 ,
which is frequency 50 cents above D#10. Acceptable range is [10, 100000] .

options.name: coeffclamp
options.description: This option is deprecated and ignored.

options.name: tlength
options.description:
Specify the transform length in time domain. Use this option to control accuracy
trade-off between time domain and frequency domain at every frequency sample.
It can contain variables:
Default value is 384*tc/(384+tc*f) .

possible_values.description: the frequency where it is evaluated

options.name: count
options.description:
Specify the transform count for every video frame. Default value is 6 .
Acceptable range is [1, 30] .

options.name: fcount
options.description:
Specify the transform count for every single pixel. Default value is 0 ,
which makes it computed automatically. Acceptable range is [0, 10] .

options.name: fontfile
options.description:
Specify font file for use with freetype to draw the axis. If not specified,
use embedded font. Note that drawing with font file or embedded font is not
implemented with custom basefreq and endfreq , use axisfile option instead.

options.name: font
options.description:
Specify fontconfig pattern. This has lower priority than fontfile . The : in the pattern may be replaced by | to avoid unnecessary
escaping.

options.name: fontcolor

possible_values.description: the frequency where it is evaluated

options.name: axisfile
options.description: Specify image file to draw the axis. This option override fontfile and fontcolor option.

options.name: axis
options.description:
Enable/disable drawing text to the axis. If it is set to 0 , drawing to
the axis is disabled, ignoring fontfile and axisfile option.
Default value is 1 .

options.name: csp
options.description: Set colorspace. The accepted values are:

options.name: cscheme
options.description:
Set spectrogram color scheme. This is list of floating point values with format left_r|left_g|left_b|right_r|right_g|right_b .
The default is 1|0.5|0|0|0.5|1 .

options.name: size
options.description:
Specify the video size for the output. For the syntax of this option,
check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is 640x512 .

options.name: rate
options.description: Set the output frame rate. Default value is 25 .

options.name: scale
options.description:
Set the frequency scale used. Allowed values are:
Default value is linear .

options.name: iscale
options.description:
Set the intensity scale used. Allowed values are:
Default value is log .

options.name: min
options.description:
Set the minimum frequency that will be used in output.
Default is 20 Hz.

options.name: max
options.description:
Set the maximum frequency that will be used in output.
Default is 20000 Hz. The real frequency upper limit
depends on input audio’s sample rate and such will be enforced
on this value when it is set to value greater than Nyquist frequency.

options.name: imin
options.description: Set the minimum intensity that will be used in output.

options.name: imax
options.description: Set the maximum intensity that will be used in output.

options.name: logb
options.description:
Set the logarithmic basis for brightness strength when
mapping calculated magnitude values to pixel values.
Allowed range is from 0 to 1 .
Default value is 0.0001 .

options.name: deviation
options.description:
Set the frequency deviation.
Lower values than 1 are more frequency oriented,
while higher values than 1 are more time oriented.
Allowed range is from 0 to 10 .
Default value is 1 .

options.name: pps
options.description:
Set the number of pixel output per each second in one row.
Allowed range is from 1 to 1024 .
Default value is 64 .

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set the output visual mode. Allowed values are:
Default value is magnitude .

possible_values.description:
Show combination of magnitude and phase.
Magnitude is mapped to brightness and phase to color.
Show unique color per channel magnitude.
Show unique color per stereo difference.

options.name: slide
options.description: Set the output slide method. Allowed values are:

options.name: direction
options.description: Set the direction method for output slide method. Allowed values are:

options.name: bar
options.description: Set the ratio of bargraph display to display size. Default is 0.

options.name: rotation
options.description:
Set color rotation, must be in [-1.0, 1.0] range.
Default value is 0 .

options.name: size
options.description:
Specify size of video. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default is 1024x512 .

options.name: rate
options.description: Set video rate. Default is 25 .

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set display mode.
This set how each frequency bin will be represented.
It accepts the following values:

options.name: ascale
options.description:
Set amplitude scale.
It accepts the following values:

options.name: fscale
options.description:
Set frequency scale.
It accepts the following values:

options.name: win_size

options.name: win_func

options.name: overlap
options.description:
Set window overlap. In range [0, 1] . Default is 1 ,
which means optimal overlap for selected window function will be picked.

options.name: averaging
options.description:
Set time averaging. Setting this to 0 will display current maximal peaks.
Default is 1 , which means time averaging is disabled.
options.default: 1 , which means time averaging is disabled

options.name: colors
options.description:
Specify list of colors separated by space or by ’|’ which will be used to
draw channel frequencies. Unrecognized or missing colors will be replaced
by white color.

options.name: cmode
options.description:
Set channel display mode.
It accepts the following values:
Default is combined .

options.name: minamp
options.description: Set minimum amplitude used in log amplitude scaler.

options.name: data
options.description:
Set data display mode.
It accepts the following values:
Default is magnitude .

options.name: channels
options.description: Set channels to use when processing audio. By default all are processed.

options.name: size
options.description:
Specify the video size for the output. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is 512x512 .

options.name: win_size
options.description: Set window size. Allowed range is from 1024 to 65536 . Default size is 4096 .

options.name: win_func
options.description:
Set window function.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is hann .

Specify the video size for the output. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is 640x512 .

options.name: slide
options.description:
Specify how the spectrum should slide along the window.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is replace .

possible_values.description: the samples start again on the left when they reach the right
the samples scroll from right to left
frames are only produced when the samples reach the right
the samples scroll from left to right
the samples start again on the right when they reach the left

options.name: mode
options.description:
Specify display mode.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is ‘ combined ’.

possible_values.description: all channels are displayed in the same row
all channels are displayed in separate rows

options.name: color
options.description:
Specify display color mode.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is ‘ channel ’.

possible_values.description: each channel is displayed in a separate color
each channel is displayed using the same color scheme
each channel is displayed using the rainbow color scheme
each channel is displayed using the moreland color scheme
each channel is displayed using the nebulae color scheme
each channel is displayed using the fire color scheme

options.name: scale
options.description:
Specify scale used for calculating intensity color values.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is ‘ sqrt ’.

options.name: fscale
options.description:
Specify frequency scale.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is ‘ lin ’.

options.name: saturation
options.description:
Set saturation modifier for displayed colors. Negative values provide
alternative color scheme. 0 is no saturation at all.
Saturation must be in [-10.0, 10.0] range.
Default value is 1 .

options.name: win_func
options.description:
Set window function.
It accepts the following values:
Default value is hann .

options.name: orientation
options.description: Set orientation of time vs frequency axis. Can be vertical or horizontal . Default is vertical .

options.name: overlap
options.description:
Set ratio of overlap window. Default value is 0 .
When value is 1 overlap is set to recommended size for specific
window function currently used.

options.name: gain
options.description:
Set scale gain for calculating intensity color values.
Default value is 1 .

options.name: data
options.description:
Set which data to display. Can be magnitude , default or phase ,
or unwrapped phase: uphase .

options.name: rotation
options.description:
Set color rotation, must be in [-1.0, 1.0] range.
Default value is 0 .

options.name: start
options.description: Set start frequency from which to display spectrogram. Default is 0 .

options.name: stop
options.description: Set stop frequency to which to display spectrogram. Default is 0 .

options.name: fps
options.description: Set upper frame rate limit. Default is auto , unlimited.

options.name: legend
options.description: Draw time and frequency axes and legends. Default is disabled.

options.name: drange
options.description:
Set dynamic range used to calculate intensity color values. Default is 120 dBFS.
Allowed range is from 10 to 200.

options.name: limit
options.description:
Set upper limit of input audio samples volume in dBFS. Default is 0 dBFS.
Allowed range is from -100 to 100.

options.name: opacity
options.description: Set opacity strength when using pixel format output with alpha component.

options.description:
Set volume color expression.
The expression can use the following variables:

options.name: t
options.description: If set, displays channel names. Default is enabled.

options.name: v
options.description: If set, displays volume values. Default is enabled.

options.name: o
options.description:
Set orientation, can be horizontal: h or vertical: v ,
default is h .

options.name: s
options.description:
Set step size, allowed range is [0, 5]. Default is 0, which means
step is disabled.

options.name: p
options.description: Set background opacity, allowed range is [0, 1]. Default is 0.

options.name: m
options.description:
Set metering mode, can be peak: p or rms: r ,
default is p .

options.name: ds
options.description:
Set display scale, can be linear: lin or log: log ,
default is lin .

options.name: dm
options.description:
In second.
If set to > 0., display a line for the max level
in the previous seconds.
default is disabled: 0.

options.name: dmc
options.description:
The color of the max line. Use when dm option is set to > 0.
default is: orange

options.name: size
options.description:
Specify the video size for the output. For the syntax of this option, check the (ffmpeg-utils)"Video size" section in the ffmpeg-utils manual .
Default value is 600x240 .

options.name: mode
options.description:
Set display mode.
Available values are:
Default value is point .

possible_values.description: Draw a point for each sample.
Draw a vertical line for each sample.
Draw a point for each sample and a line between them.
Draw a centered vertical line for each sample.

options.name: n
options.description:
Set the number of samples which are printed on the same column. A
larger value will decrease the frame rate. Must be a positive
integer. This option can be set only if the value for rate is not explicitly specified.

options.name: rate
options.description:
Set the (approximate) output frame rate. This is done by setting the
option n . Default value is "25".

options.name: split_channels
options.description: Set if channels should be drawn separately or overlap. Default value is 0.

options.name: colors
options.description: Set colors separated by ’|’ which are going to be used for drawing of each channel.

options.name: scale
options.description:
Set amplitude scale.
Available values are:
Default is linear.

options.name: draw
options.description:
Set the draw mode. This is mostly useful to set for high n .
Available values are:
Default value is scale .

possible_values.description: Scale pixel values for each drawn sample.

Set mode of operation of the filter.

Set side data type used with all modes. Must be set for select mode.

options.name: sample_rate
options.description:
Specify sample rate of output audio, the sample rate of audio from which
spectrum was generated may differ.

options.name: channels
options.description: Set number of channels represented in input video spectrums.

options.name: scale
options.description:
Set scale which was used when generating magnitude input spectrum.
Can be lin or log . Default is log .

options.name: slide
options.description:
Set slide which was used when generating inputs spectrums.
Can be replace , scroll , fullframe or rscroll .
Default is fullframe .

options.name: win_func
options.description: Set window function used for resynthesis.

options.name: overlap
options.description:
Set window overlap. In range [0, 1] . Default is 1 ,
which means optimal overlap for selected window function will be picked.

options.name: orientation
options.description:
Set orientation of input videos. Can be vertical or horizontal .
Default is vertical .

options.name: size
options.description: Set output video size. Default value is hd720 .

options.name: framerate
options.description: Set output video frame rate. Default value is 30 .

options.name: samplerate
options.description: Set output audio sample rate. Default value is 44100 .

options.name: amplitude
options.description: Set output audio beep amplitude. Default value is 0.7 .

options.name: period
options.description: Set output audio beep period in seconds. Default value is 3 .

options.name: delay
options.description: Set output video flash delay in number of frames. Default value is 0 .

options.name: cycle
options.description: Enable cycling of video delays, by default is disabled.

options.name: duration
options.description: Set stream output duration. By default duration is unlimited.

options.name: fg
options.description: Set foreground/background/additional color.

options.name: filename
options.description:
The name of the resource to read (not necessarily a file; it can also be a
device or a stream accessed through some protocol).

options.name: format_name
options.description:
Specifies the format assumed for the movie to read, and can be either
the name of a container or an input device. If not specified, the
format is guessed from movie_name or by probing.

options.name: seek_point
options.description:
Specifies the seek point in seconds. The frames will be output
starting from this seek point. The parameter is evaluated with av_strtod , so the numerical value may be suffixed by an IS
postfix. The default value is "0".

options.name: streams
options.description:
Specifies the streams to read. Several streams can be specified,
separated by "+". The source will then have as many outputs, in the
same order. The syntax is explained in the (ffmpeg)"Stream specifiers"
section in the ffmpeg manual . Two special names, "dv" and "da" specify
respectively the default (best suited) video and audio stream. Default
is "dv", or "da" if the filter is called as "amovie".
options.default: "dv", or "da" if the filter is called as "amovie"

options.name: stream_index
options.description:
Specifies the index of the video stream to read. If the value is -1,
the most suitable video stream will be automatically selected. The default
value is "-1". Deprecated. If the filter is called "amovie", it will select
audio instead of video.

options.name: loop
options.description:
Specifies how many times to read the stream in sequence.
If the value is 0, the stream will be looped infinitely.
Default value is "1".
Note that when the movie is looped the source timestamps are not
changed, so it will generate non monotonically increasing timestamps.

options.name: discontinuity
options.description:
Specifies the time difference between frames above which the point is
considered a timestamp discontinuity which is removed by adjusting the later
timestamps.

options.name: dec_threads
options.description: Specifies the number of threads for decoding

options.name: format_opts
options.description:
Specify format options for the opened file. Format options can be specified
as a list of key = value pairs separated by ’:’. The following example
shows how to add protocol_whitelist and protocol_blacklist options:

options.name: seek
options.description:
Perform seek using "av_seek_frame".
The syntax is: seek stream_index | timestamp | flags

options.name: get_duration
options.description: Get movie duration in AV_TIME_BASE units.
